sessions.title: Pre-UC Attendee Orientation
sessions.abstract: Join us for a presentation on how to get the most out of your User Conference experience. We'll be highlighting the key places to find information and providing tips on how to prepare before the conference for both virtual and in-person attendees.

sessions.title: Hilton Badge Pick-Up Social Hour
sessions.abstract: Avoid the rush of Day 1 by arriving early to pick up your attendee badge. Maximize your networking opportunities while enjoying light refreshments.

sessions.title: Badge Pick-Up Social Hour
sessions.abstract: Join us for the "Badge Pick-Up Social Hour"! Avoid the rush of Day 1 by arriving early to pick up your attendee badge. Maximize your networking opportunities while enjoying light refreshments. If you’re flying in and miss the badge pick-up at the airport, this is the perfect opportunity to still receive your badge early.

sessions.title: Attendee Orientation
sessions.abstract: Join other UC attendees for a presentation on how to get the most out of your User Conference experience. Discover insider tips and helpful tools designed to help you make meaningful connections and leave feeling confident and prepared for your week.

sessions.title: Education Social
sessions.abstract: Collaboration breeds opportunity. Spend the evening growing your professional network over complimentary drinks and food. Exchange experiences and ideas with your peers and find collaborators for your next project.

sessions.title: Public Safety and National Security Social
sessions.abstract: Take time to relax and have fun with like-minded professionals: chat about your favorite sessions, share ideas, and grow your network.

sessions.title: U.S. Army Sustainable Range Program (SRP) GIS Forum
sessions.abstract: The U.S. Army Sustainable Range Program (SRP) GIS Forum is a private meeting to U.S. Army SRP personnel only. Hosted by Ranges Training Division, this forum provides in-person, peer-to-peer collaboration. Roadmap, program initiatives, and common issues will be discussed.

sessions.title: User Conference Plenary: Part 1
sessions.abstract: This session is the first part of a three-part full-day conference plenary.  Join us for an incredible plenary session and set the stage for your UC week.  Jack Dangermond will open the plenary by sharing examples of incredible work from the GIS user community, his vision for the future and advancements in ArcGIS technology.  Throughout the day, conference attendees will see GIS technology in action through a variety of presentations and demonstrations showcasing new products and capabilities, ArcGIS updates and enhancements, and the inspiring work of the global GIS community.

sessions.title: User Conference Plenary: Part 2
sessions.abstract: This session is the second part of a three-part full-day conference plenary.  Join us for an incredible plenary session and set the stage for your UC week.  Jack Dangermond will open the plenary by sharing examples of incredible work from the GIS user community, his vision for the future and advancements in ArcGIS technology.  Throughout the day, conference attendees will see GIS technology in action through a variety of presentations and demonstrations showcasing new products and capabilities, ArcGIS updates and enhancements, and the inspiring work of the global GIS community.

sessions.title: Speaker Resource Center
sessions.abstract: Are you presenting at the User Conference? Visit the Speaker Resource Center (SRC) to finalize your presentation, practice out loud, and receive personalized coaching and feedback from Esri Instructors. The SRC is fully equipped with projectors, computers, and dedicated workspaces to help you present with confidence.

sessions.title: Presentation Skills Workshop
sessions.abstract: Learn techniques for building and delivering professional presentations including slide authoring, demo skills and public speaking fundamentals.

sessions.title: User Conference Plenary: Conclusion
sessions.abstract: This session is the third part of a three-part full-day conference plenary.  Join us for an incredible plenary session and set the stage for your UC week.  Jack Dangermond will open the plenary by sharing examples of incredible work from the GIS user community, his vision for the future and advancements in ArcGIS technology.  Throughout the day, conference attendees will see GIS technology in action through a variety of presentations and demonstrations showcasing new products and capabilities, ArcGIS updates and enhancements, and the inspiring work of the global GIS community.

sessions.title: Academic GIS Fair
sessions.abstract: Expand your education! At the Academic GIS Fair , you will have the opportunity to meet with representatives from colleges and universities to discuss their academic GIS programs and educational offerings.

sessions.title: Map Gallery Reception
sessions.abstract: The Map Gallery opens with an evening reception on Monday, July 13, where you can meet participating map authors and special display organizations and discuss their work with them.

sessions.title: Out in GIS Social
sessions.abstract: All queer and ally UC attendees are warmly invited to join us for the Out in GIS Social. Enjoy complimentary food, refreshments, and plenty of fun as you connect with GIS professionals, developers, scientists, tech experts, and others who understand the unique experiences of LGBTQIA+ and ally communities. Discover new ideas for using your skills to help make the world a safer and more inclusive place for everyone.

sessions.title: Women in GIS Social
sessions.abstract: Join Women in GIS as we celebrate the 2026 Awards and Scholarships recipients, exceptional women making their mark in geospatial fields around the world. Presented in collaboration with Esri, this ceremony honors the Distinguished Contribution Award, Young Professional Award, and WiGIS Service Award winners, alongside scholarship recipients of undergraduate, graduate, and practitioner/academic grants. These awards uplift and recognize the talent, dedication, and innovation of women across the geospatial community. Award ceremony is scheduled at 4:30pm.

sessions.title: Attendee Orientation
sessions.abstract: Join other UC attendees for a presentation on how to get the most out of your User Conference experience. Discover insider tips and helpful tools designed to help you make meaningful connections and leave feeling confident and prepared for your week.

sessions.title: User Group Meeting for The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints and Friends
sessions.abstract: This annual gathering is a chance for attendees who are members of the LDS church to connect. Employees from the Church's GIS team have graciously agreed to show examples of how they're using Esri solutions in their work, followed by a brief Q&A. We will also briefly discuss the BYU Geography Alumni Chapter and how to get involved. This event is welcome to everyone, regardless of faith tradition or alumni loyalties! Please help spread the word!

sessions.title: Morning Yoga
sessions.abstract:
Requires separate registration.  Additional details and registration link.

 

Wake up your body and mind with yoga—open to all levels. Each class starts with gentle warm-up poses, flowing through sun salutations and standing, seated, and inverted poses. The focus will be on breath control, alignment, and present-moment awareness, with modifications to suit all levels and paces.

Participants aged 18 years and over are welcome. Guests of Esri UC attendees can register on-site at the Activities Desk in Lobby D of the San Diego Convention Center beginning on Sunday, July 12, 2026.

Price
Early bird until July 9 | $35*
On-site starting July 11 | $45*
5K and two yoga sessions bundle (must sign up by July 9) | $105*

*Includes one Esri yoga mat

sessions.title: Hands-On Learning Lab
sessions.abstract: Take a self-paced lesson at the Hands-On Learning Lab. The Hands-On Learning Lab is a dedicated space to take free lessons on a variety of GIS and ArcGIS topics. Each self-paced lesson takes about one hour to complete and includes conceptual information and step-by-step software exercises. We provide laptops and the ArcGIS software needed to complete each lesson. Esri instructors are available to assist with lesson selection and answer any questions you may have.

sessions.title: Map Lounge
sessions.abstract:
Beyond navigation, maps teach, inspire, and unite us. The Map Lounge is a dynamic, interactive space where you can unwind and admire astonishing maps and powerful stories.

Tour the Interactive Map Gallery, a collection of printed and virtual maps from Esri’s global user community. Discover Storytelling with Maps, featuring exhibits that celebrate diverse voices and innovative storytelling. Join fellow GIS enthusiasts at cozy “fireside chats”. Get inspired by special displays and more.

You’ll also have access to a Hands-On Learning Lab, as well as charging stations in the Relax & Recharge Zone. 

The Map Lounge opens with an evening reception on Monday, July 14.

sessions.title: Presentation Skills Workshop
sessions.abstract: Learn techniques for building and delivering professional presentations including slide authoring, demo skills and public speaking fundamentals.

sessions.title: AEC Industry Kick-Off Meeting
sessions.abstract: The AEC Industry Kick-off session is designed to orient architecture, engineering, construction, and environmental consulting professionals to the week ahead and help them make the most of their time onsite. The session will highlight key trends shaping GIS in AEC and introduce the Esri team and partners who will be available throughout the conference. Attendees will receive a curated roadmap of must-see sessions, industry-specific demos, networking events, and social gatherings aligned to their roles and priorities.

sessions.title: Agriculture Industry Kick-Off Meeting
sessions.abstract: The Agriculture Track Kickoff sets the tone for the week by outlining the focus areas, featured sessions, and community activities. You will get a clear overview of how the track connects to your goals, whether you are modernizing field operations, scaling imagery or AI for crop insights, improving farm to fork traceability, or advancing sustainability reporting.

sessions.title: Commercial/Business Industry Kick-Off Meeting
sessions.abstract: This session will set the tone and help guide you through a focused UC week.  Join us to learn details about the activities for the business community throughout the week. We'll highlight the Business Symposium, the paper sessions, Special Interest Group meetings, and the must-see showcases and events in the Expo and UC overall.

sessions.title: Conservation Industry Kick-Off Meeting
sessions.abstract: Kick off your Esri User Conference experience with this conservation-focused orientation designed to help you make the most of your week. Learn about key sessions, events, and activities for the conservation community, where to connect with peers and Esri experts, and how to navigate UC resources to match your goals. Whether you’re new to UC or a returning attendee, this session will help you plan your time, discover opportunities to engage, and leave with practical takeaways to support your conservation mission.

sessions.title: Electric and Gas Utilities Industry Kick-Off Meeting
sessions.abstract: This session will set the tone and help guide attendees through a successful UC week.  Join us to learn details on industry demonstrations and activities throughout the week.

sessions.title: Imagery and Remote Sensing Industry Kick-Off
sessions.abstract: This session will set the tone and help guide attendees through a successful UC week. Esri experts will highlight industry happenings by different application areas (ex. Conservation, infrastructure) with an emphasis on imagery experience level (beginning, intermediate, and expert), and technologies type (drone, aerial, satellite).  We will also help connect imagery-interested users to each other by providing opportunities to connect early in the week and extend those conversations into other social events.

sessions.title: Land Records and Surveying Industry Kick-Off Meeting
sessions.abstract: Join this session to kick off Esri UC 2026 with all of your friends who are interested in surveying and GIS. Whether you are a surveyor or support high-accuracy data collection and surveying-related workflows, this session is for you. We'll provide tips and tricks for making the most of your time at UC, along with a custom agenda to serve as a roadmap for navigating the Esri UC experience — in surveyor style!
tracks: National Mapping, Navigation, and Geospatial Authorities

Topic: National Mapping, Navigation, and Geospatial Authorities

sessions.title: Ocean, Weather and Climate Industry Kick-Off Meeting
sessions.abstract: Kick off your Esri User Conference experience by joining this introductory session for Ocean, Weather, and Climate Community GIS professionals. This session will highlight key community events, featured sessions, and presentations taking place throughout the week, and point you to the best locations to connect with peers, presenters, and Esri experts. Whether you’re new to the community or a long-time contributor, this kickoff is your guide to navigating the conference and making the most of your time with the Ocean, Weather, and Climate GIS community.

sessions.title: Petroleum Industry Kick-Off Meeting
sessions.abstract: The Petroleum Industry Track Kickoff Meeting sets the stage for the week by introducing the focus areas, themes, and opportunities available throughout the track. You’ll get a high‑level overview of what’s ahead and how the sessions, discussions, and community events can help you make the most of your time here.

sessions.title: Pipeline Industry Kick-Off Meeting
sessions.abstract: The Pipeline Industry Track Kickoff Meeting sets the stage for the week by introducing the focus areas, themes, and opportunities available throughout the track. You’ll get a high‑level overview of what’s ahead and how the sessions, discussions, and community events can help you make the most of your time here.

sessions.title: Public Safety Industry Kick-Off
sessions.abstract: This session brings together leaders and practitioners across emergency management, fire rescue and EMS, wildland fire, emergency communications, law enforcement, national security, and humanitarian response to highlight what’s ahead during UC week. You’ll hear a concise overview of the key themes shaping this year’s public safety content, from real‑time operations and preparedness to response, recovery, and resilience. Industry managers and subject matter experts will preview featured sessions, demos, and Special Interest Groups (SIGs) designed specifically for public safety professionals.

sessions.title: Public Transit Industry Kick-Off Meeting
sessions.abstract: This session will set the tone and help guide attendees through a successful UC week.  Join us to learn details on industry activities throughout the week.  We’ll orient you to key industry‑focused content, showcase upcoming paper sessions, workshops, demos, and networking events, and explain how to maximize your conference experience. Get insights on emerging transit technologies, relevant ArcGIS tools, and collaborative opportunities with Esri partners. Let’s kick off a week of learning, connection, and innovation together.

sessions.title: Speaker Resource Center
sessions.abstract: Are you presenting at the User Conference? Visit the Speaker Resource Center (SRC) to finalize your presentation, practice out loud, and receive personalized coaching and feedback from Esri Instructors. The SRC is fully equipped with projectors, computers, and dedicated workspaces to help you present with confidence.

sessions.title: Telecommunications Industry Kick-Off Meeting
sessions.abstract: Join us for the Telecommunications Industry Kickoff, the opening session designed specifically for telecom, broadband, and communications infrastructure professionals. This session sets the tone for the week by grounding the industry in shared challenges, highlighting where innovation is accelerating, and helping you chart a clear path through the many telecom‑focused sessions, demos, and conversations ahead.

sessions.title: Water Utilities Industry Kick-Off Meeting
sessions.abstract: Start your week with Esri's Global Water Practice. We will be demonstrating the latest ArcGIS Solution updates and providing insight into water events and activities that are taking place during the week. Attend this session to connect with the water team and prepare for the week ahead.

sessions.title: Defense & Intelligence Symposium
sessions.abstract: In an era of contested domains, data overload, and rapid decisions, ArcGIS evolves as a complete System for Mission—integrating geospatial intelligence, operational data, and analytics into a unified environment for decision advantage from HQ to the tactical edge. This symposium shows how defense and intelligence use ArcGIS to fuse data, model complex, operational environments, synchronize planning and execution, and deliver actionable insights. Attendees will see how ArcGIS enables mission command, speeds the OODA loop, and supports decisive, coordinated action across the full mission lifecycle.

ReservationRequired: This session requires a reservation to attend. Please use Add to Schedule to reserve your spot or Join Wait List to be added to the queue.

sessions.title: AI and ArcGIS
sessions.abstract:
Join us to see how ArcGIS, enhanced with AI, is transforming GIS workflows. Learn how AI models help extract and analyze data faster at scale, while geospatial context strengthens AI decisions. Discover how AI assistants boost productivity and accessibility directly within ArcGIS apps and experiences, and how agentic AI enables automation and integration across systems using standards like MCP. Watch live demos, hear from Esri leaders, and explore how to adopt AI confidently while maintaining trusted, enterprise-grade GIS.

 

Reservations will be held until five minutes prior to the start time before seating will be opened to attendees in the on-site queue. If attendees with a reservation remove the session from their schedule, the next person on the wait list will automatically receive that open reservation. Wait lists will only be available before the conference. On-site walk-in attendance may be available dependent on room capacity and will be allowed on a first-come, first-served basis.

ReservationRequired: This session requires a reservation to attend. Please use Add to Schedule to reserve your spot or Join Wait List to be added to the queue.

sessions.title: Accessibility Essentials for GIS and Mapping
sessions.abstract: Data visualizations are increasingly used to communicate important information. Ensuring that this information is accessible for everyone is critical. Join us to learn about digital accessibility essentials for improving mapping and data visualizations and how to ensure compliance with accessibility criteria. Discover the relationship between accessibility standards and GIS and the accessibility capabilities across ArcGIS web, desktop, and mobile technologies. This presentation also introduces technologies that you can use to evaluate and remediate data visualization solutions according to best practices.

RecommendedAudience: Experience with essential workflows with Esri products.

sessions.title: Advancing Transportation Data Access with GeoAI and Imagery
sessions.abstract: This session showcases two practical ways transportation agencies are improving how they manage roadway data. One who uses modern imaging and AI to quickly identify and catalog assets, completing work that once took months with far greater speed and accuracy. The other brings roadway imagery online through automation, making it easy for staff across an organization to access critical information. Together, these methods will demonstrate how to improve safety, streamline operations, and strengthen decision making across transportation networks.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Business Analyst: Fundamentals
sessions.abstract: Curious about how to get started with ArcGIS Business Analyst? Learn how Business Analyst inspires informed decisions in site analysis, social equity, and economic development. Business Analyst's analytical capabilities extend across sectors such as commercial, healthcare, government, and more. Discover how to use the power of the AI assistant to streamline complex workflows and achieve actionable results. This presentation is designed for new users, especially those who have gained access to Business Analyst through Creator and above user types.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: What's New
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Enterprise is always evolving with new functionality and enhancements. Join us for details about what's new in recent releases, from administrative enhancements to mapping, data management, and more. Learn how these capabilities can advance and expand your organization's use of ArcGIS Enterprise.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Experience Builder: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Quickly transform your data into interactive, mobile-optimized web apps and web pages. ArcGIS Experience Builder provides a new level of configuration with flexible design, mobile optimization, smart widgets, and seamless ArcGIS integration. In this presentation, learn about the building blocks of Experience Builder and how they work together, as well as how to quickly create apps using express mode and enhance them using full mode.

Keywords: ArcGIS Experience Builder, App Building, Widget, Theme, Data Source,  Mobile optimization, Web app, Experience, Builder

sessions.title: ArcGIS Field Maps: Situational Awareness and Coordinated Workflows
sessions.abstract: Empower your teams with real-time situational awareness in the field. This presentation showcases how ArcGIS Field Maps mobile maps and location intelligence—plus the new tasks capability—work together to streamline field workflows, improve safety, and inspire smarter decisions. Learn best practices for designing maps and configuring tasks to guide inspections, data collection, and team coordination. Discover how features like location sharing and geofencing keep your workforce connected and responsive—so you can deliver results with confidence.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Hub: An Overview
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Hub brings people together by creating websites where communities can share, collaborate, and engage. Explore ArcGIS Hub's capabilities, learn about practical examples, and discover how to create geo-enabled websites without custom coding. Share trusted and curated content, foster collaboration, and actively engage your community of practice. Hub extends your community by giving stakeholders access to ArcGIS so that they may participate in projects, events, discussions, and other Hub-enabled activities.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Discover the world's foremost collection of geospatial information. ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World curates ready-to-use maps, apps, and layers from Esri and the global GIS user community. Find out how to use this content—and contribute to it—to support work across the ArcGIS system. Speakers will also showcase recent additions and updates to ArcGIS Living Atlas.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World

sessions.title: ArcGIS Location Platform: What Is It and Who Is It For?
sessions.abstract: Is ArcGIS Location Platform for you? This geospatial platform as a service (PaaS) supports developers who need to integrate location capabilities into apps, business systems, and products, and it allows them to create apps and extend ArcGIS products using ArcGIS APIs and SDKs. Learn what's new and what's different between ArcGIS Location Platform and ArcGIS Online or ArcGIS Enterprise when developing apps—and how your organization can benefit.

Keywords: location services, application development, developers, IT, Maps SDKs
RecommendedAudience: Recommended for developers, CTOs, and systems integrators.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise: Managing Large Organizations
sessions.abstract: Managing large organizations in ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise can present unique challenges for administrators. Join us to discover how you can efficiently manage content and resources by using built-in tools, optimizing user and license management workflows, and configuring organization and portal settings for enhanced management capabilities. Also, learn how you can automate these processes to make administration even easier.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Join us for an exciting exploration of ArcGIS Online. Discover the core features of Esri’s software as a service (SaaS) GIS product, and review key new capabilities in the latest update. Whether you are a seasoned pro or checking it out for the first time, do not miss this opportunity to learn all about ArcGIS Online.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro SDK for .NET: Design Patterns for Add-ins and Managed Configurations
sessions.abstract: The ArcGIS Pro SDK for .NET gives developers the tools to create solutions that feel native to ArcGIS Pro. Explore design patterns for building add-ins that align with the ArcGIS Pro interface and behaviors, ensuring that add-ins look and work like a natural part of the application. Speakers discuss design configurations that streamline ArcGIS Pro for users who need only a focused set of capabilities. Through code examples and best practices, discover how to deliver role-specific experiences that balance customization with usability.

Keywords: add-ins, design patterns, role-specific experiences
RecommendedAudience: Knowledge of ArcGIS Pro SDK.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: An Overview
sessions.abstract: New to GIS or ArcGIS desktop software? In this presentation, explore the fundamentals of ArcGIS Pro, including how to create maps and layouts, set up a file geodatabase, edit and visualize data, and perform analysis—no prior experience is required. Discover how GIS helps people solve spatial problems and make smarter decisions.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Autodesk Integration with the Esri System Using CAD and BIM Data
sessions.abstract: Explore techniques for using Autodesk CAD and BIM data, such as Civil 3D files, to support your GIS workflows in ArcGIS Pro. Learn how to geoposition your data and convert it to a geodatabase for visualization, analysis, and publishing to ArcGIS Online for sharing with your organization.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with CAD, BIM, Autodesk, DWG, DGN, Civil 3D, BIM Cloud Connection, and georeferencing.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions: State and Local Government
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Solutions help state and local governments improve operations, gain new insights, and enhance services provided to the public. Join us for an overview of this collection of solutions and learn how you can use them to maximize the investment that you are making in location-based data and ArcGIS.

sessions.title: ArcGIS for Microsoft: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Discover the value that ArcGIS for Microsoft can bring to your organization. Speakers discuss the capabilities in ArcGIS for Microsoft 365, ArcGIS for Power Platform, and ArcGIS for Microsoft Fabric that enable you to take advantage of mapping capabilities and ArcGIS data and extend mapping and analysis to everyone in your organization.

sessions.title: ArcGIS for Resilient and Inclusive Development
sessions.abstract: In this session, you will discover how peers are using ArcGIS to support resilient and inclusive development in diverse contexts. Through short showcases and discussions, participants will share analyses, data sources, and decision-support tools that integrate climate risk, infrastructure, and socio-economic data. You will discover practical uses of ArcGIS to assess vulnerability, identify inequities, support inclusive planning, and communicate insights that help communities adapt, thrive, and build resilience in the face of environmental and social change.

Keywords: Resilience, Community, Inclusive, Social, Human Resources
RecommendedAudience: Open to all levels of experience

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Building the Planning Digital Twin
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS is transforming how we design and plan communities, regions, and cities. At the heart of this transformation is the Planning Digital Twin, a dynamic, data-rich representation of the built environment that enables planners to simulate scenarios, evaluate impacts, and make informed real-time decisions. This presentation explores how ArcGIS enables you to create and maintain digital twins, combining spatial analytics, 3D visualization, and collaborative workflows.

Keywords: Digital Twins, Urban Planning

sessions.title: ArcGIS: How Maps Work
sessions.abstract: Start with the basics to become a smarter mapmaker. The essentials of good mapmaking empower you to make your maps work better and to tell a compelling story through your design. Learn the basic principles of cartographic design (classification, generalization, symbology, color, type, layout for print and web) and how you can use ArcGIS Pro to focus the message of your map. Understand how people read maps and what works well to successfully communicate your message. Step through the mapmaking process to find out how to implement better design decisions.

sessions.title: Carbon Credits, MRV, and Climate‑Driven Agriculture
sessions.abstract: This session will highlight two advanced GIS‑based approaches for MRV, carbon markets, and environmental sustainability across agricultural systems.

sessions.title: Creating Data-Savvy Residents to Enhance Trust & Accountability
sessions.abstract: Learn how two governments are elevating public understanding through data centric GIS applications. Cobb County, GA rebuilt its service line dashboard to meet EPA Lead and Copper Rule requirements, delivering transparent, up to date regulatory information directly to residents. The City of Riverside, CA consolidated scattered property data and used ArcGIS tools to better inform residents and attract developer interest.

sessions.title: Defining and Implementing Your Geospatial Strategy
sessions.abstract: Help your organization use GIS technology more effectively by creating a business-oriented geospatial strategy. Esri experts share how to define organizational GIS needs, plan and develop a strategy, and implement the strategy through both organizational and technological changes. Learn best practices to achieve your organization's geospatial goals and support end users.

sessions.title: Engaging Communities and Communicating Climate Insight Through Geospatial Tools
sessions.abstract: Discover how geospatial technologies, community engagement, and thoughtful map design can improve public understanding of climate impacts and support local decisions. Attendees will learn how clear, trustworthy climate map communication supported by AI or participatory data, connects science to community action. The session highlights GIS approaches for collecting hyperlocal climate observations, fostering collaboration, and designing map-based tools that help people interpret environmental change with confidence and plan for climate challenges.

sessions.title: Enterprise GIS Modernization and Digital Transformation
sessions.abstract: In this session, you will learn how organizations are using GIS to modernize utility and construction operations through digital twins, automation, and real-time data integration. Discover how unifying fragmented asset and operational data improves efficiency, accuracy, and collaboration while reducing reliance on manual processes. You will gain insight into scalable, location-based workflows that support proactive planning, compliance, and decision-making across campuses, ports, and other asset-intensive environments.

Keywords: Digital Twin; Utility & Construction Operations; Data Automation; Asset Management
RecommendedAudience: Basic understanding of GIS concepts; Familiarity with utilities, infrastructure, or construction workflows; Exposure to operational or field data collection processes

sessions.title: Field Workflows with ArcGIS: Best Practices
sessions.abstract: Successful field operations require thoughtful planning. This presentation highlights best practices for data collection, location accuracy, and offline workflows using ArcGIS Field Maps, ArcGIS Survey123, and ArcGIS QuickCapture. Explore strategies for scaling field workflows across your organization—for security, integration with business systems, user adoption, and managing updates—whether you are working with a disconnected environment or an enterprise-scale deployment.

sessions.title: From Floods to Hurricanes: Mapping the Unthinkable
sessions.abstract: Extreme weather events are increasing in frequency and complexity, demanding faster, more coordinated, and more data-driven emergency management. This session explores how geospatial technology is transforming disaster response and recovery across floods, debris operations, and environmental protection: turning fragmented information into actionable intelligence.

Keywords: data-driven emergency management, disaster response, environmental protection

sessions.title: GIS for Providing Operational Readiness and Situational Awareness
sessions.abstract: See how two fire agencies modernize daily operations and incident visibility with ArcGIS. Learn how agencies are coordinating training, maintenance, and public programs across the department and how GIS platforms are feeding incident command with real-time data and intelligence.

Keywords: Fire and EMS Operations, Operational Readiness, Scheduling and Coordination, Resource Management, Situational Awareness, Conflict Detection, Real-Time Visibility, Public Safety GIS, Training and Maintenance, Emergency Services Efficiency, wildfire, situational awareness, fire
RecommendedAudience: Beginner

sessions.title: Imagery: A Complete Workflow from Management and Analysis to Sharing
sessions.abstract: Learn a complete imagery workflow—from managing and organizing imagery to analyzing it and sharing results. Explore the latest cloud-based management tools, advanced analysis capabilities, and modern image exploitation workflows in ArcGIS. Whether imagery is central to your work or you are exploring new possibilities, find out how to visualize, analyze, and deliver imagery products efficiently and at scale.

Keywords: Imagery and Remote Sensing

sessions.title: Improving Property Assessment and Valuations with Data Accuracy and AI
sessions.abstract: This session explores how government and public-sector organizations are modernizing property valuation and land management through the combined power of GIS and AI to unify data, streamline workflows, and improve accuracy of valuations and assessments. You will see real examples of how modern geospatial tools address challenges related to legacy systems, evolving legal needs, data quality issues, and transparency, showcasing best practices for using ArcGIS to enhance operational efficiency and informed decision‑making.

Keywords: Valuation, Tax Assessment, Land Records, Land Administration
RecommendedAudience: Assessors, GIS Managers, Real Estate Professionals, Appraisers

sessions.title: Kids Fair
sessions.abstract:
Visit the Esri Kids Fair to introduce them to GIS through a series of educational, hands-on activities. This year’s theme is “Continents.” After completing each activity and filling out their passport, kids will earn a special prize. Kids from 5 to 14 years old are welcome to participate. A parent or guardian must be present at all times. Registration is available on-site at no additional cost.

 

Learn more about fair activities in this blog.

sessions.title: Managing Lidar Like a Pro: Tips for High-Performance Workflows
sessions.abstract: Want to get more out of your lidar data in ArcGIS? This presentation shares practical tips and tricks for managing and optimizing lidar datasets across your organization. Learn how to structure lidar collections, improve performance, and publish lidar content to the web. Speakers discuss best practices to streamline workflows for assigning coordinate systems, tiling, thinning, filtering, and sharing lidar data—helping you deliver high-quality 3D content.

RecommendedAudience: Interest in efficiently visualizing and analyzing lidar data using ArcGIS.

sessions.title: Modernizing Pipeline GIS
sessions.abstract: Join in this session to hear TC Energy talk about how it modernized its entire GIS infrastructure by upgrading to PODS 7 plus ArcGIS Pipeline Referencing and a fully spatial, object-oriented model running on ArcGIS Pro. Then, listen to Phillips 66 discuss how it leveraged Esri technology to enhance emergency preparedness, strengthen collaboration with first responders, and ultimately improve safety outcomes across facilities.

sessions.title: Putting the R in ArcGIS
sessions.abstract: Did you know that you can solve complex problems by integrating the spatial analysis and mapping capabilities of ArcGIS with thousands of open-source R libraries? Discover the newest R-ArcGIS Bridge and learn how to incorporate R capabilities into many workflows across the ArcGIS system, including downloading ArcGIS Survey123 attachments, geocoding thousands of addresses, and deploying custom geoprocessing services to ArcGIS Enterprise.

RecommendedAudience: Basic experience with R.

sessions.title: Spatial Data Science: The Art and Science of Spatial Analysis
sessions.abstract: Spatial data science is an iterative process that extends beyond the running of one tool—it is about posing questions, figuring out the appropriate data to answer those questions, finding the appropriate methods that truly answer those questions, evaluating the results, iterating until you get it right, and communicating results in compelling and effective ways. In this presentation, speakers share examples of applying this process to an important, real-world problem. The examples provide ideas and approaches to help you connect it all back to the problems that you want to help solve.

sessions.title: Spatial Intelligence for Market Analysis and Emergency Readiness
sessions.abstract: BPCL demonstrates how Esri Business Analyst is advancing its digital transformation by embedding spatial intelligence into market analysis, growth planning, and faster, more precise decision-making. ExxonMobil shares how global GIS best practices, ArcGIS Hub, partnered collaboration, and a Common Operating Picture are improving oil spill response readiness and stakeholder coordination across incident command and remote teams.

sessions.title: Strategies for Overdose Prevention and Response
sessions.abstract: Explore how GIS illuminates the dynamics of the opioid epidemic through two community-driven projects. These efforts map overdose trends, reveal service gaps, and highlight areas of intervention. Attendees will see practical workflows—from hot spot and kernel density analysis to accessibility assessments—that guide equitable harm reduction strategies and empower agencies to improve public health outcomes.

Keywords: overdose, opioid, prevention, response, epidemic, substace, misuse

sessions.title: The Smart Grid Assistant: GenAI for Maps and Field Ops
sessions.abstract: Learn how GenAI and Agentic architectures are transforming utility operations. You’ll discover how MapChat uses natural language to turn simple questions into complex spatial queries, making GIS data accessible to everyone. We’ll also explore how to integrate AI agents into ArcGIS Field Maps and ArcGIS Survey123 to provide field crews with conversational insights into asset health and vegetation management. Join us to see how LLM reasoning and ArcGIS tools combine to automate data analysis, streamline inspections, and empower your workforce with on-demand intelligence.

Keywords: Artificial Intelligence, AI,

sessions.title: Transforming OSP Planning, Design and Construction with Modern GIS
sessions.abstract: These sessions explore how telecommunications organizations are modernizing the full lifecycle of outside plant (OSP) planning, design, and construction using GIS-driven workflows and emerging technologies. Presenters will share approaches for connecting previously siloed processes, improving data accuracy, and streamlining delivery through automation, intelligent tools, and integrated platforms. Attendees will gain insight into best practices that enhance efficiency from initial network planning through construction and as‑built integration.

sessions.title: Expo
sessions.abstract: At the UC Expo, connect face to face with Esri staff and Esri Partner Network leaders to explore innovations, collaborate on ideas, learn from experts, and ignite inspiration.

sessions.title: Hands-On Learning Lab
sessions.abstract: Take a self-paced lesson at the Hands-On Learning Lab. The Hands-On Learning Lab is a dedicated space to take free lessons on a variety of GIS and ArcGIS topics. Each self-paced lesson takes about one hour to complete and includes conceptual information and step-by-step software exercises. We provide laptops and the ArcGIS software needed to complete each lesson. Esri instructors are available to assist with lesson selection and answer any questions you may have.

sessions.title: Latinx User Group Meeting
sessions.abstract: The Latinx in GIS User Group meeting is a space where GIS professionals, students, and advocates come together to connect, collaborate, and inspire one another. During our meeting, we will discuss how users are amplifying community power through GIS while creating opportunities for the next generation of Latinx leaders. This event focuses on networking, building community, strengthening Latinx Esri User Groups across the globe, and empowering others to use geospatial technology to drive meaningful social impact for our communities.

sessions.title: security.manager: Superpowers for ArcGIS Admins
sessions.abstract: This presentation will introduce security.manager, highlighting its robust capabilities for ArcGIS Enterprise administrators. Attendees will learn how security.manager extends ArcGIS Enterprise with granular access controls, simplifying compliance with organizational policies. By integrating seamlessly with ArcGIS Enterprise, security.manager empowers administrators to efficiently safeguard sensitive geospatial data and ensure secure collaboration across teams.

sessions.title: Intro to AI-Driven Reality Capture with GeoCam
sessions.abstract: Discover the easiest way to scale your own Reality Capture Practice. Starting from rapid single captures of any space to managing a complete approach for a large city (streets, indoors, aerial), utility networks (transmission, distribution, and stations), or full campus and indoor mapping environments.

sessions.title: Scaling Hydrospatial Intelligence with Fugro, EOMAP, and Esri
sessions.abstract: Fugro and EOMAP bring together Earth observation and hydrospatial analytics to extend the Esri platform with coastal and marine Geo-data. This presentation covers satellite-derived bathymetry, water quality analysis, coastal monitoring, and ArcGIS-integrated workflows built for government agencies, energy companies, utilities, climate resilience, environmental monitoring, and infrastructure programs. Real-world examples show how organizations use cloud-based geospatial data to make faster, better-informed decisions.

sessions.title: Accelerating GIS Deployments: Agile and DevOps in Action
sessions.abstract: Learn how Agile principles and DevOps practices combine to accelerate enterprise GIS deployments. This brief presentation highlights strategies for breaking down rollouts into manageable increments, prioritizing business value, and automating deployment workflows. Discover tools and lessons learned that help organizations deliver ArcGIS solutions faster while maintaining governance and quality.

Keywords: rapid deployment, templates, automation, governance, scalability
RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with templates, automation tools, and governance frameworks.

sessions.title: Adapting Real-Time Incident Monitoring and Intelligence for Emergency Management
sessions.abstract: Emergency operations centers face an overwhelming volume of data, alerts, and reporting demands. This session highlights how agencies are using GIS-driven automation and integrated intelligence platforms to cut through noise, accelerate detection, and improve decision-making.

Keywords: emergency management operations

sessions.title: Advanced Workflows and Technologies
sessions.abstract: This session pairs geological insight with modern operational efficiency. A study of glacial lineations shows how integrating outcrop, subsurface, and seismic data improves mapping of paleo‑valley features and strengthens subsurface interpretation. PureWest demonstrates how an ArcGIS Experience Builder app replaces manual reporting with an integrated, real‑time workflow that streamlines safety and environmental oversight. Together, they show how spatial data and automation enhance clarity, confidence, and decision‑making.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Dashboards: An Overview
sessions.abstract: New to ArcGIS Dashboards? This configurable web app provides data visualization of people, services, assets, and events. Learn how to configure charts, gauges, maps, and other visual elements to reflect the status and performance of the data that matters most. Convey the activities and key indicators that are most vital to meeting your organization's objectives. Speakers also share the current road map for this product.

Keywords: Dashboards, Data Visualization, Configuration, Road Map

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise Sites: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Your organization has a tremendous amount of valuable and authoritative data and content. With ArcGIS Enterprise Sites, you can create a customized web page for easy discovery of your datasets, maps, apps, and other content by your staff and community, both GIS users and non-GIS users alike. This presentation provides an overview of Sites, the latest features, best practices for site creation and content management, troubleshooting tips for common questions, and advice for how to create intuitive web pages that reflect your organization’s branding and help your users find what they need.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise on Kubernetes: Wreck It and Recover It
sessions.abstract: Many firms say that their solutions are highly available, but do the claims hold up? Ours does! In this presentation, watch as the ArcGIS Enterprise team intentionally wrecks a Kubernetes deployment and demonstrates how the system recovers quickly—with minimal to no downtime.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: An Overview
sessions.abstract: New to ArcGIS Enterprise? Learn more about Esri's software system that provides comprehensive GIS functionality on infrastructure that you control. Explore the primary workflows that include data management, mapping and visualization, analysis, and sharing. Discover how ArcGIS Enterprise can benefit you and your organization!

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Printable Reports from ArcGIS Web Applications
sessions.abstract: Learn how to generate reports and maps from the ArcGIS Enterprise print service. Find out how to configure layout and report templates in ArcGIS Pro, share your print service with reporting capabilities or as portal items, and use the service in Map Viewer and ArcGIS Experience Builder. Speakers share how to include attributes, attachments, related records, and more in reports or on layouts and discuss new ArcGIS Online enhancements, including custom layout templates. The ArcPy-based print service for advanced workflows is also briefly discussed.

Keywords: Printing, reports, ArcGIS Enterprise
RecommendedAudience: Knowledge of ArcGIS Enterprise.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Experience Builder: Building Your First Widget
sessions.abstract: Join us to learn how ArcGIS Experience Builder allows you to build immersive web apps for your organization. Discover how to use TypeScript and React to build your first widget in Experience Builder, and get tips on how to debug your widget to improve your debugging experience.

RecommendedAudience: Experience with TypeScript and React development.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Field Maps: Simplifying Field Data Collection with Forms and ArcGIS Arcade
sessions.abstract: Learn how to use dynamic forms and ArcGIS Arcade in ArcGIS Field Maps to create adaptable, easy-to-use mobile data collection workflows. The presentation provides techniques for building forms that respond to user input, enforcing data quality standards, and streamlining field processes for greater accuracy and efficiency.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Hub: Best Practices for Site Creation, Content Management, and Administration
sessions.abstract: Organizations worldwide use ArcGIS Hub to create websites for data sharing, stakeholder collaboration, and community engagement. Learn best practices for designing visually appealing and highly engaging Hub sites. Discover how to configure Hub catalogs to share content privately or as open data and set up search and download options to help stakeholders discover and use data. Explore additional best practices that will help your content shine. Finally, gain insights into administrative practices for effectively managing and growing your site.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Indoors: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Discover the power of indoor mapping with ArcGIS Indoors, an extension to ArcGIS for indoor mapping. This presentation provides an overview of ArcGIS Indoors capabilities and how to create and share floor-aware maps of your building interiors. Gain insight into how to use indoor maps to power Indoors apps for space planning, workspace reservation, indoor navigation, sharing a location, and more, as well as how licensing works.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Knowledge: Connecting Data and Analyzing Relationships
sessions.abstract: Discover how ArcGIS Knowledge transforms and connects enterprise data into a living network of insights. Learn the foundations of building and exploring knowledge graphs to uncover hidden relationships between people, places, events, and assets. This technical session introduces the key concepts, data integration patterns, and visualization tools that help analysts accelerate investigations and situational awareness by combining spatial and nonspatial data. ArcGIS Knowledge extends ArcGIS Enterprise and enables new user experiences across desktop and web applications and developer tools.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World: An Overview of Imagery Content
sessions.abstract: Are you new to imagery or looking for ways to use raster analysis? This presentation discusses the fundamentals of ready-to-use imagery in ArcGIS as well as the various types of available imagery and how to access them. Learn about a variety of available sources and best practices for using imagery in various workflows, custom apps, Map Viewer, ArcGIS Pro, and ArcGIS Notebooks.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World
Keywords: ArcGIS Living Atlas, Living Atlas, Imagery, ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise: Architectural Patterns and Practices
sessions.abstract: Think you need to choose between ArcGIS Enterprise and ArcGIS Online? Many organizations successfully use both! This presentation helps you determine how to choose the deployment that best meets your organizational needs. Discover the different hybrid patterns that you can use to capitalize on each environment’s unique strengths and plan for and build the best possible system for your organization.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online: Effective Tools to Manage Your Organization
sessions.abstract: Want to take advantage of the powerful suite of management tools that are available to ArcGIS Online administrators? Discover how to effectively manage members, roles, and privileges; organize content within your organization; and manage credit usage. Learn how to use administrative reports to better understand your organization and get helpful insights for effective resource management.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online: Processing and Analyzing Imagery
sessions.abstract: Process and analyze imagery entirely in the cloud with ArcGIS Online. This presentation discusses scalable imagery hosting, ready-to-use raster functions, and the Raster Function Editor for dynamic and persistent analysis. Discover best practices for building repeatable workflows in Map Viewer and the ArcGIS API for Python, helping you modernize and scale your imagery processes.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro Editing: What’s New in Editing
sessions.abstract: Learn all about the latest editing features in ArcGIS Pro. In this demo theater presentation, discover how to use these new editing tools and configuration options to maximize your editing workflows.

RecommendedAudience: Basic understanding of ArcGIS Pro.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro SDK for .NET: Patterns of Extensibility
sessions.abstract: Learn fundamental approaches for extending ArcGIS Pro, covering specific ArcGIS Pro SDK for .NET and ArcGIS Pro API topic areas. Speakers discuss practical considerations for your ArcGIS Pro extensions and online resources for your work.

Keywords: fundamental, Pro extensions

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Mapping and Visualization
sessions.abstract: What's new in the world of mapping and visualization in ArcGIS Pro? Join us to learn how to adjust layer and map properties, add cartographic refinements and visual effects, and organize symbols and colors into styles that will standardize how your maps are viewed.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Tips and Tricks
sessions.abstract: Discover something new with ArcGIS Pro—whether you are just getting started or have used it for years. In this presentation, learn tips and tricks to boost efficiency and productivity. Topics include mapping, data and content management, analysis, and sharing. Speaking of sharing, you also get the opportunity to share your favorite ArcGIS Pro tips with others!

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions: Elections
sessions.abstract: Need to increase public awareness and the reliability of election day operations? Discover how a collection of elections solutions can help you educate voters, minimize disruptions on election day, and improve confidence in election results.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions: Emergency Management
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Solutions help emergency management agencies prepare for, and respond to, emergency incidents. Join us for an overview of this collection of solutions and learn how you can understand hazards that may impact your community, maintain situational awareness during an incident, and rapidly assess damage after an incident.

Keywords: Emergency management, Incident operations, Damage

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions: Implementing a Utility Network Foundation
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Solutions help utilities model their utility data in an ArcGIS Utility Network and use key network management capabilities. Join us for an overview of this collection of solutions and learn how you can accelerate your utility network implementation.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions: The Gridded Reference Graphic Solution for Defense Organizations
sessions.abstract: Coordinating ground activity requires a clear, shared reference system. The Gridded Reference Graphic (GRG) solution, part of ArcGIS Solutions for Defense, helps organizations quickly generate grid-based reference products to support operational coordination and field activities. This presentation demonstrates how ArcGIS enables teams to create, manage, and share GRGs to improve tasking, communication, and coordination during clearing and ground operations.

Keywords:
Target area grids
Operational coordination
Field operations
Location referencing

sessions.title: ArcGIS StoryMaps: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Want to share the story behind your work in a stunning, modern format that informs and inspires? ArcGIS StoryMaps helps you combine dynamic content with custom maps and share it through interactive web pages and presentation slides. This introductory technical session provides an overview of what’s possible with ArcGIS StoryMaps, with inspiring examples of how organizations use the app to create engaging, map-driven experiences. Discover how easy it is to bring your stories to life with beautiful visualizations and powerful storytelling tools.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Topographic Mapping: Modernizing Topographic Production Workflows
sessions.abstract: Discover the ArcGIS Topographic Mapping extension for ArcGIS Pro and ArcGIS Enterprise. Learn how it uses ArcGIS Workflow Manager and predefined service-based workflows to manage topographic data and cartographic production. Gain valuable insights about how the latest release improves consistency, increases efficiency, and scales mapping operations, streamlining both data management and cartographic tasks across your organization.

sessions.title: ArcGIS for AutoCAD: An Overview
sessions.abstract: The free ArcGIS for AutoCAD plug-in connects CAD and GIS professionals for information sharing and decision-making. Learn how this extension enables direct editing of GIS data and precise geolocation of drawings within AutoCAD. Explore how it informs the design process with rich geospatial context and ensures that drawings are consumable within ArcGIS. Explore workflows that show how edits made in ArcGIS are fed back to AutoCAD, enabling ArcGIS and AutoCAD teams to work with the same data synchronously.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with AutoCAD, ArcGIS for AutoCAD, feature service editing, geolocation, Civil 3D, and data management.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Analyzing Water Loss for Utilities
sessions.abstract: Curious to find out how ArcGIS technology is used by water and sewer utilities? Join us to learn about the various tools in ArcGIS that can visualize, manage, and reduce water loss.

Keywords: Analysis, Operational Awareness

sessions.title: Automating Imagery Workflows with Deep Learning
sessions.abstract: Learn how geospatial artificial intelligence (GeoAI) in ArcGIS streamlines imagery workflows with automated deep learning tools. This presentation discusses the processes of creating training data, applying models for feature extraction, and running models for analysis across ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Enterprise, and ArcGIS Online. Discover how ArcGIS reduces manual effort, scales analysis, and helps you get started quickly with practical, repeatable deep learning workflows.

sessions.title: Building Trusted Environmental Intelligence with Scalable Data Integration
sessions.abstract: Environmental organizations need fast, trusted access to complex datasets. This session highlights how ArcGIS supports access to water and protected areas data through integrated collection, processing, and delivery workflows. Learn how the Protected Areas Database of the U.S. (PAD‑US), managed by the U.S. Geological Survey (USGS), is updated using ArcGIS Survey123 and Workflow Manager, and how the Missouri Department of Natural Resources built an ArcGIS Hub site and mobile app to deliver real‑time hydrology data, including stream gage, soil moisture, and flood inundation mapping powered by the National Water Model, to the public.

Keywords: data retrieval, USGS, coastal, protected areas, data interoperability

sessions.title: Conservation Planning Amidst Rapid Change
sessions.abstract: Biodiversity planning increasingly must account for cultural landscapes and accelerating land transformation. This session features GIS work that co-produces maps with traditional knowledge holders to document adaptation to climate and fragmentation, and models land-use scenarios to 2030 to evaluate conservation strategies. You will learn how GIS supports scenario evaluation, prioritization, and engagement.

Keywords: Conservation; Biodiversity

sessions.title: Creating Safer Sidewalk Networks With Real-Time and AI Insights
sessions.abstract: Discover how public works teams are using real time data, AI‑enhanced inspections, and modern field tools to better understand sidewalk conditions and identify network gaps. You will learn how organizations organize and interpret large volumes of field observations into clear, defensible priorities that improve walkability for residents and deliver greater transparency for agency leaders.

Keywords: Sidewalk Safety, Accessibility Planning, Capital Prioritization, Pedestrian Safety, Field Collection
RecommendedAudience: Ideal for public works and transportation staff, asset managers, and GIS professionals who support sidewalk or mobility programs.

sessions.title: Data Engineering in ArcGIS Pro: Preparing Data for Spatial Analysis
sessions.abstract: The spatial analysis possibilities across ArcGIS are endless, but what is the one foundational piece that every analysis has in common? Data engineering. Learn how advances in ArcGIS Pro are making this process more effective. Discover how the Data Engineering view, charts, and mapping allow you to visualize and examine data, and then explore how the Data Engineering view and geoprocessing tools help you clean and prepare your data to make it analysis-ready.

sessions.title: Digital Twins, 3D GIS and Scan-to-BIM-to-GIS
sessions.abstract: In this session, you will learn how 3D and 4D/5D GIS workflows are transforming infrastructure and construction management by unifying asset, design, schedule, and cost data in a single spatial environment. Discover how integrating BIM, reality capture, and enterprise systems into GIS improves visibility, collaboration, and decision-making across asset lifecycles and capital programs. You will gain insight into how digital twins enable more efficient operations, proactive maintenance, and real-time project intelligence for owners and delivery teams.

Keywords: Digital Twin; Utility & Construction Operations; Data Automation; Asset Management
RecommendedAudience: Basic understanding of GIS or spatial data concepts; Familiarity with BIM, asset management, or construction project workflows; Exposure to capital project delivery or infrastructure operations

sessions.title: Equity and Ethics in Spatial Analysis and AI
sessions.abstract: As spatial data science and AI become increasingly critical in our decision-making processes, it has never been more important to consider the ethical implications of our analyses. This presentation introduces a framework for spatial analysis and AI that is intersectional and community- and justice-centered; has a foundation of equity and inclusion; and promotes accountability and transparency. Learn how to ensure that equity and ethics are central to the design, evaluation, and communication of your spatial data science work.

sessions.title: Field AI Without the Office Hassle
sessions.abstract: Ever tried applying AI in the field without the right platform? Many organizations have. The challenge is that frontline teams often rely on disconnected tools built for office environments, creating inefficiencies and risks where work actually happens. Field Operations Management changes that by uniting processes, data, safety, and accountability on a field-first platform. With the right foundation, AI becomes part of the workflow—delivering cleaner data, safer operations, and better outcomes for field and GIS teams alike.

sessions.title: Field Operations: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Want to learn more about Esri's field apps and how they can support your mobile operations? Join us to learn about key features and exciting new developments in the ArcGIS Field Maps, ArcGIS Navigator, ArcGIS Survey123, and ArcGIS QuickCapture mobile apps. Discover how these apps revolutionize field operations by increasing situational awareness and collaboration, both indoors and outdoors, increasing data quality and accuracy, streamlining field processes, and providing efficient navigation.

sessions.title: Field‑Scale Agriculture Insights, Rangeland Management and Regenerative Practices
sessions.abstract: This session presents two examples of how GIS supports sustainable agriculture through field‑scale intelligence, rangeland monitoring, and regenerative practice management.

sessions.title: From Data to Decisions: Supporting Facility Management with 3D GIS
sessions.abstract: Join this session to learn how modern technology is streamlining asset lifecycle management and improving operational efficiency. Presenters will show how integrated 3D data visualization and analysis support long-term digital strategies focused on improving facility and vertical asset management. You will leave this session with a better understanding of how 3D GIS delivers insights via intuitive, user-friendly solutions.

Keywords: BIM to GIS, Digital Twins, Vertical Asset Management, Treatment facility,

sessions.title: From Field to Front Office: Modernizing Park Operations
sessions.abstract: This session explores how parks and recreation teams can modernize operations using ArcGIS tools. Learn how digital inspections with ArcGIS Survey123 improve accuracy, visibility, and response times, and how automated data integration with ArcGIS Data Pipelines streamlines updates and enhances data quality. Attendees will gain practical, scalable strategies for teams looking to digitize workflows and strengthen decision making.

Keywords: Azure, park inspections

sessions.title: GI Science: GeoAI Benchmarks and Spatial Analysis of Fentanyl Contagion
sessions.abstract: Geographic Information Science experts present a new benchmark for LLMs running spatial analysis, and spatial analysis on fentanyl cases in the US.

Keywords: AI, GeoAI benchmarks, fentanyl, spatial analysis
RecommendedAudience: data science, GeoAI

sessions.title: GeoAI for Smarter Fire and EMS Operations
sessions.abstract: Discover how geospatial artificial intelligence (GeoAI) and Esri AI assistants are transforming fire and emergency medical services (EMS) operations. Learn how ArcGIS Survey123 uses voice and image analysis for rapid field reporting and how AI-powered imagery tools support incident response, from drone mapping to automated damage detection. Find out how these innovations provide smarter resource deployment, risk reduction, and actionable insights—all within secure, validated workflows.

Keywords: Fire, EMS, GeoAI, AI, ML

sessions.title: Getting Your Data Ready for AI Applications
sessions.abstract: Is your data ready for AI? Find out how to get your GIS data ready for use by AI applications, especially agentic mapping applications. This presentation demonstrates the new Item Details assistant (beta) in action and how good documentation and metadata result in better AI apps.

Keywords: AI, Assistant, Agents

sessions.title: Global to Local to Global Geodesign for Climate Change Mitigation (GLG)
sessions.abstract: The Global to Local to Global (GLG) Climate Mitigation Project provides technical support to address climate change mitigation through a structured framework designed to support climate projects by 2050. We will demonstrate how the GLG linked tools operate at local to regional scales. They utilize global science and global optimization designs based on emissions reduction and cost targets to inform local knowledge and local designs, informing national and global policy. The free GLG tools will be shared and taught in a GLG workshop at 1pm the same day.

sessions.title: How AI Helps Keep Our Roads in Better Shape
sessions.abstract: Transportation teams are using AI with GIS to turn roadway data into the actionable information they need to collect and process at scale. By automating the detection of features and surface conditions, agencies can replace thousands of hours of manual collection with quick reviews that maintain accuracy. A map based workflow integrates AI results, highlights missing or deteriorating assets, and supports smarter maintenance planning. In this session two organizations strengthen decision making, reduces field burden, and creates a repeatable process for managing growing infrastructure needs.

sessions.title: Immersive Mapping: The Power of 3D in ArcGIS
sessions.abstract: Step beyond traditional maps and discover how 3D transforms the way that we understand and interact with spatial data. This presentation explores the full spectrum of 3D capabilities across ArcGIS—from visualization and analysis to integration with BIM, lidar, and digital twins. Learn about practical techniques for creating immersive experiences, uncovering insights only possible in 3D, and sharing content seamlessly across web and mobile. Whether you are planning cities, managing infrastructure, or building smart environments, find out how 3D can elevate GIS workflows and decision-making.

RecommendedAudience: Interest in 3D capabilities across ArcGIS.

sessions.title: Map Viewer: Bringing Your ArcGIS Pro Mapping Skills to the Web
sessions.abstract: Connect your desktop to your web GIS. Map Viewer empowers you to create performant, visually rich, and interactive web maps using familiar ArcGIS Pro concepts. Explore how techniques like data- and scale-driven symbology, custom styles, dynamic labeling, and print layouts come to life in Map Viewer's modern, web-based environment. Also, learn best practices for preparing and publishing your layers and maps to ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise, ensuring your work is optimized as scalable, web-ready solutions.

Keywords: symbology, layout, publishing workflows, performance optimization, cartography, layer preparation
RecommendedAudience: Experience with web mapping in Map Viewer.

sessions.title: Mapping for Impact with the National Geographic Society
sessions.abstract: Mapping for Impact is a core educational and operational philosophy at the National Geographic Society. It focuses on using cartography, geographic information systems (GIS), and storytelling to drive real-world change and social action. Part of National Geographic’s Storytellers Collective workshops, this exploration into the mapmaking process will help you merge data, design, and purpose to better reach your audience. Participants will learn best practices in cartography and mapping from National Geographic experts to create maps to tell stories and have.

sessions.title: Native Nations: What's Working, What's Hard, What's Next
sessions.abstract: Join the Native Nations community for a facilitated, peer-to-peer conversation with the Esri Native Nations team focused on strengthening GIS across social, environmental, and infrastructure domains—and the practical path forward. Bring one “win” and one “stuck point,” and leave with peer-tested ideas you can apply back home. You’ll engage in guided discussions shaped by participant priorities and explore approaches for communicating GIS value and evolving your program. Key themes will be shared during Wednesday’s Native Nations Symposium.

sessions.title: Opportunities to Advance Equity in Local Government
sessions.abstract: This brief presentation explores how spatial thinking is driving equitable outcomes in state and local government. Speakers highlight top trends, such as Americans with Disabilities (ADA) compliance, Title VI in transportation, broadband access, and health equity, where GIS is making a measurable impact. Speakers also share practical examples, patterns seen in the field, and downloadable tools to support equity and social justice through a geographic approach.

occurrences.room: State and Local Government Expo Theater

sessions.title: Optimizing Fire Response with GIS
sessions.abstract: Discover how two agencies improved emergency response using ArcGIS.  Learn how they launched proximity‑based dispatch across multiple departments and how refining response zones provided better protection to a growing community.

Keywords: Emergency Response Planning, Fire, Response, Tasks, Emergency Services, Public Safety, EMS
RecommendedAudience: Beginner

sessions.title: Optimizing and Managing Pipeline Rights of Way
sessions.abstract: Attend this session to learn how Enbridge Energy uses LiDAR to enhance pipeline management by delivering precise terrain geometry, enabling proactive maintenance, and lowering operational costs across 16,000+ miles of gas transmission pipelines. Also in this session, Ghana National Petroleum will discuss how it uses ArcGIS to plan pipeline routes connecting smaller, hard-to-reach oil and gas fields to existing infrastructure.

sessions.title: Providing More Targeted and Compassionate Service Delivery
sessions.abstract: Discover how GIS is transforming community well being through two powerful, data driven initiatives. San Bernardino County, CA expands equitable access to homeless services with a mobile friendly resource map that enhances outreach and cross agency coordination. Mahoning County, OH advances public health transparency with an open access suicide prevention hub that visualizes local trends and supports evidence based strategies. Together, these projects show how spatial insight drives smarter, more compassionate service delivery.

Keywords: service delivery, health, suicide prevention, transparency, target, resource

sessions.title: Python in ArcGIS: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Access the rich capabilities of ArcGIS using ArcPy and ArcGIS API for Python. Explore how to elevate data management, visualization, and spatial data science using Python, as well as the broader story of using Python within the ArcGIS ecosystem.

RecommendedAudience: Basic experience with Python.

sessions.title: Reimagining Right‑of‑Way Management with Real-Time, Automated Workflows
sessions.abstract: This session highlights how organizations are modernizing land and asset management using real‑time GIS and advanced data models. You will learn how automated change detection turns incoming plat or land‑use updates into instant, actionable alerts that improve project coordination and right‑of‑way planning. See how modern parcel management tools enhance data accuracy, streamline asset tracking, reduce costs, and support better decision‑making. Together, these examples show how ArcGIS solves complex land and infrastructure challenges.

Keywords: Surveying, Right-of-way, Transportation, Asset Management, Public Works, Infrastructure
RecommendedAudience: Surveyors, GIS Managers, Transportation Professionals, Public Works Professionals

sessions.title: Resilient Infrastructure: Digital Transformation and Vegetation Safety
sessions.abstract: Learn how leading utilities are building a "single source of truth" to drive operational excellence. You’ll discover Tacoma Power’s strategy for migrating to ArcGIS Utility Network to bridge data gaps and unify GIS with SAP for better asset management. We will also explore AXIA Energia’s Vegetation Management System, which leverages LiDAR, satellite imagery, and mobile apps to mitigate risk and ensure compliance. See how integrating spatial data with enterprise systems streamlines maintenance, enhances safety, and prepares your utility for an AI-driven future.

Keywords: Digital Twin, Vegetation management

sessions.title: The Planner’s Toolkit: Five Steps for Designing Thriving Communities
sessions.abstract: Discover the practical ways in which GIS is enhanced by AI to empower you in resolving housing, compliance, economic mobility, and engagement challenges. Understand how these tools strengthen future-proof development and planning and design for thriving communities. Gain practical insights to implement data-driven solutions that support resilient, inclusive communities.

Keywords: Urban Planning, Economic Development, Transit-Oriented Development, Regional Planning, Community Development, Permitting, Code enforcement, Building & safety

sessions.title: Using ArcGIS to Support Climate-Related Corporate Risk Analysis and Reporting
sessions.abstract: Learn how Esri used its own ArcGIS technology to support the development of its climate-related financial risk report. In this demo theater presentation, discover how geospatial analysis transforms climate risk reporting into a location intelligence workflow that enables organizations to assess asset-level exposure, evaluate risk across scenarios, and generate actionable insights. Find out how the same ArcGIS approach can support disclosure requirements while also informing resilience planning, risk prioritization, and strategic decision-making.

occurrences.room: GIS for Good Demo Expo Theater

sessions.title: A New Dimension in Reality Capture
sessions.abstract: This session will cover the new RTC family of laser scanners.

sessions.title: Creating a Business Case for an Imagery Management System with ArcGIS
sessions.abstract: Infrastructure companies face growing pressure to improve reliability, reduce risk, and operate more efficiently while managing aging assets and environmental challenges. This session explores how PwC helps Esri customers build a business case for an imagery management platform that centralizes lidar, high-resolution imagery, and remotely sensed data within the ArcGIS system. The platform also supports advanced analytics that enable earlier asset defect detection, accelerate inspections, and improve safety by reducing the need for field crews in hazardous environments.

sessions.title: Agentic AI in Practice: Patterns and Real-World Lessons
sessions.abstract: Agentic AI is introducing new approaches to automating geospatial workflows and decision-making. This insight talk provides real-world examples about how organizations are applying agentic AI within ArcGIS—focusing on what worked, common challenges, and key lessons in data preparation, orchestration, and validation. Learn about the high-level patterns and practical tips that can help you identify where agentic approaches can add value to your workflows.

Keywords: AI adoption, decision points, best practices, case studies
RecommendedAudience: AI implementation strategies, decision-making, risk managemen

sessions.title: Emergency Management Practices for Utilities
sessions.abstract: Apply emergency management practices and principles to utility GIS efforts and workflows. This brief presentation highlights the fundamentals of an emergency management response and discusses how utilities can apply planning, mitigation, response, and recovery practices within their departments. Examples of utility GIS projects within each of these areas are showcased to help you identify helpful tools and processes that you might use when preparing for emergencies on your system.

occurrences.room: State and Local Government Expo Theater

sessions.title: Migrate to the ArcGIS Utility Network in Three Months
sessions.abstract: Does migrating to the ArcGIS Utility Network feel out of reach? Discover how a standardized implementation approach dramatically reduces the time, cost, and complexity of migration. Starting with a pre-built design rooted in industry best practices, this unique path delivers a complete Utility Network implementation, with your data, data management tools, and staff training included. Learn how to achieve Utility Network migration without breaking your budget, skip dozens of design workshops, shave months off your schedule, and hit the ground running the Utility Network way.

sessions.title: NOAA User Group
