sessions.abstract: The NOAA User Group is co-hosted by NOAA's GIO team and Esri and open to all partners, collaborators, and end users of NOAA science and data. This meeting is a forum to share ideas, present accomplishments, and network.

sessions.title: The TomTom Data Behind ArcGIS: What You May Not Know
sessions.abstract: Many ArcGIS users are unaware that TomTom data helps power key ArcGIS capabilities. In this session, you’ll learn how TomTom map data contributes to ArcGIS basemaps and APIs, and how TomTom Live Traffic powers the World Traffic Service. We’ll also highlight additional opportunities to leverage TomTom data across the ArcGIS Platform and preview advanced traffic analytics use cases covered in a companion session.

sessions.title: Mapping to Impact: GIS, AI, and Connected Government
sessions.abstract: Tyler Technologies helps public sector organizations transform GIS data into connected, AI-enabled workflows that improve operational efficiency and public service delivery. Featuring the City of Homestead, Florida, this session explores how embedded AI, digital plan review, integrated systems, and location-aware insights support accelerated permitting, stronger cross-department collaboration, and more accessible community development services through a unified digital ecosystem.

sessions.abstract:

Join this SIG for an in-person opportunity to collaborate and share your expertise and experiences to address common challenges in the AEC field. Build connections, learn from peers, and bring actionable ideas back to your organization.

sessions.title: AI Development Life Cycle (AIDLC) in GeoAI
sessions.abstract: The AI Development Life Cycle transforms how geospatial applications are built. This session demonstrates Kiro, AWS's spec-driven agentic IDE, using ArcGIS steering documents to generate a production-ready ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript web map from natural-language intent. Through live demos, see how AIDLC's three phases guide AI from requirements through validated code with human approval at every gate. We also showcase two agentic applications built with Kiro that consume Esri data sources, demonstrating development modes from vibe coding to structured spec-driven workflows. Walk away understanding how AIDLC's Inception → Construction → Operations loop delivers validated geospatial code.

sessions.abstract:

Build connections, learn from peers, and bring actionable ideas back to your organization.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Data Reviewer: AI Assistants for Smarter, Faster Data Quality
sessions.abstract: New AI-powered assistants in ArcGIS Data Reviewer can streamline data validation by automatically predicting rules and guiding you through configuration tasks. Join us to discover how these assistants reduce manual effort, accelerate data readiness, and help organizations achieve high-quality, ready-to-use spatial data with less time and fewer specialized resources.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Best Practices for Configuring Backups
sessions.abstract: If you have ArcGIS Enterprise, you need a clear strategy for backup and recovery. How do you know which method is best for your organization? Technical support experts share best practices for configuring backups using the Web GIS Disaster Recovery (WebGISDR) tool.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Experience Builder: Building Your First Theme
sessions.abstract: Customize the appearance and style of your applications with ArcGIS Experience Builder! Built on the powerful Jimu framework, themes provide a structured, flexible, and extensible approach to design—ensuring a consistent and unified look and feel across your entire experience. This presentation demonstrates the theme framework in Experience Builder, theming best practices, and the steps to create a brand-aligned custom theme.

Keywords: Custom widget, debug, TypeScript, React, builder
RecommendedAudience: Experience with web development.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Indoors: Transforming Operational and Human Infrastructure
sessions.abstract: Re-imagine the facility as a dynamic asset that inspires operational excellence and enhances the workforce experience. Learn how real-time space management, seamless occupant-to-planner feedback loops, and actionable analytics inform smarter decisions, reduce disruptions, and increase resource use. Explore how facility data and workflows extend beyond GIS to empower personnel, logistics, and resource managers to collaborate, adapt environments, and optimize resources—with practical guidance on deployment strategies and data models that support workplace modernization.

Keywords: Workplace modernization, Smart facilities,  Digital workplace, Facility operations

sessions.title: ArcGIS Instant Apps: An Overview
sessions.abstract: If you want to transform your maps into interactive web apps for your users, then ArcGIS Instant Apps is the solution for you! Speakers share how to choose, design, and create apps with some popular templates. Understand how to choose the appropriate tools for your desired workflow and build a focused app experience that is accessible to all users. Speakers also share the road map for Instant Apps and discuss what's to come.

Keywords: ArcGIS Instant Apps, Web Apps, Templates

sessions.title: ArcGIS Notebooks: Getting Started with the ArcGIS Notebooks Assistant
sessions.abstract: Looking for coding assistance? Look no further! In this presentation, you will learn about the ArcGIS Notebooks assistant, a new coding assistant that can help you write better Python code and automate your workflows in ArcGIS Notebooks.

Keywords: AI, Python, Assistant

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online: Best Practices for Content Management and Item Discoverability
sessions.abstract: Help the right audience discover your content. ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise host millions of items of spatial content that can be accessed by individuals, organizations, and the public. Join us to explore how to optimize your content information (metadata) so that it can be found by searching, how to categorize items for filtering, and best practices for publishing authoritative items to the public.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: An Introduction to 3D Editing
sessions.abstract: Join us for an overview of 3D models, including editing multipatch and 3D object feature layers in ArcGIS Pro. This new functionality allows you to create new 3D geometries and edit existing ones. Learn how and why these capabilities and functionality are valuable. Explore what you can do with the new 3D geometries, as well as how to share them, and find out about future developments for 3D functionality.

RecommendedAudience: Interest in editing 3D data in ArcGIS Pro.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Managing Users with User Types
sessions.abstract: Enable your users to work with ArcGIS Pro (and so much more) through user types. This presentation demonstrates the process of adding members to an ArcGIS Online organization. Learn how to use new member defaults, roles, and groups for user type management. Working in a disconnected environment? No problem—find out how to take a license offline.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: ModelBuilder Iteration, Nesting, and Branching
sessions.abstract: Interested in using iteration, often referred to as looping or batch processing, in ModelBuilder? Using examples, speakers demonstrate how a process is executed repeatedly using iterators with different settings or datasets, how to correctly use inline variable substitution, and how to integrate an iterating nested submodel within another model. This presentation also provides best practices for iteration and introduces if-then-else conditional branching logic. Also, speakers discuss how a process can be separated into two conditional branches and then merged at the end of your model.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Approaches for Designing User‑Aware Configurable Apps
sessions.abstract: Learn how to deliver personalized experiences in ArcGIS configurable apps without custom coding. This brief presentation covers using user-specific attributes to filter and display relevant content as well as techniques to streamline tasks, improve efficiency, and create intuitive workflows—all while maintaining scalability and consistency.

Keywords: personalization, dynamic content, user-specific workflows, efficiency, scalability
RecommendedAudience: Experience with configurable app customization, dynamic filtering, and user attribute integration.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: GIS-BIM Integration for Industry Foundation Classes
sessions.abstract: Integrating BIM and GIS has become essential to bringing project content into real-world context. Explore how ArcGIS supports the international BIM standard Industry Foundation Classes (IFC). From best practices to live demonstrations, find out how ArcGIS brings spatial intelligence to open BIM exchanges, enabling data-driven decisions, improved collaboration across teams, and more efficient management of infrastructure throughout its life cycle.

Keywords: BIM, IFC, GeoBIM, CAD,AEC, Geodesign
RecommendedAudience: Experience with CAD and the AEC market.

sessions.title: Trends in State and Local Government That Shape Your Work and the GIS Profession
sessions.abstract: The landscape of government work is always changing—are you prepared for the future? Outside forces can redefine how you deliver value. sessions.abstract:
Join a conversation with your peers and Esri staff on how to empower your workforce with skills to maximize the impact of ArcGIS across your organization. Discover creative ideas and practical techniques to inspire interest, engage more people with your program, and ensure sustained capacity to support your geospatial solutions. Build connections, learn from peers, and bring actionable ideas back to your organization.

 

Reservations will be held until five minutes prior to the start time before seating will be opened to attendees in the on-site queue. If attendees with a reservation remove the session from their schedule, the next person on the wait list will automatically receive that open reservation. Wait lists will only be available before the conference. On-site walk-in attendance may be available dependent on room capacity and will be allowed on a first-come, first-served basis.

ReservationRequired: This session requires a reservation to attend. Please use Add to Schedule to reserve your spot or Join Wait List to be added to the queue.

sessions.title: A New Era of High-Definition 3D Mapping from Space
sessions.abstract: A breakthrough in space-based 3D mapping is making it possible to create high-definition terrain intelligence at a speed and scale that aerial collection alone cannot match. Built on advanced satellite tasking, a trusted spatial foundation, and automated production workflows, this capability produces 15 cm 3D maps with 3 m accuracy in all dimensions. For commercial and civil government customers, it supports sharper urban planning, infrastructure monitoring, consumer-grade mapping, disaster response, and national development missions.
tracks: National Mapping, Navigation, and Geospatial Authorities

Topic: National Mapping, Navigation, and Geospatial Authorities

sessions.title: Your Data, the Real World: When GIS and Spatial AI Align
sessions.abstract: Niantic Spatial and Esri help oil and gas teams find critical assets in complex refineries, combining precise on-site localization with ArcGIS Field Maps operational context to reduce turnaround time, increase asset uptime, and drive safer field execution.

sessions.title: Esri Academy: Learn GIS Your Way
sessions.abstract: Take an insider’s tour of Esri Academy—the online home to hundreds of instructor-led and self-paced courses, videos, massive open online courses (MOOCs), and other learning resources created by subject-matter experts. Get tips to find relevant resources, enroll in Esri learning plans, and create your own custom learning plans. Whether you are new to ArcGIS or a seasoned GIS professional, this brief presentation will show you how Esri Academy can help you achieve your goals.

Keywords: learning resources, training plans, GIS education, MOOCs, skill development
RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with GIS learning, navigating online learning platforms, and self-paced training.

sessions.title: Increase the Value of Your Permitting System
sessions.abstract: Whether or not you love your permitting system, it is likely a business system to which your organization is committed. Discover how ArcGIS applications complement permitting systems and allow you to integrate your existing permitting system to increase its value. This brief presentation will show how ArcGIS can streamline and automate permitting workflows and reveal development patterns and trends.

occurrences.room: State and Local Government Expo Theater

Keywords: Urban Planning, Community Development, Environment Review, Development, Digital Plan Review

sessions.title: Built In, Not Bolted On: ArcGIS for Infrastructure Workflows
sessions.abstract: Infrastructure decisions belong on a map — but the map belongs in the workflow. Join Deloitte to see how we've embedded ArcGIS into capital planning, water rights management, and asset prioritization for government and commercial clients. No siloed GIS. No bolted-on layers. Just location intelligence doing real work, where real decisions get made. Come see what built in, not bolted on actually looks like.

sessions.title: Real‑Time Agricultural Operations and Enterprise Workflow Modernization
sessions.abstract: This session will showcase two examples of transforming manual, fragmented agricultural workflows into unified, real‑time operational ecosystems powered by ArcGIS.

sessions.title: Accessibility Essentials for GIS and Mapping
sessions.abstract: Data visualizations are increasingly used to communicate important information. Ensuring that this information is accessible for everyone is critical. Join us to learn about digital accessibility essentials for improving mapping and data visualizations and how to ensure compliance with accessibility criteria. Discover the relationship between accessibility standards and GIS and the accessibility capabilities across ArcGIS web, desktop, and mobile technologies. This presentation also introduces technologies that you can use to evaluate and remediate data visualization solutions according to best practices.

RecommendedAudience: Experience with essential workflows with Esri products.

sessions.title: Aligning Geospatial and IT Strategies: Best Practices
sessions.abstract: Need help navigating the complex topics and processes involved in aligning GIS and information technology (IT) strategies? Join us to learn how to develop geospatial and IT strategies that are inclusive, agile, and aligned with business needs. Speakers also share how partnering early in the process allows teams to meet ever-changing technological needs more effectively.

sessions.title: Approaches to Data Governance
sessions.abstract: Data is one of your organization’s most valuable assets—but only when it is well managed and governed. This technical session discusses principles and practices for effective geospatial data governance, including roles, classification, policies, standards, and cataloging. Discover how to build a governance framework that supports compliance, privacy, and operational efficiency, offering actionable guidance to improve decision-making and data reliability.

Keywords: Compliance, privacy, data integrity, governance framework, standards enforcement, cataloging, metadata management, risk mitigation, policy alignment, operational efficiency
RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with geospatial data structures, enterprise GIS, and data governance principles.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Business Analyst: Understanding Demographic and Place-Based Data
sessions.abstract: With ArcGIS Business Analyst, you obtain data through the ArcGIS GeoEnrichment service and locally installed datasets. You can access tens of thousands of Esri-published variables for countries around the world and also add maps and data from ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World. In this demo theater presentation, discover what data is available, how it is sourced, and how you can use Esri and ArcGIS Living Atlas data within analyses, infographics, and workflows.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: An Overview
sessions.abstract: New to ArcGIS Enterprise? Learn more about Esri's software system that provides comprehensive GIS functionality on infrastructure that you control. Explore the primary workflows that include data management, mapping and visualization, analysis, and sharing. Discover how ArcGIS Enterprise can benefit you and your organization!

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Best Practices for Upgrading
sessions.abstract: Planning ahead when upgrading to a new release of ArcGIS Enterprise allows you to take advantage of new features and capabilities, stability enhancements, and improved functionality with ease. Learn how you can efficiently and effectively upgrade your deployment, including the steps to take before, during, and after an upgrade so that you can approach upgrades with confidence.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Understanding Logging
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Enterprise logs capture information about events occurring throughout your deployment. Join us to learn how to understand the different logs in ArcGIS Enterprise on Windows and Linux and how to use these logs for monitoring and troubleshooting. Speakers also share some helpful tips and tricks for using these logs in your own deployment.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Experience Builder: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Quickly transform your data into interactive, mobile-optimized web apps and web pages. ArcGIS Experience Builder provides a new level of configuration with flexible design, mobile optimization, smart widgets, and seamless ArcGIS integration. In this presentation, learn about the building blocks of Experience Builder and how they work together, as well as how to quickly create apps using express mode and enhance them using full mode.

Keywords: ArcGIS Experience Builder, App Building, Widget, Theme, Data Source,  Mobile optimization, Web app, Experience, Builder

sessions.title: ArcGIS Field Maps: Situational Awareness and Coordinated Workflows
sessions.abstract: Empower your teams with real-time situational awareness in the field. This presentation showcases how ArcGIS Field Maps mobile maps and location intelligence—plus the new tasks capability—work together to streamline field workflows, improve safety, and inspire smarter decisions. Learn best practices for designing maps and configuring tasks to guide inspections, data collection, and team coordination. Discover how features like location sharing and geofencing keep your workforce connected and responsive—so you can deliver results with confidence.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Field Maps: What's New and the Road Ahead
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Field Maps brings mapping, data capture, and task coordination together in one app for field operations. It keeps mobile teams connected with up-to-date information for situational awareness and offers powerful yet simple ways to collect accurate data. For the office, it enables workflow design, customization, and assignment management. In this presentation, explore the latest enhancements and what’s next to improve efficiency across your field workflows.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Indoors: Floor Plan Editing on the Web
sessions.abstract: Want to build an indoor GIS and empower your operations or facilities team with editing the floor plan data and do what-if scenario planning? Join us to learn how to use the web-based Floor Plan Editor to create, manage, and maintain your indoor maps. With the ArcGIS Indoors Floor Plan Editor, you can edit floor plans from engineering or scanning sources or digitize them by tracing floor plan images. With the ability to import CAD, Revit (2D), and PDF files in ArcGIS Online, indoor mapping is now made easy in ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise.

Keywords: indoor GIS, indoor maps, floor plans, indoor spaces, floor plan editing

sessions.title: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World: Basemaps Are Not Just the Background
sessions.abstract: Basemaps are the foundation for your maps and apps, but they are also part of the story that you are telling. Discover the integral role that basemaps play in developing your maps, and learn about available options through ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World and how to choose appropriate basemaps. Explore how to use tools in ArcGIS Pro and Map Viewer to build your own basemap versions with the ArcGIS Vector Tile Style Editor.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World

sessions.title: ArcGIS Maps for Adobe Creative Cloud: Integrating Design and Mapping with ArcGIS
sessions.abstract: Great maps are crucial for visual storytelling, combining research, data, and cartography. The ArcGIS plug-in for Adobe Illustrator enables cartography and cross-team collaboration by streamlining map design with graphics. Join us to explore the powerful new plug-in capabilities that bridge GIS and graphic design, saving time and inspiring creative mapmaking. Whether you are new to ArcGIS or want to increase its impact in your organization, this presentation provides essential tools and workflows for effective visualization.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Maps for Adobe Creative Cloud

sessions.title: ArcGIS Network Analyst: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Join us to learn more about how you can use ArcGIS Network Analyst to model road networks and solve transportation-related problems, such as routing fleets of vehicles, generating service areas, and performing site-selection analysis. Speakers discuss how to use the ArcGIS World Routing and Analysis Services, your own network analysis services, and local network datasets to solve transportation analysis problems. New features, such as the Waste Collection solver and Snap to Roads and Last Mile Delivery services, are also reviewed.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Accessibility Features, Tips, and Tricks
sessions.abstract: Ensure that your content is available to everyone. This presentation highlights accessibility settings and features in ArcGIS Pro. Explore accessibility topics relevant to ArcGIS Pro, and learn about the assistive and adaptive technologies that you can use to evaluate and remediate ArcGIS Pro content according to best practices and accessibility criteria.

Keywords: Accessibility, A11y, Section 508, 508, Web Content Accessibility Guidelines, WCAG, Americans with Disabilities Act, ADA, EN 301 549,

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: An Editing Overview
sessions.abstract: Whether you are a seasoned ArcGIS Pro user or just getting started, editing your data is one of the most important aspects of working in ArcGIS Pro. Speakers review common editing workflows, such as creating new features using feature templates, snapping, updating attributes, and modifying existing feature geometry using various techniques and tools.

RecommendedAudience: Basic understanding of ArcGIS Pro.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: An Introduction to Python Map Automation
sessions.abstract: Want to write your own map automation scripts using Python? This presentation introduces the mapping module and shows how it can be used to process the contents of ArcGIS Pro projects, automate exporting, and generate PDF map books.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Analyzing and Understanding Crime Hot Spots
sessions.abstract: Interested in finding out how ArcGIS Pro can help you with analyzing and understanding crime hot spots? In this demo theater presentation, learn how to use the various analysis tools in ArcGIS Pro to analyze law enforcement data to better understand crime patterns and trends. Speakers give an overview of the different tools and methods that are available in ArcGIS Pro to identify hot spots, outliers, and spatial clusters based on location, attributes, and time.

Keywords: Crime analysis, police, law enforcement, hot spot analysis

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Configuring and Using Portal Projects
sessions.abstract: Have you heard about ArcGIS Pro portal projects? Learn how to configure portal projects appropriately to accommodate single-user workflows, and understand how to set up sharing groups in your ArcGIS Enterprise portal so that multiple ArcGIS Pro users can work on the same project at the same time.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Tools for Diagnosing and Troubleshooting Issues
sessions.abstract: Have you ever encountered an error message while working in ArcGIS Pro? Learn how the experts in Esri Support Services diagnose and troubleshoot potential problems with machines and software. This presentation introduces you to existing diagnostics tools, including the ArcGIS Pro Performance Assessment Tool (PAT) and the built-in Diagnostic Monitor, to determine performance benchmarks and analyze issues such as unresponsiveness and resource limitations.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions: GIS Request Management
sessions.abstract: Need to communicate the business impact of GIS services and promote the sustainable use of GIS technology? Join us for an overview of the GIS Request Management solution and learn how you can manage the delivery of GIS services and communicate the value of the GIS program to the organization.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions: Renewable Energy
sessions.abstract: Need to develop solar or wind site asset inventories and track land acquisitions? Discover how a collection of renewable energy solutions can help you streamline solar site maps and land acquisitions.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Survey123: Best Practices for Public Data Collection Surveys
sessions.abstract: Creating a form for public data collection requires many considerations: Who is your audience? What language(s) do they speak? How will they interact with the form? How do you keep the submitted data secure?  Discover how you can address these concerns when creating your next public survey.

Keywords: public survey

sessions.title: ArcGIS Topographic Mapping Server: Automated Mapping
sessions.abstract: Produce defense topographic maps automatically with the Map Production System (MPS) on ArcGIS Topographic Mapping Server. Deploy Topographic Production Services and the MPS web app to support Topographic Map (TM), MGCP Topographic Map (MTM), and Joint Operations Graphic (JOG) products, and discover how the MPS streamlines and standardizes cartographic workflows for both ArcGIS Pro and ArcGIS Enterprise.

Keywords:
Automated map production
Defense cartography
Topographic map production
Military mapping standards
Cartographic automation

sessions.title: ArcGIS Urban and ArcGIS CityEngine: Modernizing Planning Workflows
sessions.abstract: Discover how the latest advancements in Urban and CityEngine are empowering planners and urban designers to create smarter, more efficient workflows. This presentation showcases innovative capabilities and emerging technologies that streamline planning processes, enhance collaboration, and deliver data-driven insights for urban development.

Keywords: Urban Planning, Smart Cities, 3D Modeling, Sustainable Development, Urban Design

sessions.title: ArcGIS Velocity: An Overview
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Velocity is a real-time analysis capability in both ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise, enabling organizations to ingest, analyze, and act on data from Internet of Things (IoT) sensors. Join the discussion on how to connect to virtually any type of streaming data, perform real-time analytics and processing, and automatically disseminate information and alert personnel when specified conditions occur.

Keywords: real-time GIS, real-time analytics, streaming data, IoT, sensor data, ArcGIS Velocity, Velocity for Enterprise, event processing, feeds, real-time alerts, big data analytics, data ingestion, real-time visualization, real-time mapping, situational awareness, operations, event detection, time-enabled data, high-volume data

sessions.title: ArcGIS for Excel: Using the Function Builder and Python
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS for Excel empowers you to edit data and create visualizations directly within Microsoft Excel. If you regularly use formulas in Excel and want to extend this functionality with spatial operations, this presentation is for you. The Function Builder tool expands the range of available functions, which are accessible through the formula bar or an intuitive interface. Also, speakers discuss how to incorporate Python into Excel workflows as a problem-solving tool, enabling you to view your results on a map within Excel.

Keywords: ArcGIS for Excel, data editing, data visualization, formulas in Excel, Python, integration, map visualization, share analyses, Function Builder, Microsoft, Excel

sessions.title: ArcGIS: An Overview of Image Analysis
sessions.abstract: Image analysis can seem complex—this presentation gives you a clear starting point. Learn key imagery and raster concepts and then explore core techniques like the Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI), change detection, and classification. Understand how these workflows run in ArcGIS Pro and ArcGIS Online, helping you build confidence and get more value from ArcGIS as a complete image analysis system.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Best Practices and Applied Uses for Gaussian Splats
sessions.abstract: Discover how ArcGIS enables you to generate and work with Gaussian splats. This new layer type depicts the real world with extremely high fidelity and realism. Explore how this new capability can enhance your existing workflow, and learn best practices for capturing imagery with ArcGIS reality mapping applications so that you can generate optimal Gaussian splats.

Keywords: Gaussian Splats
RecommendedAudience: Attendees with all skill levels are welcomed.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Explore Data Interactively with Extended Reality
sessions.abstract: Curious about Esri’s vision for extended reality (XR) in GIS? Join us to learn how ArcGIS integrates with Unity and Unreal Engine to power immersive experiences across industries. Learn how to design compelling augmented reality (AR) and virtual reality (VR) solutions for mobile and XR headsets like Meta Quest. Explore end-user apps such as 360 VR and the new XR Viewer. Discover how these tools transform GIS data into interactive narratives that engage audiences, enhance decision-making, and open new possibilities for geospatial storytelling.

RecommendedAudience: Interest in Esri’s vision of how extended reality applies to GIS.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: GeoAI
sessions.abstract: Interested in learning more about geospatial artificial intelligence (GeoAI) in ArcGIS? GeoAI is the integration of AI, machine learning (ML), and deep learning (DL) with GIS to produce knowledge and solve geographic problems. It is a key part of spatial analysis for tasks related to clustering and pattern detection, prediction and forecasting, and information extraction from many types of data. This presentation highlights the application of ArcGIS GeoAI capabilities across imagery, 3D, unstructured text, and feature, tabular, and time-series data and workflows.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Understanding and Supporting STAC
sessions.abstract: Simplify with STAC. The SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog (STAC) is an open standard that enables discovery and access to geospatial assets like imagery, elevation models, and point clouds across cloud and enterprise systems. Using a lightweight JSON metadata model, STAC defines spatial and temporal properties and links directly to source data. Explore the core elements of STAC—Catalogs, Collections, and Items—and understand how ArcGIS uses them to improve interoperability and enable scalable, standards-based workflows.
tracks: National Mapping, Navigation, and Geospatial Authorities

Keywords: STAC, OGC, open source, imagery, standards, catalog
Topic: National Mapping, Navigation, and Geospatial Authorities
RecommendedAudience: Understanding of imagery.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Using Drones for Public Safety
sessions.abstract: Discover how ArcGIS uses drone technology to support public safety workflows. Capture and process imagery for creating digital twins in 2D and 3D as well as work with live and recorded video to enhance situational awareness.

RecommendedAudience: Experience with drones.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Web Development Strategies
sessions.abstract: New to developing web applications and services with ArcGIS? This presentation is for you. Speakers provide an overview of the platform architecture and discuss important technologies that you can use in your development. Learn how to build web apps using configurable apps and builders, and maximize productivity by building custom apps with ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript. Gain a better understanding of the ArcGIS technologies and capabilities that are most useful for your work.

sessions.title: Connected Conservation: Turning Shared Data Into Coordinated Action
sessions.abstract: Conservation at scale requires systems that connect people, workflows, and decisions. This session features GIS-based approaches that centralize field observations, facilitate operational planning, and knowledge-sharing, and translate them into actionable insights. From private-lands delivery linked to project planning and financial tracking, to a global aquarium collaboration supporting marine species conservation through digital contribution and storytelling, speakers show how GIS platforms help partners coordinate and accelerate impact.

Keywords: Conservation, field observation, marine species

sessions.title: Consistency at Scale: Governance for Configurable Apps
sessions.abstract: Ensuring consistent design and branding across multiple configurable apps can be challenging. This brief presentation shares practical governance strategies to enforce standards for design, accessibility, and branding at scale. Learn how to streamline app deployment and maintain uniformity while supporting flexibility for diverse teams.

Keywords: governance, design consistency, accessibility, branding, deployment standards
RecommendedAudience: Experience with governance, design consistency, accessibility, branding, and deployment standards.

sessions.title: Construction Planning, Scheduling and Project Controls
sessions.abstract: In this session, you will learn how location intelligence can transform construction planning, estimating, and day-to-day execution by connecting schedules, forecasts, and operational data to real-world context. Discover how map-first workflows make complex information accessible to non-technical users, improve visibility into plan versus actual performance, and support faster, more confident decision-making. You will gain insight into how spatially enabled scheduling and reporting improve productivity, safety, and operational alignment across construction teams.

Keywords: Construction Planning; Spatial Scheduling; Plan vs Actual; Location Intelligence
RecommendedAudience: Basic understanding of construction or AEC workflows; Familiarity with project scheduling or estimating concepts; General awareness of GIS or spatial data concepts

sessions.title: Delivering Smarter Field Operations With Real Time Intelligence
sessions.abstract: You will learn how connected field tools and real time information improve fleet, facility, and street operations. Discover how mobile maps, telematics, and dashboards support faster response, better route coverage, and stronger accountability while replacing paper workflows and legacy systems.

Keywords: Field Operations, Field Mobility, Route Management, Operations Visibility, Performance Tracking
RecommendedAudience: Designed for fleet and operations professionals, and GIS staff who support work management or routing. Some experience with mobile applications, vehicle data, or operational dashboards will help attendees get the most from this session.

sessions.title: Designing Better Park Experiences
sessions.abstract: This session shows how parks teams use GIS to improve both event management and wayfinding. Learn how map based tools replace manual planning, enhance coordination, and support real time communication. See how data driven analysis and automated mapping guide consistent signage and better visitor experiences. Attendees will gain practical, scalable strategies for modernizing workflows and designing more intuitive, accessible park environments.

Keywords: real-time, open spaces

sessions.title: Education and Workforce Development: Cultivating Knowledge and Career Skills
sessions.abstract: Education is the foundation for a prosperous, equitable, and sustainable society. Millions of students and scholars globally use ArcGIS and Esri learning resources for data visualization, problem solving, and storytelling to promote a deeper understanding of natural systems and human societies. The Esri Education Program cultivates spatial literacy from grade school through higher education, preparing students for careers across a range of industries. Learn about available resources to nurture the next generation of problem solvers.

occurrences.room: GIS for Good Demo Expo Theater

Keywords: Professional development, learning, student

sessions.title: Empowering EOCs & DOCs with Real-Time Data & Analytics for Better Decisions
sessions.abstract: As disasters grow more complex and fast-moving, emergency managers are turning to integrated GIS platforms to anticipate risk and operate with greater precision. This session showcases how agencies are combining real-time data, automation, and spatial intelligence to build a common operating picture across jurisdictions for emergency operations centers and department operations centers.

Keywords: spatial intelligence, common operating picture

sessions.title: Enterprise Geodatabase: Demonstrating Branch Versioning Workflows
sessions.abstract: Interested in branch versioning? This presentation explores versioning management workflows related to branch versioning using ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Enterprise, and the REST API. Join us to see the following workflows (and more) in action: creating and deleting versions, editing named versions, changing version ownership, reconciling, and posting versions.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with ArcGIS Pro and enterprise geodatabases.

sessions.title: From Legacy to Leading-Edge: Evolving Parcel Management at Scale
sessions.abstract: This session highlights how organizations are modernizing land records by migrating large parcel datasets to modern, cloud‑based parcel management systems. Learn how teams are using ArcGIS Parcel Fabric, Attribute Rules, and Tasks to streamline editing, improve data quality, reduce downtime, and support high‑volume land information workflows. You will see how modern tools enable real‑time error detection, easier data conflation, better collaboration, and more accurate, accessible parcel information for both internal users and the public.

Keywords: Data Accuracy, Workflow Automation, Parcel Management, Land Administration, Land Records
RecommendedAudience: GIS Managers, Land Surveyors, Parcel Mappers, County Officials, Real Estate Professionals, Tax Assessors

sessions.title: Geospatial Excellence in Action: Stories from the Field
sessions.abstract: Discover how organizations are solving mission-critical challenges by applying five strategic pillars: business, governance, systems, engagement, and capacity. This dynamic presentation features rapid-fire mini case studies, each highlighting a pillar in action. Gain practical insights from real-world field stories, illustrating how these pillars inspire innovation, collaboration, and success in diverse geospatial environments.

Keywords: Strategic pillars, business alignment, governance, systems integration, engagement strategies, capacity building, innovation, collaboration, success stories, organizational maturity

sessions.title: Global to Local to Global Geodesign for Climate Change Mitigation (GLG): Open Workshop
sessions.abstract: The Global to Local to Global (GLG) Climate Mitigation Project workshop will share, teach and apply the GLG user tools. Small teams will work at national or local-regional scales as projected to 2050. Each participant should bring a modern laptop computer. GLG provides a scalable workflow across diverse jurisdictions and geographic scales based on projected climate and environmental changes by 2050. It utilizes global science and global optimization designs based on emissions reduction and cost targets to inform local knowledge and local designs, in turn informing national and global policy.

sessions.title: Identifying Fraud Patterns to Protect Public Benefits
sessions.abstract: Explore how two counties are strengthening program integrity by uncovering hidden patterns in EBT theft. Colusa County’s fraud tracking dashboard reveals real time hotspots and supports targeted investigations, while San Bernardino County’s spatial temporal analysis modernizes how agencies detect trends, coordinate with partners, and safeguard vital benefits. Together, these efforts show how smarter insight protects vulnerable residents and enhances public service accountability.

Keywords: public health, fraud, ebt, benefit, dashboard, identify, accountability

sessions.title: Map Viewer: Editing Essentials
sessions.abstract: Ready to level up your editing skills? Learn how to configure a web map and data for editing workflows and how to add, delete, and update features and their attributes. This presentation also explores the latest enhancements to editing tools in Map Viewer, including forms, templates, drawing tools, snapping, placing a grid, and batch attribute updates.

Keywords: feature updates, attribute management, live data editing, permissions, map maintenance, web maps
RecommendedAudience: Experience with editing data in Map Viewer.

sessions.title: Operational Ocean Intelligence: APIs, Models, and WebGIS for Marine Management
sessions.abstract: Discover how modern GIS workflows, API integrations, and scalable modeling are transforming marine management. Attendees will learn how dynamic ocean data from vessel activity to habitat indicators, can be integrated into ArcGIS to support real-time monitoring, enforcement, and ecological assessment. The featured presentations will demonstrate how reproducible geoprocessing, machine-learning-based habitat classification, and API-driven data pipelines operationalize ocean intelligence and reduce data barriers, enabling transparent, interoperable systems for conservation and coastal resilience.

sessions.title: Pipeline GIS Solutions
sessions.abstract: In this session, ExxonMobil will discuss how it uses Esri technology to better engage with local clans and gain access to land outside the pipeline easement, which is essential for pipeline work programs. Also, hear Kinetik discuss how it migrated from a legacy system to ArcGIS Field Maps to unify field data collection and accessibility.

sessions.title: Project Homeland Cascading Infrastructure and National Graph
sessions.abstract: When critical infrastructure fails, it rarely fails alone. Project Homeland unites MITRE and Esri to bridge the gap between national scale infrastructure intelligence and the real time decisions local responders make during cascading disruptions. This session introduces access to National Knowledge Graph and shows how linking systems, dependencies, and risks can improve situational awareness. We invite attendees to share needs, challenges, and scenarios to shape the next phase of this work and strengthen national resilience.

sessions.title: Redistricting that Connects Census Data to Community Voice
sessions.abstract: This session highlights how accessible mapping approaches help communities better understand district boundaries and the impacts of different scenarios. You will learn how scenario modeling and demographic insight support thoughtful discussions and informed choices, enabling clearer communication throughout the process.

Keywords: Public Engagement, Scenario Modeling, Boundary Planning, Fair Representation, District Planning
RecommendedAudience: GIS professionals, election and redistricting staff, policy analysts, election leaders, government teams supporting boundary design and public engagement.

sessions.title: Reimagining Elections with Enterprise GIS
sessions.abstract: The election process faces growing demands for transparency and efficiency. Accurate location data is the foundation for accessible elections. GIS is essential to meet these growing demands and expectations and offers capabilities far beyond traditional polling-place maps. This brief presentation explores how GIS drives an enterprise approach to election management and empowers election offices to plan strategically, operate seamlessly, and engage voters effectively throughout the election cycle.

occurrences.room: State and Local Government Expo Theater

Keywords: vote, elections, voter, ballot,

sessions.title: Safer by Design: Improving Risk Management and Emergency Planning
sessions.abstract: Maintaining a safe campus requires collaboration across many business units and multiple sources of information. The ability of ArcGIS to integrate data by location and create a common operating picture is crucial for planning and response. Learn how universities are connecting GIS with other systems to manage risk and avoid critical incidents, as well as to support real-time decision making and respond to those emergencies that do occur.

Keywords: SharePoint, Low-code, integration

sessions.title: Spatial Statistics Overview: From Means and Medians to Machine Learning
sessions.abstract: Join us to step through the Spatial Statistics toolbox in ArcGIS Pro and explore a variety of example applications and range of questions that can be answered. The capabilities include describing the spatial distribution of your data, mining for spatial and multivariate patterns, and making spatially informed predictions. If you are new to spatial statistics, this presentation is a great way to familiarize yourself with these powerful tools and methods. If you have been using spatial statistics for a while, come discover some of the latest advancements.

sessions.title: The Role of Nonprofits in Housing Policy
sessions.abstract: The housing crisis requires a multi-faceted approach that goes beyond just the traditional planning department. In this session, we'll see how a non-profit organization is taking a unique approach to analyzing the housing situation in communities.

Keywords: housing affordability, faith-based land, corporate-owned land
RecommendedAudience: nonprofits

sessions.title: Unlocking the Power of GIS: Real-Time Operations and Predictive Maintenance
sessions.abstract: In this session you will learn how two organizations leveraged GIS in innovative ways to build a comprehensive view of their networks, resulting in reduced maintenance costs, increased efficiency, and more strategic planning. Presenters will share how they are leveraging ArcGIS to drive real-time operations, predictive maintenance, and customer-centric services.

Keywords: Asset management, Digital transformation, Sewerage management, Operations

sessions.title: Your Blueprint to an Enterprise GIS in State and Local Government
sessions.abstract: What is an enterprise approach? The definition of enterprise GIS has changed dramatically as technology has advanced and peer technologies have strengthened capabilities. This presentation discusses the environment that needs to be created to take GIS from a project-specific to a mission-critical business system. Explore why an organization-wide implementation of GIS is necessary to maximize your return on investment, establish more GIS users within your organization, and apply the geographic approach at scale.

Keywords: Enterprise GIS, enterprise-wide approach, GIS leadership, AI, ML, digital twin, peer technologies, IT executive, permitting, asset management, system of record

sessions.title: Admin, Backup, and Clean your ArcGIS Organization with GEO Jobe
sessions.abstract: Do you want to streamline your administration tasks and backup or migrate your content? Then this session is for you. This session showcases proven tools that act as force multipliers, allowing admins to focus on the power of ArcGIS rather than getting bogged down in routine tasks. We will go over Admin Tools for ArcGIS, Backup My Org (backup and migration), Clean My Org, Symphony for ArcGIS (automation), and Manage My Attachments. Come to see this session to learn more!

sessions.title: Business Symposium
sessions.abstract:
Find out how leading companies across industries are integrating mapping and analytics into enterprise-wide business intelligence systems to accelerate growth and drive operational efficiency.



 

Reservations will be held until five minutes prior to the start time before seating will be opened to attendees in the on-site queue. If attendees with a reservation remove the session from their schedule, the next person on the wait list will automatically receive that open reservation. Wait lists will only be available before the conference. On-site walk-in attendance may be available dependent on room capacity and will be allowed on a first-come, first-served basis.

ReservationRequired: This session requires a reservation to attend. Please use Add to Schedule to reserve your spot or Join Wait List to be added to the queue.

sessions.title: National Geospatial Symposium
sessions.abstract:
Join other GIS professionals, government leaders, policy makers, and technology teams to learn how communities of practice are sharing geospatial data and working across organizational boundaries to address common challenges.  Gain insights and see practical examples of how to improve data access, engage stakeholders, and strengthen collaboration using modern geospatial tools.

 

Reservations will be held until five minutes prior to the start time before seating will be opened to attendees in the on-site queue. If attendees with a reservation remove the session from their schedule, the next person on the wait list will automatically receive that open reservation. Wait lists will only be available before the conference. On-site walk-in attendance may be available dependent on room capacity and will be allowed on a first-come, first-served basis.

ReservationRequired: This session requires a reservation to attend. Please use Add to Schedule to reserve your spot or Join Wait List to be added to the queue.

sessions.title: Geospatial Change Management for Organizational Buy-in
sessions.abstract: How do you ensure that the solutions that you create attract the adoption needed to create value for your organization? Explore a structured approach for success. In this brief presentation, experts discuss key strategies to elevate awareness of how GIS solutions affect the organizational mission, how to communicate in a way that resonates with leaders and all stakeholders, and how to gain support for the solutions that you deliver.

Keywords: adoption strategies, organizational alignment, stakeholder communication, change management
RecommendedAudience: Experience with stakeholder engagement, communication planning, and organizational alignment strategies.

sessions.title: Solutions That Keep Elections Transparent and Reliable
sessions.abstract: This brief presentation provides an overview of the election solutions that help organizations engage voters, manage election operations, and share authoritative election results.

occurrences.room: State and Local Government Expo Theater

sessions.title: AI Native Reality Capture & Mapping for Indoor and Campus Environments
sessions.abstract: Learn how GeoCam enables a comprehensive approach to mapping and reality capture for campuses, facilities, and indoor spaces. The most effective way to build accurate data for Esri’s ArcGIS Indoors. We will cover capture options (indoor and outdoor), different data outputs and deployment methods to meet a variety of data security requirements (including on-prem options with Dell / NVIDIA).

sessions.title: ROK Command: A New Era of GIS Managed Cloud Services
sessions.abstract: This presentation will introduce ROK Command™, a groundbreaking operational intelligence platform built to transform how modern GIS organizations manage, optimize, and evolve their ArcGIS environments. Attendees will learn how ROK Command™ prepares your environment for the future, keeps your platform running at peak performance, and ensures the data layers beneath it all remain healthy, stable, and optimized. No lag, no limits, no guesswork. Command your ArcGIS Enterprise in real-time.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Business Analyst: Using Your Data in Analyses, Reports, and Infographics
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Business Analyst connects to content throughout ArcGIS, including content housed in your organization, ArcGIS Online, and ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World. Join us to learn how to add your own data and Esri’s data to analyses and infographics as well as how to share your work more widely and collaborate using ArcGIS Experience Builder, ArcGIS StoryMaps, and ArcGIS Dashboards.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Data Pipelines: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Do you spend hours repeating the same data preparation steps just to update a feature layer in your ArcGIS organization? ArcGIS Data Pipelines is a no-code, visual data engineering capability designed to streamline data preparation workflows. Create, run, and schedule workflows with an intuitive drag-and-drop interface. Connect to multiple data sources, blend and integrate datasets, and publish hosted layers for use across your organization. Learn about the key features and start simplifying your data integration today.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Administering Your Deployment
sessions.abstract: Are you administering and optimizing an ArcGIS Enterprise deployment on Windows or Linux? Learn best practices for administrators and get an overview of advanced system and service settings. Speakers also discuss working with administrative endpoints, defining data stores, configuring utility services, and locating and exploring log files.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Included Tools for Migrating Content
sessions.abstract: Moving ArcGIS Enterprise from one environment to another is a strategic endeavor. Whether you are moving content from a development to a staging environment or from a physical machine to the cloud, ArcGIS Enterprise on Windows and Linux includes tools to help you migrate your content. Get a high-level overview of these included tools: the Web GIS Disaster Recovery (WebGISDR) tool, import/export operations, and the ArcGIS API for Python.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Publishing Content and Services
sessions.abstract: Get your content into the right hands. Join us to explore the process of publishing content and services using ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Enterprise, and ArcGIS Server. Learn about necessary infrastructure components and how to publish various service types, such as map services and feature services, using your data.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Experience Builder: Best Practices for Building Exciting Apps
sessions.abstract: Join this action-packed presentation to learn best practices for designing and building exciting apps. Whether you are building a web app or a web site, you will come away with tips to meet your precise design goals while supercharging your workflows. Learn how to properly size and position elements, choose color palettes that pop, use ArcGIS Arcade expressions to dynamically display data, and customize your apps to truly stand out. Get ideas to make your app work across all screen sizes.

Keywords: ArcGIS Experience Builder, Best practice, Design, Experience, Web app

sessions.title: ArcGIS Field Maps and ArcGIS Pro: Distributing Data with Feature Service Sync
sessions.abstract: Whether you are new to distributing data or have prior experience, this presentation has something for you. Join us to discover how to use the feature service sync capability in ArcGIS and learn various data synchronization workflows using ArcGIS Field Maps and ArcGIS Pro.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with distributing data in the ArcGIS system.

sessions.title: ArcGIS GeoAnalytics Engine: Applications and Use Cases
sessions.abstract: Want to learn how to apply spatial analysis to big data with Apache Spark and Python? Using live demonstrations and projects built by Esri’s product team, speakers explore ArcGIS GeoAnalytics Engine and guide you through analytic workflows, including the new raster analytics capabilities. The demonstrations will also include reading data from a cloud data store, chaining SQL functions and analysis tools for vector and raster analysis, and viewing and saving results.

sessions.title: ArcGIS GeoEvent Server: Migrating Workflows to ArcGIS Velocity
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS GeoEvent Server has powered real-time event processing in ArcGIS Enterprise. Now, ArcGIS Velocity is available for ArcGIS Enterprise, so organizations can modernize and scale their real-time analytics workflows using advanced real-time architecture. This presentation provides practical guidance for transitioning GeoEvent Server workflows to Velocity, including best practices for connecting to real-time feeds, designing equivalent or enhanced analytic logic, managing performance, and integrating with the broader ArcGIS system.

Keywords: GeoEvent Server, migrate GeoEvent, Velocity migration, migration patterns, real-time workflows, streaming data, processors, feeds, event processing, GeoEvent best practices, real-time modernization, Enterprise real-time, data integration, migration guidance, real-time architecture, real-time transformation, sensor integration, ArcGIS Enterprise real-time
RecommendedAudience: Experience with GeoEvent Server.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Hub: Mobilizing Volunteers for Community Impact
sessions.abstract: Discover how ArcGIS Hub transforms collaboration and volunteer management. Learn to recruit, organize, and engage community volunteers effectively with powerful mapping and engagement tools. This brief presentation showcases practical applications for streamlining volunteer efforts, enhancing community participation, and maximizing the impact of civic initiatives. Find out how cities are using Hub to build stronger, more resilient communities through organized volunteer programs.

occurrences.room: State and Local Government Expo Theater

Keywords: Volunteer management, community engagement, stakeholder collaboration, Homeless Point-in-Time (PIT) Count, Adopt-A programs

sessions.title: ArcGIS IPS for Indoor Positioning: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Bring the blue dot indoors with ArcGIS IPS, Esri’s indoor positioning product. Learn how ArcGIS IPS lets you locate yourself and others inside a building or facility in real time. Speakers provide a product overview and technology background, as well as use cases, trends, and the road map for what's to come.

Keywords: Indoor positioning, indoor maps, asset inspection, asset maintenance, safety, security, operations

sessions.title: ArcGIS Knowledge: Connecting Data and Analyzing Relationships
sessions.abstract: Discover how ArcGIS Knowledge transforms and connects enterprise data into a living network of insights. Learn the foundations of building and exploring knowledge graphs to uncover hidden relationships between people, places, events, and assets. This technical session introduces the key concepts, data integration patterns, and visualization tools that help analysts accelerate investigations and situational awareness by combining spatial and nonspatial data. ArcGIS Knowledge extends ArcGIS Enterprise and enables new user experiences across desktop and web applications and developer tools.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Discover the world's foremost collection of geospatial information. ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World curates ready-to-use maps, apps, and layers from Esri and the global GIS user community. Find out how to use this content—and contribute to it—to support work across the ArcGIS system. Speakers will also showcase recent additions and updates to ArcGIS Living Atlas.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise: Better Together
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise are better together for your business workflows. Join us to learn about the key differences and similarities between ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise and how they enhance organizational effectiveness through content sharing. Discover how to set up collaborations, configure settings, and manage shared content. Explore common workflows like sharing maps and layers and using a mix of content from ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online: Hosted Feature Layer Views
sessions.abstract: Create unique windows into your data—customized for your audience. Discover how hosted feature layer views in ArcGIS Online can be used to meet audience needs while ensuring data integrity and privacy.

RecommendedAudience: Experience working with hosted feature layers.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: An Overview
sessions.abstract: New to GIS or ArcGIS desktop software? In this presentation, explore the fundamentals of ArcGIS Pro, including how to create maps and layouts, set up a file geodatabase, edit and visualize data, and perform analysis—no prior experience is required. Discover how GIS helps people solve spatial problems and make smarter decisions.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Geoprocessing Tips and Tricks
sessions.abstract: Want to learn more about geoprocessing? Join us for an overview of geoprocessing and spatial analysis, data management, conversion, and related workflows in ArcGIS Pro. Discover capabilities to improve your productivity when using geoprocessing. Speakers discuss geoprocessing history, environment settings, parallel processing, automated workflows with batch processing, ModelBuilder, Python, scheduling and sharing tools, and new tools for solving new and old spatial problems efficiently.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Tips and Tricks for Working with Legends
sessions.abstract: Explore legends in ArcGIS Pro. Learn more about the components that make up legends and some of the many ways that they can be configured. Also, learn how you can use legend styles, shortcuts, and the Contents pane to streamline legend creation for future layouts.

RecommendedAudience: Experience with layouts, mapping, and visualization.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions:  Asset Management
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Solutions help public works and utilities develop authoritative asset inventories and understand the condition of their assets. Join us for an overview of this collection of solutions and learn how you can streamline asset inventories, monitor asset conditions, and address critical needs in a timely manner.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions: Environment and Natural Resources
sessions.abstract: Need to understand the impact of new development on sensitive natural areas? Discover how a collection of environmental and natural resources solutions can help you conserve and protect natural areas.

sessions.title: ArcGIS StoryMaps: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Want to share the story behind your work in a stunning, modern format that informs and inspires? ArcGIS StoryMaps helps you combine dynamic content with custom maps and share it through interactive web pages and presentation slides. This introductory technical session provides an overview of what’s possible with ArcGIS StoryMaps, with inspiring examples of how organizations use the app to create engaging, map-driven experiences. Discover how easy it is to bring your stories to life with beautiful visualizations and powerful storytelling tools.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Survey123: AI Assistants
sessions.abstract: AI assistants are revolutionizing the ArcGIS experience by using the capabilities of generative AI. These assistants facilitate content creation, data introspection, and summarization as well as provide insightful suggestions, recommendations, and assistance in code writing. In this presentation, learn practical tips for getting the most out of AI assistants in ArcGIS Survey123.

sessions.title: ArcGIS for Teams: Empowering Collaboration
sessions.abstract: Want to maximize the capabilities of ArcGIS for Teams? Speakers provide guidance about configuring your Microsoft Teams tenant, accessing AI beta features, and enabling ArcGIS access in team meetings. Also, speakers demonstrate both how to use Microsoft Power Automate flows with ArcGIS within Teams and how you can integrate Microsoft Copilot and ArcGIS for Teams, all with an emphasis on geo-collaboration!

Keywords: ArcGIS for Teams, Teams, Microsoft, Integration, Mapping, Collaboration, Meeting, Microsoft Teams tenant configuration, AI, Beta, Assistants, Power Automate, Flows, Automation, Copilot

sessions.title: ArcGIS for Telecommunications: Location Intelligence in Network Life-Cycle Management
sessions.abstract: Explore how ArcGIS enhances telecommunications through spatial intelligence across the network life cycle. Learn how location-based insights improve planning, construction, operations, and infrastructure management. Discover how organizations use the Telecom Domain in ArcGIS Utility Network to build network information management systems for telecom and fiber networks. Also, find out how ArcGIS supports digital twins, enabling accurate network visualization, simulation, and data-driven decision-making.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Automating and Enhancing Apps for Accessibility and Localization
sessions.abstract: Learn how to implement scalable GIS solutions that support accessibility and internationalization for broader audiences. Configure dynamic pop-ups and images with ArcGIS Arcade expressions; enable multiple language translations in ArcGIS Dashboards; and automate workflows with ArcGIS Notebooks and Microsoft Power Automate. This presentation highlights accessibility enhancements, such as keyboard navigation and screen reader compatibility. Speakers provide best practices for extending ArcGIS products to support your GIS solutions through automation, accessibility, and internationalization.

Keywords: Accessibility, A11y, Section 508, 508, Web Content Accessibility Guidelines, WCAG, Americans with Disabilities Act, ADA, EN 301 549,

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Data Exploration and Visualization for Spatial Analysis
sessions.abstract: Join us to learn visualization techniques for analyzing and effectively communicating your data using ArcGIS Pro and Map Viewer in ArcGIS Online. Discover how different methods, from maps and charts to 3D scenes, can help you compare categories and amounts, visualize distributions and frequency, explore relationships and correlations, and understand change over time or distance.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Improving Airfield Data Management with AI and Automation
sessions.abstract: Use ArcGIS and AI to streamline your airfield data collection and analysis. This presentation demonstrates how to extract airfield sign data from photos taken in the field and then store them using the ArcGIS Aviation Airports extension. Also, learn about a web-based application that analyzes obstacles around the airfield to ensure that protected airspace remains clear of obstruction.

Keywords: airfield, obstacle surfaces, obstacle assessment, runway, obstacle analysis
RecommendedAudience: Experience with obstacle surfaces in ArcGIS Aviation Airports.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Maximizing the Value of Demographic Content
sessions.abstract: Whether you work in ArcGIS Online or ArcGIS Pro as a GIS expert or novice, there is something for everyone in this presentation. Get tips and tricks for bringing a demographic perspective into your projects. Learn how to find, apply, visualize, and share ready-to-use demographic content from Esri Demographics, the U.S. Census Bureau, and global statistical agencies in your analyses and workflows.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World
Keywords: ArcGIS Living Atlas, Demographics, Socioeconomics, Living Atlas

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Wildland Fire and Large Language Models
sessions.abstract: Wildland fire is an inherently spatial problem, and AI can help you understand where things occur. Join us to understand the "art of the possible" for geospatial artificial intelligence (GeoAI) and wildfire data. Explore how Esri is enhancing ArcGIS as a system to use AI across all aspects of mapping and spatial analytics. Speakers demonstrate a system designed specifically for generative AI, large language models, and a natural language processing capability that can help you envision the promise and challenges of AI in the wildfire arena.

Keywords: AI, Large Language Model, Wildfire, Wildland Fire, Intelligence

sessions.title: Build It Faster: AI-Assisted Geospatial Apps for Defense Missions
sessions.abstract: Defense missions move quickly, but building the geospatial apps that support them often requires time and specialized development skills. This demo theater presentation shows how AI-assisted development with ArcGIS can accelerate the process. Learn how AI helps to generate code, connect data, and configure workflows so that you can rapidly build and deploy geospatial apps that support operational needs across the geospatial enterprise.

Keywords:
AI-assisted development
Agentic AI
Data integration
Mission apps
Geospatial app development

sessions.title: Building Resilient Enterprise GIS: Scaling Programs, and Navigating to Azure
sessions.abstract: This session brings together practical lessons from enterprise GIS leaders on building programs and platforms that can scale, adapt, and endure. Crescent Energy’s paper -  Building Enterprise GIS to Scale and Outlast You, focuses on creating resilient GIS programs through strong teams, governed and trusted data, thoughtful design, automation, field support, etc. While ExxonMobil’s paper, Journey to Azure: The Good, the Bad, & the Ugly, shares lessons from migrating a large, complex ArcGIS Enterprise from on-premises infrastructure to a disconnected Azure environment.

sessions.title: Digital Twins and ArcGIS
sessions.abstract:
Digital twins are evolving from static models to connected systems that support operations and better decisions. This spotlight session grounds the Digital Twin Maturity Model in real customer journeys across conservation, state and local government, and AEC. Speakers share practical examples—from descriptive twins to more connected, predictive, and scenario driven approaches—highlighting real challenges, data, integration, and organizational hurdles. The session closes with practical next steps and related UC sessions.

 

Reservations will be held until five minutes prior to the start time before seating will be opened to attendees in the on-site queue. If attendees with a reservation remove the session from their schedule, the next person on the wait list will automatically receive that open reservation. Wait lists will only be available before the conference. On-site walk-in attendance may be available dependent on room capacity and will be allowed on a first-come, first-served basis.

ReservationRequired: This session requires a reservation to attend. Please use Add to Schedule to reserve your spot or Join Wait List to be added to the queue.

sessions.title: Digital Workflows in Action: GIS-Based Asset Management for Treatment Facilities
sessions.abstract: In this session you will discover how GIS based mobile applications have enabled digital workflows focused on data collection and asset management within treatment facilities. Presenters will share how they were able to quickly collect asset information, including condition data to build a GIS-based asset registry. There will also be discussion focused on integration with their CMMS, enabling seamless data exchange and data-driven asset management.

Keywords: Vertical Asset Management, System Integration, Wastewater

sessions.title: Elevating Permitting And Service Delivery With Automated Workflows
sessions.abstract: Discover how public works and utility staff improve permitting and service delivery by adopting modern, automated processes that streamline work across teams. You will learn how organizations simplify reviews, improve communication with applicants, and reduce delays by connecting information in a more consistent digital environment.

Keywords: Process Automation, Service Delivery, Permitting, Workflow Automation, Right Of Way
RecommendedAudience: A good fit for permitting and development services teams, utility and public works, and GIS professionals involved in enterprise workflows.

sessions.title: Environmental Analysis, Remediation and Resilience
sessions.abstract: In this session, you will learn how advanced GIS and GeoAI workflows are being used to support environmental management, risk mitigation, and climate resilience in challenging and remote environments. Discover how deep learning, remote sensing, and spatial analysis enable scalable insights, reduce reliance on field-intensive methods, and improve long-term decision-making. You will gain perspective on how these approaches support regulatory compliance, safety, and community-informed planning across reclamation and coastal resilience initiatives.

Keywords: GeoAI; Environmental Resilience; Remote Sensing; Climate Risk
RecommendedAudience: Basic understanding of GIS and spatial analysis; Familiarity with environmental or infrastructure projects; Introductory knowledge of remote sensing or data analytics

sessions.title: Esri Geoportal Server: Cataloging Metadata
sessions.abstract: Create centralized, searchable catalogs. Esri Geoportal Server is a free, open-source solution that empowers organizations to manage and share geospatial resources across intranets or the open web, supporting interoperability with ArcGIS and other technologies. Learn about key features, deployment, and customization options, and explore real-world examples from the global Geoportal Server community that is creating open, discoverable data ecosystems.
tracks: National Mapping, Navigation, and Geospatial Authorities

Keywords: geoportal server, open source, metadata, geospatial catalogs, standards,
Topic: National Mapping, Navigation, and Geospatial Authorities

sessions.title: From Raw Data to Custom App: Data Engineering, Mapping, and Web Development
sessions.abstract: Join this dynamic technical session where speakers will build applications from the ground up. Starting with raw data, they will demonstrate how you can automate data ingestion into ArcGIS Online and then cover key techniques for creating and managing a service. Next, they will discuss authoring the web map, including cartography tips and tricks, and, finally, building a custom web app from scratch with the ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript that uses the web map as the starting point to drive the visualization and experience around the data.

Keywords: data pipeline, feature service, web map, javascript
RecommendedAudience: Knowledge of the concept of web applications and experience with any programming language.

sessions.title: Geospatial Change Management: Preparing Your Organization for GIS
sessions.abstract: Whether you are implementing ArcGIS for the first time or expanding GIS access to new departments, success relies on people embracing your solutions. Attend this highly interactive technical session to discover strategies that will increase the pace of adoption, the number of people using the solutions that you configure, and the skill level of GIS users. Understand how to effectively communicate business value and learn about creative ways to spark interest in GIS capabilities across business units.

Keywords: Adoption strategies, stakeholder engagement, business value communication, organizational readiness, change management framework, user onboarding, cross-department collaboration, GIS expansion, training programs, cultural transformation
RecommendedAudience: Understanding of organizational change-management principles, familiarity with GIS adoption strategies, ability to communicate business value, and experience with stakeholder engagement.

sessions.title: Getting Started with AI Assistants
sessions.abstract: AI assistants in ArcGIS use the power of generative AI to streamline everyday workflows. Join this technical session to learn about the various assistants that are available and how to get started.

sessions.title: Information Integration Improves Educational Facility Efficiency
sessions.abstract: Educational facilities include a variety of building types with diverse functions. Managing these facilities requires collaboration across multiple business units and business systems. Location provides a framework for integrating information to gain a holistic view of operations. With a shared understanding of spaces and assets, administrators can use those resources more efficiently to support learning.

Keywords: facility management, real-time monitoring

sessions.title: Integrating Linear Referencing Services with Other Business Systems
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Roads and Highways and ArcGIS Pipeline Referencing support integration with external business systems that maintain linear referenced information outside the geodatabase. This presentation highlights the patterns for linking these external systems with the Esri linear referencing system (LRS) and then updating them at regular intervals as the LRS changes.

sessions.title: Introduction to Reality Mapping with Drone, Aerial, and Satellite Imagery
sessions.abstract: Reality mapping creates accurate digital representations of the physical world using imagery. Discover how ArcGIS reality mapping capabilities let you generate accurate and up-to-date 2D and 3D data products from drone, aerial, or satellite imagery, and how these can be used to enhance your GIS. Learn about the different apps supporting reality mapping within ArcGIS, the Reality Engine, and its outputs. Speakers provide an overview of the workflow and present common uses for the generated data.

sessions.title: Leveraging ArcGIS for Problem Solving in Emergency Management
sessions.abstract: Disasters and emergencies are inherently geospatial – responders and emergency managers need to understand the “where” of an event, whether it’s the path of a hurricane, perimeter of a wildfire, earthquake, tsunami, or the location of a 911 call. The power of ArcGIS can help solve problems, as its flexibility enables users to create a platform to support the efficient and effective sharing of GIS data across all levels of government, the private sector, and non-profit organizations.

Keywords: geospatial, disasters

sessions.title: Modernizing Gas Utility Network Information Management
sessions.abstract: Join this session to hear Southwest Gas talk about its migration to the ArcGIS Utility Network from its legacy Geometric Network and ArcFM platform. Then, Spire Energy will discuss creating a reference document to help end users resolve post-utility network-upgrade errors on their own, and the resulting benefits to all.

Keywords: Migration

sessions.title: NG911: Putting GIS Into Action to Revolutionize Emergency Communications
