sessions.abstract: Next Generation 911 (NG911) modernizes emergency communications by improving the resilience of 911 services, facilitating interoperability between agencies, reducing misroutes, and supporting voice, text, and video for callers that cannot speak, hear, or communicate clearly. Learn how accurate GIS data is built and maintained to enable location based routing and faster response, and hear real-world examples of how NG911 and GIS together save lives and protect property in ways legacy 911 could not.

sessions.title: Predictive Analysis: Exploring Relationships with Regression and Causal Analysis
sessions.abstract: Join us to learn how to uncover relationships and patterns using various regression techniques. Explore the fundamentals of linear regression and using geography to explore how relationships vary spatially. Also, learn about the new Causal Inference Analysis tool, which applies methods that allow you to go beyond the adage “correlation does not imply causation.”

sessions.title: Real-Time Intelligence Workflows for Law Enforcement
sessions.abstract: Learn how law enforcement agencies modernize intelligence workflows using ArcGIS. This session highlights how GIS can supports data collection, spatial analysis, and real-time decision support through a variety of tools. See how departments securely connect crime data, operational insights, and collaborative applications to deliver actionable intelligence to agency personnel.

sessions.title: Spatially Enabling  Business Systems
sessions.abstract: Location matters—whether you are managing assets, optimizing logistics, or enhancing customer engagement. This focused presentation explores how integrating spatial data and GIS capabilities into core business systems, including platforms like ServiceNow, enables smarter decisions and improves operational efficiency.

Keywords: location intelligence, business integration, operational efficiency
RecommendedAudience: Experience with spatial data integration, business system workflows, and interoperability.

sessions.title: Strengthening Election Security and Transparency with Real Time Operations
sessions.abstract: Discover how election offices use real time insight to reduce uncertainty and improve situational awareness across their operations. You will learn how location intelligence supports clear communication and enables predictable workflows that help teams stay ahead of issues and maintain stronger accountability.

Keywords: Operational Awareness, Election Security, Real Time Insight, Chain of Custody, Public Engagement
RecommendedAudience: Election administrators, operations leads, GIS staff supporting election workflows, government managers seeking improved visibility and more predictable operations.

sessions.title: The Geographic Approach: Advancing Your Nonprofit Mission
sessions.abstract: There is more to GIS than the technology itself—it is a system for change. That is why nonprofits are geoenabling their operational workflows to increase community impact by enhancing their data with rich demographics, environmental layers, and more. In this demo theater presentation, learn how to identify needs, allocate resources effectively, and design data-driven strategies that prioritize resources for the communities that you serve.

occurrences.room: GIS for Good Demo Expo Theater

Keywords: NGO, fundraise, fundraising, NPO, 501c, charity, charitable, foundation, donor

sessions.title: Transportation Planning Through Automated and Collaborative Workflows
sessions.abstract: Learn how automated GIS workflows replace manual processes to deliver faster insights, safer operations, and more informed planning. By centralizing data and detecting conflicts early, agencies improve collaboration, reduce costs, and strengthen decision‑making across programs. Two organizations will demonstrate how they implemented these GIS‑driven approaches to transform their operations.

sessions.title: Unifying Data and Delivering Solutions to Address Homelessness
sessions.abstract: Discover how two communities use GIS to better understand and respond to homelessness. By integrating multisource data—police and fire calls, public works reports, service funding, and cleanup operations—these projects uncover hotspots, improve data quality, and enhance transparency. Through automated workflows, dashboards, and interactive maps, GIS streamlines cross department collaboration, supports leadership decision making, and delivers an accessible view of community needs and city actions.

Keywords: homeless, data, dashboard, unshelter

sessions.title: What You Need to Know About the National Spatial Reference System Update
sessions.abstract: The National Spatial Reference System (NSRS), managed by the National Geodetic Survey (NGS), is getting a big upgrade to make geospatial references more accurate, accessible, and consistent all across the country. This change will bring benefits to everyone working with or managing spatial data. In this session, the NGS will share the latest updates, including exciting advances in geodetic reference frames, new standards, and improved tools for better positioning and measurement.

sessions.title: Historic Preservation User Group Meeting
sessions.abstract: Hosted by the National Trust for Historic Preservation, this group meeting will discuss uses of GIS for historic preservation initiatives. This will include a review of an ongoing project around Route 66, followed by an open discussion of projects, ideas, and best practices for leveraging GIS in historic preservation projects.

sessions.title: How Central Arkansas Water Built a Platform for Growth on Microsoft Azure with CyberTech
sessions.abstract: Central Arkansas Water (CAW) partnered with CyberTech to modernize its legacy ArcGIS system, moving to a scalable cloud-based GIS on Microsoft Azure. This expanded access from 75 to 200 users and enabled real-time mobile data for field crews. The system delivers 99.99% availability, handles millions of requests, and improves emergency response and compliance. By bringing workflows like valve inspections in-house, CAW reduced costs and positioned GIS as a strategic platform for innovation, advanced analytics, and future capabilities.

sessions.title: Executing GIS Projects Using an Agile Framework
sessions.abstract: The Agile methodology—a reactive, iterative approach to project execution—delivers value to customers faster and with fewer issues. In this focused presentation, Esri project managers share how they use this approach to complete successful GIS projects.

Keywords: project delivery, stakeholder collaboration, continuous improvement, sprint planning, backlog management, team alignment, iterative development, customer engagement, workflow optimization, performance metrics
RecommendedAudience: Experience with project planning, stakeholder engagement, and iterative delivery.

sessions.title: Mappy Hour Expo Social
sessions.abstract: Connect with the people behind the technology at the Expo Social in Halls A-D. Meet Esri partners, spark conversations with Esri staff and experts, and mingle with peers from across the community in a setting designed for connection, collaboration, and great conversations.

sessions.title: How Trimble Connects Field to Office in the ArcGIS Universe
sessions.abstract: For asset owners in government, utilities, and natural resources, success requires confidence at every turn. The gap between field reality and GIS records creates risk; the Trimble and Esri partnership closes it. Learn how a seamless field-to-finish workflow ensures a single source of truth for your assets. See how high-accuracy GNSS and AI-driven reality capture tools can be used to populate ArcGIS to help utilities manage networks, DOTs accelerate inspections and conservationists scale stewardship. See how interconnecting the physical and digital worlds transforms raw data into intelligence.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Business Analyst: Explore the Next Generation UX
sessions.abstract: Join us for an engaging session on the future of ArcGIS Business Analyst's UX. Explore the reimagined user experience, learn about its roadmap, and get an early look at new AI-powered capabilities under development. Connect directly with Business Analyst experts and help shape the future direction of the product’s user experience.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Connectors for Power Automate: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Take your automations beyond just notifications. The ArcGIS Connectors for Power Automate allow key spatial tasks to be set up as automated processes, all using a no-code, drag-and-drop experience. Learn about a few currently available connectors and functions that can enhance your process automations, and find out how to extend your flows with spatial recipes, including using create, read, update, and delete operations on feature layers.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Data Reviewer: Improving Data Quality for Land Records Management
sessions.abstract: Learn how to implement ArcGIS Data Reviewer to support validation of features typically used for land records management. This presentation highlights validation methods and best practices for implementing ArcGIS Data Reviewer.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Expanding Beyond the Base Deployment
sessions.abstract: Find out how to design and configure a base deployment of ArcGIS Enterprise and how to extend the deployment with additional capabilities through server roles, such as ArcGIS GIS Server, ArcGIS GeoEvent Server, ArcGIS Notebooks Server, ArcGIS Workflow Manager, and more. Learn about the APIs that are available with these additional capabilities; custom extract, transform, and load (ETL) tools; and analytics and system administration. This presentation also includes best practices, such as workload separation, and helpful tips to ensure a successful ArcGIS Enterprise deployment.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Experience Builder: Getting Started with Developer Edition
sessions.abstract: Quickly build web apps and web pages with no to low code. ArcGIS Experience Builder is highly configurable with flexible design, mobile optimization, smart widgets, and seamless ArcGIS integration. It is extensible, so you can add your own tools to deliver production-ready solutions fast. Learn the developer edition from installation, collaboration, and deployment to upgrade, including importing apps from ArcGIS Online, adding custom widgets, and integrating with ArcGIS Enterprise.

Keywords: Install, custom widget, deploy, integration, upgrade, builder
RecommendedAudience: Experience with web development.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Geostatistical Analyst: Spatial Interpolation Using Kriging
sessions.abstract: Kriging is the most widely used interpolation method in GIS. Speakers provide a solid foundation of this method and share best practices, assumptions of the methods, and how to interpret the results. See how Empirical Bayesian Kriging (EBK) allows accurate interpolation with minimal options in both 2D and 3D. In 2D, learn how to use explanatory variables to improve the interpolation. In 3D, learn how to explore the results as a continuous voxel layer.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Hub: Best Practices for Site Creation, Content Management, and Administration
sessions.abstract: Organizations worldwide use ArcGIS Hub to create websites for data sharing, stakeholder collaboration, and community engagement. Learn best practices for designing visually appealing and highly engaging Hub sites. Discover how to configure Hub catalogs to share content privately or as open data and set up search and download options to help stakeholders discover and use data. Explore additional best practices that will help your content shine. Finally, gain insights into administrative practices for effectively managing and growing your site.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Indoors: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Discover the power of indoor mapping with ArcGIS Indoors, an extension to ArcGIS for indoor mapping. This presentation provides an overview of ArcGIS Indoors capabilities and how to create and share floor-aware maps of your building interiors. Gain insight into how to use indoor maps to power Indoors apps for space planning, workspace reservation, indoor navigation, sharing a location, and more, as well as how licensing works.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online: Best Practices for Hosted Feature Layers
sessions.abstract: Learn to use hosted feature services in your apps effectively. This presentation explores how the feature service REST API makes apps more efficient and reliable. You will also learn the recommended workflows for updating, editing, and syncing feature service data.

RecommendedAudience: Experience creating hosted feature layers in ArcGIS Online.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: An Introduction to ModelBuilder
sessions.abstract: Automate your geoprocessing workflows with ModelBuilder in ArcGIS Pro. Join us to learn how ModelBuilder can be used to create automated workflows by adding data and tools and connecting them or creating workflows from an existing project history. This presentation introduces beginners to the model diagram, types of elements and tools, connections, and best practices to organize and document analysis workflows. Speakers also discuss migration best practices, editing model schema, debugging methods, working through model reports, and efficiency tips that can help you to be more productive.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Autodesk Integration with the Esri System Using CAD and BIM Data
sessions.abstract: Explore techniques for using Autodesk CAD and BIM data, such as Civil 3D files, to support your GIS workflows in ArcGIS Pro. Learn how to geoposition your data and convert it to a geodatabase for visualization, analysis, and publishing to ArcGIS Online for sharing with your organization.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with CAD, BIM, Autodesk, DWG, DGN, Civil 3D, BIM Cloud Connection, and georeferencing.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Deploying Across Your Organization
sessions.abstract: Learn about the installation and configuration options in ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Online, and ArcGIS Enterprise for supporting a multimachine deployment of ArcGIS Pro. Find out how to customize your users’ experience and enable their success with using ArcGIS Pro by setting up default basemaps; configuring organization-determined application settings, including access to Favorites; and sharing preconfigured project templates.

Keywords: Administration, IT admin, admin
RecommendedAudience: Understanding of IT administration skills and best practices in deploying software to multiple users.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Getting Started with the ArcGIS Pro Assistant
sessions.abstract: Meet the new ArcGIS Pro assistant (beta) and learn practical tips for getting the best out of it. This presentation provides expert guidance on how the ArcGIS Pro assistant uses generative AI and addresses the security and privacy considerations that matter most when providing AI capabilities to your organization. Do not miss this chance to ask questions and join the dedicated Early Adopter Community for the ArcGIS Pro assistant.

Keywords: AI, assistant, pro

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Interactive Flood Simulation
sessions.abstract: Curious about how to model flooding risks using dynamic simulations? Join us to learn how to configure flood scenarios in ArcGIS Pro and use simulation to evaluate the resiliency of current and future landscapes to increasing rainfall and water levels. Then, discover how you can export these analysis results for additional workflows and sharing with others.

Keywords: Urban planning, Disaster Management, Emergency Response, Environment, Climate, Simulation, Flood, Water, Plume, Pollution

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Parcel Editing Tips and Tricks
sessions.abstract: Do you want to be a pro at maintaining parcels in ArcGIS Pro? Learn tips and tricks to improve efficiency with parcel editing and maintenance workflows. In this presentation, learn how to perform an annexation/road widening, automate data loading from CAD, and run repeatable workflows using tasks. Other topics include improving spatial accuracy with point updates and creating parcel title maps. Throughout the presentation, speakers will provide tips for parcel editing, data entry, and parcel mapping.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with ArcGIS Pro.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions: Law Enforcement Solutions
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Solutions help law enforcement agencies monitor crime trends and track the effectiveness of response activities. Join us for an overview of this collection of solutions and learn how you can identify emerging trends, plan evidence-based responses, and share important safety information with the public.

Keywords: Law Enforcement, Police, Crime Reduction, Community policing, Crime Analysis, Crime Analyst

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions: The Military Ground Mobility Solution for Operational Planning
sessions.abstract: Terrain, weather, and time shape military operations and influence how forces move and operate in the field. This demo theater presentation introduces the Military Ground Mobility solution, which provides advanced capabilities for cross-country mobility analysis. Learn how to use this ArcGIS solution to compile terrain data and assess terrain trafficability for operational planning. Find out how the solution supports decision-making, improves situational awareness, and provides commanders with actionable insight into both friendly and enemy unit movements.

Keywords:
Terrain analysis
Cross-country mobility
Terrain trafficability
Operational planning

sessions.title: ArcGIS Spatial Analyst: An Overview
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Spatial Analyst is widely used by numerous organizations daily for spatial modeling and analyzing raster data and images to solve common real-world problems. Join us to explore how to analyze terrains, select the best locations, discover spatial patterns, find the best routes, manage risk, and detect patterns. Discover how this functionality enhances your analyses and stay updated on the latest improvements.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Survey123: What's New and the Road Ahead
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Survey123 brings together the power of geography and forms. Organizations worldwide use ArcGIS Survey123 to modernize everyday workflows. Join us to learn about the new features that were added in the past year and what’s coming next for Survey123!

sessions.title: ArcGIS for Excel: An Overview
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS for Excel enables seamless integration between ArcGIS and Microsoft Excel. This technical session is intended for users of both Excel and ArcGIS, whether you are looking to map tabular or ArcGIS data within Excel, or you are an ArcGIS user seeking to enhance your workflow with Excel’s capabilities. Discover the use of geocoding to enable spatial analysis of Excel data, as well as methods for aggregating, filtering, and sharing this data. Also, learn about functionalities such as editing features and attributes that are synchronized with ArcGIS feature layers.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: An Overview of Image Analysis
sessions.abstract: Image analysis can seem complex—this presentation gives you a clear starting point. Learn key imagery and raster concepts and then explore core techniques like the Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI), change detection, and classification. Understand how these workflows run in ArcGIS Pro and ArcGIS Online, helping you build confidence and get more value from ArcGIS as a complete image analysis system.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Understanding and Supporting the OGC GeoPackage
sessions.abstract: Discover the Open Geospatial Consortium (OGC) GeoPackage, an open, standards-based, and platform-independent file format for storing and exchanging geospatial information. Built on top of a SQLite database, a GeoPackage is a container for all kinds of data, including vector features, raster maps, and nonspatial data, making it useful for sharing and using geographic data across applications and devices. Speakers discuss the format and its capabilities and explore practical applications using ArcGIS.

Keywords: sql lite, geopackage, OGC, standard, data formats,
RecommendedAudience: Experience with databases.

sessions.title: Architecture Diagramming for ArcGIS Systems
sessions.abstract: Documenting your architecture is critical to communicating design intent and specifics with various stakeholders, and, when it is done right, it has a big impact on the long-term success of your system. This demo theater presentation explores architectures of different scopes (concept, logical, and physical) and at different scales before further examining the different diagramming approaches and styles. Speakers also review the new diagramming resources that are available through the ArcGIS Architecture Center.

Keywords: visualization, diagramming standards, stakeholder alignment, architecture templates, performance optimization, governance models, integration patterns, cloud architecture, security considerations, architecture documentation, resource planning.
RecommendedAudience: Experience with system architecture.

sessions.title: Best Practices for Capturing Drone Imagery
sessions.abstract: Learn best practices for capturing drone imagery with ArcGIS Flight so that you can create high-quality 2D and 3D mapping products in ArcGIS Reality, as well as capture and create geospatial video. Discover the flight planning essentials, and review the challenges that a drone pilot might encounter and how to address them. Speakers guide you through a complete planning and capture scenario using ArcGIS Flight.

Keywords: Drones/UAS
RecommendedAudience: Attendees with all skill levels are welcomed.

sessions.title: Creating and Managing 3D Content
sessions.abstract: Learn new ways to create and maintain 3D content—including large-scale 3D reality meshes, Gaussian splats, city-scale 3D objects, point clouds, detailed 3D buildings, BIM models, underground 3D networks, and 3D voxel layers. This presentation explores the different workflows for creating, editing, and publishing 3D layers in ArcGIS—as well as publishing and updating 3D web layers and existing 3D content.

RecommendedAudience: Interest in 3D layers.

sessions.title: Deep Learning in ArcGIS: From Foundations to Real-World Applications
sessions.abstract: Explore how deep learning is applied across ArcGIS. This presentation provides a high-level overview of key concepts, common challenges, and best practices, followed by real-world examples, including emerging approaches with oriented imagery. Gain a practical understanding of how to move from experimentation to scalable, production-ready workflows.

Keywords: Model lifecycle management, reproducibility, feature engineering, data pipelines, MLOps, model deployment, hosted services, automation, scalability, enterprise workflows
RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with GIS, spatial data, AI, and automation.

sessions.title: Designing and Implementing Well-Architected Systems
sessions.abstract: The ArcGIS Well-Architected Framework introduces the concepts of system patterns and architecture practices as key inputs to an architecture design process. But how do you actually apply these concepts to a system that you need to design? Join us to explore how to use and build on the ArcGIS system patterns and learn how to apply architectural pillars to your design to ensure that you are considering the right options, aligning to your organization's goals, and building a resilient, capable system that will encourage adoption.

RecommendedAudience: Experience with system architecture.

sessions.title: Emergency Management Programs of the Future
sessions.abstract: Emergency management programs are evolving, and so are the tools that support them. Join us to explore how modern tools—such as geospatial artificial intelligence (GeoAI), generative AI, drones with volumetric debris estimates, advanced analytics, real-time integration and alerting, enhanced field mobility, 3D visualization, and virtual reality—are transforming the field of emergency management. Learn how to integrate these technologies into your operations to create a smarter, faster, and more resilient program that is ready to meet today’s challenges and tomorrow’s demands.

Keywords: Emergency Management, GeoAI, ML, VR,

sessions.title: Esri Technical Certification: Advancing GIS Careers and Programs
sessions.abstract: Find out how certifying GIS knowledge and ArcGIS skills can benefit both individuals and organizations for whom ArcGIS is mission-critical software. In this brief presentation, speakers describe the latest roster of exams and exam-preparation resources, how you can identify the right certification for you, and what’s coming later this year.

RecommendedAudience: Interest in certification, GIS knowledge assessment, and career advancement planning.

sessions.title: Field Operations: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Want to learn more about Esri's field apps and how they can support your mobile operations? Join us to learn about key features and exciting new developments in the ArcGIS Field Maps, ArcGIS Navigator, ArcGIS Survey123, and ArcGIS QuickCapture mobile apps. Discover how these apps revolutionize field operations by increasing situational awareness and collaboration, both indoors and outdoors, increasing data quality and accuracy, streamlining field processes, and providing efficient navigation.

sessions.title: Fine-Tuning Field Operations with High-Accuracy GPS and Sensors
sessions.abstract: Do you know where your assets are? Keep teams safe, speed up data capture, meet regulatory compliance, and enable faster future responses by locating assets. Discover how Esri’s field apps work with external sensors and receivers—including the global navigation satellite system (GNSS), laser scanners, offset capture tools, and line locators—to turn those benefits into reality through high-accuracy, efficient data collection. Explore real-world examples and learn configuration options and connectivity workflows that you can apply in your own operations.

Keywords: high accuracy, GPS, GNSS, data collection

sessions.title: Inspiring Action Through the Global Climate Geodesign Competition
sessions.abstract: Climate stability requires smart designs that lower carbon emissions and increase sequestration. The Global Climate Geodesign Competition (GCGC) celebrates spatial urban, metropolitan, regional, or national designs that maximize climate benefits, whether these designs are already existing, prepared or currently in development, or specially designed for this competition. Meet winners of the competition and learn how to get involved in the International Geodesign Collaboration.

occurrences.room: GIS for Good Demo Expo Theater

Keywords: Urban and regional planning, mitigation, resilience

sessions.title: Public Works as a Platform for Innovation
sessions.abstract: Innovation in public works begins with leadership. This presentation demonstrates how public works departments are transitioning from paper-based processes to enterprise platforms that provide real-time awareness, automation, and advanced analytics. Discover how forward-thinking teams are integrating technologies like digital twins, drones, 3D printing, AI, indoor GIS, virtual reality (VR), and augmented reality (AR) into their operations. Learn how your organization can become an innovation lab, where new ideas are tested, scaled, and delivered.

Keywords: public works, advanced technology, digital twin, real-time, AI, VR, drones, 3D, asset management, paperless workflows, Indoors, velocity, Hub, Field Maps, enterprise, public sector

sessions.title: Python in ArcGIS: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Access the rich capabilities of ArcGIS using ArcPy and ArcGIS API for Python. Explore how to elevate data management, visualization, and spatial data science using Python, as well as the broader story of using Python within the ArcGIS ecosystem.

RecommendedAudience: Basic experience with Python.

sessions.title: Reality Mapping for Infrastructure
sessions.abstract: Drone imagery is transforming how infrastructure is inspected, maintained, and understood. Join us to learn how ArcGIS Reality mapping tools streamline the process—from drone flights to shareable 2D and 3D products. Speakers demonstrate how to manage drone imagery, generate high-accuracy orthomosaics and point clouds, and produce mesh and digital elevation models for infrastructure assets. Find out how these products integrate directly with ArcGIS for inspection workflows, field collaboration, and engineering analysis.

sessions.title: Science Symposium
sessions.abstract:
Explore topics such as climate change, sustainability, visualization, geodesign of Earth's future, and related growth in geospatial technology for the benefit of science and society.

 

Reservations will be held until five minutes prior to the start time before seating will be opened to attendees in the on-site queue. If attendees with a reservation remove the session from their schedule, the next person on the wait list will automatically receive that open reservation. Wait lists will only be available before the conference. On-site walk-in attendance may be available dependent on room capacity and will be allowed on a first-come, first-served basis.

ReservationRequired: This session requires a reservation to attend. Please use Add to Schedule to reserve your spot or Join Wait List to be added to the queue.

sessions.title: The National Spatial Reference System Update: Insights from the Experts
sessions.abstract: Hear from a panel of experts in the geospatial industry as they discuss the upcoming National Spatial Reference System (NSRS) update. You will learn why organizations are excited to implement the new datums, gain some tips to prepare your organization for the updates, and discover helpful hints for a smooth(er) transition.

sessions.title: Transforming AEC Workflows with Enterprise Imagery Management and GeoAI
sessions.abstract: Join us for a demonstration of how organizations can centralize imagery as a system of record, use distributed raster analytics for large-scale processing, and deliver high-resolution imagery products to engineers, field crews, and executives through intuitive web applications. Explore how ArcGIS Image Server automates the production of elevation models, time-enabled mosaics, change detection layers, and multisensor data products, reducing the burden on desktop users and accelerating project insights.

Keywords:
GeoAI
Aerial imagery management

sessions.title: Using Logs and Diagnostic Tools to Troubleshoot Feature Services
sessions.abstract: Daunted by trying to identify the source of errors in workflows using an enterprise geodatabase and feature services? This presentation explores how to collect and analyze various logs. Gain insight into using these ArcGIS logs and DBMS (database management system) traces to troubleshoot and correct unexpected behaviors, empowering you to streamline operations and enhance system reliability.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with enterprise geodatabases and feature services.

sessions.title: Using Spatial Intelligence for Safety in Public Transit
sessions.abstract: Explore how GIS tools can transform transit safety operations. Learn about integrating real-time incident and CAD feeds, critical infrastructure monitoring, and crime analytics into a unified dashboard that supports rapid response and proactive planning. Speakers share examples that illustrate the use of location-aware analytics to pinpoint high-risk stations, optimize patrol routes, and coordinate emergency communications with internal and external stakeholders. The brief presentation equips transit agencies with actionable spatial strategies to enhance rider security and operational resilience.

occurrences.room: State and Local Government Expo Theater

Keywords: Public transit, public safety, transit safety, safety, security, LA28, World Cup, situational awareness, common operating picture.

sessions.title: Solving Complex Business Challenges with Advanced Parcel Data Analysis
sessions.abstract: Complex business needs often require advanced data analysis techniques. In this session, join the ReportAll team as we talk to real world practitioners about the way they solve business challenges by combining our parcel data and other geospatial data layers to enable advanced analyses. We’ll walk through examples of data extraction, model building, AI-powered data management, and more. Additionally, we’ll suggest how these examples can apply to a wide range of industries.

sessions.title: Meet the Speakers Social
sessions.abstract: Connect with our Spotlight session speakers in a relaxed, informal setting. Network, ask questions, and exchange ideas with presenters and fellow attendees—no formal agenda, just great conversations.

sessions.title: South Central Arc User Group (SCAUG)
sessions.abstract:
The South Central Arc User Group (SCAUG) would like to invite you to attend the Annual Esri UC Social!!!
It will be located at the San Diego Convention Center-Room 28C/D & the adjoining patio overlooking the beautiful San Diego Bay & Coranado Bridge.
There will be snacks & drinks cosponsored by Trimble Unity Maintain, AllTerra Central Inc, & SCAUG.

You do not need to be a SCAUG Member or from Oklahoma, Texas, Louisiana, or Mississippi to attend. Everyone is welcome so bring a friend & see how much SCAUG continues to grow!!! 
 

RSVP with SCAUG

 

See you at the annual Esri UC SCAUG Social!!!

sessions.title: Asia Pacific Regional User Group Meeting
sessions.abstract: A forum for Esri Asia Pacific customers and distributors to connect, collaborate, and stay informed on the latest regional developments. The session will provide key updates on business priorities, product innovations, and strategic initiatives across the region, while also offering a platform to share insights, discuss challenges, and highlight opportunities. Attendees can engage directly with regional leaders and peers, exchange best practices, and strengthen partnerships within the Esri Asia Pacific community

sessions.title: European Regional User Group Meeting
sessions.abstract:
We have a series of presentations given by the Esri team in Europe, including welcomes and highlights of the most relevant events in the region over the past year, as well as a list of upcoming events where we want our users to participate until the end of the year.

Afterwards, we have client presentations, which generally showcase innovative uses of the technology for the region, serving as inspiration for others. We also have discussion and Q&A sessions. Following this, we continue the dialogue and exchange of experiences on the balconies of the convention center.

sessions.title: West Asia, Middle East and Africa Regional User Group Meeting
sessions.abstract: A forum for Esri  WAMEA (West Asia, Middle East and Africa) customers and distributors to connect, collaborate, and stay informed on the latest regional developments. The session will provide key updates on business priorities, product innovations, and strategic initiatives across the region, while also offering a platform to share insights, discuss challenges, and highlight opportunities. Attendees can engage directly with regional leaders and peers, exchange best practices, and strengthen partnerships within the Esri WAMEA community

sessions.title: Business Social
sessions.abstract: Let’s take a minute to unwind and socialize with your fellow Business Symposium attendees and Esri Staff. Join your peers as you gather to network and discuss the latest in geospatial technology.

sessions.title: Electric & Gas and Telecommunications Social
sessions.abstract: Electric & Gas Utilities and Telco Social is your opportunity to meet, mingle, and share your conference experiences. Join us!

sessions.title: Energy Resources and Mining Social
sessions.abstract: Connect with your peers, unwind, and enjoy an evening of networking and community.

sessions.title: GIS for Good Social
sessions.abstract: Your opportunity to mingle, connect, and unwind with like‑minded professionals.

sessions.title: National Government Social
sessions.abstract: Network with other GIS professionals in national government over food and drinks. Chat with peers to learn how other agencies are innovating with location technology.

sessions.title: Transportation Social
sessions.abstract: Add some playtime to your work time. Join us to connect and reconnect with your peers.

sessions.title: Water Utilities and Water Resources Social
sessions.abstract: Take time to relax and connect with like‑minded professionals in a casual, social setting.

sessions.title: International Regional User Group Social
sessions.abstract: Connect with other international Esri users for an evening of fun and socializing. Grab a drink and a snack, and start a conversation with colleagues from around the world.

sessions.title: Esri 5K/Fun Run/Walk
sessions.abstract:
Requires seperate registration.  Additional details and registration link.

 

Take a scenic walk or run along a flat waterfront route on the stunning San Diego coast, passing through Embarcadero Marina Park North and over the boardwalk. Enjoy chip timing for accurate results and connect with the Esri user community as you share the experience with fellow participants. Whatever your pace, it’s all about fun and camaraderie!

 

Participants aged 18 years and older are welcome. Guests of Esri UC attendees can register on-site at the Activities Desk in Lobby D of the San Diego Convention Center beginning on Sunday, July 12, 2026.

sessions.title: Morning Yoga
sessions.abstract:
Requires separate registration.  Additional details and registration link.

 

Wake up your body and mind with yoga—open to all levels. Each class starts with gentle warm-up poses, flowing through sun salutations and standing, seated, and inverted poses. The focus will be on breath control, alignment, and present-moment awareness, with modifications to suit all levels and paces.

Participants aged 18 years and over are welcome. Guests of Esri UC attendees can register on-site at the Activities Desk in Lobby D of the San Diego Convention Center beginning on Sunday, July 12, 2026.

Price
Early bird until July 9 | $35*
On-site starting July 11 | $45*
5K and two yoga sessions bundle (must sign up by July 9) | $105*

*Includes one Esri yoga mat

sessions.title: Hands-On Learning Lab
sessions.abstract: Take a self-paced lesson at the Hands-On Learning Lab. The Hands-On Learning Lab is a dedicated space to take free lessons on a variety of GIS and ArcGIS topics. Each self-paced lesson takes about one hour to complete and includes conceptual information and step-by-step software exercises. We provide laptops and the ArcGIS software needed to complete each lesson. Esri instructors are available to assist with lesson selection and answer any questions you may have.

sessions.title: Map Lounge
sessions.abstract:
Beyond navigation, maps teach, inspire, and unite us. The Map Lounge is a dynamic, interactive space where you can unwind and admire astonishing maps and powerful stories.

Tour the Interactive Map Gallery, a collection of printed and virtual maps from Esri’s global user community. Discover Storytelling with Maps, featuring exhibits that celebrate diverse voices and innovative storytelling. Join fellow GIS enthusiasts at cozy “fireside chats”. Get inspired by special displays and more.

You’ll also have access to a Hands-On Learning Lab, as well as charging stations in the Relax & Recharge Zone. 

The Map Lounge opens with an evening reception on Monday, July 14.

sessions.title: Real Estate SIG
sessions.abstract:
Join a conversation with your peers and Esri staff to discover how best-in-class realtors and managers use ArcGIS to give their sales teams the confidence that they need to win business and be successful. Real estate teams use ArcGIS to help their clients make fast decisions about properties, to optimize investment portfolios, and to uncover and share critical insights about markets, neighborhoods, and sites. Build connections, learn from peers, and bring actionable ideas back to your organization.

 

Reservations will be held until five minutes prior to the start time before seating will be opened to attendees in the on-site queue. If attendees with a reservation remove the session from their schedule, the next person on the wait list will automatically receive that open reservation. Wait lists will only be available before the conference. On-site walk-in attendance may be available dependent on room capacity and will be allowed on a first-come, first-served basis.

ReservationRequired: This session requires a reservation to attend. Please use Add to Schedule to reserve your spot or Join Wait List to be added to the queue.

sessions.title: U.S. Army Sustainable Range Program (SRP) GIS Forum
sessions.abstract: The U.S. Army Sustainable Range Program (SRP) GIS Forum is a private meeting to U.S. Army SRP personnel only. Hosted by Ranges Training Division, this forum provides in-person, peer-to-peer collaboration. Roadmap, program initiatives, and common issues will be discussed.

sessions.title: Women and GIS SIG
sessions.abstract:
Join a conversation with your peers and Esri staff about the importance of having technologists, leaders, and communicators who reflect the diversity of the populations that you serve to enhance the advancement of GIS and government. In this SIG, your peers share their perspectives as professionals in the GIS community, their challenges, and solutions for creating more influential and productive organizations. Conversations focus on experiences, development, allyship, and mentorship. Build connections, learn from peers, and bring actionable ideas back to your organization.

 

Reservations will be held until five minutes prior to the start time before seating will be opened to attendees in the on-site queue. If attendees with a reservation remove the session from their schedule, the next person on the wait list will automatically receive that open reservation. Wait lists will only be available before the conference. On-site walk-in attendance may be available dependent on room capacity and will be allowed on a first-come, first-served basis.

ReservationRequired: This session requires a reservation to attend. Please use Add to Schedule to reserve your spot or Join Wait List to be added to the queue.

sessions.title: Forestry Industry Kick-Off Meeting
sessions.abstract: The Commercial Forestry Track Kickoff provides a clear overview of the sessions and themes you will see throughout the week. We will introduce the focus areas for this year’s track and outline how geospatial technology supports key forestry activities, including planning, monitoring, operations, and sustainability efforts. Join us to get oriented, understand what to expect, and identify the sessions that best align with your goals.

sessions.title: Mining Industry Kick-Off Meeting
sessions.abstract: This session will set the tone and help guide attendees through a successful UC week. Join us for a fast-paced orientation to Mining-focused sessions, demos, and networking. We will cover current mining industry trends (exploration, operations, closure, tailings) and the geospatial trends shaping the sector (GeoAI, imagery, real-time dashboards, field mobility, 3D, and integration). Walk away with a personalized plan for the week and connections to the Esri Mining community.

sessions.title: Speaker Resource Center
sessions.abstract: Are you presenting at the User Conference? Visit the Speaker Resource Center (SRC) to finalize your presentation, practice out loud, and receive personalized coaching and feedback from Esri Instructors. The SRC is fully equipped with projectors, computers, and dedicated workspaces to help you present with confidence.

sessions.title: Acting on Risk to Strengthen Community Health
sessions.abstract: Understanding risk is the first step to protecting community health. This session highlights two GIS driven approaches that assess hazards before action is taken: NYC’s interactive Jurisdictional Risk Assessment helps residents visualize threats in their neighborhoods, while an ArcGIS based workflow in Arizona strengthens lead in water testing across schools. By transforming risk data into accessible, real time insights, these projects empower informed decisions that enhance safety, preparedness, and long term community well being.

Keywords: at-risk, vulnerable, hazard, data, safety, preparedness

sessions.title: Analytics and Data Science and ArcGIS
sessions.abstract:
Join fellow analysts to explore the latest trends and innovations in spatial data science. Engage with industry experts, gain insights into emerging technologies, and discover new opportunities for informed decision-making. Experience live demonstrations of ArcGIS analysis capabilities, from data engineering to spatial machine learning. Don't miss this chance to learn more about transforming data into actionable information and overcoming challenges with spatial context.

 

Reservations will be held until five minutes prior to the start time before seating will be opened to attendees in the on-site queue. If attendees with a reservation remove the session from their schedule, the next person on the wait list will automatically receive that open reservation. Wait lists will only be available before the conference. On-site walk-in attendance may be available dependent on room capacity and will be allowed on a first-come, first-served basis.

ReservationRequired: This session requires a reservation to attend. Please use Add to Schedule to reserve your spot or Join Wait List to be added to the queue.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Dashboards: An Overview
sessions.abstract: New to ArcGIS Dashboards? This configurable web app provides data visualization of people, services, assets, and events. Learn how to configure charts, gauges, maps, and other visual elements to reflect the status and performance of the data that matters most. Convey the activities and key indicators that are most vital to meeting your organization's objectives. Speakers also share the current road map for this product.

Keywords: Dashboards, Data Visualization, Configuration, Road Map

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Security Best Practices
sessions.abstract: Secure your ArcGIS Enterprise deployments. Learn how to protect yourself from common threats and attacks and how to harden your environment using multiple layers of security. Speakers discuss various aspects of the environment, including the operating system, accounts, databases, the network, and firewalls. Join us to learn about security options in ArcGIS Enterprise that help protect your site from attack.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Experience Builder: Building Your First Widget
sessions.abstract: Join us to learn how ArcGIS Experience Builder allows you to build immersive web apps for your organization. Discover how to use TypeScript and React to build your first widget in Experience Builder, and get tips on how to debug your widget to improve your debugging experience.

RecommendedAudience: Experience with TypeScript and React development.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World: Basemaps Are Not Just the Background
sessions.abstract: Basemaps are the foundation for your maps and apps, but they are also part of the story that you are telling. Discover the integral role that basemaps play in developing your maps, and learn about available options through ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World and how to choose appropriate basemaps. Explore how to use tools in ArcGIS Pro and Map Viewer to build your own basemap versions with the ArcGIS Vector Tile Style Editor.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World

sessions.title: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World: Storytelling with Maps
sessions.abstract: Every map tells a story—but making sure that story is clear and resonates with its audience requires thought and effort. Get tips on how to set your maps and GIS work up for success when it comes to getting your message across, and also find out how you can use ArcGIS StoryMaps to support your maps and make their stories come to life.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World
Keywords: ArcGIS StoryMaps, story, storytelling, ArcGIS Living Atlas, stories

sessions.title: ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript: Building an App with Components
sessions.abstract: Want to build a web app with ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript? This presentation provides a step-by-step guide for building web apps using the web components available with ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript and Calcite Design System. Starting from a web map, learn how to add mapping capabilities, charts, UI design elements, and more to make a responsive, cohesive, and informative experience. Find out how to combine Calcite and the SDK components and capabilities to create a compelling web mapping app with maximum productivity.

RecommendedAudience: Knowledge of the concept of web applications and experience with any programming language.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise: Administering Your Organization
sessions.abstract: Manage your ArcGIS environment efficiently. This presentation explores ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise organizations, including management of members and shared content using groups and folders. Learn to customize your organization's home page, set the default basemap and extent, and configure security settings. Also, receive guidance about resource usage, credit consumption, and allocation among members.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise: Better Together
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise are better together for your business workflows. Join us to learn about the key differences and similarities between ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise and how they enhance organizational effectiveness through content sharing. Discover how to set up collaborations, configure settings, and manage shared content. Explore common workflows like sharing maps and layers and using a mix of content from ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro Tasks: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Want to implement repeatable workflows, improve efficiency, and promote best practices when using ArcGIS Pro? Discover how tasks in ArcGIS Pro can be used to your advantage. A task is a set of preconfigured steps that guide you and other users through a workflow or business process. Learn how to run, create, and configure a task in the Task Designer, as well as how to share a task between projects and organizations in ArcGIS Pro.

RecommendedAudience: Basic understanding of ArcGIS Pro.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: An Introduction to Python Map Automation
sessions.abstract: Want to write your own map automation scripts using Python? This presentation introduces the mapping module and shows how it can be used to process the contents of ArcGIS Pro projects, automate exporting, and generate PDF map books.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Explore and Visualize Your Attributes
sessions.abstract: When it comes to GIS, a map is only the beginning. Learn how you can get the most out of your tables, configure pop-ups that put your data in the spotlight, and present your data by creating and sharing reports.

Keywords: pop-up, popup, pop-ups, popups, table, tables, report, reports

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Mapping and Visualization
sessions.abstract: What's new in the world of mapping and visualization in ArcGIS Pro? Join us to learn how to adjust layer and map properties, add cartographic refinements and visual effects, and organize symbols and colors into styles that will standardize how your maps are viewed.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Preparing Your Data for the NSRS 2022 Shift
sessions.abstract: The United States is currently revising its geodetic and vertical datums, as well as the State Plane system. Is your data ready for the NSRS 2022 shift? Join us to learn more about the history of the NAD83 (North American Datum of 1983) readjustments. Speakers discuss how to convert existing data in ArcGIS Pro to the latest NAD83 adjustments (2011/PA11/MA11) and how to identify incorrect NAD83 adjustments assigned to your datasets.

Keywords: map projections, coordinate systems, transformations, datums, NSRS 2022
RecommendedAudience: Experience with coordinate systems and transformations.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: What's New
sessions.abstract: Discover what's new in ArcGIS Pro! Speakers highlight the product capabilities that were added to ArcGIS Pro in the past year. Find out how this new functionality can help you work more productively and spark ideas for solving problems in new ways. Explore new options for efficiently maintaining, visualizing, and analyzing your data in 2D, 3D, and 4D. Speakers also share tips for staying current on product developments throughout the year.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Survey123: AI Assistants
sessions.abstract: AI assistants are revolutionizing the ArcGIS experience by using the capabilities of generative AI. These assistants facilitate content creation, data introspection, and summarization as well as provide insightful suggestions, recommendations, and assistance in code writing. In this presentation, learn practical tips for getting the most out of AI assistants in ArcGIS Survey123.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Utility Network: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Do you manage utilities? This presentation provides insight into network management capabilities that enable next-generation analytics and experiences for any utility system. Learn about updates from development that showcase current and emerging functionality as they apply to utility information challenges, and get a preview of the road ahead for network management for utilities.

RecommendedAudience: Basic understanding of geodatabase and utility network concepts.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Velocity: An Overview
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Velocity is a real-time analysis capability in both ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise, enabling organizations to ingest, analyze, and act on data from Internet of Things (IoT) sensors. Join the discussion on how to connect to virtually any type of streaming data, perform real-time analytics and processing, and automatically disseminate information and alert personnel when specified conditions occur.

Keywords: real-time GIS, real-time analytics, streaming data, IoT, sensor data, ArcGIS Velocity, Velocity for Enterprise, event processing, feeds, real-time alerts, big data analytics, data ingestion, real-time visualization, real-time mapping, situational awareness, operations, event detection, time-enabled data, high-volume data

sessions.title: ArcGIS for AutoCAD: An Overview
sessions.abstract: The free ArcGIS for AutoCAD plug-in connects CAD and GIS professionals for information sharing and decision-making. Learn how this extension enables direct editing of GIS data and precise geolocation of drawings within AutoCAD. Explore how it informs the design process with rich geospatial context and ensures that drawings are consumable within ArcGIS. Explore workflows that show how edits made in ArcGIS are fed back to AutoCAD, enabling ArcGIS and AutoCAD teams to work with the same data synchronously.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with AutoCAD, ArcGIS for AutoCAD, feature service editing, geolocation, Civil 3D, and data management.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: GeoAI
sessions.abstract: Interested in learning more about geospatial artificial intelligence (GeoAI) in ArcGIS? GeoAI is the integration of AI, machine learning (ML), and deep learning (DL) with GIS to produce knowledge and solve geographic problems. It is a key part of spatial analysis for tasks related to clustering and pattern detection, prediction and forecasting, and information extraction from many types of data. This presentation highlights the application of ArcGIS GeoAI capabilities across imagery, 3D, unstructured text, and feature, tabular, and time-series data and workflows.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Support for Geospatial Standards
sessions.abstract: Geospatial standards are the key to unlocking the value of spatial information. Learn about the key geospatial standards from the Open Geospatial Consortium (OGC) and International Organization for Standardization (ISO) and explore their practical application within ArcGIS. These standards—which underpin interoperability across diverse systems—define geometry, coordinate systems, and data formats for reliable data exchange, as well as metadata and web service standards for discovery and use.

Keywords: standards, OGC, ISO, data, metadata, webservices, Simple Features, CRS, GML, geoJSON

sessions.title: Automating Imagery Workflows with Deep Learning
sessions.abstract: Learn how geospatial artificial intelligence (GeoAI) in ArcGIS streamlines imagery workflows with automated deep learning tools. This presentation discusses the processes of creating training data, applying models for feature extraction, and running models for analysis across ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Enterprise, and ArcGIS Online. Discover how ArcGIS reduces manual effort, scales analysis, and helps you get started quickly with practical, repeatable deep learning workflows.

sessions.title: Automating Spatial Insight: Scalable Workflows from Maps to Live Imagery
sessions.abstract: Advances in Python automation and ArcGIS workflows are transforming how scientists and analysts validate data, generate maps, and manage live imagery services. This session shows how Python-driven automation reduces manual GIS work while improving reliability, consistency, and timeliness. Through two case studies, attendees see automated data validation, standardized symbology, dynamic map regeneration, and publishing across ArcGIS platforms, plus an end-to-end automated imagery pipeline from acquisition to live multidimensional services.

sessions.title: Automating and Integrating Field Operations
sessions.abstract: Discover how to automate and integrate field operations. This presentation explores how automation reduces manual steps, supports collaboration, and provides live visibility into progress. Learn how Esri’s field apps connect with business systems, updating data across platforms like work order systems, AutoCAD, Microsoft tools, and more to streamline work before, during, and after fieldwork.

sessions.title: Beat the Heat with Geospatial Data and Analytics
sessions.abstract: In this session, you will discover how peers are applying ArcGIS to map and understand urban heat in real-world contexts. Through short showcases and discussions, participants will share workflows, data sources, and design choices using satellite imagery, land surface temperature, land cover, and contextual data. You will discover practical approaches to analyzing urban heat islands, communicating risk, and supporting climate resilience, while learning from others’ successes, challenges, and creative uses of ArcGIS Pro and ArcGIS Online.

Keywords: Heat, Climate, Resilience, Urban
RecommendedAudience: Open to all levels of experience

sessions.title: Data Collection That Makes the Invisible Impossible to Ignore
sessions.abstract: Discover how community‑driven data collection can reshape equity, health, and local decision‑making, especially for nonprofit organizations advocating for underserved communities. This featured user session shows how GIS turns field data into evidence that informs investment, visibility, and action. Attendees will gain repeatable methods for gathering community insights, standardizing field data, and transforming raw observations into compelling, decision‑ready maps and dashboards.

Keywords: nonprofit, npo,

sessions.title: Detect, Alert, Act: GeoAI Workflows for Real-Time Conservation
sessions.abstract: This session spotlights advanced GIS workflows that convert imagery and real-time disturbance signals into coordinated conservation response. Learn how deep learning classification, NASA’s OPERA disturbance alerts, ArcGIS Pro AI assistants, and ArcGIS Field Maps tasking can centralize detection, speed interpretation, and operationalize action in the field, helping leading conservation organizations intervene earlier and protect vulnerable habitats before losses become irreversible.

Keywords: Conservation; Imagery; AI

sessions.title: GIS as a Mission-Critical Capability for Small Governments and Nonprofits
sessions.abstract: GIS technology is revolutionizing disaster management by enhancing speed, accuracy, and coordination across various levels of government and with key stakeholders. Join us as we explore various implementations include using data for damage mapping, tracking, and integrating imagery.

sessions.title: Kids Fair
sessions.abstract:
Visit the Esri Kids Fair to introduce them to GIS through a series of educational, hands-on activities. This year’s theme is “Continents.” After completing each activity and filling out their passport, kids will earn a special prize. Kids from 5 to 14 years old are welcome to participate. A parent or guardian must be present at all times. Registration is available on-site at no additional cost.

 

Learn more about fair activities in this blog.

sessions.title: Managing Lidar Like a Pro: Tips for High-Performance Workflows
sessions.abstract: Want to get more out of your lidar data in ArcGIS? This presentation shares practical tips and tricks for managing and optimizing lidar datasets across your organization. Learn how to structure lidar collections, improve performance, and publish lidar content to the web. Speakers discuss best practices to streamline workflows for assigning coordinate systems, tiling, thinning, filtering, and sharing lidar data—helping you deliver high-quality 3D content.

RecommendedAudience: Interest in efficiently visualizing and analyzing lidar data using ArcGIS.

sessions.title: Managing Port Data with an Operational Twin: From Collection to Insight
sessions.abstract: Discover how to streamline port operations by integrating bathymetric and terrestrial data with auxiliary datasets into a unified management workflow. Learn how these workflows support asset cataloging, monitoring, and change detection, while enabling clear visualization in 2D and 3D. Speakers will demonstrate how user-driven customization simplifies data identification and accelerates decision-making for maintenance and planning tasks.

Keywords: Ports, operations, data integration, bathymetry, bathymetric data, terrestrial data, 2D, 3D, visualization, infrastructure, environmental, metadata, catalog, change detection
RecommendedAudience: Basic understanding of elevation data management and data formats.

sessions.title: Modernizing National Mapping with GIS
sessions.abstract: Explore how National Mapping and Geospatial Authorities are streamlining the way they prepare, manage, produce, and deliver timely,  authoritative information through automation and modernized approaches. Join us to learn from the innovative work being done by your peers!
tracks: National Mapping, Navigation, and Geospatial Authorities

Keywords: topographic mapping, national mapping, mapping, generalization, automation, cartography
Topic: National Mapping, Navigation, and Geospatial Authorities

sessions.title: Multi-Scale Planning for Outdoor Recreation and Community Spaces
sessions.abstract: Discover how GIS supports outdoor access at multiple scales. Appalachian Regional Commission and the Trust for Public Land use multi factor spatial analysis to identify recreation opportunities across a broad region, integrating environmental, equity, economic, and planning data to guide investments. Montgomery Parks applies suitability modeling to prioritize locations for community gardens, evaluating site conditions, access, and community needs. Learn best practices for transparent, data driven decision making.

Keywords: outdoor recreation, suitability analysis, Model Builder

sessions.title: Planning, Design, Storytelling and Public Engagement
sessions.abstract: In this session, you will learn how GIS is being integrated into architectural and urban planning workflows to support collaboration, analysis, and storytelling. Discover how spatial data and 3D visualization help connect design intent with real-world context, quantify impacts related to climate, equity, and sustainability, and communicate complex planning scenarios clearly to stakeholders. You will gain insight into how GIS enables data-driven design, scenario planning, and community engagement from early visioning through implementation.

Keywords: Planning, Design, Storytelling & Public Engagement
RecommendedAudience: Basic understanding of GIS or spatial data concepts; Familiarity with architectural, urban design, or planning workflows; Interest in data visualization or scenario-based decision making

sessions.title: Scaling 3D and Enterprise GIS from Exploration to Mine Development
sessions.abstract: This session highlights two real‑world GIS workflows showing how mining companies turn spatial data into faster decisions and clearer communication. Orla Mining uses ArcPy automation and ArcGIS 3D tools to transform borehole and core‑logging data into exploratory scenes and an interactive dashboard. Skeena Gold + Silver shows how Esri technology scales from exploration to an enterprise GIS supporting permitting, sustainability, investor relations, and engineering. Attendees gain practical patterns for automation, 3D visualization, and connecting teams as projects advance.

sessions.title: Strategic Asset Management: A GIS-Driven Approach
sessions.abstract: Speakers in this session will present how they are strategically managing sewer and water infrastructure assets using a GIS-based approach. They will share how a risk-based asset management strategy was developed, leveraging GIS and integrating customized risk frameworks, decision trees, and visualization tools. They will show how their approach has improved system reliability, supports proactive maintenance, and strengthens long-term infrastructure resilience.

Keywords: Condition assessment, Capital planning, Facilities management, Geospatial integration

sessions.title: The Housing Problem Unlocked
sessions.abstract: Where is the most effective place to put a new housing development? What housing will my community need in 20 years? This session will teach you how to answer these questions.

Keywords: affordable housing, housing capacity

sessions.title: Transforming Land Surveying with Modern Data Practices and Automation
sessions.abstract: This session explores how organizations strengthen land records, surveying workflows, and infrastructure planning using GIS and AI. Learn how post‑disaster parcel recovery efforts use coordinated datasets, survey collaboration, and sustainable data practices to restore boundary accuracy and data integrity. You will see how combining GIS and BIM4I enhances transportation projects by combining spatial context with infrastructure modeling. Together, these examples demonstrate practical, thoughtful use of ArcGIS to solve complex real‑world challenges.

Keywords: Land Records, Land Administration, Parcel Management, Surveying, Transportation
RecommendedAudience: GIS Managers, Land Surveyors, Parcel Mappers, County Officials, Real Estate Professionals

sessions.title: Transforming Stormwater Management: Efficiency, Compliance, and Innovation
sessions.abstract: In this session, the first presenter will share how they have successfully enhanced the efficiency of urban stormwater management operations while improving flood prevention and maintaining compliance with state mandates. The second presentation focuses on how integrating Esri solutions and deep learning tools strengthened its capabilities in LiDAR and imagery data extraction, flood modeling, impact analysis, 3D visualization, project development, and public engagement. Don't miss the opportunity to learn how ArcGIS enhances the effectiveness of stormwater management efforts.

Keywords: Stormwater management, Floodplain mapping, Data-driven management,

sessions.title: Urban and Plantation Canopy Intelligence
sessions.abstract: Attendees will explore how GIS, remote sensing, automation, and interactive visualization are transforming both urban canopy management and large-scale forestry production.

sessions.title: Expo
sessions.abstract: At the UC Expo, connect face to face with Esri staff and Esri Partner Network leaders to explore innovations, collaborate on ideas, learn from experts, and ignite inspiration.

sessions.title: Hands-On Learning Lab
sessions.abstract: Take a self-paced lesson at the Hands-On Learning Lab. The Hands-On Learning Lab is a dedicated space to take free lessons on a variety of GIS and ArcGIS topics. Each self-paced lesson takes about one hour to complete and includes conceptual information and step-by-step software exercises. We provide laptops and the ArcGIS software needed to complete each lesson. Esri instructors are available to assist with lesson selection and answer any questions you may have.

sessions.title: The Integration Layer for AI: FME, MCP, and 400+ Formats at Enterprise Scale
sessions.abstract: Not every data challenge fits a no-code tool. When your workflows need REST APIs, OAuth, multi-format output, or reliable connections at enterprise scale, the integration layer matters. FME handles data across 400+ formats — spatial, non-spatial, real-time, and legacy — and now connects it all to AI agents via Model Context Protocol. In this session, we'll walk through what enterprise-grade data integration actually looks like: live demo, real workflows, and an honest conversation about where simple tools hit their limits and what to reach for when they do.

sessions.title: Intro to AI-Driven Reality Capture with GeoCam
sessions.abstract: Discover the easiest way to scale your own Reality Capture Practice. Starting from rapid single captures of any space to managing a complete approach for a large city (streets, indoors, aerial), utility networks (transmission, distribution, and stations), or full campus and indoor mapping environments.

sessions.title: Transforming GIS Operations through AI Workflows You Can Leverage Today
sessions.abstract: Explore tangible examples of how modern GIS apps empower your entire organization with embedded AI functionality to elevate operations. See how natural language AI assistants and image recognition support users in the field, streamline workflows for GIS managers, and improve organizational outcomes. We will showcase practical applications of AI tools designed to enhance spatial workflows, speed up configuration, and elevate team capacity to meet growing demands. Perfect for GIS professionals looking to unlock smarter, faster processes.

sessions.title: Improving Watershed Planning with Hydro Conditioned DEMs
sessions.abstract: Presenters in this session will share how GIS, LiDAR, and hydro-conditioned digital elevation models have been used to drive smarter watershed planning. Learn how innovative planning, flood modeling, and conservation strategies are delivering water quality benefits.

Keywords: Watershed planning, Hydro-conditioning, Best management practices, LiDAR

sessions.title: Achieving Better Environmental Outcomes with the Latest GIS Capabilities
sessions.abstract: Environmental challenges are intensifying, so GIS professionals must innovate to protect ecosystems and communicate scientific data. GIS remains a critical tool for these efforts. Today, new capabilities can assist with these tasks. From streamlining permitting to advancing stewardship and achieving better environmental outcomes, find out how GIS and geospatial artificial intelligence (GeoAI) are transforming how organizations map their approach to a more sustainable future.

Keywords: Environmental protection, wildlife, outdoor recreation, parks, climate, natural resources

sessions.title: Advancing Mapping and Charting in the Maritime Domain
sessions.abstract: Discover how maritime organizations are advancing their hydrographic workflows through automation, GeoAI, and advanced analytics. Join us to explore different approaches that have streamlined data management and enhanced collaboration with stakeholders.
tracks: National Mapping, Navigation, and Geospatial Authorities

Keywords: Mapping, Maritime, Hydrography, Charting, Nautical, Marine
Topic: National Mapping, Navigation, and Geospatial Authorities

sessions.title: ArcGIS API for Python: An Overview of the Modules
sessions.abstract: The ArcGIS API for Python provides a scripting and automation interface for your web GIS. Explore various modules and discover how the API allows you to administer your GIS organization, manage your GIS content, and perform spatial analysis and data science. Learn how to map and visualize your data, differentiate between modules, and work with web maps and apps.

Keywords: scripting, automating, administer, organization
RecommendedAudience: Basic experience with Python.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Arcade: Mapping and Visualization
sessions.abstract: Extend what is possible in ArcGIS Pro using ArcGIS Arcade—an expression language designed for use with ArcGIS. Learn how Arcade can enhance map visualization, label formatting, field calculations, attribute rules, and pop-ups.

RecommendedAudience: Beginner to intermediate experience using ArcGIS Arcade.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Business Analyst: Fundamentals
sessions.abstract: Curious about how to get started with ArcGIS Business Analyst? Learn how Business Analyst inspires informed decisions in site analysis, social equity, and economic development. Business Analyst's analytical capabilities extend across sectors such as commercial, healthcare, government, and more. Discover how to use the power of the AI assistant to streamline complex workflows and achieve actionable results. This presentation is designed for new users, especially those who have gained access to Business Analyst through Creator and above user types.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Data Pipelines: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Do you spend hours repeating the same data preparation steps just to update a feature layer in your ArcGIS organization? ArcGIS Data Pipelines is a no-code, visual data engineering capability designed to streamline data preparation workflows. Create, run, and schedule workflows with an intuitive drag-and-drop interface. Connect to multiple data sources, blend and integrate datasets, and publish hosted layers for use across your organization. Learn about the key features and start simplifying your data integration today.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Data Reviewer: Improving Data Quality for Water Utilities
sessions.abstract: Learn how to implement ArcGIS Data Reviewer to support validation of water features in the ArcGIS Utility Network. This presentation highlights additional methods and implementation best practices for enhancing the validation capability in the utility network. Speakers also share valuable QA/QC (quality assurance/quality control) recommendations for migration to the ArcGIS Utility Network.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with ArcGIS Utility Network and the foundational ArcGIS Solutions templates is recommended.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Securing the Defense and Intelligence Infrastructure
sessions.abstract: As adversaries evolve and threats become more complex, securing ArcGIS Enterprise is essential. Learn the tools and practices used to harden deployments for defense and intelligence communities. Discover how to protect data, enforce access controls, and monitor system activity to ensure that the geospatial infrastructure is secure, resilient, and mission-ready.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Enterprise on Amazon Web Services
Keywords: ArcGIS Enterprise security, System hardening, Defense and intelligence security, Access control, Identity management, Zero trust architecture
RecommendedAudience: Experience with ArcGIS Enterprise administration workflows

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Understanding Logging
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Enterprise logs capture information about events occurring throughout your deployment. Join us to learn how to understand the different logs in ArcGIS Enterprise on Windows and Linux and how to use these logs for monitoring and troubleshooting. Speakers also share some helpful tips and tricks for using these logs in your own deployment.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Experience Builder: Best Practices for Building Exciting Apps
sessions.abstract: Join this action-packed presentation to learn best practices for designing and building exciting apps. Whether you are building a web app or a web site, you will come away with tips to meet your precise design goals while supercharging your workflows. Learn how to properly size and position elements, choose color palettes that pop, use ArcGIS Arcade expressions to dynamically display data, and customize your apps to truly stand out. Get ideas to make your app work across all screen sizes.

Keywords: ArcGIS Experience Builder, Best practice, Design, Experience, Web app

sessions.title: ArcGIS Field Maps: Simplifying Field Data Collection with Forms and ArcGIS Arcade
sessions.abstract: Learn how to use dynamic forms and ArcGIS Arcade in ArcGIS Field Maps to create adaptable, easy-to-use mobile data collection workflows. The presentation provides techniques for building forms that respond to user input, enforcing data quality standards, and streamlining field processes for greater accuracy and efficiency.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Indoors: Space Planning and Workplace Management
sessions.abstract: Do you want to help your organization with GIS-based space planning? Join us to learn how ArcGIS Indoors Space Planner streamlines indoor space management for enhanced workspace utilization. Discover how to address key challenges such as business unit space allocation and occupant space assignments. Speakers also demonstrate how to offer a web and native mobile experience to support employees in finding the people, places, assets, and amenities needed and scheduling spaces to work and collaborate when in the office.

Keywords: indoor maps, indoor spaces, space management, space planning, workplace management, space planners, hybrid workplace, back to office, indoor GIS

sessions.title: ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript: Capabilities and Techniques
sessions.abstract: Explore key capabilities of ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript and learn the fundamentals of the SDK. Speakers share techniques and capabilities for importing data, basic layer styling, editing, 2D and 3D visualization, client-side analysis, shortcuts for building your UI, and more.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise: Architectural Patterns and Practices
sessions.abstract: Think you need to choose between ArcGIS Enterprise and ArcGIS Online? Many organizations successfully use both! This presentation helps you determine how to choose the deployment that best meets your organizational needs. Discover the different hybrid patterns that you can use to capitalize on each environment’s unique strengths and plan for and build the best possible system for your organization.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online: Creating Accessible Content
sessions.abstract: Ensure that your content is available to everyone. Join us to explore digital accessibility in ArcGIS Online, including capabilities of ArcGIS web technologies and how to ensure compliance with accessibility criteria and guidelines.
