Speakers discuss the assistive and adaptive technologies that you can use to evaluate and remediate ArcGIS Online content according to best practices.

Keywords: Accessibility, A11y, Section 508, 508, Web Content Accessibility Guidelines, WCAG, Americans with Disabilities Act, ADA, EN 301 549,

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online: Processing and Analyzing Imagery
sessions.abstract: Process and analyze imagery entirely in the cloud with ArcGIS Online. This presentation discusses scalable imagery hosting, ready-to-use raster functions, and the Raster Function Editor for dynamic and persistent analysis. Discover best practices for building repeatable workflows in Map Viewer and the ArcGIS API for Python, helping you modernize and scale your imagery processes.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online: What's New
sessions.abstract: Join us for an exciting exploration of the latest features and functionalities in the most recent update of ArcGIS Online. Learn about the latest enhancements that can amplify your GIS experience. You will not only identify new features that your organization can use but also understand their value and potential impact. Do not miss this opportunity to maximize your use of ArcGIS Online.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pipeline Referencing: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Learn how ArcGIS Pipeline Referencing can manage and edit pipeline networks event data in a spatial linear referencing system (LRS). Speakers provide an overview of the product and how to manage linear referenced pipeline data and data editing—including pipe replacements, linear referenced event behaviors, and event characteristic editing. Also, learn about a REST API to extend LRS capabilities across the enterprise, as well as integration with ArcGIS Utility Network.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: An Editing Overview
sessions.abstract: Whether you are a seasoned ArcGIS Pro user or just getting started, editing your data is one of the most important aspects of working in ArcGIS Pro. Speakers review common editing workflows, such as creating new features using feature templates, snapping, updating attributes, and modifying existing feature geometry using various techniques and tools.

RecommendedAudience: Basic understanding of ArcGIS Pro.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: An Introduction to Labels and Annotation
sessions.abstract: Text is an essential part of any map. Join us for an introduction to map labeling, converting labels to annotation, and using graphic text and annotation in ArcGIS Pro. Understand the differences between labels, annotation, and graphic text, and get to know the editing and geoprocessing tools that are available for modifying text in your maps.

Keywords: Labelling

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Getting Started with the ArcGIS Pro Assistant
sessions.abstract: Meet the new ArcGIS Pro assistant (beta) and learn practical tips for getting the best out of it. This presentation provides expert guidance on how the ArcGIS Pro assistant uses generative AI and addresses the security and privacy considerations that matter most when providing AI capabilities to your organization. Do not miss this chance to ask questions and join the dedicated Early Adopter Community for the ArcGIS Pro assistant.

Keywords: AI, assistant, pro

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Traveling Through Time
sessions.abstract: Geographic data has a location in space, and often it has a time component that is just as important. For example, a lightning strike has both a time and a place, while a power outage has both an extent and a duration. Join us to learn how to model time in ArcGIS Pro for visualization and analysis, as well as how to ensure that this information is communicated accurately across the ArcGIS system. Speakers discuss time formats, time zones, time-aware services, temporal animation videos, streamed layers, handling large time extents, and much more. Your time will be well spent!

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions: Address Data Management
sessions.abstract: Addresses are essential for emergency services, permitting, and taxation functions in government. Join us for an overview of the Address Data Management solution, and learn how you can maintain an authoritative address repository and continuously improve the quality of your address data.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions: Fire Service
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Solutions help fire service agencies understand risks in their community and respond to calls for service. Join us for an overview of this collection of solutions and learn how you can understand hazards, or risks, that may impact your community, collect pre-plan data, and visualize fire and emergency medical service (EMS) incidents.

Keywords: Fire Service, Accreditation, Risk Reduction, Wildfire, Fire operations, Firefighters

sessions.title: ArcGIS Spatial Analyst: Exploring Movement and Distance Analysis
sessions.abstract: New to movement and distance analysis? Join us as speakers share diverse industry applications. Find out how connecting locations using paths and corridors aids infrastructure planning by siting roads or pipelines over varied terrains, environmental management through wildlife corridor identification and crossing placement, and humanitarian aid by increasing access to resources such as healthcare facilities or education. Plus, learn about key capabilities, best practices, and recent enhancements.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Velocity: Applying Real-Time Analytics
sessions.abstract: Explore the analytic power of ArcGIS Velocity. This presentation demonstrates how to detect incidents in real time, enrich sensor data, automate processing using scheduled big data analytics, and uncover patterns and anomalies in high-volume historical datasets.

Keywords: real-time analytics, big data analytics, scheduled analytics, Arcade track functions, stateful processing, data enrichment, pattern detection, anomaly detection, incident detection, streaming data workflows, analytics modeling, batch analytics, temporal analysis, complex event processing, Velocity processors, track-based analysis
RecommendedAudience: Experience with ArcGIS Velocity.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: A System for National Security
sessions.abstract: Discover how ArcGIS enhances the Command, Control, Communications, Computers, Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance (C4ISR) framework by strengthening strategic planning, intelligence fusion, and operational decision-making. Analysts can use ArcGIS to integrate various intelligence sources, construct threat assessments, and support mission execution with logistics awareness and asset visibility. This technical session also highlights the benefits of real-time data ingestion, AI-assisted decision support, and AI agents for link analysis, entity resolution, and anticipating second-order effects.

Keywords: Command, Control, Target Development, Multi-INT, data fusion, Intelligence cycle, mission threads

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Building the Planning Digital Twin
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS is transforming how we design and plan communities, regions, and cities. At the heart of this transformation is the Planning Digital Twin, a dynamic, data-rich representation of the built environment that enables planners to simulate scenarios, evaluate impacts, and make informed real-time decisions. This presentation explores how ArcGIS enables you to create and maintain digital twins, combining spatial analytics, 3D visualization, and collaborative workflows.

Keywords: Digital Twins, Urban Planning

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Why Floor-Aware Indoor Maps Are Critical for Ensuring Public Safety
sessions.abstract: Emergencies can occur anywhere and at any scale. Incidents inside buildings pose unique challenges, especially in large, complex structures or tactical situations. Discover why detailed GIS-based indoor maps are crucial for locating callers and resources. Learn how telecommunicators and first responders can use floor-aware indoor maps to enhance situational awareness, improve coordination, and affect better outcomes. Understand how you can use ArcGIS to build, maintain, share, and use floor-aware indoor maps to better protect our communities.

Keywords: Emergency Communications, 911, school safety, indoor maps, floor plans, indoor location, Z-Axis, 3D

sessions.title: Authoring Attribute Rules
sessions.abstract: Wishing you had an in-depth guide to attribute rules? If so, then this presentation is for you! Learn all about authoring attribute rules to enhance the editing experience and improve data quality. Starting with the basics of creating simple attribute rules using the Arcade function reference, the speakers then gradually introduce more complex Arcade expressions to develop more comprehensive attribute rules.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with geodatabases.

sessions.title: Automation, GeoAI and Advanced Analytics
sessions.abstract: In this session, you will learn how automation and task-driven GIS workflows can dramatically improve efficiency, accuracy, and data sharing in complex, data-intensive projects. Discover how scripted tools, repeatable tasks, and web-enabled delivery streamline the movement of information from office to field, support real-time decision-making, and reduce manual effort. You will gain insight into applying these approaches to construction, environmental, and infrastructure workflows operating under challenging conditions.

Keywords: Workflow Automation; Field-to-Office Integration; Data Processing; ArcGIS Pro
RecommendedAudience: Basic familiarity with GIS and ArcGIS Pro; Exposure to field data collection or operational workflows; Introductory understanding of automation or scripting concepts

sessions.title: Biodiversity Intelligence and Nature‑Positive Forest Management
sessions.abstract: This session highlights how NatureServe, FSC, and SFI use ArcGIS, authoritative biodiversity data, and intuitive spatial tools to help certified forest managers identify conservation values, assess species risks, and support nature positive forestry decisions.

sessions.title: Building the Next Generation of Land Administration Systems
sessions.abstract: This session highlights how organizations modernize land and property management by upgrading legacy GIS systems, migrating millions of parcels to modern parcel fabrics, and automating workflows with advanced tools like attribute rules, tasks, and survey plan checks. It also explores how web-based GIS applications improve public services by centralizing property information, simplifying tax inquiries, and enhancing data‑driven decision‑making for urban planning and land administration.

Keywords: Land Registry, Land Records, Cadastre, Real Estate, Urban Planning, Data Integration, Land Administration
RecommendedAudience: Land Registry Officials, GIS Managers, Real Estate Professionals, Urban Planners, Government Officials, Land Administration Professionals

sessions.title: Data Driven Asset Evaluation for Safer Transit
sessions.abstract: You will learn how Long Beach Transit and the Chicago Transit Authority strengthen asset management and compliance by applying structured inspection methods to stops, stations, and rail infrastructure. You will see how Long Beach Transit uses systemwide bus stop surveys to evaluate accessibility and safety, and how the CTA applies automated data integration to identify construction issues, monitor progress, and support decisions on major capital work. These practices show how consistent data standards and validated field information improve safety, reliability, and long term planning.

Keywords: Asset Management, ADA compliance, Safety Review, Infrastructure Evaluation, Capital Planning, Stop Inventory, Data Driven Planning
RecommendedAudience: City/Regional Planner, Transit Planner, Mobility Program Manager, GIS Analyst, Executive Director, Capital Planner, Mobility Programs Manager, Transit Analyst

sessions.title: Empowering Your Workforce with GIS
sessions.abstract: Close the skills gap and enable your workforce to have a bigger impact with GIS. Technology is often implemented in a way that leaves people feeling lost, frustrated, or unsure of where to begin. This technical session explores simple and effective techniques for aligning Esri learning resources with your technology goals.

Keywords: Skills development, workforce enablement, training resources, professional growth, Esri Academy, learning plans, capacity building, adoption strategies, technology alignment, user empowerment
RecommendedAudience: Basic understanding of GIS concepts, familiarity with Esri learning resources, and interest in workforce development and training strategies.

sessions.title: Enabling Health Policy & Research with Accessible Data
sessions.abstract: Discover how two hospital systems are transforming data access and research through modern, secure, insight driven approaches. South Carolina’s Revenue and Fiscal Affairs Office replaces a legacy system with dynamic ArcGIS apps that deliver faster, clearer access to statewide hospitalization data while protecting sensitive information. At Children’s Hospital of Philadelphia, the Arcus program enables spatially enabled pediatric research by integrating clinical, environmental, and social data—advancing reproducible, privacy preserving innovation.

sessions.title: Exploring 3D Workflows with Profile Graphs
sessions.abstract: Want to create and analyze profile graphs for 3D workflows? Discover ArcGIS Pro tools such as Elevation Profile and Visibility Analysis, surface profiles, and line-based profile graphs, with a focus on property manipulations. This presentation showcases tools for creating profile graphs that help measure human-scale geography in urban settings, providing practical methods for visualizing elevation and spatial relationships in 3D environments.

Keywords: Charting and Communication

sessions.title: From Grain to Glass: The Mapping and Analytics Behind the Beer
sessions.abstract: Discover how Columbia Distributing and Molson Coors are revolutionizing the beverage industry with ArcGIS. Learn how mapping and analytics empower smarter distribution, optimize retail strategies, and ensure product safety. See how ArcGIS is telling the stories for American farmers, strengthening partnerships, and delivering stronger growth.

Keywords: Logistics, Manufacturing, Retail, Supply Chain, Agriculture, Sustainability

sessions.title: GIS for Good: Programs and Initiatives at Esri
sessions.abstract: Join other GIS professionals to unite around something powerful: the ability of GIS technology to connect people, inspire positive change, and shape a more sustainable future for everyone. Explore how seeing the world through a location-based lens can help make a bigger impact on people’s lives, the health of our planet, shared prosperity, and global peace. Learn more about the ways that GIS is being used for good around the world. Speakers also discuss the Esri programs, initiatives, and special offerings that are designed to help users maximize their collective impact.

occurrences.room: GIS for Good Demo Expo Theater

Keywords: Discount licensing, humanitarian, conservation, nonprofit, sustainable development, global health

sessions.title: Geocoding in ArcGIS Pro: Tips and Tricks for Working with Locators
sessions.abstract: Are you making the most of the latest geocoding enhancements introduced in ArcGIS Pro? This presentation highlights newly added and productivity-enhancing workflows in geocoding with ArcGIS Pro. Learn about topics such as maximizing the rematching functionality, automating common geocoding workflows with the Python module, modifying locator properties to optimize geocoding results, and exporting locators and configured layers for use in other ArcGIS applications.

Keywords: Geocoding, Locators, Addresses
RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with ArcGIS Pro.

sessions.title: Integrating Data to Tackle Complex National Security Challenges
sessions.abstract: This session highlights how enterprise GIS transforms national security by integrating and analyzing diverse datasets. The first paper shows ArcGIS Knowledge graph databases organizing Critical Minerals and Materials (CMM) supply chain data for better strategic decisions. The second paper describes ArcGIS Enterprise streamlining intelligence for the Brazilian Air Force, enabling secure, real-time access. Together, these examples demonstrate enterprise GIS as a key tool for data integration, collaboration, and situational awareness in both national security and defense intelligence contexts.

sessions.title: Learning Journeys: Education, Professional Development, and Certification
sessions.abstract: The GIS industry is dynamic and people enter the profession from many directions, bringing unique combinations of educational backgrounds and prior work experiences. This session explores innovative practices in GIS education as well as the importance of professional development throughout one’s career to maintain and enhance skills. No matter where your GIS journey starts, many resources are available to guide you along the path of career advancement.

Keywords: Education, Lifelong learning, professional development, certification

sessions.title: Map Viewer: Creating Beautiful Web Maps
sessions.abstract: Want to make high-quality web maps with Map Viewer? In this presentation, speakers teach skills related to layer symbology, basemaps, pop-ups, labeling, visual hierarchy, data attribution, blend modes, effects, and other design concepts.

Keywords: design principles, visual hierarchy, web maps, cartography, layouts, user engagement, map clarity, storytelling with maps

sessions.title: Mapping for Impact with the National Geographic Society
sessions.abstract: Mapping for Impact is a core educational and operational philosophy at the National Geographic Society. It focuses on using cartography, geographic information systems (GIS), and storytelling to drive real-world change and social action. Part of National Geographic’s Storytellers Collective workshops, this exploration into the mapmaking process will help you merge data, design, and purpose to better reach your audience. Participants will learn best practices in cartography and mapping from National Geographic experts to create maps to tell stories and have.

sessions.title: Mobility Strategies that Move Your Community Forward
sessions.abstract: Make sure your communitiy implements an accessible and sustainable mobility strategy driven by the right tools and modern methods. Learn how organizations are tackling transportation planning with a geographic approach.

Keywords: transit-oriented development, engagement, regional transportation
RecommendedAudience: transportation planner

sessions.title: NISAR for Geospatial Applications
sessions.abstract: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) uses microwave remote sensing to image the earth’s surface on a regular cadence, day or night, even through clouds and smoke. Join this meeting hosted by NASA and Esri to learn about the recently launched NISAR mission in terms of its capabilities, data availability, and how to use it in geospatial analysis workflows. The presentation will include demonstrations of working with the data in ArcGIS Pro.

sessions.title: Optimizing Asset Management and Operations in Rail Through Advanced GIS
sessions.abstract: These sessions showcase how modern GIS technologies are transforming rail asset management and operational decision‑making. Presenters will share approaches for integrating 2D and 3D data, analyzing safety and performance patterns, and centralizing information to support long‑term maintenance, operational efficiency, and risk mitigation. Attendees will gain insight into how spatial analytics, digital twins, and advanced geoprocessing tools can streamline workflows, strengthen collaboration, and improve the reliability and safety of rail networks.

sessions.title: Planning Sustainable Communities with a Digital Twin
sessions.abstract: In planning initiatives, a digital twin integrates data models and technologies for a common collaboration platform. In this focused presentation, learn how planners are using a digital approach to understand patterns, trends, and changing needs within a community. Discover what is possible when you connect data, people, and processes in a living model to optimize workflows that achieve justifiable policies, equitable solutions, and sustainable outcomes.

occurrences.room: State and Local Government Expo Theater

Keywords: Urban Planning, Regional Planning, Community Development, 3D,

sessions.title: Real-World Examples: Designing and Managing Mobile Data Collection Systems
sessions.abstract: Learn how to design and manage mobile data collection systems that deliver accurate, timely field information for better decision-making. This brief presentation discusses key considerations, such as data architecture, user experience, and governance, by describing real-world examples. Whether you are building a new system or improving an existing one, gain practical insights to support mission-critical operations.

Keywords: mobile data collection, governance, user experience, system design
RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with mobile workflows, data architecture, and governance practices.

sessions.title: Smarter Environmental Compliance and Reclamation with GIS, Weather, and GeoAI
sessions.abstract: This session presents two practical approaches to improving environmental performance in mining using location intelligence and AI. Vale shows how GIS maps environmental control assets and integrates emissions and meteorological data—such as wind direction—to identify vulnerable zones and support compliance. Stantec shares reclamation cases using GeoAI models like tree count and crack detection to monitor vegetation regrowth and geomorphic stability. Together, they demonstrate how combining operational data and GeoAI creates repeatable, data‑driven workflows that enhance efficiency.

sessions.title: Spatially Enabled AI: A New Approach to Mapping
sessions.abstract: Discover how AI is reframing geography from a system of visualization to a system focused on insight and decision-making. This presentation explores how large language models, spatial data, and agent-based methods work together to support conversational, scenario-driven understanding. Learn how spatially enabled AI helps leaders ask better questions, evaluate trade-offs, and act with greater confidence.

Keywords: Spatial AI, geospatial reasoning, knowledge graphs, semantic modeling, agent workflows, prompt-driven analysis, decision intelligence, automation, spatial data services, API integration
RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with GIS, spatial data, AI, and automation.

sessions.title: Streamlining Complexity: Agile Strategies for Utility Transformation
sessions.abstract: Learn how to navigate network information management while modernizing legacy systems. You’ll discover how PLN simplified a million-kilometer network into a streamlined data model to improve loss analysis and asset reliability across 17,000 islands. We’ll also explore CPS Energy’s phased migration strategy for gas and electric networks, including how they managed a simultaneous ERP change and ADMS integration. Gain a roadmap for transitioning to the Utility Network, improving topological validation, and building a scalable, integrated foundation for the future grid.

sessions.title: Strengthening Utility Management through ArcGIS Utility Network Integration
sessions.abstract: In this session you will discover how GIS based mobile applications have enabled digital workflows focused on data collection and asset management within treatment facilities. Presenters will share how they were able to quickly collect asset information, including condition data to build a GIS-based asset registry. There will also be discussion focused on integration with their CMMS, enabling seamless data exchange and data-driven asset management.

Keywords: Vertical Asset Management, System Integration, Wastewater

sessions.title: The Open Geospatial Consortium Meet-Up
sessions.abstract: Join OGC at Esri UC for a meetup with OGC leadership, members, and geospatial professionals shaping the future of open standards. Get the latest on JSON-FG, openEO, trusted data, Building Blocks, and AI-ready geospatial infrastructure. Whether you are already involved in OGC, exploring membership, or simply interested in the future of geospatial technology, this is your chance to exchange ideas, meet peers, and learn how you can participate in shaping the standards that matter.

sessions.title: Trail Mapping & Maintenance with Shared Data and Real-Time Workflows
sessions.abstract: Learn how trail managers are modernizing both statewide trail mapping and day-to-day maintenance through shared GIS workflows and real-time data. This session highlights how agencies unify fragmented trail datasets into a single authoritative network to support cross-agency collaboration, land management decisions, and long-term planning—while also connecting field observations directly to dashboards for faster maintenance response. Together, these approaches create an end-to-end, transparent system that improves coordination, safety, and trail stewardship.

Keywords: open space, walkability, data governance

sessions.title: Web Apps: Choosing the Right App Builder
sessions.abstract: Web apps in ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise help you guide users through your maps and data. But how do you select the right web app builder for your audience? Learn the right questions to ask when choosing between ArcGIS Experience Builder, ArcGIS Instant Apps, ArcGIS StoryMaps, ArcGIS Dashboards, and ArcGIS Web Editor.

sessions.title: Where Priorities Meet Capacity: Collaborative Conservation with WebGIS
sessions.abstract: Conservation success depends on revealing where priorities, interest, and capacity align. This session showcases interactive, web-based GIS tools that turn conservation data into action through storytelling, landowner decision support, and capacity mapping. Learn how Web GIS helps landowners, partners, and stakeholders identify opportunities, coordinate efforts, and collaborate more effectively to deliver measurable conservation outcomes.

Keywords: Conservation, data, web-GIS

sessions.title: Connecting GIS and Hydraulic Modeling with Autodesk Water Infrastructure
sessions.abstract: Discover how Autodesk Water Infrastructure solutions are helping utilities connect GIS-centric workflows with hydraulic and stormwater modeling through enhanced Esri integrations. This session will highlight advancements across InfoWater Pro, InfoWorks ICM and WS Pro, and Info360 that streamline data exchange, improve model accuracy, and support more connected planning, operations, and network resilience. Learn how to leverage ArcGIS and Autodesk Water Infrastructure solutions together to modernize water management and decision-making.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Unlocking the Power of Configurable Apps with HTML, CSS, and ArcGIS Arcade
sessions.abstract: Do you work with ArcGIS Arcade in configurable apps such as ArcGIS Dashboards and ArcGIS Experience Builder? Join us to explore how to combine Arcade with HTML and CSS to customize user experiences across configurable apps. This focused presentation highlights practical, low-code techniques for creating more engaging and functional apps, empowering you to take your app configurations to the next level.

Keywords: low-code customization, styling, scripting, enhanced UX, app configuration
RecommendedAudience: Experience with HTML, CSS, Arcade scripting, and app customization.

sessions.title: Navigating the FAA’s Safety Management Mandate
sessions.abstract: Location plays a critical role in every aspect of the Safety Management System (SMS) mandated by the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), from operational safety to risk mitigation. With the requirement to implement an SMS fast approaching, are you prepared? This brief presentation explores how to develop a plan using existing tools and capabilities to support operational safety, provide greater spatial and temporal awareness, facilitate the mitigation of risks, increase efficiency, and support regulatory compliance.

occurrences.room: State and Local Government Expo Theater

Keywords: Airports, Safety Management Systems, FAA, SMS

sessions.title: NASA Disasters Program User Group Meeting
sessions.abstract: Geospatial data is essential during disasters, but time and capacity limits often hinder its use. How can we ensure critical Earth observations reach GIS practitioners in a timely, usable form? Discover how the NASA Disasters Program streamlines access to Earth observation data through openly available web services. We will showcase real‑time decision-making support and discuss recent responses such as Hurricane Melissa, flooding in Hawaii this March, and Typhoon Sinlaku to show how NASA’s data, services, and coordination framework can strengthen blue‑sky readiness and gray‑sky impact.

sessions.title: Turning Spatial Insight into Actionable Asset Decisions
sessions.abstract:
GIS remains powerful for infrastructure understanding. In 2026, organizations pair GIS with AI-supported analysis to surface trends, assess risk, and inform planning. This session examines how spatial data strengthens asset management—from condition assessments to long-term strategies. Real examples demonstrate GIS-driven insight supporting operational efficiency and resilience.
Learn how spatial analysis reveals infrastructure priorities, the role of GIS in asset management, real-world applications in planning and operations, and how spatial context aligns investments with growth and risk.

sessions.title: Airports SIG
sessions.abstract:
Join a conversation with your peers and Esri staff about the challenges that arise as airports continue to experience growth. Increasing demands require more intelligent operations and improved safety, efficiency, and sustainability to ensure compliance, profitability, and exceptional passenger experiences. In this SIG, share your experiences and priorities with people at other airports who face the same challenges. Get a greater understanding of how GIS and technology trends are being used to meet existing and emerging needs in 2026 and beyond. Build connections, learn from peers, and bring actionable ideas back to your organization.

ReservationRequired: This session requires a reservation to attend. Please use Add to Schedule to reserve your spot or Join Wait List to be added to the queue.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Configurable Apps: Accelerating App Deployments
sessions.abstract: Speed matters when deploying ArcGIS configurable apps at scale. This brief presentation highlights best practices for rapid implementation using templates, automation tools, and governance frameworks. Learn how to streamline workflows, reduce manual effort, and maintain consistency across multiple apps and teams.

Keywords: rapid deployment, templates, automation, governance, scalability
RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with templates, automation tools, and governance frameworks.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Excalibur: Web-Based Imagery and Video Workflows
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Excalibur unifies imagery and geospatial video in a modern web-based experience. Learn how to work with image services, tiled layers, web maps, and video in a web browser to streamline analysis and remote inspections. This presentation shows how Excalibur’s tools, tasking workflows, and video capabilities help teams annotate, analyze, and share products efficiently for faster, more collaborative operations.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Experience Builder: Getting Started with Linear Referencing
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Roads and Highways and ArcGIS Pipeline Referencing have a new web-based experience for querying and editing linear referencing data within ArcGIS Experience Builder. Join us to learn about these new widgets and how to deploy them and utilize them within template or custom applications to support linear referenced event editing and other workflows on the web.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Field Maps: Supporting Geotechnical Workflows
sessions.abstract: Curious about how ArcGIS Field Maps streamlines data collection and manages workflows and resources? Join us to explore how, with Field Maps, you can transform your geotechnical workflows in linear infrastructure projects. Learn how to apply ArcGIS and its wealth of data for precise planning, from drilling geotechnical borings to setting soil sample locations. This presentation demonstrates enhanced navigation and stationing capabilities that empower teams to communicate effectively between field crews and office experts.

Keywords:
ArcGIS Field Maps
Mobile GIS
Geotechnical workflows

sessions.title: ArcGIS Hub: Getting Started with the ArcGIS Hub Assistant
sessions.abstract: Make content in ArcGIS Hub Premium more discoverable with the ArcGIS Hub assistant. Discover how to configure your own Hub search assistants and learn about defining curated content to be searched, setting tone, AI guardrails, and more.
tracks: National Mapping, Navigation, and Geospatial Authorities

Keywords: Hub, AI
Topic: National Mapping, Navigation, and Geospatial Authorities

sessions.title: ArcGIS Knowledge: Analysis Use Cases with Knowledge Graphs
sessions.abstract: Learn how to use knowledge graphs to help you uncover network vulnerabilities and identify groups or clusters of entities that are similar or closely connected to each other. Speakers explore how graph analytics can be combined with traditional geospatial analytics to reveal patterns like these in your data. In addition, discover how to integrate unstructured text data using natural language processing (NLP) into a knowledge graph for advanced analysis.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World: Best Practices for Transforming Data into Insights
sessions.abstract: Have you ever struggled to communicate the value of your GIS products to audiences? In this presentation, learn the best practices to bring more meaning and clarity to layers, maps, stories, and applications. Speakers demonstrate how to start with a geographic approach to analyze and integrate datasets and then use visualization techniques for specific audiences to improve engagement.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World
Keywords: ArcGIS Living Atlas, Geographic Approach, Living Atlas, analysis, content, sustainability, workflows

sessions.title: ArcGIS Maps SDKs for Game Engines: An Overview and What's New
sessions.abstract: Discover how ArcGIS Maps SDKs for Game Engines enable you to use Unity and Unreal Engine to build immersive 3D and extended reality (XR) experiences with real-world data. Explore several new features, including support for point cloud and point scene layers, identifying and selecting scene layers, and using custom materials to highlight areas of interest and analysis results. This presentation also includes a preview of what's coming next.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Maps SDKs for Game Engines
RecommendedAudience: Experience developing with a game engine.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise: Best Practices for Distributed Collaboration
sessions.abstract: Are you implementing distributed collaboration? Do you want to know more about communication, syncing, and identifying issues within a distributed collaboration? Learn from Esri technical support experts to understand how they identify network, data, or component issues. These experts share the questions, tests, and tools that they use to identify a problem in a distributed collaboration.

Keywords: Collaboration, Distributed Collaboration, ArcGIS Enterprise, ArcGIS Online
RecommendedAudience: Knowledge of ArcGIS Enterprise or ArcGIS Online.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online: Building and Maintaining High-Demand Web Maps
sessions.abstract: Elevate your web applications. Join us to learn the essentials of building and maintaining high-demand web maps, including efficient data management, seamless user interactions, and effective tracking and analysis of user engagement to boost map performance. Discover best practices for creating responsive views that captivate your audience, and master high-frequency data updates to keep your applications dynamic.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions:  Crime Analysis
sessions.abstract: Need to implement data-driven strategies that reduce crime and improve operations? Discover how the Crime Analysis solution helps organizations use mapping and spatial analytics to manage incident data, conduct tactical and strategic analysis, perform investigative analysis, and share information products with decision makers.

Keywords: Crime analysis, Crime analyst, Incident analysis, Law enforcement, Police, Intelligence

sessions.title: ArcGIS StoryMaps Briefings: Present Anywhere—Online or Offline
sessions.abstract: Create interactive presentations with ArcGIS StoryMaps Briefings. Discover how the ArcGIS StoryMaps Briefings app enables access to your briefings in any environment, including offline scenarios. Experience a live tablet demonstration and learn how to produce, brand, and share slide-based briefings using ArcGIS content. Speakers provide onboarding steps and enterprise examples.

Keywords: Briefings, presentation, offline
RecommendedAudience: Experience with ArcGIS StoryMaps, ArcGIS content, and creating presentations.

sessions.title: ArcGIS for Autodesk Forma: Bringing GIS Context to Early Designs
sessions.abstract: Bring real-world geospatial context into early designs. Architects and designers can access and visualize 3D GIS layers—for zoning, flood risk, and infrastructure—directly in ArcGIS for Autodesk Forma (Site Design) to support informed site analysis and design exploration. Using an example from Austin, Texas, learn how spatial context shapes better outcomes and how Esri and Autodesk collaborate on cloud interoperability.

Keywords: ArcGIS for Autodesk Forma, GIS&BIM integration

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Combatting Fraud, Waste, and Abuse
sessions.abstract: Expose fraud with ArcGIS. Fraud and financial crime investigators use ArcGIS AllSource and ArcGIS Knowledge to explore and analyze spatial, nonspatial, structured, and unstructured data to understand and recognize exploitation. Analyze and visualize data through multiple views. Maps, timelines, and link charts assist analysts in identifying fraudulent activity efficiently and disseminating the intelligence that is needed to support investigations.

Keywords: Fraud, Financial Crimes
RecommendedAudience: Knowledge of intelligence analysis and fraud.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Data-Driven Operations for Public Safety
sessions.abstract: Explore how ArcGIS is a platform for data integration, allowing you to bring together many different types of data from various sources to support and enhance your public safety mission through a geographic approach to problem-solving. Speakers discuss effective best practices for integrating and managing content and share lessons learned from agencies that have combined content from many sources to optimize decision-making and resource allocation.

Keywords: Real-time, Public Safety

sessions.title: Conservation SIG
sessions.abstract:
Join a conversation with peers and Esri staff centered on community, learning, and collaboration in conservation. This SIG creates space to come together, exchange perspectives, and learn from one another about protecting, restoring, and caring for biodiversity. Examples involving digital twins are shared to focus the conversation on applied practice. The SIG emphasizes relationships, shared challenges, and success across conservation efforts. Build connections, learn from peers, and bring actionable ideas back to your organization.

sessions.title: EPA GIS User Group Meeting

sessions.title: Geospatial AI Foundation and Realization
sessions.abstract:
We are at a turning point in Geospatial technology. AI is no longer a future capability it is available today, embedded in the platforms GIS professionals already use. The question is no longer whether to act, but how to act with confidence and a plan.

Join Esri , CyberTech, and customers navigating the Geospatial AI journey as they walk through a practical framework - from strategic alignment and data readiness to scaling AI across the enterprise.

Seating is limited and available by registration only.

As geospatial systems become more connected, governing them well is key to addressing challenges. Build connections, learn from peers, and bring actionable ideas back to your organization.

sessions.abstract: Want to map the location of your indoor assets? Discover how to use indoor maps created with ArcGIS Indoors in ArcGIS Field Maps to perform field data collection within facilities. Explore how to use 360-degree photos for a photorealistic experience of the local asset conditions using ArcGIS Indoors. Also, speakers demonstrate how ArcGIS IPS is used with ArcGIS Field Maps to equip mobile workers with real-time location information (with the blue dot) and enable them to update asset locations within facilities.

Geospatial data has become a critical tool for insurance carriers, from assessing the risk of policies and portfolios to managing response efforts for large loss events. Build connections, learn from peers, and bring actionable ideas back to your organization.

sessions.title: Map Lounge Fireside Chat: Maps that Inspire
sessions.abstract:
In a world filled with information, attention is precious. Maps have a unique ability to stop us, draw us in, and make us look twice. But what makes a map captivating? Why do some maps fade into the background while others spark curiosity, wonder, or even awe? In this session, Esri cartographers will showcase how they use thoughtful design — composition, contrast, hierarchy, and storytelling principles — to visualize data into compelling stories that engage viewers.

Please note that seating is limited, however standing room will be available.

sessions.abstract: Join us to explore how GIS transforms humanitarian mine action by mapping hazardous areas, assessing contamination, and analyzing risk to guide safe clearance. Discover how spatial intelligence drives prioritization, accelerates operations, and strengthens protection for vulnerable communities, enabling data-driven decisions that save lives and restore land for sustainable recovery.

sessions.abstract: Managing the Right-of-Way (ROW) requires absolute data certainty. This session explores how Trimble’s high-precision Geospatial tools and GIS-centric Unity Permit (formerly Cityworks PLL) create a seamless "Confidence Loop". By integrating centimeter-accurate field data directly into your Esri environment, you ensure regulatory compliance, reduce ROW conflicts, and build a more resilient community.

sessions.title: Seeing the Battlespace Through Connected Intelligence
sessions.abstract: Defense missions generate continuous observations about people, places, assets, and events. This presentation demonstrates how ArcGIS connects these observations into managed objects and relationships that persist across space and time. By linking sensor detections, intelligence reporting, and operational data, analysts and commanders can identify patterns, maintain object custody, and understand first-, second-, and third-order effects throughout the operational environment. Learn how ArcGIS integrates disconnected data into a complete operational picture that supports real-time mission decisions.

Keywords:
Connected intelligence
Object-based analysis
Battlespace awareness
Multi-source data fusion

sessions.abstract: Want to find out how to minimize disruption on roadways? sessions.title: Council of Governments/ MPO Lunch Meet-up
sessions.abstract: An opportunity for staff of different Council of Governments to get together, discuss relevant topics, and exchange information and set up spaces for ongoing collaboration. Please bring a lunch with you.

sessions.title: High-Precision Asset Lifecycle Management Integrating Trimble Unity with ArcGIS
sessions.abstract: Salt Lake City Department of Public Utilities (SLCDPU) pioneered an end-to-end asset lifecycle by integrating Trimble Cityworks with ArcGIS Utility Network. This session demonstrates a scalable methodology for transforming complex permitting into a digital workflow, ensuring infrastructure is captured accurately before it is buried. Learn how permitting triggers high-precision field inspections using GNSS rovers, and how SLCDPU leverages branch versioning and tracing for data integrity. This GIS-centric blueprint automates maintenance and provides confidence at every turn of the asset lifecycle.

sessions.title: Governing Your Enterprise
sessions.abstract: Explore key strategies for effective governance within your organization. In this brief presentation, gain a clear understanding of what enterprise governance entails and why it is crucial for organizational success. Discover practical tips and best practices to streamline governance processes and enhance decision-making within your enterprise.

Keywords: policy enforcement, compliance management, organizational alignment, risk mitigation, accountability frameworks, decision governance, operational oversight, strategic planning, performance monitoring, leadership practices

sessions.title: A Playbook to Map, Model, and Mobilize Transit Planning
sessions.abstract: Discover how transit agencies can leverage GIS for data-driven planning and systemwide strategies. LACMTA analyses multimodal travel patterns, equity indices, demographics, and Mobility Hub initiatives to reduce vehicle miles travelled and expand regional mobility for the 2028 Olympics and beyond. NJ TRANSIT’s LAND Plan transforms its first real-property geodatabase into revenue, using site-suitability analysis and 3D visualizations to generate housing, jobs, and other wins. Attendees will leave with actionable ArcGIS workflows for sustainable, community-focused transit and mobility planning.

Keywords: Travel Behavior Analysis, Transit Corridor Analysis, Multimodal Mobility Planning, Non Fare Revenue Generation, Long Term Capital Planning, Site Suitability Modeling,
RecommendedAudience: City/Regional Planner, Transit Planner, Mobility Program Manager, GIS Analyst, Executive Director, Capital Planner, Mobility Programs Manager, Transit Analyst

sessions.title: Achieving Wellness through Needs Assessments and Connected Care
sessions.abstract: Discover how two health systems are transforming community well being through dynamic, insight driven approaches. Children’s National reimagines its Community Health Needs Assessment using visual storytelling that brings equity gaps and local context to life. Loma Linda University Health streamlines post discharge support with personalized, location based resource navigation that bridges social determinants of health (SDOH) screening and real world care. Together, these projects show how thoughtful health care design improves resource allocation, coordination, and health outcomes.

Keywords: hospital, data

sessions.title: Approaches to Organizational Governance
sessions.abstract: As organizations increasingly rely on geospatial systems for mission-critical operations, structured governance is essential. This technical session explores building and sustaining an effective governance framework aligned with enterprise goals. Speakers review organizational structure, geospatial maturity, and ArcGIS Enterprise management, highlighting role definition, policy enforcement, and stewardship. Learn to match governance strategies to your organization and discover best practices for change management, accountability, and long-term success.

Keywords: Organizational structure, geospatial maturity, policy enforcement, stewardship, accountability, governance framework, change management, compliance, enterprise goals, long-term success
RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with organizational governance, enterprise GIS, and change management.

sessions.title: ArcGIS API for Python: Content Management
sessions.abstract: Moving content between organizations is a fundamental task confronted by web GIS managers. The ArcGIS API for Python provides various options for doing so, based on the specifics of your organization and your desired workflow. In this presentation, speakers establish some standard terms around cloning, migration, and promotion; explore how the clone_items() method works; and briefly discuss migrating group content. Also, learn best practices for working with specific item types and discover troubleshooting strategies for when items may not clone or migrate as expected.

Keywords: Content management, automation, Python, scripting
RecommendedAudience: Basic experience with Python.

sessions.title: ArcGIS AllSource: An Overview and What's New
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS AllSource is desktop analysis software that is built for intelligence analysts working in the military, civilian intelligence agencies, law enforcement, and the private sector. Learn about the key features and capabilities of this powerful desktop application, such as temporal analysis and visualization with the timeline, link analysis, and other key analysis workflows to help solve challenging problems. This technical session will help you understand what you can do with ArcGIS AllSource and find out what's coming in the next release.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Business Analyst Web App: Analyzing Health Trends and Community Needs
sessions.abstract: The ArcGIS Business Analyst Web App improves health outcomes and better serves communities through informed decision-making. This brief presentation discusses how to map health and demographics data to identify health trends and service gaps. Discover how to use Business Analyst tools to find optimal locations for clinics, vaccination sites, and health education programs, enabling you to create a safer, healthier community.

occurrences.room: State and Local Government Expo Theater

Keywords: Health, equity, health department, public safety, health services

sessions.title: ArcGIS Dashboards: Best Practices
sessions.abstract: If you are an ArcGIS Dashboards user, do you know what makes a good dashboard? A dashboard should provide key insights at a glance and effectively communicate what is important. This presentation shares tips for getting the most out of your dashboards with best practices specific to various patterns of use. Discover how to communicate status and evaluate performance. Get ideas for new ways to present information and visualize your data effectively.

Keywords: Dashboards, Tips and Tricks, Best Practices, Data Visualization

sessions.title: ArcGIS Data Interoperability: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Do you want to learn how to integrate hundreds of data formats, by import or export, and solve data quality and data engineering challenges along the way? ArcGIS Data Interoperability is Esri's no-code extract, transform, and load (ETL) extension for ArcGIS Pro and ArcGIS Enterprise. Speakers will discuss the Workbench, Data Inspector and Quick Translate apps in the extension, plus the Quick Import and Quick Export system tools, as well as change data capture (CDC), automating metadata along with data, ETL with cloud data, and advanced topics like maintaining ArcGIS Knowledge graphs.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with data integration and synchronization, whether manual, scripted, or automated.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Data Reviewer: An Overview
sessions.abstract: How can ArcGIS Data Reviewer, an ArcGIS Pro and ArcGIS Enterprise extension, improve GIS data quality and reduce your data management costs? Learn the basics of GIS data quality and practices for capturing quality requirements and identifying quality-related capabilities in ArcGIS. Discover how to use ArcGIS Data Reviewer's integration in attribute rules to prevent and detect poor quality data. Visual review workflows and tools for identifying sources of error, such as missing, miscoded, or spatially inaccurate features, which cannot be found through automation, are also highlighted.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise on Kubernetes: Is It for Me?
sessions.abstract: Discover the lessons learned by customers who prepared to use ArcGIS Enterprise on Kubernetes. How did they decide that Kubernetes was right for them? What is it like to deploy the software? What do they want to achieve with the technology? Come with your questions about the benefits of ArcGIS Enterprise on Kubernetes and how to get ready for it. Also, check out the related presentations ArcGIS Enterprise on Kubernetes: Availability, Scalability, and Operability and ArcGIS Enterprise on Kubernetes: Wreck It and Recover It.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise on Kubernetes: Tips and Tricks
sessions.abstract: Gain practical, actionable tips for supporting ArcGIS Enterprise on Kubernetes. By learning how to gather relevant information, interpret logs, and check system health, you will be better prepared to resolve problems if they arise.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Integrating Cloud Services
sessions.abstract: Get connected! ArcGIS Enterprise supports connecting to and using a variety of services in several cloud environments, including cloud object storage like AWS S3, Azure Storage, and Google Cloud Storage, as well as cloud databases, like AWS Aurora, Azure SQL, and Google Cloud SQL. In this presentation, you will learn about these integration options, how they can benefit you, and how to get started.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Enterprise on Amazon Web Services
Keywords: Cloud, Cloud-native services, ArcGIS Enterprise
RecommendedAudience: Experience with ArcGIS Enterprise.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: What's New
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Enterprise is always evolving with new functionality and enhancements. Join us for details about what's new in recent releases, from administrative enhancements to mapping, data management, and more. Learn how these capabilities can advance and expand your organization's use of ArcGIS Enterprise.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Image Analyst: An Introduction
sessions.abstract: Transform ArcGIS Pro into a powerful image analysis workstation. This presentation introduces the ArcGIS Image Analyst extension and its advanced tools for multispectral and hyperspectral analyses, synthetic aperture radar (SAR) data, and geospatial artificial intelligence (GeoAI) for feature extraction. Learn how Image Analyst supports stereo, video, and time-series workflows, expanding your ability to work with complex imagery and unlock deeper analytical insights.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Knowledge: Graph Analytics with Spatial and Temporal Context
sessions.abstract: Connected data in ArcGIS Knowledge helps analysts or investigators identify groups, discover vulnerabilities, and find connections. In this technical session, learn how to combine graph analytics with traditional geospatial analytics to reveal similar patterns. This presentation goes "beyond the basics" to explain how to apply graph algorithms like centrality and filtered paths with temporal constraints. Find out how to construct openCypher graph queries on your own or using AI. Speakers also describe how Python notebooks can help operationalize analysis and scale insights across the enterprise.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise: An Overview of ArcGIS Arcade
sessions.abstract: Want to know how to enhance your maps and apps by using ArcGIS Arcade? If so, you cannot miss this presentation, which will empower you to understand and use Arcade expressions effectively in an ArcGIS Online or ArcGIS Enterprise environment. Join us and learn how Arcade can help you tell engaging stories, create rich visualizations, and provide dynamic content for your audience. Gain best practices as well as tips and tricks to fine-tune your maps and keep them fast and responsive.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online: Best Practices for Hosted Feature Layers
sessions.abstract: Learn to use hosted feature services in your apps effectively. This presentation explores how the feature service REST API makes apps more efficient and reliable. You will also learn the recommended workflows for updating, editing, and syncing feature service data.

RecommendedAudience: Experience creating hosted feature layers in ArcGIS Online.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Geoprocessing Tips and Tricks
sessions.abstract: Want to learn more about geoprocessing? Join us for an overview of geoprocessing and spatial analysis, data management, conversion, and related workflows in ArcGIS Pro. Discover capabilities to improve your productivity when using geoprocessing. Speakers discuss geoprocessing history, environment settings, parallel processing, automated workflows with batch processing, ModelBuilder, Python, scheduling and sharing tools, and new tools for solving new and old spatial problems efficiently.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Integrating BIM Data
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Pro empowers architects, engineers, and GIS professionals to collaborate by integrating BIM data into a shared geospatial environment. With native support for Industry Foundation Classes (IFC) and Autodesk Revit files as feature layers, ArcGIS Pro enables seamless 3D visualization and access to rich attribute data. Discover how to create building scene layers, share models through interactive web scenes, and integrate BIM into GIS workflows earlier in design—bridging spatial analysis and design for clearer communication, smarter planning, and better decision-making.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with BIM data formats like IFC and Revit.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Layouts and Map Series
sessions.abstract: Want to create and customize layouts and map series more effectively? Join us to discover new functionality in ArcGIS Pro layouts as well as information and tricks to help streamline your layout workflows. Learn about the various types of map series that are available in ArcGIS Pro as well as when and how to use them to make many similar layouts quickly.

RecommendedAudience: Experience with layouts, mapping, and visualization.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Sharing Web Layers, Updating Web Maps, and Packaging Projects
sessions.abstract: Share and update GIS content efficiently to your ArcGIS Online or ArcGIS Enterprise organization. Join us for an overview of using ArcGIS Pro to share web layers, web maps, and project packages. Explore essential workflows that enable easy access to your work across desktop, web, and mobile applications and learn how to use ArcPy to automate the process of sharing web layers.

Keywords: publishing, feature service, portal

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Visualizing Large Datasets Using Clustering and Feature Binning
sessions.abstract: Explore and retrieve meaningful information, even when many point features overlap. Join us to learn about two visualization techniques in ArcGIS Pro that can represent overlapping point features as clusters or in feature bins. These techniques help you notice trends and patterns that might be almost impossible to comprehend otherwise. Gain an understanding of how to enable these options, related best practices, and how you can share as services. Speakers also discuss newly supported NoSQL databases that are highly optimized to process millions of features.

sessions.title: ArcGIS QuickCapture: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Learn how organizations use the ArcGIS QuickCapture mobile app for rapid field data collection. Attend this presentation and discover how you can create your own rapid data collection apps with QuickCapture! Speakers also highlight recently added features.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Spatial Analyst: Revealing Spatial Patterns with Density Analysis
sessions.abstract: Density analysis helps to find concentration and hot spots, but critical patterns are not just 2D—they exist in 3D space and evolve over time. This presentation moves beyond basic heat maps to show you how to master density analysis in 2D, 3D, and 4D. Join us to learn about key tools such as the Kernel Density tool, the Space Time Kernel Density tool with netCDF and voxel layers, and the Percentile Contours tools. Learn how to move from point observations to density to strategies, visualize 3D phenomena, track dynamic hot spots, and quickly identify core areas for proactive decision-making.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Utility Network: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Do you manage utilities? This presentation provides insight into network management capabilities that enable next-generation analytics and experiences for any utility system. Learn about updates from development that showcase current and emerging functionality as they apply to utility information challenges, and get a preview of the road ahead for network management for utilities.

RecommendedAudience: Basic understanding of geodatabase and utility network concepts.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Workflow Manager: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Optimize your workflows with ArcGIS Workflow Manager, a scalable work management system for ArcGIS Enterprise and ArcGIS Online. The Workflow Manager framework lets organizations capture and standardize repeatable processes, incorporate automation to streamline workflows, communicate effectively, and encourage adherence to standards and best practices. Join us to learn what Workflow Manager is and how it can boost both GIS and non-GIS operations. Topics include a product overview, key concepts and capabilities, common use cases, building workflows, and monitoring and managing work.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with ArcGIS Enterprise and the services-based architecture.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Analyzing Water Loss for Utilities
sessions.abstract: Curious to find out how ArcGIS technology is used by water and sewer utilities? Join us to learn about the various tools in ArcGIS that can visualize, manage, and reduce water loss.

Keywords: Analysis, Operational Awareness

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Data Exploration and Visualization for Spatial Analysis
sessions.abstract: Join us to learn visualization techniques for analyzing and effectively communicating your data using ArcGIS Pro and Map Viewer in ArcGIS Online. Discover how different methods, from maps and charts to 3D scenes, can help you compare categories and amounts, visualize distributions and frequency, explore relationships and correlations, and understand change over time or distance.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Map Wizardry
sessions.abstract: Sit back and enjoy this inspiring cartographic ride! In this presentation, speakers share some truly mesmerizing maps to demonstrate the cartographic power and flexibility of the ArcGIS system. Go beyond the defaults and discover how you can bend ArcGIS to your every cartographic whim to bring data to life using innovative and imaginative techniques. See maps that you maybe have not even considered, let alone thought possible, and learn ideas and workflows that you can use creatively in your own work. Get inspired to create compelling maps and make them sing and dance in new and exciting ways.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Understanding and Supporting OGC Web Coverage Service
sessions.abstract: The Open Geospatial Consortium (OGC) Coverages standard turns complex data into actionable insight. OGC Coverages can represent and access space-varying and time-varying data—such as satellite imagery, elevation models, and climate datasets—in an interoperable framework. This presentation demonstrates OGC Coverages principles and how ArcGIS supports them for efficient handling of large raster and gridded data, enabling spatiotemporal analysis, modeling, and visualization.

Keywords: OGC, WCS, Image Services, coverages, standards, APIs
RecommendedAudience: Experience with imagery services.

sessions.title: ArcPy: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Python is a powerful yet easy-to-learn open-source programming language that is widely used and supported in ArcGIS. Speakers focus on ArcPy, a Python site package that is the entry point for accessing and automating the built-in functionality of both ArcGIS Pro and ArcGIS Enterprise. Learn how you can use ArcPy to automate your spatial analysis and data processing workflows. Also, discover how to manage Python environments and packages in ArcGIS Pro.

RecommendedAudience: Basic experience with Python.

sessions.title: Authoritative Geospatial Fabric
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS incorporates data from across the national security enterprise into a single, trusted geospatial framework. This presentation demonstrates how data is integrated, governed, and kept in sync so that teams at every level work with the same accurate picture. The result is improved situational awareness, less time spent reconciling data, and faster decision-making across missions.

Keywords:
Authoritative geospatial fabric

Data integration

Enterprise geospatial framework

Data governance

Authoritative data

sessions.title: Connecting Communities to Nature Through GIS
sessions.abstract: Engaging communities through hands-on learning is essential for conservation awareness. This demo theater presentation highlights how GIS enables organizations to design and deliver impactful nature-based education programs, fostering curiosity, participation, and stewardship. Discover how spatial intelligence can connect people to nature, inspire learning, and create lasting community-led conservation outcomes.

occurrences.room: GIS for Good Demo Expo Theater

Keywords: Nature Based Education, IUCN

sessions.title: Coordinate Systems and Transformations: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Want a better understanding of  coordinate systems and transformations? Learn about supported coordinate system types, their details, and how they are represented through well-known ID (WKID) or well-known text (WKT) in ArcGIS. When data layers use different geographic or vertical coordinate systems, you must use transformations to align the data in a map or scene. Learn how to filter the nearly 7,900 available coordinate systems to find the correct one and how to select the appropriate geographic and vertical transformation path for your project.

Keywords: map projections, coordinate systems, transformations, datums

sessions.title: Facility Security Using 3D and Floor-Aware Maps
sessions.abstract: Use 3D mesh imagery and 3D facility data to plan and respond to safety and security issues. This presentation discusses 3D evacuation planning, indoor and outdoor camera visibility, and sharing and presenting analysis outputs with decision makers.

sessions.title: From Operational to Outstanding: Leveling Up Your Land Records
sessions.abstract: Do you want your land records in the best shape but you cannot keep up with the latest GIS advancements? If so, attend this presentation to discover how you can enhance workflow efficiency, improve customer service, and ensure policy compliance through technology. Learn to use AI, automate workflows, and improve collaboration for the best land-record success. Whether you oversee parcel mapping or assessment and taxation in the assessor’s office, you will gain valuable oversights.

Keywords: parcel, assessor, land management, tax

sessions.title: GIS for Wildfire Management in Brazil
sessions.abstract: See how Brazil advances wildfire management with Esri GIS. Learn how their innovative programs provide monitoring and interagency sharing of wildfire data including risk mapping, detection, response, and recovery.

Keywords: Wildfire, Environmental and natural resources management, Emergency and disaster management, National government, Conservation, GIS, wildfire monitoring, fire mitigation, satellite imagery, Cerrado biome, wildfire mitigation, wildfire preparedness, environmental management.
RecommendedAudience: Beginner

sessions.title: Gas Utility Network Information Management Journeys
sessions.abstract: Attend this session to hear Puget Sound Energy discuss the configuration and GIS best practices it prioritized in its migration from its legacy GIS to Esri's ArcGIS Utility Network. The Energy Cooperative will then talk about how planning, collaboration, and a right-sized implementation strategy made its modernization effort a success.

sessions.title: Geodatabase: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Have geodatabase questions? Learn all about this key data storage and data management framework for ArcGIS, and explore the aspects of the geodatabase information model that enhance your data with spatial and relational integrity rules, data validation, business logic, and advanced behavior.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with ArcGIS Pro.

sessions.title: Getting Started with AI Assistants
sessions.abstract: AI assistants in ArcGIS use the power of generative AI to streamline everyday workflows. Join this technical session to learn about the various assistants that are available and how to get started.

sessions.title: Imagery: Working with Multidimensional Rasters
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS supports a wide range of multidimensional data formats, including Network Common Data Form (NetCDF), Hierarchical Data Format (HDF), General Regularly Distributed Information in Binary (GRIB), and Zarr, as well as satellite imagery time series. During this demo theater presentation, learn how to manage, visualize, and analyze these datasets; uncover patterns through charts and voxel-based exploration; and compute trends over time. Examples from ArcGIS Pro and ArcGIS Online are provided.

sessions.title: Indoor GIS, Facilities and Digital Twins
sessions.abstract: In this session, you will learn how GIS is being used to modernize infrastructure and construction operations through automation, digital twins, and real-time data integration. Discover how unifying operational, asset, and field data reduces manual effort, improves accuracy and compliance, and enables proactive decision-making. You will gain insight into scalable, location-based workflows that support utilities, campuses, ports, and other asset-intensive environments operating in complex, always-on conditions.

Keywords: Digital Twin; Infrastructure Operations; Data Automation; Asset & Utility Management
RecommendedAudience: Basic understanding of GIS concepts and spatial data; Familiarity with infrastructure, utilities, or construction workflows; Experience with enterprise or operational data environments

sessions.title: Indoor GIS: Building Custom Indoor Mapping and Positioning Apps
sessions.abstract: Learn how to use ArcGIS Experience Builder to develop a low-code or no-code custom app for indoor mapping. Also, discover how to use ArcGIS Maps SDK for Kotlin and ArcGIS Maps SDK for Swift to add indoor mapping and positioning to your native mobile apps for iOS and Android.

Keywords: indoor mapping, developer technologies, custom apps, low code, no code, custom applications, indoor positioning

sessions.title: Innovating Species Protection: Smarter Habitat & Compliance Workflows
sessions.abstract: Discover how modern digital workflows are transforming wildlife protection across diverse environments. Learn how teams are streamlining habitat management, improving species monitoring, and strengthening permit and compliance processes through connected field operations, shared data, and real time coordination. See how these approaches accelerate response times, improve transparency, and support stronger conservation outcomes—from upland preserves to coastal nesting beaches.

Keywords: sea turtle, endangered species, conservation

sessions.title: Innovative Hydrography: Leveraging Arc Hydro and AI for Science and Conservation
sessions.abstract: Join this session to learn about the importance of linking data to hydrography and how new Arc Hydro workflows and tools for linear referencing, event creation and maintenance support science, policy, and conservation. You will also learn how AI and innovative workflows are being used to integrate 3DEP and lidar data into the 3D Hydrography Program, providing benefits for flood mapping, infrastructure planning, and environmental stewardship.

Keywords: 3D Hydrography program, Arc Hydro workflows, Improved feature delineation

sessions.title: Modern GIS Workflows for Smarter Mine Planning and Quality
sessions.abstract: This session highlights GIS workflows that modernize mine decision‑making by converting field and sensor data into actionable intelligence. Nutrien uses SLAM‑based mobile scanning to capture underground travel ways as point clouds and derive clearance metrics suporting maintenance planning, then shares results through ArcGIS Experience Builder for better coordination. Hydro Mineração Paragominas shows how ArcGIS Field Maps, ArcGIS Survey123, and ArcGIS Dashboards enable near real‑time pit data collection integrated with geological models. Together, these workflows improve planning efficiency and reduce manual reporting

sessions.title: Official Statistics: Modern Data Dissemination Methods
sessions.abstract: This session will provide a unique look at two different approaches to data dissemination. One approach will focus on the problem of bringing together data from multiple sources, while the second not only visualizes data but also shows us how to analyze the same data, including temporal change.

Keywords: statistics, data, dissemination, temporal, demographics

sessions.title: Operationalizing Location Intelligence for Tactical and Field Teams
sessions.abstract: Location intelligence is no longer confined to analysts behind a screen. Modern operations demand that the same spatial awareness used in command centers and back offices be extended to tactical teams in the field. By integrating tradecraft with mobile technology, real-time data, 3D visualizations, tracking capabilities, and diverse information sources, field teams gain direct access to mission intelligence, enhancing awareness, collaboration, and decision-making for every operation.

Keywords: Tactical Operations, Intelligence Tradecraft, Field Operations, 3D

sessions.title: Optimizing Urban Forests with Data‑Driven Management & Tree Insights
sessions.abstract: Urban forestry programs deliver essential environmental, social, and economic benefits—from stormwater mitigation and urban cooling to improved air quality, carbon reduction, and mental well being. This session brings together two ArcGIS powered projects that demonstrate how local governments can modernize urban forest management through real time data collection, operational optimization, and transparent public communication.

Keywords: shade, tree canopy

sessions.title: Road Repairs and Inspections Simplified
sessions.abstract: Learn how two organizations are strengthening roadway operations by unifying inspection, reporting, and cost‑tracking processes in a single workflow. By replacing scattered tools and manual communication, teams gain clearer insight into repair needs, associated costs, and inspection coverage. Standardized data collection improves decision‑making, speeds up response to roadway damage, and supports better planning for future improvements—all while reducing duplicated efforts.

sessions.title: Solving the Skills Gap
sessions.abstract: To maximize value from GIS technology, organizations need an enthusiastic workforce that can confidently apply ArcGIS capabilities and the power of location data. Attend this brief presentation to discover the latest Esri resources to prepare new users, continually grow skills, support the professional development needs of novice and experienced GIS users alike, and deliver data-driven insights across your organization.

Keywords: skills development, training, professional growth, learning resources
RecommendedAudience: Interest in GIS learning and workforce development planning.

sessions.title: Spatial Monitoring and Compliance‑Driven Field Intelligence
sessions.abstract: Attendees will learn how leading forestry organizations are applying GIS, remote sensing, and enterprise spatial workflows to modernize field operations, strengthen oversight, and meet expanding sustainability and reporting expectations.

sessions.title: Strategies for Reducing Health Risk & Improving Quality-of-Life
sessions.abstract: There are practical interventions that governments can conduct to reduce health risk and hazards to community well-being. City of Chattanooga, TN focuses on civic engagement and public information tools to involve the community in their tree planting initiative. California EPA’s CalHeatScore delivers hyper local heat risk insights and access to lifesaving resources.

sessions.title: The Nature Conservancy Staff and Partners Meeting
sessions.abstract: All TNC staff, TNC-Esri Authorized Entity staff and Conservation partners are welcome to join this informal meeting. After introductions we will highlight TNC Conservation & Geospatial Systems Team’s work and preview our new Fiscal Year (FY27) priorities. Q&A will wrap up the meeting.

sessions.title: The Planning Digital Twin
sessions.abstract: A 3D visualization doesn't just help the public understand development projects, it also provides increased analytic capabilities, enabling real-time evaluation of design impacts on a community. Learn how to create your own digital twin to enhance project planning and implementation.

Keywords: digital twin, 3D, drone

sessions.title: Use any CDE with GIS to Create DigitalTwin Using OpenBIM Models and Point Clouds
sessions.abstract: Most organizations operating at the intersection of BIM and GIS face the same structural problem: their data resides in multiple CDEs — sometimes different CDEs across departments, projects, or stakeholders — and no single platform is able to communicate fluently with all of them. This session addresses and solves the problem: it is demonstrated how usBIM.geotwin, ACCA’s cloud-based GIS platform, natively connects with all major CDEs — including ACCA, Autodesk, Bentley, Dassault, and Trimble — to extract OpenBIM models in IFC format and integrate them directly into an ArcGIS environment

sessions.title: Native Nations Symposium
sessions.abstract:
The Native Nations Symposium is a celebration of community, collaboration, and the transformative power of geospatial technology. Hear from industry leaders, partners and GIS experts who are utilizing geospatial technology to tackle challenges, create opportunities, and strengthen connections across tribal communities.

 

Reservations will be held until five minutes prior to the start time before seating will be opened to attendees in the on-site queue. If attendees with a reservation remove the session from their schedule, the next person on the wait list will automatically receive that open reservation. Wait lists will only be available before the conference. On-site walk-in attendance may be available dependent on room capacity and will be allowed on a first-come, first-served basis.

speakers.name: David Vogt
speakers.org: Bureau of Indian Affairs-Branch of Geospatial Support

ReservationRequired: This session requires a reservation to attend. Please use Add to Schedule to reserve your spot or Join Wait List to be added to the queue.

sessions.title: Improving Transit On-Time Performance
sessions.abstract: Interested in how to measure a transit system's efficiency? This brief presentation provides an overview of the Transit On-Time Performance solution that helps organizations track on-time performance metrics in real time and analyze historical trends.

occurrences.room: State and Local Government Expo Theater

sessions.title: Patterns for Building AI-Ready Interfaces
