sessions.abstract: As AI becomes embedded in GIS applications, user interfaces must evolve to support natural language, AI-generated insights, and context-aware experiences. This focused presentation highlights practical patterns for building AI-ready ArcGIS web applications, with a focus on conversational UI components, data structure, and integration points for AI workflows. Brief examples that use the ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript and ArcGIS Experience Builder are shared to provide a high-level approach and starter patterns that you can build on.

Keywords: User experience (UX) design, human-centered design, application architecture, design patterns, system integration, web development, developer workflows, modern UI frameworks, intelligent applications, decision support systems, real-time interaction, automation, scalability, platform extensibility, digital transformation
RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with AI concepts and web application design and workflows.

sessions.title: From Data to Outcomes: How PwC and Cyient Are Delivering Value with Microsoft Fabric + Esri
sessions.abstract: Organizations are seeking to integrate enterprise and geospatial data to drive insights and efficiency. In this session, Microsoft and partners like PwC and Cyient showcase real-world solutions built with Microsoft Fabric and Esri technologies. They highlight key use cases in Energy & Utilities and the results achieved, concluding with guidance on how organizations can begin their own Fabric + Esri journey.

sessions.title: Fine Tuning AI Models with GeoCam
sessions.abstract: Learn how to apply the latest open weight models for increased detection, segmentation and scene understanding. Using machine learning to map objects in 3D is a complex tasks compared to 2D satellite or aerial views. We will show you how using terrestrial or indoors imagery from GeoCam you can fine tune and optimize your own AI models.

sessions.title: NASA Earth Science User Group Meeting
sessions.abstract: Join NASA Earth Science to discuss the geospatial utilization of Earth Observation data and learn how to enable science through GIS-ready data, services, tools, and applications. We will discuss current activities, upcoming initiatives, and foster community collaboration. Engagement opportunities will include hearing from existing, new, and potential users to help drive future efforts.

sessions.title: A Public Transit Playbook: Good Data, Better Technology, and Best Practices
sessions.abstract: By adopting a cohesive, location-focused enterprise strategy, transit agencies can elevate their service performance. Explore ways to improve transit systems in five key areas using a geographic approach: planning, operations, maintenance, customer experience, and employee and public safety. In this presentation, speakers showcase key technology, including geospatial artificial intelligence (GeoAI), as tools that help optimize decision-making and management of assets. Learn about the importance of geospatial competence and how to train transit staff to use spatial data effectively, empowering them to make informed choices.

Keywords: AI, GeoAI, enterprise, mobility, economic mobility, transit, public transit, transportation

sessions.title: Agentic Spatial Intelligence in Site Selection Workflows
sessions.abstract: What if your GIS answered questions instead of just displaying layers? Cotality delivers deep location intelligence—spanning parcels, hazard risks, and ownership—for the spatial agentic era. Via conversational interfaces and agents, we turn natural language into semantic insights for real estate, telecom, and more. This session showcases a site selection use case, displaying how agents resolve addresses, analyze environmental risks, & filter fields automatically. See how combining Cotality with Esri infrastructure shifts manual workflows to agentic ones, accelerating your strategic decisions.

sessions.title: ArcGIS API for Python: Administering ArcGIS Online
sessions.abstract: Want to automate common administration tasks? Join us to learn how to use ArcGIS API for Python to manage your organization with scripting. Discover common workflows for managing users and their credits and licenses, as well as managing groups and content. This presentation also provides some content migration strategies that you can implement.

RecommendedAudience: Basic experience with Python.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Connectors for Power Automate: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Take your automations beyond just notifications. The ArcGIS Connectors for Power Automate allow key spatial tasks to be set up as automated processes, all using a no-code, drag-and-drop experience. Learn about a few currently available connectors and functions that can enhance your process automations, and find out how to extend your flows with spatial recipes, including using create, read, update, and delete operations on feature layers.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Extending with the ArcGIS Enterprise SDK
sessions.abstract: Take your ArcGIS Enterprise deployment further with the ArcGIS Enterprise SDK. Explore tools for creating custom data feeds, implementing the new service interceptors, and building server object extensions (SOEs) and server object interceptors (SOIs). Gain insights into how these technologies can help you customize ArcGIS Enterprise to meet unique business needs and deliver innovative solutions.

Keywords: SDK, Custom Data Feeds
RecommendedAudience: Experience with ArcGIS Enterprise.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Integrating Cloud Services
sessions.abstract: Get connected! ArcGIS Enterprise supports connecting to and using a variety of services in several cloud environments, including cloud object storage like AWS S3, Azure Storage, and Google Cloud Storage, as well as cloud databases, like AWS Aurora, Azure SQL, and Google Cloud SQL. In this presentation, you will learn about these integration options, how they can benefit you, and how to get started.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Enterprise on Amazon Web Services
Keywords: Cloud, Cloud-native services, ArcGIS Enterprise
RecommendedAudience: Experience with ArcGIS Enterprise.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Experience Builder: Tips and Tricks
sessions.abstract: Discover the power of ArcGIS Experience Builder. Learn how to use URL parameters to zoom to features by attributes, change the table background, use ArcGIS Arcade expressions to display data dynamically, copy and paste features, filter multiple layers at once, create a single app for multiple audiences, deliver multilingual apps, and more.

Keywords: Tips, Tricks, Experience, ArcGIS Experience Builder, Tips and tricks, Arcade expression, Multilingual apps

sessions.title: ArcGIS Field Maps: What's New and the Road Ahead
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Field Maps brings mapping, data capture, and task coordination together in one app for field operations. It keeps mobile teams connected with up-to-date information for situational awareness and offers powerful yet simple ways to collect accurate data. For the office, it enables workflow design, customization, and assignment management. In this presentation, explore the latest enhancements and what’s next to improve efficiency across your field workflows.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Hub: An Overview
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Hub brings people together by creating websites where communities can share, collaborate, and engage. Explore ArcGIS Hub's capabilities, learn about practical examples, and discover how to create geo-enabled websites without custom coding. Share trusted and curated content, foster collaboration, and actively engage your community of practice. Hub extends your community by giving stakeholders access to ArcGIS so that they may participate in projects, events, discussions, and other Hub-enabled activities.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript: Building Agentic Mapping Applications
sessions.abstract: Meet the new JavaScript Maps SDK assistant! Learn how to use the new Assistant web component in the SDK to build agentic mapping applications, including how to get started and how to extend it to use your own agents.

Keywords: AI, Assistant, SDK, Agent
RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with the ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Notebooks: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Are you familiar with ArcGIS Notebooks and how to use it to automate your GIS workflows? ArcGIS Notebooks is a Jupyter notebook experience that is integrated within ArcGIS. This presentation highlights the capabilities of ArcGIS Notebooks in ArcGIS Enterprise and ArcGIS Online. Discover how you can apply notebooks toward your administrative, content management, and analytical workflows.

RecommendedAudience: Basic experience with Python.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise: Administering Your Organization
sessions.abstract: Manage your ArcGIS environment efficiently. This presentation explores ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise organizations, including management of members and shared content using groups and folders. Learn to customize your organization's home page, set the default basemap and extent, and configure security settings. Also, receive guidance about resource usage, credit consumption, and allocation among members.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise: Managing Large Organizations
sessions.abstract: Managing large organizations in ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise can present unique challenges for administrators. Join us to discover how you can efficiently manage content and resources by using built-in tools, optimizing user and license management workflows, and configuring organization and portal settings for enhanced management capabilities. Also, learn how you can automate these processes to make administration even easier.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro Editing: Tips and Tricks
sessions.abstract: Do you want to learn how to maximize the use of editing tools, templates, and guides and start using them to their fullest potential? The topics in this presentation are focused on configuring the ArcGIS Pro editing options to suit your project needs, authoring editing templates to streamline data creation, and how to set up tool options to support your workflows. Speakers discuss how to customize the UI and also provide shortcuts to help increase productivity.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with editing in ArcGIS Pro and the geodatabase.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Creating Vector Tiles
sessions.abstract: Want to create vector tiles using ArcGIS Pro? This presentation provides an overview of this process in ArcGIS. Learn how to author a map for efficient vector tile creation by using scale-based symbology, alternate symbols, and display filters. Discover how to create and publish vector tiles as well as how to edit vector tile styles.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Getting the Most from the Maplex Label Engine
sessions.abstract: The Maplex Label Engine can help you produce high-quality cartographic output. Join us to learn about this ArcGIS Pro functionality, which can optimize the positions of labels to make your maps look great. This presentation includes practical use cases for various label placement techniques, along with tips about label expressions, text formatting tags, and workflows that maximize the Maplex Label Engine's capabilities to create superior maps.

Keywords: labelling
RecommendedAudience: Experience labeling maps in ArcGIS Pro.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Roads and Highways: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Join us to learn how ArcGIS Roads and Highways is used to manage and edit roadway network centerline data and roadway characteristic and asset data on a spatial linear referencing system (LRS). In this presentation, get a product overview along with explanations of supported roadway data editing workflows, linear referenced event behaviors, event characteristic editing, geoprocessing tools to support data transformation and analysis, and a REST API to extend LRS capabilities across the enterprise.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with linear referencing.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Spatial Analyst: Understanding Terrain Using Surface Analysis Tools
sessions.abstract: Terrain analysis is key to many workflows. Join us to learn how to use the ArcGIS Spatial Analyst Surface toolset to gain valuable insights from terrain data—from topographic characteristics to landform classification. In this presentation, learn the principles, use cases, and ways to use the Surface toolset in terrain analysis through various examples such as river morphology, feature detection, and more.

sessions.title: ArcGIS StoryMaps: What’s New and the Road Ahead
sessions.abstract: Find out about the newest available features in ArcGIS StoryMaps. Learn about improved authoring tools, mobile optimization, templates, AI-assisted writing, and expanded capabilities for the ArcGIS StoryMaps Briefings app. This presentation also previews upcoming releases.

Keywords: What’s new, roadmap, AI
RecommendedAudience: Experience with ArcGIS StoryMaps.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Survey123: Best Practices for Public Data Collection Surveys
sessions.abstract: Creating a form for public data collection requires many considerations: Who is your audience? What language(s) do they speak? How will they interact with the form? How do you keep the submitted data secure?  Discover how you can address these concerns when creating your next public survey.

Keywords: public survey

sessions.title: ArcGIS Utility Network: Migration Strategies
sessions.abstract: Discover practical strategies and tools to migrate to ArcGIS Utility Network. This presentation highlights Esri’s Migration Toolset and the Utility Network Migration Wizard, explores different implementation options—from quick transitions to fully configured advanced data models—and examines the balance between cost, configuration effort, and scalability to help organizations plan successful migrations.

RecommendedAudience: Basic understanding of geodatabase and utility network concepts.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Web Editor: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Explore ArcGIS Web Editor, a new app in ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise. Web Editor is a focused data editing app for simple, advanced, and domain-specific editing workflows. Using Web Editor, GIS professionals can now work in a web browser, supplementing their desktop editing. Share configurations with domain and knowledge workers to help empower them with self-service editing and confidence. Learn about the various Web Editor capabilities and upcoming plans for the app.

sessions.title: ArcGIS as a Project Portfolio Management System
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS can revolutionize your project management by offering a unified view across your entire project portfolio. This presentation demonstrates how ArcGIS integrates seamlessly with enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, schedules, and critical design and planning data, keeping all stakeholders informed with real-time insights. Discover how ArcGIS supports every phase of the project life cycle—from early planning through execution and monitoring—enhancing collaboration and empowering smarter, data-driven decisions.

Keywords:
ERP Systems
project lifecycle management

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Extract, Transform, and Load Patterns
sessions.abstract: Many tools ingest different data types into the ArcGIS system. There are also many ways to transform that data while it is loading. Explore the overall principles and specific examples of extract, transform, and load (ETL) techniques using the ArcGIS Data Interoperability extension, the ArcGIS Data Pipelines app (a software-as-a-service option), and Python notebooks (in ArcGIS Pro and ArcGIS Online). This presentation is ideal for those who are comfortable with coded workflows.

Keywords: FME, Data Interoperability, ETL

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Multi-INT Fusion at the Edge
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS creates a geospatial decision environment that is used across the intelligence enterprise. Real-time sensor feeds, operational data, and mission context are brought together into a shared operational view. By serving as the authoritative geospatial layer across systems, ArcGIS enables better collaboration, clearer insights, and faster decisions.

Keywords: Geospatial decision environment, Common operational picture, Intelligence enterprise, Real-time sensor integration, File knowledge graphi

sessions.title: ArcGIS: The Best Coordinate Systems for Spatial Analysis
sessions.abstract: Coordinate systems can make or break your spatial analysis. This presentation aims to uncomplicate this complex subject. Speakers explain how to get the most accurate results when performing spatial analysis. Topics include when to use geodesic or planar calculations of distance and area, when to use ellipsoidal or gravity-related heights, and which projections are appropriate for analysis, data storage, and correctly (and creatively) mapping your results.

RecommendedAudience: Experience with coordinate systems in ArcGIS (or attended the introductory technical session).

sessions.title: Best Practices for Capturing Drone Imagery
sessions.abstract: Learn best practices for capturing drone imagery with ArcGIS Flight so that you can create high-quality 2D and 3D mapping products in ArcGIS Reality, as well as capture and create geospatial video. Discover the flight planning essentials, and review the challenges that a drone pilot might encounter and how to address them. Speakers guide you through a complete planning and capture scenario using ArcGIS Flight.

Keywords: Drones/UAS
RecommendedAudience: Attendees with all skill levels are welcomed.

sessions.title: Bridging the Gap: GIS Solutions for Stakeholder Engagement and Collaboration
sessions.abstract: Stakeholder engagement is essential to the success of utility projects. In this session presenters will share how they have used GIS to clearly communicate to communities they serve as well as seamlessly share information with stakeholders across various organizations. Attend this session to hear how ArcGIS is being used to provide a visual narrative format for utility projects and streamline data sharing and project planning.

Keywords: Infrastructure planning, Utility coordination, Geospatial storytelling,

sessions.title: Building Trusted AI Solutions with ArcGIS
sessions.abstract: Trust is critical for adopting AI in geospatial decision-making. This insight talk highlights the key considerations behind responsible AI in ArcGIS, from the AI Code of Conduct, AI baseline reviews, and AI transparency cards to life-cycle regulatory controls, and how to support transparency in AI-driven workflows.

Keywords: data readiness, AI preparation, data quality, automation, structured data
RecommendedAudience: Data preparation, data quality assurance, AI integration readiness

sessions.title: Climate Action: Data, Tools, and Workflows
sessions.abstract: Join us as we bring together climate thought leaders and GIS professional who are using the geographic approach to unite people, empower positive change, and build a more sustainable future for all. In this demo theater presentation, speakers showcase some of the most useful climate data, tools, and workflows that are available as well as discuss use cases and capabilities from the government space.

occurrences.room: GIS for Good Demo Expo Theater

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World

sessions.title: Design Better ArcGIS Systems with the ArcGIS Architecture Center
sessions.abstract: Designing and optimizing ArcGIS systems is complex, but you do not have to start from scratch. Test studies from the ArcGIS Architecture Center can guide improvements for performance, scalability, and the user experience. Discover how to evaluate your current practices, apply well-architected principles, and make informed design decisions to build more efficient, reliable ArcGIS systems.

RecommendedAudience: Experience with system architecture and design.

sessions.title: Enterprise Geodatabase: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Curious about multiuser enterprise geodatabases? Join us to get an overview of multiuser enterprise geodatabases, including the basics of creating an enterprise geodatabase, loading and securing data, editing, and performing essential administration tasks.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with the geodatabase information model.

sessions.title: Enterprise Geodatabase: Demonstrating Branch Versioning Workflows
sessions.abstract: Interested in branch versioning? This presentation explores versioning management workflows related to branch versioning using ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Enterprise, and the REST API. Join us to see the following workflows (and more) in action: creating and deleting versions, editing named versions, changing version ownership, reconciling, and posting versions.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with ArcGIS Pro and enterprise geodatabases.

sessions.title: Esri YPN: Career Clinic for Young Professionals
sessions.abstract: This session is an interactive session designed to help early-career GIS professionals navigate common career questions with confidence. Attendees will engage in focused group discussions on topics like résumés, networking, skill-building, and career direction, while learning directly from peers and professionals who have walked similar paths. This session emphasizes practical advice, open conversation, and real-world insight—giving participants clear next steps they can use immediately in their careers.

sessions.title: Flood Readiness & Habitat Protection with ArcGIS: Risk & Levee Monitoring
sessions.abstract: State and local agencies face growing challenges as climate change intensifies flood hazards and regulatory requirements increase for environmental protection. This session highlights how the New Jersey Department of Environmental Protection (NJDEP) and the Kings River Conservation District (KRCD) are using ArcGIS technologies to modernize water related planning, monitoring, and compliance.

Keywords: flood risk, riparian flood

sessions.title: From Chaos to Clarity: Transforming USAR Operations with AI and GIS
sessions.abstract: Explore how fire and emergency medical services (EMS) teams use GIS and AI for urban search and rescue (USAR) operations during major disasters. This presentation demonstrates real-world examples from recent major incidents, where Esri tools enabled rapid mapping, resource tracking, and AI-powered damage assessment. Learn how USAR teams use drone imagery, real-time dashboards, and AI assistants to accelerate rescue, improve situational awareness, and save lives.

Keywords: Fire, EMS, GeoAI, AI, ML

sessions.title: Gas Utility GIS Mobility Innovation
sessions.abstract: Join this presentation to hear GASMIG discuss how it reduced costs, streamlined decision-making, improved coordination, and increased efficiency by implementing a new field data-collection process, integrated with ArcGIS Utility Network, to monitor construction activities in real time through dashboards and other capabilities. Then Enbridge Gas Distribution will discuss its systems-based, integrated Field GIS solution that links planning, execution, and records across its work orders, CIS, and GIS systems.

sessions.title: GeoAI for Emergency Management and Damage Assessments
sessions.abstract: AI and machine learning (ML) are transforming how emergency managers use imagery to prepare for and respond to disasters. Speakers showcase how AI and ML accelerate the processing and analysis of imagery datasets, enabling faster, more accurate insights on both blue-sky and gray-sky days. Learn how these technologies support rapid damage assessment, situational awareness, and mitigation planning, with an emphasis on the critical role of a strong imagery foundation in ensuring that your data is well organized, current, and accessible.

sessions.title: Geocoding: What’s New
sessions.abstract: Curious about the latest advancements in ArcGIS geocoding? Learn about topics such as geocoding with ArcGIS Pro and ArcGIS Online, interactive geocoding, batch geocoding, address rematching, custom locators, and new features. Also, get recommendations for extending your learning with workshops and sessions that go into more detail on the topics presented here.

Keywords: Geocoding, Locators, Addresses
RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with ArcGIS Pro.

sessions.title: Geospatial Foundation Models and Embeddings
sessions.abstract: Geospatial foundation models offer new ways to learn from large, diverse, and often unlabeled geospatial datasets. This presentation explores how geospatial artificial intelligence (GeoAI) is evolving to integrate foundation models into analytical workflows. Topics include remote sensing foundation models, location embeddings, and geospatial vision-language models, with an overview of how Esri is working in this space. Speakers also discuss how embeddings work in geospatial analysis and the potential implications for environmental, urban, and other application areas.

Keywords: artificial intelligence, AI, GeoAI, analysis, analytics, data science, embeddings, foundation models

sessions.title: Geospatial Strategy: Advanced Topics and Best Practices
sessions.abstract: Take your organization’s geospatial strategy beyond the basics. This technical session explores advanced techniques for aligning GIS initiatives with core business objectives and delivering measurable outcomes. Learn how to translate strategic vision into actionable plans, prioritize investments, and apply proven frameworks to ensure success. Speakers share practical examples of applying strategy in real-world scenarios, from defining key performance indicators (KPIs) to scaling solutions across the enterprise, so you can turn vision into impact.

Keywords: Strategic vision, actionable plans, investment prioritization, proven frameworks, KPI definition, scaling solutions, enterprise alignment, measurable outcomes, best practices, impact delivery

sessions.title: How to Build a Data-First Organization
sessions.abstract: A data first approach is transforming how governments solve problems, streamline operations, and engage communities. From Austin’s GeoHub—where open data becomes actionable maps and stories—to São Paulo’s DataSampa that integrates environmental, social, mobility, and infrastructure data into dashboards, analyses, and reports; these examples show how GIS breaks down silos, accelerates decisions, and builds more transparent, connected, and efficient digital government.

sessions.title: How to Guide Equitable Public Health Policy
sessions.abstract: Discover how two organizations are strengthening equitable public health planning through informed investment and improved preparedness. Washington State’s updated Environmental Health Disparities Map improves access, sustainability, and community input for identifying disproportionately burdened neighborhoods. City of Austin’s predictive GIS tool maps future service deserts using spatial statistics and hazard models to guide equitable emergency planning.

sessions.title: Increase Transparency with Your Community
sessions.abstract: Does your community know what developments are happening? Do they understand the intricacies of long term plans? Discover how to create public-facing web apps that can keep residents up-to-date on what's going on in their community.

Keywords: development, engagement, comprehensive plan
RecommendedAudience: urban planners, community leaders, developers

sessions.title: Map Viewer: Creating Beautiful Web Maps
sessions.abstract: Want to make high-quality web maps with Map Viewer? In this presentation, speakers teach skills related to layer symbology, basemaps, pop-ups, labeling, visual hierarchy, data attribution, blend modes, effects, and other design concepts.

Keywords: design principles, visual hierarchy, web maps, cartography, layouts, user engagement, map clarity, storytelling with maps

sessions.title: Next-Gen Utility Management: See It, Track It, Optimize It
sessions.abstract: Discover how leading electric utilities are leveraging ArcGIS to drive operational excellence and business value. First, see how TVA is revolutionizing environmental compliance with stunning 3D digital twins in ArcGIS Online. Then explore how MEA is transforming distribution operations with real-time fleet tracking for its innovative PowerBank mobile battery storage project, thereby boosting operational resilience and customer reliability.

Keywords: 3D

sessions.title: Predictive Analysis: Modeling Data Using Statistics and Machine Learning
sessions.abstract: Modeling helps uncover patterns to explain events and predict values and occurrences. This presentation reviews random forest, XGBoost, and methods to evaluate prediction models. Explore the Presence-Only Prediction tool, which uses a maximum entropy approach to predict the location of phenomena with only known presence data. Finally, learn about a method for estimating time until an event occurs. Throughout this presentation, speakers emphasize making these methods accessible through clear, conceptual explanations.

sessions.title: Putting GIS to Work in 911 for Improved Situational Awareness
sessions.abstract: GIS has long supported 911 telecommunicators and first responders by improving situational awareness. As technology evolves, tools like indoor maps and real-time common operating pictures enhance insight and response. Learn how accurate GIS data is built and maintained, and explore real-world examples of how modern GIS helps save lives and protect property through timely, detailed information.

sessions.title: Real-Time GIS: The Road Ahead
sessions.abstract: Discover the future of real-time GIS at Esri. With ArcGIS Velocity available for ArcGIS Enterprise on Windows and Linux, organizations can now deploy real-time analyses in self-hosted environments. This presentation highlights new capabilities, deployment options, and the product road map. Speakers also discuss the future of ArcGIS GeoEvent Server and how to plan the transition to ArcGIS Velocity.

Keywords: real-time GIS roadmap, ArcGIS Velocity for Enterprise, Velocity deployment, real-time roadmap, future of real-time, GeoEvent Server future, GeoEvent migration, real-time strategy, Enterprise deployment options, hybrid deployment, real-time modernization, system architecture, roadmap session, Esri roadmap

sessions.title: The Path to Innovative Park Planning and Operations
sessions.abstract: Parks and recreation departments are modernizing to meet evolving community needs. This presentation demonstrates how real-time data, digital twins, and 3D design transform park planning, operations, and engagement. Learn how your peers uncover hidden opportunities, advance equity, and design parks that promote health, economic vitality, and resilience—turning parks into essential assets for connected, thriving communities.

Keywords: parks, recreation, public grounds, facilities, facility management, digital twin, real-time, climate, resilience, operations, maintenance, equity, asset management, 3D

sessions.title: Trends in State and Local Government That Shape Your Work and the GIS Profession
sessions.abstract: The landscape of government work is always changing—are you prepared for the future? Outside forces can redefine how you deliver value. This presentation reviews three trend categories that matter the most: technology innovations such as AI, machine learning, Internet of Things (IoT), and digital twins; legislative and policy shifts that affect funding and unfunded mandates; and government challenges, such as economic mobility, housing, and infrastructure. Speakers discuss how addressing these three trends can elevate the visibility and impact of your GIS work.

Keywords: trends, government, legislative, policy, infrastructure, economic mobility, AI, ML, digital twin, IoT, internet of things

sessions.title: Understanding Community Needs through a Geographic Approach
sessions.abstract: Using GIS and Statistics to better understand critical issues facing society today. In this session, we will look at two key issues that focus on health indicators and use GIS and statistics to better understand interventions for improved community outcomes.

Keywords: health indicators, statistics, community, interventions, GIS, population

sessions.title: Unifying Data to Turn a Nonprofit's Mission into Action
sessions.abstract: Explore how two organizations use GIS to unify complex datasets, standardize field information, and guide more equitable investments. From assessing park access to tracking affordable housing progress, both projects rely on GIS to integrate surveys, spatial data, and real‑time updates into clear, actionable insights. This session ensures attendees learn repeatable methods to strengthen data quality, streamline workflows, and use evidence‑based mapping to prioritize resources and improve community outcomes.

Keywords: parks, housing, nonprofit

sessions.title: Web Apps: Accessibility Features, Tips, and Tricks
sessions.abstract: Designing accessible web apps ensures that all audiences can easily interact with and understand the information that you want to share. This presentation demonstrates the capabilities of ArcGIS Experience Builder, ArcGIS Instant Apps, and other useful ArcGIS web app builders. Join us to learn about optimizing support for screen readers, keyboard navigation, and other assistive technologies. Also, learn about testing for accessibility and the tools and techniques that can help you develop inclusive app experiences.

Keywords: Accessibility, A11y, Section 508, 508, Web Content Accessibility Guidelines, WCAG, Americans with Disabilities Act, ADA, EN 301 549,

sessions.title: ArcGIS Configurable Apps: Cloud Migration Patterns
sessions.abstract: Migrating configurable apps to the cloud—whether software as a service (SaaS) or hybrid—requires careful planning and proven patterns. This brief presentation introduces key migration strategies for ArcGIS configurable apps, including considerations for architecture, data flow, and security. Learn how to choose the right deployment model, avoid common issues, and ensure a smooth transition that supports scalability and performance.

Keywords: loud migration, SaaS, hybrid deployment, scalability, performance optimization
RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with cloud architecture, migration planning, SaaS and hybrid deployment strategies, and security considerations.

sessions.title: Strengthening Nonprofit and Government Partnerships
sessions.abstract: Nonprofits fill critical gaps where government resources cannot fully reach, providing services and advocating for causes that affect vulnerable communities. Join us to find out how GIS can create a powerful bridge between nonprofits and governments, enabling collaboration, data-driven decision-making, and targeted outreach to at-risk populations. This brief presentation will equip you with strategies to use GIS to create stronger partnerships and more effective community impacts.

occurrences.room: State and Local Government Expo Theater

Keywords: nonprofit, foundation, equity, policy,

sessions.title: First American Data & Analytics Geospatial Property Intelligence for ArcGIS
sessions.abstract: First American Data & Analytics is one of the largest property datasets added to ArcGIS and introduced to the Esri ecosystem. Powered by the industry's strongest property data foundation—with #1 positions across ownership, parcel boundaries, title, transactions, HOA, active property data, and document images—it helps teams move beyond parcel basics. This session shows how ownership, precise address, unit-address, building, land, and transaction context delivered via Feature Services and web feature layers improve site selection, land and infrastructure planning, and map-based decisions.
tracks: National Mapping, Navigation, and Geospatial Authorities

Topic: National Mapping, Navigation, and Geospatial Authorities

sessions.title: Esri Awards Ceremony
sessions.abstract: Join us as we celebrate excellence, innovation, and impact across the global GIS community at the Esri User Conference Awards Ceremony. This special event honors individuals, organizations, and teams whose work demonstrates the transformative power of GIS in addressing real world challenges.

sessions.title: ArcGIS API for Python: Working with Web and Mobile Apps
sessions.abstract: Discover the fundamentals of using the ArcGIS API for Python's .apps module, with a focus on essential concepts for working with web and mobile applications. Explore the potential and constraints of automation within web and mobile apps, highlighting opportunities for streamlining workflows. Also, speakers explain how integrating the Python API enhances existing app-based processes, offering insights into optimizing efficiency and functionality.

RecommendedAudience: Experience writing Python scripts using the ArcGIS API for Python.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Business Analyst: Getting Started for State and Local Governments
sessions.abstract: Make data-driven decisions by combining advanced mapping, analytics, and authoritative demographic, economic, and community data. Find out how your organization can evaluate community needs, identify service gaps, analyze economic conditions, and better visualize the local business environment to understand who they serve and where resources are needed most. Learn how intuitive tools such as site suitability analysis and information products such as infographics can support informed and strategic decision-making across government departments.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Data Reviewer: Improving Data Quality for Water Utilities
sessions.abstract: Learn how to implement ArcGIS Data Reviewer to support validation of water features in the ArcGIS Utility Network. This presentation highlights additional methods and implementation best practices for enhancing the validation capability in the utility network. Speakers also share valuable QA/QC (quality assurance/quality control) recommendations for migration to the ArcGIS Utility Network.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with ArcGIS Utility Network and the foundational ArcGIS Solutions templates is recommended.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Editing Across Environments with Distributed Collaboration
sessions.abstract: Are you interested in seamlessly sharing maps, apps, layers, and more with other organizations? Distributed collaboration is based on a foundation of trust between participating organizations and is motivated by common goals and initiatives that support data access and sharing. These collaborations are useful for many workflows, including providing ArcGIS Enterprise content to the public through ArcGIS Online, making data visible across different departments in an organization, and managing field data collections.

Keywords: Editing, Distributed Collaboration
RecommendedAudience: Knowledge of ArcGIS Enterprise or ArcGIS Online.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Observability
sessions.abstract: Observability is key to a resilient ArcGIS Enterprise. This presentation introduces core concepts and patterns for Portal for ArcGIS, ArcGIS Server, and ArcGIS Data Store. Practical examples with the latest features and tools for monitoring and diagnosing your deployments are showcased. Learn how to boost operational awareness and explore upcoming improvements and best practices for high-performing environments.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Enterprise on Amazon Web Services
Keywords: Observability, logging, ArcGIS Enterprise
RecommendedAudience: Experience with ArcGIS Enterprise.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Printable Reports from ArcGIS Web Applications
sessions.abstract: Learn how to generate reports and maps from the ArcGIS Enterprise print service. Find out how to configure layout and report templates in ArcGIS Pro, share your print service with reporting capabilities or as portal items, and use the service in Map Viewer and ArcGIS Experience Builder. Speakers share how to include attributes, attachments, related records, and more in reports or on layouts and discuss new ArcGIS Online enhancements, including custom layout templates. The ArcPy-based print service for advanced workflows is also briefly discussed.

Keywords: Printing, reports, ArcGIS Enterprise
RecommendedAudience: Knowledge of ArcGIS Enterprise.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Field Maps: Optimizing Utility Network Workflows for the Field
sessions.abstract: Support for utility networks in the field has expanded, enabling teams to view, edit, and perform traces—even without a connection. Learn how to optimize utility network experiences in the field to support common workflows such as locating assets, capturing and documenting assets with high precision, and managing inspections. Gain insights into upcoming enhancements that will further support utility workflows.

Keywords: utility network
RecommendedAudience: Experience with utility networks.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World: Ready-to-Use Imagery for GeoAI
sessions.abstract: Acquiring, hosting, and managing aerial and satellite imagery might not be feasible for many organizations. Manually extracting information from imagery can be time-consuming and expensive. AI and deep learning can help save time and money through automation, but not everyone can train their own models. With ready-to-use imagery layers and pretrained deep learning models, ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World provides everything that you need to extract information and insights from imagery.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World
Keywords: Imagery, Landsat, Analysis, ArcGIS Living Atlas

sessions.title: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World: Tips and Tricks
sessions.abstract: Are you using ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World to its full potential? With ArcGIS Living Atlas, you have access to spatial content that is available for use in ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Pro, among other common GIS workflows. Join us to learn some tips and tricks to implement in your work when using ArcGIS Living Atlas. Go beyond the basics to customize layers, maps, and apps for your needs. Gain best practices for improving your existing workflows, both analytically and cartographically. Save time and kick-start your GIS workflows by using the power of ArcGIS Living Atlas and the Geographic Approach.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise: Automating Data Updates
sessions.abstract: The number of hosted feature layers is at an all-time high, and many of these layers are frequently updated from local data sources such as comma-separated value (CSV) files or file geodatabases. Learn how to automate data updates using the ArcGIS API for Python. Speakers discuss how to create a notebook that appends a file geodatabase to an existing hosted feature layer, enabling you to add new records and update existing data.

RecommendedAudience: Experience creating and managing hosted feature layers.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise: Visualizing Change over Time on the Web
sessions.abstract: Elevate your time-based visualization skills. In this demo theater presentation, explore various data profiles and techniques for effectively displaying extensive datasets both spatially and temporally. Whether you are a novice or experienced user, you will gain valuable knowledge for enhancing your web-based data visualizations and making complex information accessible and engaging for your audience.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro Editing: Managing Feature Attributes and Related Data
sessions.abstract: Want to elevate your editing game? Learn best practices for managing feature attributes and related data for new and existing features using the ArcGIS Pro editor. Speakers also discuss topics such as configuring feature templates, working with relationships, using attribute rules, and applying contingent attribute values.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with editing in ArcGIS Pro and working with attributes.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: 3D Tips and Tricks
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Pro offers numerous ways to create effective, inspiring 3D views. Learn common tips and tricks when working with properties, such as illumination, vertical exaggeration, and texturing, and know when to use local scenes versus global scenes. Discover how to access and configure relevant properties. Explore how to use advanced symbology and shadows to make scenes appear realistic, as well as simple ways to create, add, and edit 3D models.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: An Introduction to ModelBuilder
sessions.abstract: Automate your geoprocessing workflows with ModelBuilder in ArcGIS Pro. Join us to learn how ModelBuilder can be used to create automated workflows by adding data and tools and connecting them or creating workflows from an existing project history. This presentation introduces beginners to the model diagram, types of elements and tools, connections, and best practices to organize and document analysis workflows. Speakers also discuss migration best practices, editing model schema, debugging methods, working through model reports, and efficiency tips that can help you to be more productive.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Configuring Parcel Management
sessions.abstract: Want to simplify parcel workflows without custom code? This brief presentation shows how ArcGIS Pro and the parcel fabric empower organizations to configure rules, topology, and editing tools to meet business needs efficiently. Learn how configuration accelerates deployment, reduces maintenance, and supports evolving requirements—all while maintaining data integrity.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with parcel fabric configuration, topology rules, editing workflows, and data integrity practices.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: No-Code Customization Options to Boost Your Productivity
sessions.abstract: Increase your productivity with built-in capabilities like Command Search, the Quick Access Toolbar, and contextual keyboard shortcuts. Discover simple ways to customize the ArcGIS Pro interface, including the ribbon, to improve the efficiency of your workflows.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Roads and Highways: What's New and the Road Ahead
sessions.abstract: Buckle up for this presentation about the newest features and capabilities of ArcGIS Roads and Highways for ArcGIS Pro and ArcGIS Enterprise. Also, speakers share a preview of what's coming in future releases.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with ArcGIS Roads and Highways.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions: Sewer and Stormwater Solutions
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Solutions help stormwater agencies maintain stormwater systems and comply with government regulations. Join us for an overview of this collection of solutions and learn how you can streamline asset inventories, routinely inspect stormwater assets, and share essential information with stakeholders.

sessions.title: ArcGIS for SharePoint: Going Spatial
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS for SharePoint enables the integration of maps, spatial data, and GIS functionality into SharePoint sites and applications. Explore how to convert SharePoint lists into point, polyline, or polygon maps and how to import ArcGIS data. The presentation covers functions such as geotagging, geosearch, and EXIF data extraction. Learn about the latest updates, including the ability to geotag by dragging documents to a map, adding document links to layers that are shared with ArcGIS, and creating, editing, and deleting features within ArcGIS for SharePoint.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Creating and Using Web Tools
sessions.abstract: Join us to learn how to build, share, and use web tools that enable you to run custom analytics services in ArcGIS web, desktop, and mobile apps. Web tools start as geoprocessing tools in ArcGIS Pro or as notebooks or analysis models in ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise. This presentation provides best practices for configuring geoprocessing services, accessing data, and handling Python library dependencies, as well as tips for making web tools easy to use, including the use of validation code and scheduling a web tool.

RecommendedAudience: Experience with creating custom geoprocessing models or script tools, sharing data to ArcGIS Enterprise, and using Python.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Designing Thematic Maps
sessions.abstract: There is rarely a right or wrong way to map empirical data, but thematic maps can look very different as a result of your design decisions. Join us to learn how thematic map types can shape the message of your map. This presentation conveys how well-known maps and map types can be reinvented by using alternative thematic techniques. Learn how to use ArcGIS Pro mapping renderers effectively and explore workflows using ArcGIS Maps for Adobe Creative Cloud.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World
ArcGIS Maps for Adobe Creative Cloud

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Hydrologic Analysis and Floodplain Delineation
sessions.abstract: Performing flood and hydrologic analysis in ArcGIS involves key tools and workflows. Learn the principles behind the hydrologic tools in ArcGIS Spatial Analyst and the Arc Hydro toolbox to create various hydrologic datasets for hydro landscape characterization and refine data for better flood forecasting. Explore hydrologic and hydraulic modeling, delineate floodplain and map future flood risk in ArcGIS, and customize web applications for flood mapping.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Managing Crime Reduction Strategies
sessions.abstract: There is often a disconnect between the work that analysts do when they identify crime trends and hot spots and how that analysis is applied to police operations. In this demo theater presentation, speakers discuss how ArcGIS is used to identify, plan, monitor, and evaluate crime reduction strategies. Using the Crime Problem Management solution, police personnel can interactively create patrol areas, design the tactical plan and assign officers to problem areas, monitor ongoing police activities, and, finally, evaluate outcomes of any place-based policing strategy.

Keywords: police, law enforcement, evidence-based policing, hot spot policing

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Metadata for Sharing, Discovery, and AI Readiness
sessions.abstract: Metadata—the information about your data—is essential for content discovery, geospatial data reuse, governance, and AI readiness. Discover how metadata is managed across ArcGIS and explore strategies to simplify and unify the experience. This presentation introduces tools and workflows for creating and maintaining metadata in ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Enterprise, ArcGIS Online, and ArcGIS Hub. Speakers share best practices, demonstrate end-to-end workflows, and showcase metadata’s role in sharing, discovery, and AI readiness.

RecommendedAudience: Basic understanding of ArcGIS products and metadata concepts.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Supporting Intelligence and Investigations
sessions.abstract: The intelligence cycle is smarter with GIS. Learn how defense and civilian intelligence, law enforcement, and private-sector professionals use GIS tools for data integration, analysis, visualization, and dissemination. Uncover patterns, trends, and relationships with advanced analysis techniques such as temporal analysis, link analysis, imagery analysis, and other key workflows to create dynamic information products, including 2D and 3D maps, imagery, timelines, graphs, link charts, and more.

Keywords: Intelligence, Law Enforcement, Investigations
RecommendedAudience: Knowledge of intelligence and desktop analysis.

sessions.title: Cloud-Native Georelational Data Distribution
sessions.abstract: Analytic workflows gain a major boost when data feels local to a powerful client like ArcGIS Pro. Today’s “serverless” technologies make that possible—allowing direct access to cloud-resident files as if they were part of a high-performance, spatially enabled database. Learn how to query data in cloud object stores or from URLs using SQL, define dynamic views, and even perform time travel by exploring historical datasets when periodic updates are archived through advanced data models.

Keywords: cloud native, geoparquet, python, data formats, SQL, database

sessions.title: Demystifying Blending Modes
sessions.abstract: Blending modes are powerful cartographic tools, but how they work and what they do are not always clear. This presentation equips you with methods in ArcGIS Pro and Map Viewer that help you design workflows to enhance satellite imagery, mask or highlight features, visualize data, and even simulate lighting effects.

Keywords: Map Viewer, cartography, visualization

sessions.title: Digital Twins for Event Planning and Emergency Management
sessions.abstract: Move beyond static models to integrated systems that deliver real-time insights. Explore how external data sources—such as weather, sensors, and operational platforms—combine with GIS to create dynamic environments that support rapid decision-making. Speakers share proven patterns for connecting existing systems and automating updates, ensuring that digital twins remain accurate and actionable throughout operations. Public safety and infrastructure examples show how these solutions scale from planning to live response without requiring 3D expertise.

Keywords: Digital Twins, 3D GIS, Real time, Emergency management

sessions.title: Enhancing ArcGIS for Power BI with the ArcGIS Connectors for Power Automate
sessions.abstract: Level up your GIS power with ArcGIS for Power BI. Learn how to create flows using your ArcGIS organizational services in Power Automate and work with both Power BI data and ArcGIS data. Find out about some of the latest features, including updating ArcGIS data symbology and joining data in Power BI to ArcGIS data. Discover how you can create automated processes with ArcGIS Connectors for Power Automate, all with no-code, drag-and-drop tools for both desktop and cloud flows.

sessions.title: Enterprise Geodatabase: Multiuser Editing with Feature Services
sessions.abstract: Be sure not to miss this overview of multiuser editing in enterprise geodatabases, including branch versioning and nonversioned archiving. Explore setting up data for short and long transactional editing, sharing data with the organization, and common editing workflows.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with the geodatabase information model.

sessions.title: Geodatabases: Generating Schema Reports
sessions.abstract: Are you looking to analyze, refine, or document your geodatabase designs? In this technical session, speakers show you how to do just that using the Generate Schema Report tool. Find out firsthand how this tool can provide a comprehensive overview of the geodatabase structure and serve as a useful data-modeling tool for identifying areas of improvement in your geodatabase design.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with ArcGIS Pro and geodatabases.

sessions.title: Geospatial Change Management: Preparing Your Organization for GIS
sessions.abstract: Whether you are implementing ArcGIS for the first time or expanding GIS access to new departments, success relies on people embracing your solutions. Attend this highly interactive technical session to discover strategies that will increase the pace of adoption, the number of people using the solutions that you configure, and the skill level of GIS users. Understand how to effectively communicate business value and learn about creative ways to spark interest in GIS capabilities across business units.

Keywords: Adoption strategies, stakeholder engagement, business value communication, organizational readiness, change management framework, user onboarding, cross-department collaboration, GIS expansion, training programs, cultural transformation
RecommendedAudience: Understanding of organizational change-management principles, familiarity with GIS adoption strategies, ability to communicate business value, and experience with stakeholder engagement.

sessions.title: Imagery in ArcGIS: What's New with Deep Learning
sessions.abstract: Stay up to date on the latest deep learning advancements in ArcGIS. This presentation highlights new capabilities in ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Enterprise, ArcGIS Online, and the ArcGIS API for Python that make deep learning faster and more accessible. Understand how ArcGIS applies modern AI to satellite, aerial, drone, video, and scientific data, and explore new tools and workflows that streamline information extraction and automated analysis across your organization.

sessions.title: Immersive Mapping: The Power of 3D in ArcGIS
sessions.abstract: Step beyond traditional maps and discover how 3D transforms the way that we understand and interact with spatial data. This presentation explores the full spectrum of 3D capabilities across ArcGIS—from visualization and analysis to integration with BIM, lidar, and digital twins. Learn about practical techniques for creating immersive experiences, uncovering insights only possible in 3D, and sharing content seamlessly across web and mobile. Whether you are planning cities, managing infrastructure, or building smart environments, find out how 3D can elevate GIS workflows and decision-making.

RecommendedAudience: Interest in 3D capabilities across ArcGIS.

sessions.title: Map Viewer: Performing Spatial Analysis
sessions.abstract: Discover how to turn your hosted data into shareable and actionable information by performing spatial analysis in ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise. Explore the analysis capabilities in Map Viewer, including ModelBuilder, for creating and automating analysis workflows directly in the web browser. Through real-world examples, learn how to use built-in authoritative content to perform tasks, such as geoenrichment, joins, and image classification, and easily integrate with other apps. Speakers also share best practices and tips to help you make the most of web-based spatial analysis.

sessions.title: Map Viewer: Strategies for Visualizing Data in Related Tables
sessions.abstract: Have you ever wanted to render data in related tables? Speakers demonstrate strategies for how you can achieve that visualization in a performant way using joined views and field calculation in Map Viewer. Also, discover how ArcGIS Data Pipelines can regularly bring in your data in a format that can easily be mapped with other feature layers in your web map.

Keywords: design principles, visual hierarchy, web maps, cartography, layouts, user engagement, map clarity, storytelling with maps

sessions.title: Modernizing Facilities Management for the Future
sessions.abstract: Managing facilities with paper, PDF files, spreadsheets, and disconnected systems is no longer sustainable. This brief presentation explores how organizations are modernizing operations by replacing outdated workflows with smarter, connected systems. Learn how leaders are improving space utilization, accelerating response times, and enhancing safety through indoor GIS, vertical asset management, real-time awareness, and digital twin technology. Discover how to turn your facilities management into responsive, efficient, and future-ready operations.

occurrences.room: State and Local Government Expo Theater

Keywords: facilities, facility management, vertical asset management, digital twin, real-time, public grounds, asset management, paperless workflows, Indoors, GeoBIM, IPS, AutoCAD, Field Maps, space management, portfolio management, space planning, public sector

sessions.title: New in LP360: Lidar, Imagery and 3D Mapping Workflows
sessions.abstract: Join GeoCue for a look at the latest updates in LP360, our lifst, 3D point cloud, and photogrammetry software. This session will highlight new tools designed to help geospatial professionals process data faster, improve point cloud classification, create deliverables, and get more value from both lidar and imagery, to help you prepare your data for ArcPro We’ll cover recent enhancements, including improved photo and colorization tools, AI-assisted classifications, 3D visualization capabilities, and workflow updates that make it easier to move from raw data to final deliverables inside LP360.
tracks: National Mapping, Navigation, and Geospatial Authorities

Topic: National Mapping, Navigation, and Geospatial Authorities

sessions.title: Persistent Threat Custody
sessions.abstract: Object-centric warfare reframes ArcGIS around the relationships among people, places, assets, and events. Transform disconnected data into living, breathing, authoritative intelligence. This demo theater presentation shows how integrating and visualizing those objects in ArcGIS enables commanders to detect patterns faster; understand first-, second-, and third-order effects; and make decisive decisions in real time.

Keywords: OBP, OBI, ABI, Structured Object Management, Ontology, OMS, Object Management System

sessions.title: Simplifying the Use of AI in Local Government
sessions.abstract: AI used to be reserved for well-resourced agencies. However, with the rapidly advancing capabilities and models in ArcGIS, governments of all sizes use AI in their day-to-day operations. This demo theater presentation offers practical examples and case studies for you to see how accessible AI capabilities are within ArcGIS products to extract assets, identify blighted properties, predict road icing conditions, and more. By breaking down barriers to adoption, GIS ensures that innovation is not just for the tech elite but is a practical resource for every community.

sessions.title: Solutions for Conservation Intelligence
sessions.abstract: From global biodiversity targets to on-the-ground action, GIS continues to redefine how conservation organizations plan, monitor, and deliver results. This demo theater presentation examines how ArcGIS Solutions and datasets from ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World support protected area management, community engagement, and ecosystem restoration, enabling data-driven strategies that align with national and international conservation goals.

occurrences.room: GIS for Good Demo Expo Theater

Keywords: Conservation, PAM, Protected Area Management, Global Hex, ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World

sessions.title: Tracing Utility Networks in Water and Sewer Scenarios
sessions.abstract: You implemented a utility network. What's next? Join us to get the answers to your questions about how you can use your utility network beyond just its robust data integrity.

Keywords: Analysis, Network Management

sessions.title: Web Apps: Choosing the Right App Builder
sessions.abstract: Web apps in ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise help you guide users through your maps and data. But how do you select the right web app builder for your audience? Learn the right questions to ask when choosing between ArcGIS Experience Builder, ArcGIS Instant Apps, ArcGIS StoryMaps, ArcGIS Dashboards, and ArcGIS Web Editor.

sessions.title: Locusview - Going Beyond As-Builts: Award-Winning DCM & Mobile GIS for Utilities
sessions.abstract: Join Locusview, winner of the 2025 Esri IMGIS Best Mobile GIS Partner award, to discover how we transform inefficient capital & operational workflows into a unified digital thread. We will showcase our new SAP S/4HANA connector and LocusviewTRACE, our AI-powered solution for gas material traceability. Learn how we optimize field-to-office workflows through deep integrations with industry leaders like SBS, Trimble, and Eos. We will also share updates on our 1,200-crew electric DCM deployment, new wins in the gas market, and upcoming mobile GIS capabilities for the O&M market.

sessions.title: AEC Social
sessions.abstract: Reconnect with other GIS professionals in architecture, engineering, and construction. Discuss your latest projects, chat about what’s new in GIS!

sessions.title: Esri Advantage Program Social
sessions.abstract: Join a special evening social for members of our Advantage Program. Chat with peers and Esri staff about your geospatial strategy over drinks and light refreshments.

sessions.title: Forestry & Agriculture Social
sessions.abstract: Meet with other GIS professionals in forestry and agriculture for an evening of networking and light refreshments. Catch up with colleagues about the latest work being done in your field.

sessions.title: State & Local Government Social
sessions.abstract: Keep your conference energy high, where you can share experiences and connect with other professionals.

sessions.title: YPN Social
sessions.abstract: Keep your conference energy high, where you can share experiences and connect with other emerging GIS professionals.

sessions.title: Morning Yoga
sessions.abstract:
Requires separate registration.  Additional details and registration link.

 

Wake up your body and mind with yoga—open to all levels. Each class starts with gentle warm-up poses, flowing through sun salutations and standing, seated, and inverted poses. The focus will be on breath control, alignment, and present-moment awareness, with modifications to suit all levels and paces.

Participants aged 18 years and over are welcome. Guests of Esri UC attendees can register on-site at the Activities Desk in Lobby D of the San Diego Convention Center beginning on Sunday, July 12, 2026.

Price
Early bird until July 9 | $35*
On-site starting July 11 | $45*
5K and two yoga sessions bundle (must sign up by July 9) | $105*

*Includes one Esri yoga mat

sessions.title: Banking and Financial Services SIG
sessions.abstract:
Join a conversation with your peers and Esri staff to discover how geospatial technology has a critical role in helping financial services providers transform their market strategies. Your peers at banks, credit unions, and investment firms discuss how geospatial tools, data, and thinking have helped uncover new market opportunities and improve customer engagement. Build connections, learn from peers, and bring actionable ideas back to your organization.

 

Reservations will be held until five minutes prior to the start time before seating will be opened to attendees in the on-site queue. If attendees with a reservation remove the session from their schedule, the next person on the wait list will automatically receive that open reservation. Wait lists will only be available before the conference. On-site walk-in attendance may be available dependent on room capacity and will be allowed on a first-come, first-served basis.

ReservationRequired: This session requires a reservation to attend. Please use Add to Schedule to reserve your spot or Join Wait List to be added to the queue.

sessions.title: Black in GIS SIG
sessions.abstract:
Join a conversation with your peers and Esri staff about advancing opportunity, community, representation, and justice. This SIG highlights the organization’s origin and mission, including a discussion on building inclusive geospatial communities. Build connections, learn from peers, and bring actionable ideas back to your organization.

 

Reservations will be held until five minutes prior to the start time before seating will be opened to attendees in the on-site queue. If attendees with a reservation remove the session from their schedule, the next person on the wait list will automatically receive that open reservation. Wait lists will only be available before the conference. On-site walk-in attendance may be available dependent on room capacity and will be allowed on a first-come, first-served basis.

ReservationRequired: This session requires a reservation to attend. Please use Add to Schedule to reserve your spot or Join Wait List to be added to the queue.

sessions.title: Hands-On Learning Lab
sessions.abstract: Take a self-paced lesson at the Hands-On Learning Lab. The Hands-On Learning Lab is a dedicated space to take free lessons on a variety of GIS and ArcGIS topics. Each self-paced lesson takes about one hour to complete and includes conceptual information and step-by-step software exercises. We provide laptops and the ArcGIS software needed to complete each lesson. Esri instructors are available to assist with lesson selection and answer any questions you may have.

sessions.title: Map Lounge
sessions.abstract:
Beyond navigation, maps teach, inspire, and unite us. The Map Lounge is a dynamic, interactive space where you can unwind and admire astonishing maps and powerful stories.

Tour the Interactive Map Gallery, a collection of printed and virtual maps from Esri’s global user community. Discover Storytelling with Maps, featuring exhibits that celebrate diverse voices and innovative storytelling. Join fellow GIS enthusiasts at cozy “fireside chats”. Get inspired by special displays and more.

You’ll also have access to a Hands-On Learning Lab, as well as charging stations in the Relax & Recharge Zone. 

The Map Lounge opens with an evening reception on Monday, July 14.

sessions.title: Renewables Industry Kick-Off Meeting
sessions.abstract: The Renewables Industry Track Kickoff Meeting sets the stage for the week by introducing the focus areas, themes, and opportunities available throughout the track. You’ll get a high‑level overview of what’s ahead and how the sessions, discussions, and community events can help you make the most of your time here.

sessions.title: Speaker Resource Center
sessions.abstract: Are you presenting at the User Conference? Visit the Speaker Resource Center (SRC) to finalize your presentation, practice out loud, and receive personalized coaching and feedback from Esri Instructors. The SRC is fully equipped with projectors, computers, and dedicated workspaces to help you present with confidence.

sessions.title: Advancing Park Equity Through GIS‑Driven Prioritization and Planning
sessions.abstract: See how organizations are advancing park equity by pairing transparent GIS prioritization models with modern decision-support apps. Learn how Austin, TX used weighted overlay analysis combining equity, access, and environmental factors—plus site readiness—to target undeveloped parks for investment and share results with the public. Then explore how Trust for Public Land modernized a mission-critical tool to deliver scenario modeling, 10‑minute walk analysis, and automated reporting in a fast, intuitive web experience.

Keywords: restroom access, service gaps, community visioning, suitability analysis, model builder, needs assessment

sessions.title: Advancing Telecom Data Management and Collaboration with Modern GIS Workflows
sessions.abstract: These sessions explore how telecommunications teams are strengthening data management, operational visibility, and cross‑department collaboration through modern GIS workflows. Presenters will share approaches for connecting field and office processes, unifying network information, and enabling real‑time insight across diverse teams. Attendees will learn best practices for reducing data silos, improving communication, and supporting more accurate, efficient decision‑making throughout the telecom lifecycle.

sessions.title: ArcGIS API for Python: Automating Tools to Monitor Your Organization
sessions.abstract: Join us to learn how to automate your organizational monitoring with ArcGIS API for Python. Learn how Python API integrates with ArcGIS Enterprise to provide a complete overview of consumption and usage and to simplify tasks such as logging and reporting. Speakers will also explore some tools to monitor your organization in ArcGIS Online.

RecommendedAudience: Basic experience with Python.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Dashboards: Best Practices
sessions.abstract: If you are an ArcGIS Dashboards user, do you know what makes a good dashboard? A dashboard should provide key insights at a glance and effectively communicate what is important. This presentation shares tips for getting the most out of your dashboards with best practices specific to various patterns of use. Discover how to communicate status and evaluate performance. Get ideas for new ways to present information and visualize your data effectively.

Keywords: Dashboards, Tips and Tricks, Best Practices, Data Visualization

sessions.title: ArcGIS Earth: Transforming 3D Data into Engaging Cinematic Experiences
sessions.abstract: Explore the powerful movie-creation capabilities of ArcGIS Earth. Speakers guide you through the process of transforming 3D data into engaging cinematic experiences. Integrated animation tools in ArcGIS Earth enable you to visually present data and create unique stories that captivate your audience. This presentation discusses everything about ArcGIS Earth, from the basics of movie creation to advanced editing techniques, ensuring that you leave with the skills that you need to produce professional-quality movies.

RecommendedAudience: Interest in the powerful movie-creation capabilities of ArcGIS Earth.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: The Road Ahead
sessions.abstract: Curious about the future of ArcGIS Enterprise? From imagery to big data to data science, ArcGIS Enterprise is your organization's foundation for powerful data management, mapping and visualization, and analysis capabilities. Speakers share plans for upcoming ArcGIS Enterprise releases across Windows, Linux, and Kubernetes.

RecommendedAudience: Experience using ArcGIS Enterprise.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript: What's New and the Road Ahead
sessions.abstract: Learn about the most important advancements in ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript that were introduced over the last year. Also, be inspired with new ideas for your web apps. This presentation includes a preview of several future capabilities, along with a short- and long-term road map.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Monitor: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Join us for an overview of ArcGIS Monitor, which complements ArcGIS Enterprise by providing tools that monitor the health, performance, and usage of your enterprise GIS implementations. Learn how to set up and configure ArcGIS Monitor, configure alerts and notifications, and create analysis views. Familiarity with ArcGIS Enterprise is recommended.

Keywords: ArcGIS Monitor, Observability, ArcGIS Enterprise
RecommendedAudience: Experience with ArcGIS Enterprise.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Notebooks: Spatial Data Science Workflows
sessions.abstract: Strengthen your spatial data science workflows by integrating ArcGIS Notebooks with ArcGIS Pro. Speakers describe how to detail a problem, use a notebook to outline and test potential solutions, write Python code to make a geoprocessing process repeatable, and then use the rich mapping, visualization, and spatial analysis capabilities of ArcGIS Pro.

RecommendedAudience: Basic experience with Python.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro Editing: Configuring and Using Feature Templates
sessions.abstract: In this introductory presentation, speakers discuss how to configure feature templates to help increase productivity. Topics include how to set default tools and attributes for feature templates. Speakers also discuss best practices for using group and preset templates to create multiple features in a single task.

RecommendedAudience: Basic understanding of ArcGIS Pro.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro Tasks: Tips and Tricks
sessions.abstract: Want helpful best practices regarding repeatable workflows in ArcGIS Pro? Discover how you can run and design tasks that are more efficient. Topics include the step palette, selection management, verification and quality assurance, getting and setting attributes, embedding panes, using a task history table, and more.

RecommendedAudience: Experience with authoring and using ArcGIS Pro tasks.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Explore and Visualize Your Attributes
sessions.abstract: When it comes to GIS, a map is only the beginning. Learn how you can get the most out of your tables, configure pop-ups that put your data in the spotlight, and present your data by creating and sharing reports.

Keywords: pop-up, popup, pop-ups, popups, table, tables, report, reports

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Interactive Flood Simulation
sessions.abstract: Curious about how to model flooding risks using dynamic simulations? Join us to learn how to configure flood scenarios in ArcGIS Pro and use simulation to evaluate the resiliency of current and future landscapes to increasing rainfall and water levels. Then, discover how you can export these analysis results for additional workflows and sharing with others.

Keywords: Urban planning, Disaster Management, Emergency Response, Environment, Climate, Simulation, Flood, Water, Plume, Pollution

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: What's New
sessions.abstract: Discover what's new in ArcGIS Pro! Speakers highlight the product capabilities that were added to ArcGIS Pro in the past year. Find out how this new functionality can help you work more productively and spark ideas for solving problems in new ways. Explore new options for efficiently maintaining, visualizing, and analyzing your data in 2D, 3D, and 4D. Speakers also share tips for staying current on product developments throughout the year.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions: Defense Solutions
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Solutions help defense agencies improve military operations. Join us for an overview of this collection of solutions and learn how you can plan field operations, understand the impact of weather on operations, and conduct mobility analysis that improves military operations.

Keywords: Military, Defense, Operations, Cross Country Mobility, Weather Operations, Helicopter Landing Zones, HLZ

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions: Water Utilities
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Solutions help water utilities maintain water distribution systems and comply with government regulations. Join us for an overview of this collection of solutions and learn how you can streamline asset inventories, routinely inspect water distribution assets, and share essential information with stakeholders.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Survey123: What's New and the Road Ahead
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Survey123 brings together the power of geography and forms. Organizations worldwide use ArcGIS Survey123 to modernize everyday workflows. Join us to learn about the new features that were added in the past year and what’s coming next for Survey123!

sessions.title: ArcGIS: How Maps Work
sessions.abstract: Start with the basics to become a smarter mapmaker. The essentials of good mapmaking empower you to make your maps work better and to tell a compelling story through your design. Learn the basic principles of cartographic design (classification, generalization, symbology, color, type, layout for print and web) and how you can use ArcGIS Pro to focus the message of your map. Understand how people read maps and what works well to successfully communicate your message. Step through the mapmaking process to find out how to implement better design decisions.

sessions.title: Digital Twins Set the Foundation for Efficient Operations
sessions.abstract: GIS-based digital twins are the standard for maintaining facilities, passenger safety, work orders, lease and asset management. In this session, see how two major hub airports are operating generating and leveraging a digital twin to manage their operations. One of the airports is scanning their facilities to generate floor plans into BIM for better space management, lease accuracy, and improving sustainability. Another airport is integrating multiple asset management systems into GIS as one unified platform for work orders and wayfinding, setting the foundation for their digital twin.

Keywords: digital twin, asset management, indoor, lease management, work order, safety, passenger, facilities, facility, BIM
