sessions.title: Drone Reality Mapping: A Complete Workflow from Capture to Insights
sessions.abstract: Drones can offer a clear, accurate, and up-to-date view of the world. This presentation goes beyond the basics to guide you through a practical end-to-end workflow—from flight planning, image collection, and processing into 2D and 3D data products through reality mapping to insight extraction and dissemination. Gain valuable knowledge that you can apply to your organization's specific needs.

RecommendedAudience: Basic understanding of drone mapping.

sessions.title: Enterprise Geodatabase: Multiuser Editing with Feature Services
sessions.abstract: Be sure not to miss this overview of multiuser editing in enterprise geodatabases, including branch versioning and nonversioned archiving. Explore setting up data for short and long transactional editing, sharing data with the organization, and common editing workflows.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with the geodatabase information model.

sessions.title: Enterprise Geodatabase: Performance Troubleshooting
sessions.abstract: Would exploring real-world troubleshooting cases help you uncover comparable challenges in your organizations? Explore various troubleshooting techniques for working through performance-related issues with enterprise geodatabases, maps, and feature services. Review real-world performance scenarios and how they were resolved through troubleshooting, and learn about tools that you can use to troubleshoot cases in your own organization.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with using or managing an enterprise geodatabase.

sessions.title: From Insight to Impact: ArcGIS Dashboards for Conservation Action
sessions.abstract: This session shows how modern web GIS patterns turns complex conservation data into targeted action. Learn how interactive web-based GIS tools summarize multi-scale information to guide planning, support fundraising and proposals, and coordinate partners. In this session, you will learn how GIS integrated wildlife telemetry with additional context around parcels and habitats to pinpoint priority outreach areas that reduce threats and improve outcomes across a species’ range.

Keywords: Conservation,  planning, fundraising, proposals.

sessions.title: How Accurate Data Elevates Government Impact
sessions.abstract: Discover how two communities are transforming community well-being and infrastructure management through high accuracy data and GIS powered accountability. Learn how Philadelphia integrated fragmented datasets with virtual inspections to target problem properties and restore community norms, while Anaheim, California captured and processed high-resolution drone imagery of urban areas for more evidence-based decision-making on development, public safety, and infrastructure management.

sessions.title: Imagery: What’s New
sessions.abstract: Discover what’s new in imagery across the ArcGIS system. Get a comprehensive overview of the latest enhancements in ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Enterprise, and ArcGIS Online for managing, analyzing, visualizing, and sharing imagery at scale. Find out how ArcGIS continues to evolve to support a wide range of imagery and raster data—including satellite, aerial, and drone imagery; scientific datasets; data cubes; geospatial video; scanned historical maps; and elevation data.

sessions.title: Introduction to Reality Mapping with Drone, Aerial, and Satellite Imagery
sessions.abstract: Reality mapping creates accurate digital representations of the physical world using imagery. Discover how ArcGIS reality mapping capabilities let you generate accurate and up-to-date 2D and 3D data products from drone, aerial, or satellite imagery, and how these can be used to enhance your GIS. Learn about the different apps supporting reality mapping within ArcGIS, the Reality Engine, and its outputs. Speakers provide an overview of the workflow and present common uses for the generated data.

sessions.title: Kids Fair
sessions.abstract:
Visit the Esri Kids Fair to introduce them to GIS through a series of educational, hands-on activities. This year’s theme is “Continents.” After completing each activity and filling out their passport, kids will earn a special prize. Kids from 5 to 14 years old are welcome to participate. A parent or guardian must be present at all times. Registration is available on-site at no additional cost.

 

Learn more about fair activities in this blog.

sessions.title: Modern Spatial Data Pipelines for Renewables
sessions.abstract: Explore how modern spatial Data Pipelines, automation, and AI are reshaping renewable energy development and enabling stronger, data driven decision-making.

sessions.title: Predictive Analysis: Modeling Data Using Statistics and Machine Learning
sessions.abstract: Modeling helps uncover patterns to explain events and predict values and occurrences. This presentation reviews random forest, XGBoost, and methods to evaluate prediction models. Explore the Presence-Only Prediction tool, which uses a maximum entropy approach to predict the location of phenomena with only known presence data. Finally, learn about a method for estimating time until an event occurs. Throughout this presentation, speakers emphasize making these methods accessible through clear, conceptual explanations.

sessions.title: Protecting Children from Health Hazards in Their Community
sessions.abstract: Children and youth may be exposed to environmental risks that threaten their health. Discover how a Fannie Mae funded partnership with Children’s National Hospital and The Children’s Law Center maps pediatric asthma cases against housing code violations, to identify unsafe properties and guide proactive inspections and remediation. See how City of Austin is using spatial analysis of tobacco retailer proximity to highlight youth exposure risks and support equitable zoning and prevention strategies.

sessions.title: Put Your Community on the Map
sessions.abstract: Discover how location data can power economic development in a community. From showcasing neighborhood amenities to predicting what  will be needed to support future growth, the right tools can do it all.

Keywords: mobility, growth, development, infrastructure
RecommendedAudience: planning

sessions.title: Reactive to Predictive: Scaling Spatial Analysis for Infrastructure Resilience
sessions.abstract: This session shows how geospatial technology delivers lasting business value beyond emergency response. A flood response dashboard became an enterprise system for infrastructure oversight and transparency, while predictive analytics applied to long-term rainfall data support forward‑looking planning for government. Both examples demonstrate how GIS enables smarter, more resilient operational and infrastructure investment decisions.

sessions.title: Resilient Foundations: Scaling GIS for Growth and Users
sessions.abstract: Learn how to manage large-scale transitions in your GIS infrastructure while streamlining complex workflows. You’ll discover the City of Denton’s phased journey from a Geometric Network to the Utility Network to support rapid population growth using Schneider Electric’s XI suite. We’ll also dive into Duke Energy’s innovative use of Python and webhooks to automate content migration for over 20,000 Portal users. Gain insights into maintaining governance, reducing migration risks, and building a scalable, automated foundation for both network information management and enterprise-wide success.

sessions.title: Sustainable Land Cover Management with ArcGIS
sessions.abstract: In this session, you will discover how peers are using ArcGIS to support sustainable land use management across diverse landscapes. Through short showcases and discussions, participants will share analyses, data sources, and decision-support tools used to balance development, conservation, and ecosystem services. You will discover practical uses of ArcGIS Image Server, ArcGIS Hub, ArcGIS Dashboards, ArcGIS StoryMaps, and ArcGIS Survey123 to monitor land use change, evaluate trade-offs, integrate environmental and socio-economic data, and communicate insights for more sustainable land use decisions.

Keywords: Landuse, Landcover, Deforestation
RecommendedAudience: Open to all levels of experience

sessions.title: Turning Scientific Data into Action with User-Centered Tools
sessions.abstract: Attendees will learn how to translate complex scientific datasets into intuitive, user-ready experiences that support exploration, analysis, and real-world decisions. Using two examples, NASA PO.DAAC’s GIS-enabled data access tools and a watershed-scale streamflow decision support application, the session presents practical strategies for making large datasets meaningful to diverse audiences. The presentations will highlight how to choose the right GIS tools for storytelling and analysis, balance scientific complexity with usability, and design interfaces for both expert and public users.

sessions.title: Wildfire Operations in California Using GIS
sessions.abstract: Explore two complementary wildfire initiatives from California focusing on how geospatial tools are providing support for evacuation notifications and keeping hazard data current to support response operations and keep the public informed.

Keywords: GIS-based emergency management, Wildfire response Zone-based communication, Wildfire, AI, GeoAI, Hazard Risk, Emergency Response, Emergency Preparedness, Public Safety, Hazard Mitigation, State Government
RecommendedAudience: Beginner

sessions.title: Expo
sessions.abstract: At the UC Expo, connect face to face with Esri staff and Esri Partner Network leaders to explore innovations, collaborate on ideas, learn from experts, and ignite inspiration.

sessions.title: Hands-On Learning Lab
sessions.abstract: Take a self-paced lesson at the Hands-On Learning Lab. The Hands-On Learning Lab is a dedicated space to take free lessons on a variety of GIS and ArcGIS topics. Each self-paced lesson takes about one hour to complete and includes conceptual information and step-by-step software exercises. We provide laptops and the ArcGIS software needed to complete each lesson. Esri instructors are available to assist with lesson selection and answer any questions you may have.

sessions.title: Imagery Has Entered the Chat: Eagleview Workflows in ArcGIS
sessions.abstract: High-resolution imagery is no longer just something to view on a map. See how Eagleview is bringing imagery, data, and AI into ArcGIS workflows, including Oriented Imagery Layers, GeoAI tools, disaster response, ArcGIS Survey123 field collection, ArcGIS Experience Builder apps, and dashboards. Attendees will learn how government, utilities, AEC, emergency management, assessment, public safety, and infrastructure teams can turn imagery into conversational, contextual, and actionable intelligence.

sessions.title: AI Native Reality Capture and Mapping for Local Governments with GeoCam
sessions.abstract: Learn about our customized data packs and how you can extend and customize them using our editing tools for ArcGIS. If you need data for your ArcGIS Solutions for Local Government, we’ll show you how GeoCam can provide you with operational data that is ready to go along with plug and play apps that you can use to both create and QAQC your own data.

sessions.title: Choose the Blue Button: Accelerate your Esri AVD deployment with the Esri AVD Accelerator
sessions.abstract: Organizations need more than remote access—they need a fast, secure way to deliver the right tools and data to the right users. This 30-minute session highlights how the Esri AVD Accelerator streamlines deployment of secure, ready-to-use ArcGIS Pro environments in Azure. Through a practical walkthrough, attendees will see how it reduces setup complexity, improves consistency, and speeds time to production. The focus is on outcomes: faster deployment, repeatable delivery, and better support for high-performance Esri workloads without deep technical overhead.

sessions.title: AI for Good: AI and ArcGIS for Humanitarian Response
sessions.abstract: Geospatial artificial intelligence and AI-assisted GIS accelerate humanitarian response by rapidly analyzing hazards, vulnerability, and impacts. In this demo theater presentation, speakers discuss how AI helps automate mapping, guide decisions, and improve situational awareness when every minute matters.

occurrences.room: GIS for Good Demo Expo Theater

Keywords: Efficiency, Impact, Outcomes, Resilience, AI

sessions.title: A Flightpath to Success: Using GIS Across Your Airport Enterprise
sessions.abstract: Are you using your GIS investment to the fullest extent of its capabilities? Intelligent airports view GIS as essential in all operations, from planning and airside and landside operations to critical, real-time decision-making. Discover how to use GIS to drive more intelligent operations. In this demo theater presentation, see how ArcGIS facilitates asset management, integrates with other critical enterprise systems, optimizes indoor spaces, enables comprehensive analytics, and empowers AI implementations. Discover how spatial strategies can drive smart decisions now and prepare your airport for future trends and industry requirements.

Keywords: Airports, Smart Airports, Passenger Experience, Facilities, Real-time, Realtime, Part 139, Inspections, FAA

sessions.title: Advancing Broadband Equity Through GIS-Driven Analytics and Access Mapping
sessions.abstract: These sessions highlight how GIS‑powered analytics and broadband access mapping are transforming efforts to understand connectivity gaps, guide funding decisions, and promote digital equity. Presenters will share approaches for integrating diverse datasets, conducting robust gap analyses, and visualizing broadband needs at local, regional, and national scales. Attendees will learn how modern GIS tools support data‑driven decision‑making, improve transparency, and strengthen collaboration across stakeholders working to expand equitable broadband access.

sessions.title: Advancing Public Works Asset Strategies With Real Time Data
sessions.abstract: You will learn how agencies build enterprise asset strategies by bringing together reliable data, real time updates, and consistent practices that guide long term planning. You will understand how unified information supports clearer decisions about maintenance needs, investments, and service priorities.

Keywords: Asset Management, Data Governance, Program Planning, Lifecycle Management, Capital Planning
RecommendedAudience: Best suited for asset, engineers, and GIS administrators responsible for infrastructure programs and system integrations.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Apps: Observability and Health Checks
sessions.abstract: Reliable performance is critical for ArcGIS apps at scale. This focused presentation explores strategies and tools for monitoring app health, tracking key performance indicators, and implementing proactive checks to prevent downtime. Learn best practices for observability, alerting, and maintenance to ensure consistent user experiences and system resilience.

Keywords: system monitoring, performance optimization, uptime assurance, reliability engineering, diagnostic tools, telemetry, service resilience, automated alerts, incident response, operational analytics
RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with performance monitoring, observability tools, and alerting systems.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Data Reviewer: Automating Quality Control
sessions.abstract: Automated workflows using ArcGIS Data Reviewer improve data quality, streamline maintenance, and decrease risk for data consumers—and this presentation teaches you how! Discover how automated checks detect common errors in your data and how you can implement these workflows using ArcGIS Pro. Additionally, speakers explore examples of data quality requirements across different industries.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Earth: Applying Custom Configurations to Simplify Stakeholder Engagement
sessions.abstract: Help your stakeholders focus on insights rather than technical details. Learn how to configure ArcGIS Earth for your audience by creating custom configurations that streamline access to authoritative geospatial data. Preconfigure portal connections, default basemaps, camera positions, and mission-critical layers, so users have exactly what they need, with no manual setup required. Discover deployment strategies to distribute configurations, standardize the user experience, and reduce onboarding time.

Keywords: Configuration distribution, Authoritative data
RecommendedAudience: Experience with configuration in ArcGIS Earth.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Administering Your Deployment
sessions.abstract: Are you administering and optimizing an ArcGIS Enterprise deployment on Windows or Linux? Learn best practices for administrators and get an overview of advanced system and service settings. Speakers also discuss working with administrative endpoints, defining data stores, configuring utility services, and locating and exploring log files.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Cloud Automation Tooling
sessions.abstract: Explore Esri’s cloud automation tools for ArcGIS Enterprise on Amazon Web Services and Microsoft Azure. Learn the differences between Cloud Builder, CloudFormation, PowerShell Desired State Configuration (DSC), Chef cookbooks and how to choose the right resources and documentation. Find out how to deploy distributed architecture and apply best practices for security, networking, scaling, and automation. Speakers also discuss autoscaling patterns and cloud-native modernization strategies.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Enterprise on Amazon Web Services
Keywords: Cloud, ArcGIS Enterprise, automation, deployment
RecommendedAudience: Experience with ArcGIS Enterprise.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Preparing and Planning for 12.X
sessions.abstract: Learn everything you need to know to successfully transition from ArcGIS Enterprise 11.x to 12.x. This presentation discusses new features and enhancements in ArcGIS Enterprise 12.0 and 12.1, key considerations before upgrading, best practices for migration workflows, and strategies for modernizing your web apps and preparing your content for a smooth upgrade. Whether you are planning an immediate upgrade or just thinking about the future, this presentation will help you get ready.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Experience Builder: Tips and Tricks
sessions.abstract: Discover the power of ArcGIS Experience Builder. Learn how to use URL parameters to zoom to features by attributes, change the table background, use ArcGIS Arcade expressions to display data dynamically, copy and paste features, filter multiple layers at once, create a single app for multiple audiences, deliver multilingual apps, and more.

Keywords: Tips, Tricks, Experience, ArcGIS Experience Builder, Tips and tricks, Arcade expression, Multilingual apps

sessions.title: ArcGIS Instant Apps: Unlocking Web Maps with Bookmarks, Charts, Symbology, and More
sessions.abstract: Learn how to build lightweight, interactive web apps. Discover how web apps can unlock capabilities in your web map—such as symbology, pop-ups, bookmarks, and charts—to help you communicate more effectively with your audience. Speakers also discuss how web apps allow users to edit simple attributes of the data if there is an editable layer in your web map.

Keywords: ArcGIS Instant Apps, Web apps, bookmarks, charts, symbology, pop-ups, interactive, web maps

sessions.title: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World: Simplify Your Workflows with Environmental Content
sessions.abstract: Enrich your own data with ready-to-use global environmental content available in ArcGIS Living Atlas. Learn how to access and explore content, use the latest analysis tools to address critical environmental issues, and apply cartographic and visualization best practices to produce clear, understandable, and sharable maps for your audience.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World
Keywords: ArcGIS Living Atlas, Environment, Live Feeds, Climate, Disaster Response, Living Atlas

sessions.title: ArcGIS Maps SDKs for Native Apps: An Overview and What’s New
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Maps SDKs for Native Apps enable you to build applications with .NET, Flutter, Qt, Swift, and Kotlin that work with the ArcGIS system and run directly on desktop and mobile devices—online or offline. Over the last year, several new features have been introduced, including support for on-device spatial analysis, local 3D scenes, shared templates to streamline feature editing, and new data sources like geospatial PDFs. Join us to learn more about these and other improvements and get a preview of what’s coming next.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Maps SDKs for Native Apps
RecommendedAudience: Experience developing a native app.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise: Optimizing ArcGIS Arcade Workflows
sessions.abstract: Enhance your maps and apps with ArcGIS Arcade. This presentation will empower you to understand and use Arcade expressions effectively in ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise. Learn how to write, test, and debug Arcade expressions; evaluate their performance impact; and explore diverse use cases such as data-driven styling, pop-up customization, and field calculation.

RecommendedAudience: Experience using ArcGIS Arcade.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online: Printing Web Maps
sessions.abstract: Learn the workflow for making print layout templates available to users in your ArcGIS Online organization. Speakers demonstrate direct use cases and practical printing examples from Map Viewer, including how to save printouts as items, workflow considerations, and more. Explore layout items in ArcGIS Pro and the process for creating and using them for printing online.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online: What's New
sessions.abstract: Join us for an exciting exploration of the latest features and functionalities in the most recent update of ArcGIS Online. Learn about the latest enhancements that can amplify your GIS experience. You will not only identify new features that your organization can use but also understand their value and potential impact. Do not miss this opportunity to maximize your use of ArcGIS Online.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Parcel Fabric: Deploying to ArcGIS Enterprise and ArcGIS Online
sessions.abstract: You have migrated your data to a parcel fabric. Interested in learning how to deploy the parcel fabric to ArcGIS Enterprise or ArcGIS Online? Join us to learn about these two deployment options and the differences between them. Speakers demonstrate how to deploy a parcel fabric to both ArcGIS Enterprise and ArcGIS Online and how to configure your map and project for successful editing after deployment.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with editing in ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Enterprise, and ArcGIS Online.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: An Introduction to Labels and Annotation
sessions.abstract: Text is an essential part of any map. Join us for an introduction to map labeling, converting labels to annotation, and using graphic text and annotation in ArcGIS Pro. Understand the differences between labels, annotation, and graphic text, and get to know the editing and geoprocessing tools that are available for modifying text in your maps.

Keywords: Labelling

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Layouts and Map Series
sessions.abstract: Want to create and customize layouts and map series more effectively? Join us to discover new functionality in ArcGIS Pro layouts as well as information and tricks to help streamline your layout workflows. Learn about the various types of map series that are available in ArcGIS Pro as well as when and how to use them to make many similar layouts quickly.

RecommendedAudience: Experience with layouts, mapping, and visualization.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: The Road Ahead
sessions.abstract: Interested in upcoming releases of ArcGIS Pro? In this presentation, members of the ArcGIS Pro development team share an early look at new and improved capabilities that are currently in the works for future releases of ArcGIS Pro.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions: Real-Time Operations
sessions.abstract: Need better visibility into operations as conditions change throughout the day? Join us for an overview of the real-time operations solutions, which help organizations monitor live data to respond more effectively to winter weather, street maintenance, and transit performance.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Spatial Analyst: Finding the Best Locations Using Suitability Modeler
sessions.abstract: Suitability modeling can help in a wide variety of scenarios, from identifying the best place to site a housing development or corporate headquarters to choosing which areas to conserve for a park or wildlife habitat. Suitability Modeler’s interactive graphical user interface makes creating a suitability model easy. Learn about the iterative exploratory process of creating a model and how the immediate feedback from interactive maps, plots, and statistics helps you make more-informed decisions. Speakers also discuss submodels and the latest functionality for evaluating your models.

sessions.title: ArcGIS StoryMaps: What’s New and the Road Ahead
sessions.abstract: Find out about the newest available features in ArcGIS StoryMaps. Learn about improved authoring tools, mobile optimization, templates, AI-assisted writing, and expanded capabilities for the ArcGIS StoryMaps Briefings app. This presentation also previews upcoming releases.

Keywords: What’s new, roadmap, AI
RecommendedAudience: Experience with ArcGIS StoryMaps.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Survey123: Building Custom Reports
sessions.abstract: Learn how to turn Survey123 data into high-quality, customizable PDF reports. This presentation covers best practices for including tables, photos, and maps, and shows you how to organize and design reports to meet your specific needs. Discover how to create outputs that communicate results effectively.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Topographic Mapping: Cartographic Production for Defense Map Products
sessions.abstract: Explore how to use ArcGIS Topographic Mapping to produce defense-standard maps, including Joint Operations Graphic-Air (JOG-A), MGCP Topographic Map (MTM), Topographic Map (TM), Tactical Pilot Chart (TPC), and Operational Navigation Chart (ONC). Learn how specification-driven processes in ArcGIS Pro and ArcGIS Enterprise support standardized templates, automated production, and scalable operations, improving consistency and efficiency across defense mapping tasks.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with cartography and ArcGIS Enterprise concepts.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Utility Network: Advanced Network Management
sessions.abstract: Does your utility network need a boost? Join us to learn all about configuring, troubleshooting, and optimizing the utility network. Get answers to sophisticated questions specific to the network model as well as updates on ongoing activities for web, desktop, and mobile platforms.

RecommendedAudience: Experience with geodatabases and utility networks.

sessions.title: ArcGIS for Microsoft: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Discover the value that ArcGIS for Microsoft can bring to your organization. Speakers discuss the capabilities in ArcGIS for Microsoft 365, ArcGIS for Power Platform, and ArcGIS for Microsoft Fabric that enable you to take advantage of mapping capabilities and ArcGIS data and extend mapping and analysis to everyone in your organization.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Hydrologic Analysis and Floodplain Delineation
sessions.abstract: Performing flood and hydrologic analysis in ArcGIS involves key tools and workflows. Learn the principles behind the hydrologic tools in ArcGIS Spatial Analyst and the Arc Hydro toolbox to create various hydrologic datasets for hydro landscape characterization and refine data for better flood forecasting. Explore hydrologic and hydraulic modeling, delineate floodplain and map future flood risk in ArcGIS, and customize web applications for flood mapping.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Integrating Open-Source Intelligence
sessions.abstract: Information gaps are not always resolved through traditional intelligence methods. Many military and intelligence agencies integrate open-source intelligence (OSINT) by gathering and analyzing publicly available data. This demo theater presentation shows how ArcGIS capabilities incorporate OSINT into geospatial infrastructure and apply data analysis to transform open information into actionable intelligence that supports operational effectiveness.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World
Keywords: OSINT, Data Extraction, Text Analysis, Situational Awareness, Open Source

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Planning and Managing Special Events
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS provides a comprehensive geospatial framework that enables planners, public safety agencies, and event organizers to effectively prepare for, manage, and evaluate large special events. Through real-time data integration, situational awareness tools, and advanced spatial analysis, ArcGIS enhances decision-making and operational coordination across the entire event life cycle.

Keywords: Solutions, Special Event, Watch Center

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Power Restoration Management for Gas Utilities
sessions.abstract: Gas utilities and their customers need the most up-to-date information during outage events. This presentation demonstrates how you can use ArcGIS Enterprise, ArcGIS Field Maps, ArcGIS Dashboards, and ArcGIS Experience Builder together to streamline gas outage management and restoration for distribution networks. Automated ArcGIS Utility Network traces and outage polygons identify affected customers, while Field Maps supports relighting efforts in the field. Dashboards provides real-time outage and restoration insights, and Experience Builder enables customers to receive real-time updates throughout the event.

sessions.title: Climate Action: Working with Nature & Community to Build Resilience & Equity
sessions.abstract: Find inspiration for your resilience strategy; increase equity for your community or restore mangroves for a nature-based approach to simultaneously increase resilience and biodiversity. Learn some tricks of the trade to help your community prioritize data driven actions by coupling national methodologies with localized data to produce insightful visualizations.  Then discover a powerful approach to create a mangrove restoration roadmap, at scale, by analyzing satellite and drone data to inform local actions.

sessions.title: Empowering the Enterprise: Emergency Maps and Data Integrity
sessions.abstract: Learn how to build resilient, scalable GIS workflows for emergency response and long-term data governance. You’ll discover how the rapid deployment of ArcGIS Webmaps and ArcGIS Dashboards streamlined damage assessment and restoration during recent wildfires. We’ll also explore a comprehensive geospatial system that leverages ArcGIS Pro and AGOL to enhance data quality, workflow tracking, and collaboration. Join us to see how modernizing your GIS architecture empowers field crews in a crisis while ensuring enterprise-wide data integrity and efficiency.

sessions.title: Esri YPN: The Power of Saying Yes to the Right Opportunities
sessions.abstract: Early in your career, every opportunity can feel important, but not every opportunity is the right one. Whether it is volunteering at an event, presenting your work, writing a blog post, joining a meetup, or accepting a stretch assignment, the key is learning how to say yes with intention. In this session, attendees will hear real stories, explore practical decision-making tools, and reflect on how small “yes” moments can build confidence, expand skills, strengthen professional relationships, and open unexpected career doors. Attendees will leave with a simple framework for evaluating opportunities, overcoming hesitation, and choosing one meaningful next step in their GIS career journey.

sessions.title: GeoAI with Feature, Tabular, and Time-Series Data
sessions.abstract: Geospatial artificial intelligence (GeoAI) techniques are suitable for extracting information from data such as imagery and text but can also be applied to vector, tabular, and time-series data. In this technical session, learn how to integrate ArcGIS with dozens of machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) models to help you solve clustering, regression, classification, and prediction problems, as well as create forecasts for the future based on historical time-series data. Speakers also explain how automated machine learning (AutoML) can be used to gain new insights in your data and help boost your productivity in your machine learning tasks.

sessions.title: Geocoding in ArcGIS Pro: Tips and Tricks for Working with Locators
sessions.abstract: Are you making the most of the latest geocoding enhancements introduced in ArcGIS Pro? This presentation highlights newly added and productivity-enhancing workflows in geocoding with ArcGIS Pro. Learn about topics such as maximizing the rematching functionality, automating common geocoding workflows with the Python module, modifying locator properties to optimize geocoding results, and exporting locators and configured layers for use in other ArcGIS applications.

Keywords: Geocoding, Locators, Addresses
RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with ArcGIS Pro.

sessions.title: Geospatial AI in Practice for Infrastructure Organizations
sessions.abstract: AI technologies are transforming the electric grid and how electric utilities function. Join us to learn how advancements in geospatial artificial intelligence (GeoAI) can be used in a practical manner for everyday use by your organization to increase productivity.

sessions.title: Geospatial Intelligence for Coastal and Wetland Resilience
sessions.abstract: Explore how emerging geospatial approaches, from machine learning to multi-sensor analysis, advance understanding of coastal and wetland resilience under climate and development pressures. Attendees will learn how integrating satellite imagery, LiDAR, field observations, and AI-driven analytics reveals ecosystem change, vulnerability, and carbon dynamics beyond traditional methods. The session shows how modern GIS workflows turn complex environmental data into actionable insights for resource management, climate adaptation, and science-based decision-making.

sessions.title: Getting Your Data Ready for AI Applications
sessions.abstract: Is your data ready for AI? Find out how to get your GIS data ready for use by AI applications, especially agentic mapping applications. This presentation demonstrates the new Item Details assistant (beta) in action and how good documentation and metadata result in better AI apps.

Keywords: AI, Assistant, Agents

sessions.title: Holistic GIS Strategy for Modernizing Park Services and Workforce Adoption
sessions.abstract: Parks and recreation agencies are rethinking how they connect data, workflows, and staff to deliver faster, smarter services. This session spotlights Fairfax County’s use of integrated location intelligence in ArcGIS Pro for defensible visitation insights and benchmarking, alongside Montgomery Parks’ proven approach to scaling GIS adoption and engagement. See how organizations unlock actionable insights, boost workforce efficiency, and drive more confident, equitable decisions.

Keywords: governance, encroachment, trail

sessions.title: How Airports Strengthen GIS Maturity
sessions.abstract: Integrating foundational air and landside data with other mission-critical business systems is essential for safety, compliance, and efficiency. You will hear from one airport who has experienced a digital transformation to empower staff with tools to better maintain facilities, keeping staff and passengers safe, while ensuring compliance with regulations. A state aeronautics division will discuss how they guarantee protection of their airspace.

Keywords: airside, landside, data, maturity, facilities, facility, business system, integration, safety, compliance, aeronautics

sessions.title: Imagery in ArcGIS: What's New with Deep Learning
sessions.abstract: Stay up to date on the latest deep learning advancements in ArcGIS. This presentation highlights new capabilities in ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Enterprise, ArcGIS Online, and the ArcGIS API for Python that make deep learning faster and more accessible. Understand how ArcGIS applies modern AI to satellite, aerial, drone, video, and scientific data, and explore new tools and workflows that streamline information extraction and automated analysis across your organization.

sessions.title: Improving ASCE Grades for U.S. Infrastructure
sessions.abstract: The American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE) assesses and grades the condition and performance of U.S. infrastructure networks every four years. Join us to explore what the latest ASCE report card (presented with ArcGIS StoryMaps) means for your community, learn actionable steps to improve local infrastructure, and discover how GIS can accelerate progress. This brief presentation features expert insights and practical strategies for decision makers on how GIS can help improve infrastructure grades.

occurrences.room: State and Local Government Expo Theater

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World
Keywords: ASCE, engineering, federal funding, asset management, federal mandate,

sessions.title: Live Podcast with EPR: Digital Twins, Building Community, and the Future of GIS
sessions.abstract: Environmental Professionals Radio (EPR) comes to the Esri User Conference! Join this lively, live podcast featuring voices from The Nature Conservancy, Esri, and the Society for Conservation GIS. We’ll dive into digital twins, explore how mapping helps translate science into spatial insight, and discuss how conservation professionals can keep pace with an ever evolving technology landscape. Expect sharp insights, practical takeaways, and plenty of laughs from EPR hosts Laura and Nic along the way.

sessions.title: Map Viewer: An Overview of Smart Mapping
sessions.abstract: Find out how smart mapping in Map Viewer helps you create visually appealing and informative maps. In one click, try the map styles that are available in smart mapping to explore your data. In a second click, discover how a theme fine-tunes the map to emphasize what is important and deemphasize what is not important to help you decide what fits your purpose. Lastly, smart mapping functionality enables you to apply your knowledge of the subject and the purpose of a map to maximize its visual appeal. This presentation also discusses how aggregation provides insights and how Map Viewer makes it easy to adjust color schemes, set class breaks, and change symbols.

sessions.title: Mapping Wildfire Risk and Resilience with GIS
sessions.abstract: Explore how GIS technologies are advancing wildfire hazard analysis and community resilience. Learn how data-driven insights and collaborative platforms are shaping proactive wildfire strategies across diverse landscapes.

Keywords: Climate-vulnerability, Fire Hazard Severity, Electrical Transmission Lines, Environmental Injustice, Carbon-negative, Wildfire, ArcGIS Hub, Wildfire Recovery, Wildfire Mitigation, Natural Disaster, Community Support
RecommendedAudience: Beginner

sessions.title: Permitting Made Spatial
sessions.abstract: While many agencies still rely on manual permit review processes that can slow timelines and strain resources, some are leading the way forward. Discover how two organizations modernized their workflows using ArcGIS tools to reduce errors, improve service delivery, and create a more coordinated review experience.

Keywords: zoning, permitting, parcel
RecommendedAudience: urban planners, public works, permitting, environmental planners, transportation planners

sessions.title: Scaling Energy Siting and Transparency with ArcGIS
sessions.abstract: Learn how ArcGIS accelerates energy generation planning and public transparency for government. This session explores automated, defensible site-suitability analysis across large geographic regions alongside a national energy geoportal integrating authoritative data and citizen-facing apps. GIS has supported faster decisions, trusted governance, and scalable workflows for the energy transition of these government organizations.

sessions.title: Sensitivity Analysis: Embracing Uncertainty in Analysis Results
sessions.abstract: Spatial data science workflows guide decision-making across organizations, but most analyses downplay the impact of analytical uncertainty. Analytical uncertainty comes in many forms, including attribute uncertainty (for example, uncertain results from survey estimates) and aggregation uncertainty (for example, questioning the scale of your analysis). This technical session introduces the Assessing Sensitivity toolset and methods to help GIS professionals embrace and use uncertainty in their analysis.

sessions.title: Spatial Analysis for Public Safety: Territory Design to Spatiotemporal Trends
sessions.abstract: This session will explore how advanced GIS tools can transform justice operations. Learn how law enforcement can optimize administrative territory design by integrating geospatial and socio-economic variables to enhance crime prevention.  Next, learn how tools from the Spatial Statistics toolbox were used to identify shifting spatiotemporal trends in community supervision, including Space Time Cubes and Emerging Hot Spot Analysis.

sessions.title: Spatially Driven Project Acceleration
sessions.abstract: Learn how renewable energy teams are linking enterprise GIS, Salesforce, and cloud platforms to accelerate siting decisions and maintain alignment across project and land data.

sessions.title: Design in Context: Connected Autodesk and ArcGIS Workflows
sessions.abstract: Explore how Autodesk and Esri are connecting GIS and design workflows across the AEC lifecycle. This session highlights how teams use the Autodesk Connector for ArcGIS across products in the AEC Collection and Autodesk Forma to bring GIS data into planning, design, and delivery workflows. See how planners use Forma for contextual conceptual design, how engineers and designers leverage Civil 3D with ArcGIS data, and how as-built project information can be shared back to ArcGIS Online to keep GIS systems current and connected.

sessions.title: Elevating Your Parcels with 3D Cadastre
sessions.abstract: Is your organization responsible for managing land information? Do you find yourself wishing that you could better represent interests above the ground, such as air rights and condominiums, or below the ground for buried infrastructure and minerals? If so, this brief presentation is for you! Join us and explore the power of managing your parcels in 3D.

occurrences.room: State and Local Government Expo Theater

sessions.title: GIS Hero: Prove the Value of GIS through These Innovative Uses of Parcel Data
sessions.abstract: GIS professionals often must help their organization fully appreciate the value that GIS can provide. In this session, we talk with professionals from multiple industries to learn how they are helping their companies increase the buy-in for GIS teams, systems, and processes.  We will highlight the innovative ways these professions are using and sharing parcel data within their organizations to create more demand for GIS services and to expand access to GIS across more teams.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Apps: Powering Your Apps with R
sessions.abstract: Discover how to use the {arcgis} metapackage to build automated workflows that integrate R's data wrangling and analytical capabilities with ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise. This demo theater presentation shows you how to read and update feature services, process ArcGIS Survey123 responses with custom business logic, and visualize transformed data in ArcGIS Dashboards for organizational collaboration. Learn how R-powered automation streamlines repetitive data processing tasks while maintaining data quality controls and organizational standards.

RecommendedAudience: Basic experience with R.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Data Reviewer: Improving Data Quality in Your Address Repository
sessions.abstract: Address data is critical to efficient delivery of services such as emergency response, billing, elections management, and cost-effective commerce in a community. Learn how ArcGIS Data Reviewer can help detect errors in and improve the quality of your authoritative address repository.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with address data management ArcGIS Solutions is recommended.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise on Kubernetes: Availability, Scalability, and Operability
sessions.abstract: Curious about how you can configure ArcGIS Enterprise on Kubernetes to meet availability and scaling needs, while also ensuring operability? Speakers discuss the technical patterns of use and concepts with the Kubernetes architecture and share real-world examples of how Kubernetes enables ArcGIS Enterprise to scale for unpredictable demand or a very high number of services. Learn how these settings optimize performance. Also, check out the related presentations ArcGIS Enterprise on Kubernetes: Is It for Me?, ArcGIS Enterprise on Kubernetes: Wreck It and Recover It, and ArcGIS Enterprise on Kubernetes: Tips and Tricks.

sessions.title: ArcGIS GeoBIM: Streamlining Success from Bid to Handoff
sessions.abstract: Enhance project efficiency from the initial bid to the final handoff. By integrating geospatial data with BIM, you can improve collaboration, enhance data accuracy, and streamline workflows throughout the project life cycle. This presentation demonstrates how using ArcGIS GeoBIM can lead to significant cost savings, reduced project timelines, and improved stakeholder satisfaction.

Keywords: BIM integration

sessions.title: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World: An Overview of Open Data Content
sessions.abstract: Get acquainted with some of ArcGIS Living Atlas’s most useful datasets, including global data sources such as OpenStreetMap and Overture Maps. ArcGIS Living Atlas includes a large and growing collection of open data layers that can be easily accessed in ArcGIS and used to create maps and apps, perform analysis, and generate derivative datasets and services. Learn how to access and work with this content in ArcGIS, as well as how to contribute your own authoritative data to open data projects.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World
Keywords: ArcGIS Living Atlas, ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World, Living Atlas, Open Data, Open

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro SDK for .NET: Working with Desktop Application Markup Language
sessions.abstract: In ArcGIS Pro and ArcGIS Pro add-ins, Desktop Application Markup Language (DAML) is used to define all UI elements. Join us to learn about both DAML basics and more advanced functionality.

Keywords: DAML, UI, advanced capability
RecommendedAudience: Knowledge of ArcGIS Pro SDK.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: ModelBuilder Iteration, Nesting, and Branching
sessions.abstract: Interested in using iteration, often referred to as looping or batch processing, in ModelBuilder? Using examples, speakers demonstrate how a process is executed repeatedly using iterators with different settings or datasets, how to correctly use inline variable substitution, and how to integrate an iterating nested submodel within another model. This presentation also provides best practices for iteration and introduces if-then-else conditional branching logic. Also, speakers discuss how a process can be separated into two conditional branches and then merged at the end of your model.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions: 3D Buildings
sessions.abstract: Visualize your community in an immersive way. Join us for an overview of the 3D Buildings solution to learn how your organization can develop a standard set of 3D layers.

Keywords: 3D content, 3D Buildings, Digital Twin, Planning, Development

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions: Planning and Development
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Solutions help planning agencies effectively manage land-use regulations and understand the impact of development in a community.  Join us for an overview of this collection of solutions and learn how you can manage land-use and zoning inventories, review land development, understand development impacts, and inform internal and external stakeholders.

Keywords: Planning, Development, Land Use, Zoning, Plan Review, Public Engagement

sessions.title: ArcGIS for Teams: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Extend your GIS collaborations with ArcGIS for Teams. The ArcGIS for Teams app can be used in Teams for personal, meeting, and team planning and discussion. Join us to learn about searching and sharing content and how ArcGIS content can be searched for in Copilot, as well as how groups can sketch and annotate live on an ArcGIS map during a Teams meeting.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Managing Crime Reduction Strategies
sessions.abstract: There is often a disconnect between the work that analysts do when they identify crime trends and hot spots and how that analysis is applied to police operations. In this demo theater presentation, speakers discuss how ArcGIS is used to identify, plan, monitor, and evaluate crime reduction strategies. Using the Crime Problem Management solution, police personnel can interactively create patrol areas, design the tactical plan and assign officers to problem areas, monitor ongoing police activities, and, finally, evaluate outcomes of any place-based policing strategy.

Keywords: police, law enforcement, evidence-based policing, hot spot policing

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Mission-Ready Imagery
sessions.abstract: Tracking targets across various sensors that exist on proliferated satellite constellations, ground and air sensors, and even commercial systems requires a strong data management strategy and a comprehensive imagery system. The Defense and Intelligence community has access to many sources of remotely sensed data, but curating this data to support accurate detections at scale remains a challenge. This demo theater presentation highlights areas where imagery can enhance traditional analysis, identifies potential challenges, and proposes solutions.

Keywords: Spatial Asset Management, Mission-ready imagery, Multi-sensor fusion, Satellite imagery, Airborne ISR, Remote sensing

sessions.title: Unlocking the Underground: Subsurface GIS Workflows
sessions.abstract: Dig below the surface with 3D analytic capabilities for subterranean workflows. Learn about 3D spatial queries, feature and surface intersection detection, fence diagram construction, and applications that can help you communicate effectively to your stakeholders.

sessions.title: Vision Zero in Action: Safer Streets Through Smarter Insights
sessions.abstract: Making our transportation networks safer is a spatial problem, and solving it requires a multidisciplinary, systems-based approach. This brief presentation explores how organizations are advancing Vision Zero by using location technology to analyze crash fatality networks, assess equity, prioritize projects, engage communities, and justify funding. To build a Vision Zero program, you need the tools that make it possible. Learn how spatial thinking turns bold goals into measurable impact.

sessions.abstract: Discover what’s next in high‑accuracy GNSS field data collection at this Eos Positioning Systems and Esri lunch‑and‑learn. Explore new sensor technology built for ArcGIS Field Maps and ArcGIS Survey123, including advanced RTK workflows, tilt compensation for GIS users, and a LiDAR-powered smart handle that makes mapping as-builts, assets in trenches, and other situations safer and more efficient than before. Perfect for anyone modernizing mobile data collection. Includes real user stories and new-feature previews. Lunch provided to first 100 attendees (exhibitors/sponsors/partners ineligible).

sessions.title: From Traffic Data to Mobility Insights with ArcGIS Online
sessions.abstract: Discover how organizations are transforming mobility planning and policy decisions using TomTom Traffic Analytics within ArcGIS Online. Through real-world examples from Esri distributors and their customers, learn how historical traffic insights enhance spatial analysis, support evidence-based decision-making, and reveal transportation trends. The session will also demonstrate an automated ArcGIS Notebooks workflow that integrates TomTom Traffic Analytics APIs and converts results into interactive web maps and dashboards for scalable analysis and reporting.

sessions.title: Geospatial Reasoning with AI: Implementing Agentic Workflows
sessions.abstract: Curious about how agentic AI is transforming automated geospatial workflows? This insight talk introduces agentic workflows in a GIS context, from querying feature services to chaining multistep spatial analysis. Explore targeted examples that use ArcGIS APIs and large language model (LLM) orchestration tools. Learn where agentic approaches can be applied and how to identify candidate workflows, without discussing full implementations in detail.

Keywords: agentic AI, workflow automation, reasoning, advanced analytics, automation strategies
RecommendedAudience: Experience with ModelBuilder, Python, agentic AI concepts, AI-driven process design, and spatial data analysis.

sessions.title: Policy Map Portfolios: Improving Dialog with Government Executives
sessions.abstract: Every leader must understand the community’s people, housing, infrastructure, and issues. Policy maps are powerful tools for state and local government executives to visualize and analyze these topics for informed decision-making. These maps provide a spatial representation of policy-related information, such as demographic trends, infrastructure development, and service accessibility. In this brief presentation, learn how to enhance policy development, monitor implementation progress, and ensure equitable service delivery for communities through geospatial analysis.

occurrences.room: State and Local Government Expo Theater

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World
Keywords: legislation, policymaker, broadband, demographics, homeless, advocacy, health, equity, census, ACS,

sessions.title: Next-Gen Geospatial Solutions: From System of Record to a Single Source of Truth
sessions.abstract: Explore the next generation of geospatial solutions and learn what it means to go from traditional systems of record to a true single source of truth. This presentation delves into strategies for modernizing geospatial approaches to address today’s complex business challenges while setting you up for future success. Learn how innovative solutions can extend organizational reach, improve decision-making, and empower your team to solve problems more effectively while preparing for the demands of tomorrow.

sessions.title: Advancing Community Insight: Mapping for Crisis, Equity & Social Well Being
sessions.abstract: Discover how Los Angeles County and its partners are using modern mapping to drive smarter, more equitable action. See how Architectural Resources Group, with the Los Angeles Conservancy and World Monuments Fund rapidly documented wildfire impacted heritage using official and community sourced insights. Then explore how the county’s Poverty Alleviation Initiative turned millions of data points into a compelling public dashboard that spotlights need and guides targeted investment.

Keywords: heritage, demographic

sessions.title: ArcGIS Arcade: Mapping and Visualization
sessions.abstract: Extend what is possible in ArcGIS Pro using ArcGIS Arcade—an expression language designed for use with ArcGIS. Learn how Arcade can enhance map visualization, label formatting, field calculations, attribute rules, and pop-ups.

RecommendedAudience: Beginner to intermediate experience using ArcGIS Arcade.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Business Analyst: Maximizing Workflows Using Advanced Techniques
sessions.abstract: Want to maximize the full scope of ArcGIS Business Analyst capabilities across ArcGIS? Join us to explore workflows like benchmark comparisons and suitability analysis to understand how you can use location intelligence to inform decisions. Speakers also discuss new features, such as cross-border analysis and advanced target marketing using the next generation of Tapestry. Find out how to use AI assistance to uncover workflows and map demographic attributes effectively, and learn some tips and tricks and best practices within Business Analyst.

RecommendedAudience: Experience with mapping or site selection in any Business Analyst product.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Dashboards: Efficiency Throughout the Project Life Cycle
sessions.abstract: Incorporate and maintain transparency throughout the project life cycle by using ArcGIS Dashboards. With real-time data visualization and analytics, you can effectively monitor project progress, track key performance indicators, and facilitate informed decision-making. This presentation demonstrates several practical applications, illustrating how dashboards improve collaboration, increase transparency, and strengthen stakeholder engagement.

Keywords:
ArcGIS Dashboards
Project management
Project lifecycle

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Administration Workflows for ArcGIS Data Pipelines
sessions.abstract: Are you an ArcGIS Enterprise administrator looking to streamline complex data integration workflows? ArcGIS Data Pipelines is a no-code, visual data engineering capability that makes it easy to prepare and integrate data for mapping and analytics. This presentation outlines the requirements for installing and configuring ArcGIS Data Pipelines in ArcGIS Enterprise. Speakers also demonstrate optimization techniques using configurable server resource properties and explore strategies for scaling your Data Pipelines Server site to improve performance and automate workflows effectively.

RecommendedAudience: Suggested experience with administering ArcGIS Enterprise.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Best Practices for Configuring Backups
sessions.abstract: If you have ArcGIS Enterprise, you need a clear strategy for backup and recovery. How do you know which method is best for your organization? Technical support experts share best practices for configuring backups using the Web GIS Disaster Recovery (WebGISDR) tool.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Data Storage Strategies
sessions.abstract: Data is an integral part of ArcGIS, providing the foundation for your mapping, analysis, and decision-making workflows. Where you store your data impacts how you access, edit, validate, secure, and scale it. This presentation highlights the storage options that are available in ArcGIS, from ArcGIS Data Store and enterprise geodatabases to cloud storage and cloud data warehouses. Get a high-level understanding of the data storage landscape in ArcGIS Enterprise and how you can use these different storage options in your own organization.

RecommendedAudience: Experience working with data in ArcGIS Enterprise.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Field Maps: Optimizing Utility Network Workflows for the Field
sessions.abstract: Support for utility networks in the field has expanded, enabling teams to view, edit, and perform traces—even without a connection. Learn how to optimize utility network experiences in the field to support common workflows such as locating assets, capturing and documenting assets with high precision, and managing inspections. Gain insights into upcoming enhancements that will further support utility workflows.

Keywords: utility network
RecommendedAudience: Experience with utility networks.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Hub: Collaborating and Engaging with Stakeholders and Community Members
sessions.abstract: Seeking to strengthen community ties? ArcGIS Hub helps organizations collaborate and engage with their communities. Learn how to manage and organize real-world projects and initiatives, communicate progress toward shared goals, and use built-in engagement tools to foster stakeholder and community participation. Speakers highlight ArcGIS Hub projects, initiatives, and the latest engagement tools, such as Hub events and discussion boards.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Instant Apps: Configuring an AI Chat Web App with the Data Explorer Template
sessions.abstract: Preview the new Data Explorer template in ArcGIS Instant Apps! This template, the first Instant Apps template to use an AI chat window to explore maps and layers, is in development. When you create a web app with the Data Explorer template, you can use natural language processing in a chat, which enables you to easily search and filter locations, run analyses, or summarize data on a map. Speakers explain how to prepare the required dataset, properly configure the app, and build the optimal app interface for your audience.

Keywords: ArcGIS Instant Apps, Web app,  Data Explorer, AI, natural language, beta

sessions.title: ArcGIS Monitor: Enabling Observability in ArcGIS Systems
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Monitor provides observability for ArcGIS systems through holistic monitoring of ArcGIS Enterprise, its infrastructure, and web services from ArcGIS Online and third-party enterprise sites. In this technical session, explore techniques and strategies with analysis views and component relationship graphs that enable observability across multiple enterprise GIS deployments. You will also preview the ArcGIS Monitor Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server.

Keywords: Observability, Monitoring, Logs, ArcGIS Enterprise
RecommendedAudience: Knowledge of ArcGIS Enterprise

sessions.title: ArcGIS Notebooks: Getting Started with the ArcGIS Notebooks Assistant
sessions.abstract: Looking for coding assistance? Look no further! In this presentation, you will learn about the ArcGIS Notebooks assistant, a new coding assistant that can help you write better Python code and automate your workflows in ArcGIS Notebooks.

Keywords: AI, Python, Assistant

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise: An Overview of ArcGIS Arcade
sessions.abstract: Want to know how to enhance your maps and apps by using ArcGIS Arcade? If so, you cannot miss this presentation, which will empower you to understand and use Arcade expressions effectively in an ArcGIS Online or ArcGIS Enterprise environment. Join us and learn how Arcade can help you tell engaging stories, create rich visualizations, and provide dynamic content for your audience. Gain best practices as well as tips and tricks to fine-tune your maps and keep them fast and responsive.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online: The Road Ahead
sessions.abstract: Whether you are a GIS professional, a decision maker, or just interested in the future of GIS technology, join us for an exciting look at the road ahead for ArcGIS Online. Speakers provide an overview of the key areas that the product team is working on, including enhancements, new features, and strategic directions.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: An Introduction to Presentations
sessions.abstract: Showcase your data and tell compelling stories with presentations in ArcGIS Pro. A presentation enables you to create a collection of pages using maps, images, videos, and text that you can display in full-screen mode from ArcGIS Pro. Learn the workflow to create a presentation, from adding pages to adjusting properties. Also, find out how to create a set of pages that transition and interact well. Discover how to customize the full-screen experience and how to share the presentation.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Configuring and Using Portal Projects
sessions.abstract: Have you heard about ArcGIS Pro portal projects? Learn how to configure portal projects appropriately to accommodate single-user workflows, and understand how to set up sharing groups in your ArcGIS Enterprise portal so that multiple ArcGIS Pro users can work on the same project at the same time.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Editing and Managing Parcels
sessions.abstract: Do you create and edit parcels using ArcGIS Pro? Join us to learn how to efficiently manage, edit, and ensure the integrity of your parcel data in ArcGIS Pro. This presentation covers common parcel editing workflows such as merging and splitting parcels, maintaining historic parcels, and visualizing parcel lineage within record-driven workflows. Discover best practices for ensuring data integrity and resolving common data quality issues. Speakers demonstrate the use of parcel fabric as the most efficient way to maintain and edit parcel data.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with geodatabase design, ArcGIS Enterprise, and geoprocessing.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Python Map Automation Beyond the Basics
sessions.abstract: Ready to learn advanced capabilities of the arcpy.mp module in ArcGIS Pro? The arcpy.mp API is growing well beyond the capabilities of arcpy.mapping. Join us to discover the latest improvements to the API. Also, learn about extended support for Esri’s Cartographic Information Model (CIM), which includes new entry points for easier and better-performing Python CIM access.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Tips and Tricks
sessions.abstract: Discover something new with ArcGIS Pro—whether you are just getting started or have used it for years. In this presentation, learn tips and tricks to boost efficiency and productivity. Topics include mapping, data and content management, analysis, and sharing. Speaking of sharing, you also get the opportunity to share your favorite ArcGIS Pro tips with others!

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions: Public Works and Transportation
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Solutions help public works and transportation agencies maintain right-of-way assets, coordinate capital investments, and engage the public in effective ways. Join us for an overview of this collection of solutions, and learn how you can streamline asset inventories, monitor operations in real time, and share essential services with the public.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Spatial Analyst: Best Practices Beyond the Defaults
sessions.abstract: Is your raster analysis taking too long or generating unexpected results? This demo theater presentation goes beyond the basics to provide tips and tricks for effective spatial analysis. Learn why properly setting geoprocessing environments such as cell size, mask, and extent is the most critical step that many users skip. Discover how to choose the right tool for the job—from density analysis to zonal operations. Learn about performance optimization, using parallel processing and GPUs, and avoiding common projection issues. Leave with actionable techniques to make your workflows smarter, faster, and more accurate.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Workflow Manager: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Optimize your workflows with ArcGIS Workflow Manager, a scalable work management system for ArcGIS Enterprise and ArcGIS Online. The Workflow Manager framework lets organizations capture and standardize repeatable processes, incorporate automation to streamline workflows, communicate effectively, and encourage adherence to standards and best practices. Join us to learn what Workflow Manager is and how it can boost both GIS and non-GIS operations. Topics include a product overview, key concepts and capabilities, common use cases, building workflows, and monitoring and managing work.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with ArcGIS Enterprise and the services-based architecture.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Extending Configurable Apps
sessions.abstract: Go beyond the basics and unlock advanced customization options in ArcGIS configurable apps. This technical session explores extensibility strategies such as enhancing app design by combining ArcGIS Arcade, CSS, and HTML, using notebooks and web tools for data processing, and streamlining workflows with webhooks. This presentation is perfect for power users and developers who want to elevate their app configurations.

Keywords: Customization, styling, interactive UI, low-code, advanced configuration, user experience design, dynamic content, app scalability, performance optimization, maintainability
RecommendedAudience: Knowledge of ArcGIS configurable apps, Arcade scripting, HTML and CSS basics, and app customization.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Integrate a First-Person Perspective into Your GIS with Oriented Imagery
sessions.abstract: What if you could add geospatial context to street-level 360 images of your assets before sharing with stakeholders? Inspect full-resolution drone imagery of your sites from an intuitive perspective, in the context of vector data and 3D mesh? Automatically manage mobile phone images from fieldworkers and integrate them into your GIS? Learn how you can use the oriented imagery capability of ArcGIS to manage and share difficult-to-map imagery, then inspect and analyze full-resolution source images from an intuitive, first-person perspective—all in the context of 3D data, vector data, and more.

Keywords: Oriented Imagery
RecommendedAudience: Attendees with all skill levels are welcomed.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Map Wizardry
sessions.abstract: Sit back and enjoy this inspiring cartographic ride! In this presentation, speakers share some truly mesmerizing maps to demonstrate the cartographic power and flexibility of the ArcGIS system. Go beyond the defaults and discover how you can bend ArcGIS to your every cartographic whim to bring data to life using innovative and imaginative techniques. See maps that you maybe have not even considered, let alone thought possible, and learn ideas and workflows that you can use creatively in your own work. Get inspired to create compelling maps and make them sing and dance in new and exciting ways.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Maritime Security
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS web applications and analytical tools can provide your organization with robust maritime and border security. Speakers demonstrate various methods for integrating and analyzing disparate static and dynamic data with ArcGIS, including best practices for using authoritative datasets (organizational and partner) and incorporating your own data layers and feeds. Additionally, speakers provide an overview for how to communicate with leadership by distilling the information into actionable decisions for force readiness and mission status.

Keywords: Maritime, National Security

sessions.title: Automating and Integrating Field Operations
sessions.abstract: Discover how to automate and integrate field operations. This presentation explores how automation reduces manual steps, supports collaboration, and provides live visibility into progress. Learn how Esri’s field apps connect with business systems, updating data across platforms like work order systems, AutoCAD, Microsoft tools, and more to streamline work before, during, and after fieldwork.

sessions.title: Building Future Ready Services Through Real Time and Intelligent Operations
sessions.abstract: Discover how modern technologies help organizations move from reactive fixes to future ready services. You will learn how combining historical trends, real time inputs, and connected systems supports smarter planning and clearer decision making. This session highlights how advanced technologies strengthen operational awareness, improve long term preparation, and help teams communicate more confidently with leadership and the community.

Keywords: Predictive Analytics, Digital Twin, Service Foresight, Risk Prioritization, Operational Planning
RecommendedAudience: Ideal for sanitation, utilities, facilities, and innovation teams, along with GIS professionals working on analytics or three dimensional projects.

sessions.title: Change Management for Continuous Adoption
sessions.abstract: Launching a GIS solution is just the beginning—true success depends on sustained adoption. This brief presentation explores proven change management strategies that help organizations move beyond deployment and embed ArcGIS into everyday workflows. Learn how to communicate benefits clearly, engage stakeholders, secure leadership support, and provide training and resources that build confidence. Speakers share practical approaches for fostering a culture of continuous learning, reducing resistance, and ensuring your GIS investment delivers long-term value.

Keywords: organizational culture, stakeholder alignment, adoption strategy, user engagement, leadership buy-in, behavioral change, training programs, performance improvement, continuous support, value realization

sessions.title: Coastal Resilience Through Indigenous Knowledge and Citizen Science
sessions.abstract: Explore how geospatial tools strengthen coastal resilience by centering community participation, local knowledge, and ecosystem-scale monitoring. Attendees will learn how collaborative data collection through citizen science, Indigenous partnerships, and ecological mapping enhances coastal and climate decision-making. The session highlights approaches that integrate field observations, drones, remote sensing, traditional ecological knowledge, and public-facing maps to produce actionable insights and inclusive GIS workflows that support nature-based solutions and community engagement.

sessions.title: Community Engagement for Environmental Policy
sessions.abstract: Explore how geospatial innovation empowers organizations to build more transparent, equitable, and community driven environmental programs. By combining spatial analysis, real time data, and modern GIS workflows, organizations can identify service gaps, strengthen public health and sustainability initiatives, and foster deeper collaboration with residents and partners. From identifying equitable access to waste services to advancing sustainable pest management, geospatial innovation drives smarter policies, stronger partnerships, and measurable impact.

Keywords: pesticide, pest, solid waste, environmental justice

sessions.title: Configuring ArcGIS for High-Accuracy Data Success in the Field and the Office
sessions.abstract: It is no secret that high-accuracy data can now be collected in GIS, passed between platforms, and made accessible across project teams—but do you ever wonder how well your data’s spatial integrity handles challenges with projecting on the fly and other mysterious transformations? This technical session covers best practices for configuring ArcGIS for use in the office, the field, and across other platforms, such as Autodesk, giving your engineers, project managers, and builders the confidence that they need to make mission-critical decisions.

Keywords: Surveying, land surveying, engineering, AEC, construction, infrastructure, public works, transportation

sessions.title: Coordinating Conservation Operations with GIS
sessions.abstract: Through field mobility, real-time situational awareness, and location-based coordination, GIS helps conservation organizations balance competing demands on the landscape, whether managing recreational use against ecological sensitivity or deploying resources where threats to species and habitat are greatest. This demo theater presentation highlights how GIS brings these layers together into a single operational view by connecting people, assets, and environmental conditions to location.

occurrences.room: GIS for Good Demo Expo Theater

Keywords: Field operations, land management, wildlife management, parks management, protected areas management

sessions.title: Enabling Activity-Based Intelligence Through GIS
sessions.abstract: Discover how ArcGIS can transform movement data from cell phone locations, GPS tags, or AI video detections into actionable insights. Learn how powerful ArcGIS tools can analyze activity-based intelligence, enhance data collection processes, and visualize results in real time across desktop and web platforms. Join us to master techniques for characterizing activity and solving complex analytical problems with ease.

Keywords: Movement, ArcGIS Pro, GPS, Track, ABI, Activity, Defense, Intelligence

sessions.title: Esri U.S. Demographics: What’s New
sessions.abstract: Learn what’s new in the latest U.S. update for Esri Demographics. Speakers introduce new data, measures, and tutorials for the latest data release and show how you can get started working with it in both ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Pro.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World
Keywords: ArcGIS Living Atlas, Esri Demographics, Business Analyst, Living Atlas, Tapestry

sessions.title: From Drones to Satellites: Measuring Habitat and Species Health
sessions.abstract: This session showcases how drones and satellite imagery are advancing habitat and species health assessments in the face of environmental change. Speakers demonstrate UAV‑derived elevation models for projecting coastal inundation and restoration planning, alongside comparisons of drone and satellite data to evaluate vegetation structure and wildlife habitat quality. Together, you will learn how GIS integrates imagery into spatial analysis workflows to improve accuracy, overcome data limitations, and guide conservation action.

Keywords: Conservation, UAV‑derived, satellite imagery

sessions.title: How to Build Effective Health Decision-Support Tools
sessions.abstract: Data is the foundation for effective health policy. This session highlights how organizations build data hubs, and health data exploration tools to transform complex datasets into meaningful insight. The American Cancer Society uses data visualization and storytelling tools to reveal disparities and guide screening strategies to reduce the burden of lung cancer. Nurse-Family Partnership uses the Family Opportunity Atlas to identify who needs support and where to focus efforts.

sessions.title: Improving Housing Policy in Five Steps
sessions.abstract: As communities struggle to provide housing capacity, particularly affordable housing, for residents, planning and housing departments are being called upon to provide feasible solutions to the shortage. Learn how a geographic and data-driven approach to housing can offer a viable policy for communities that meets the needs of residents, developers, and other stakeholders.

occurrences.room: State and Local Government Expo Theater

Keywords: housing, economic development, planning, equity, HUD, brownfields, community development, zoning, Transit-Oriented Development

sessions.title: Indoor GIS: Bring Reality Capture Indoors with 360 Imagery and Lidar
sessions.abstract: Interested in learning how to use 360-degree photos and lidar scans inside your facilities? Discover how to create floor-aware indoor maps and import 360-degree photos as a layer to your indoor GIS using ArcGIS Indoors. Speakers share best practices for working with lidar data to produce indoor floor plans and building an oriented imagery dataset with a 360-degree photo series. Find out how you can import 360-degree photos from reality capture platforms or directly from consumer-grade 360-degree cameras, align them to indoor floor plans, and visualize spaces in 2D, 2.5D, and 3D.

sessions.title: Lidar Classification and Feature Extraction
sessions.abstract: Discover how to classify LAS-formatted point cloud data using interactive and automated tools to identify ground, buildings, vegetation, power lines, rail tracks, and other assets. Explore how deep learning solutions can improve object detection and classification. Learn methods for extracting features, such as 3D buildings and trees, from classified point clouds, and understand how to integrate these features into 3D basemaps.
