Gain practical insights about techniques that support infrastructure maintenance and urban planning, and learn how to transform raw data into valuable information.

Keywords: Lidar, Point Clouds, 3D Analyst
RecommendedAudience: Experience with the ArcGIS 3D Analyst extension in ArcGIS Pro.

sessions.title: Map Viewer: Tips and Tricks
sessions.abstract: Join this fun and informative journey through Map Viewer to explore diverse layer styles, informative pop-ups, and more. This presentation aims to show you something that you did not know previously and take your Map Viewer knowledge to the next level.

sessions.title: National Environmental and Infrastructure Intelligence for Government
sessions.abstract: Brazil's federal agencies use ArcGIS to integrate environmental and transportation data into national platforms that support policy development, regulatory oversight, and long‑term planning. See how interactive maps and dashboards improve their transparency, coordination, and evidence‑based decision‑making across government.

sessions.title: Nonprofit Volunteer Management Done Right
sessions.abstract: Discover how a Team Rubicon, a veteran-led humanitarian organization that serves global communities before, during, and after disasters, uses GIS to improve volunteer impact. Learn shared strategies for streamlined field data, real‑time awareness, and informed action. Take home repeatable workflows and tactics to scale volunteer impact, coordinate teams, and accelerate decisions.

Keywords: volunteer management, npo, field

sessions.title: Operational Excellence in Renewable Energy
sessions.abstract: This session presents two examples of how GIS supports operational visibility, stakeholder alignment, and day-to-day decision support in renewable energy programs.

sessions.title: Pattern Analysis: Finding Clusters Using Statistics and Machine Learning
sessions.abstract: Whenever we look at a map, we naturally organize and cluster what we see to make better sense of it. Join us for an introduction to tools in ArcGIS Pro that identify clusters in space, attribute values, and time. This presentation discusses statistical techniques such as hot spot and outlier analysis, including new methods to assess sensitivity to attribute uncertainty in your results, and machine learning clustering techniques that include density-based and spatial multivariate clustering. Speakers explain each technique conceptually and describe its use in workflows.

sessions.title: Smarter Roads Happen in Real Time
sessions.abstract: Discover how transportation agencies are using ArcGIS to power real-time operations, from multimodal city traffic management to statewide winter response. Learn how the City of San Marcos, TX integrates bus tracking, road and rail closures, cameras, micromobility, and safety alerts, while the PA Turnpike’s digital toolkit unifies incident reporting, plow performance, and speed restrictions. See how scalable, map centric solutions improve safety, mobility, and data driven decision making for agencies of any size.

sessions.title: Unlock the Modern Toolkit for Planners
sessions.abstract: As more and more tasks fall under the responsibility of planners, the need for modern tools increases. Learn how planners can increase workload capacity with tools that complete common tasks quickly and simultaneously improve the experience of residents and stakeholders.

Keywords: zoning, regional planning
RecommendedAudience: urban planners, community leaders

sessions.title: Water Resources Symposium
sessions.abstract:
Learn about Esri’s latest technologies and capabilities, specifically tailored to the needs of water resources professionals. Watch live demonstrations to see these powerful tools in action. Take advantage of the valuable opportunity to expand your knowledge, connect with fellow professionals, and engage with industry experts on a variety of topics, including data availability, advanced hydro tools, wetland modeling, linear referencing, and source water assessment.



 

Reservations will be held until five minutes prior to the start time before seating will be opened to attendees in the on-site queue. If attendees with a reservation remove the session from their schedule, the next person on the wait list will automatically receive that open reservation. Wait lists will only be available before the conference. On-site walk-in attendance may be available dependent on room capacity and will be allowed on a first-come, first-served basis.

ReservationRequired: This session requires a reservation to attend. Please use Add to Schedule to reserve your spot or Join Wait List to be added to the queue.

sessions.title: Web Apps: Accessibility Features, Tips, and Tricks
sessions.abstract: Designing accessible web apps ensures that all audiences can easily interact with and understand the information that you want to share. This presentation demonstrates the capabilities of ArcGIS Experience Builder, ArcGIS Instant Apps, and other useful ArcGIS web app builders. Join us to learn about optimizing support for screen readers, keyboard navigation, and other assistive technologies. Also, learn about testing for accessibility and the tools and techniques that can help you develop inclusive app experiences.

Keywords: Accessibility, A11y, Section 508, 508, Web Content Accessibility Guidelines, WCAG, Americans with Disabilities Act, ADA, EN 301 549,

sessions.title: Where to Invest First: Mapping Priority Needs for Infrastructure Investment
sessions.abstract: GIS helps governments identify where infrastructure investment will matter most. First, hear from American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE) on their national-scale Report Card, a benchmark report released every four years to assesses the quality of the U.S.’s infrastructure. ASCE showcases their use of spatial analysis and storymaps to pinpoint communities most in need of funding. Then, you will hear how the State of Michigan leverages GIS tools to streamline applications for community loans for water infrastructure, to ensure they achieve more impactful allocation of infrastructure dollars.

sessions.title: Where Matters: How to Unlock the Power of Location to Advance Your Mission
sessions.abstract: Join Deloitte Geospatial Leaders for an energizing session on how organizations can unlock the power of location to address complex business challenges. Drawing on Deloitte’s geospatial experience, its alliance with Esri, and AI-enabled, human-in-the-loop approach, this presentation will highlight how geospatial technology can improve resilience, speed decision-making, and drive mission impact. Attendees will also hear about real-world examples—from AI-powered asset and vegetation inspections to smarter permitting—that show how Geo+AI can turn location data into actionable insight at scale.
tracks: National Mapping, Navigation, and Geospatial Authorities

Topic: National Mapping, Navigation, and Geospatial Authorities

sessions.title: ArcGIS: API Integration
sessions.abstract: Are you looking to streamline operations and unlock location intelligence through integration? Join us to learn how to connect ArcGIS with enterprise systems using APIs. This brief presentation provides practical strategies for using REST and webhooks to enable real-time data exchange, automate workflows, and maintain security.

Keywords: system integration, enterprise connectivity, data interoperability, workflow automation, secure communication, real-time synchronization, application extensibility, cross-platform solutions, operational efficiency, location intelligence enablement
RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with API development, REST services, webhooks, and security best practices.

sessions.title: Intro to AI-Driven Reality Capture with GeoCam
sessions.abstract: Discover the easiest way to scale your own Reality Capture Practice. Starting from rapid single captures of any space to managing a complete approach for a large city (streets, indoors, aerial), utility networks (transmission, distribution, and stations), or full campus and indoor mapping environments.

sessions.title: Accelerating Geospatial Discovery with Agentic AI
sessions.abstract: tbd

sessions.title: A Lesson in Bringing Broadband to More Residents
sessions.abstract: From informing residents of available broadband resources to designing, constructing and delivering new fiber, mapping is a critical component for accelerating broadband equity for underserved communities. Hear how the State of Maine uses an accessible, multilingual app to connect residents with local digital literacy resources. See how the State of California deployed 8,000 mile fiber network through effective route planning, permit tracking, and construction coordination.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Data Reviewer:  Advanced Data Validation
sessions.abstract: Learn how to implement ArcGIS Data Reviewer to support your organization’s constantly evolving data quality requirements. This presentation focuses on best practices when integrating quality control capabilities that streamline maintenance workflows, improve quality, and increase transparency by highlighting data liabilities to stakeholders and data consumers.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with ArcGIS Pro and ArcGIS Data Reviewer.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise and ArcGIS Online: Image Management at Scale
sessions.abstract: Manage, analyze, and deliver imagery at scale using ArcGIS Enterprise and ArcGIS Online. This presentation covers optimized data models, cloud-optimized formats, and SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog (STAC) workflows. Discover how ArcGIS Pro, ArcPy, and the ArcGIS API for Python streamline imagery discovery and automation, as well as how scalable raster analysis turns large imagery collections into meaningful insights.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Security Best Practices
sessions.abstract: Secure your ArcGIS Enterprise deployments. Learn how to protect yourself from common threats and attacks and how to harden your environment using multiple layers of security. Speakers discuss various aspects of the environment, including the operating system, accounts, databases, the network, and firewalls. Join us to learn about security options in ArcGIS Enterprise that help protect your site from attack.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Field Maps: Enhancing Design Collaboration from Field to Office
sessions.abstract: Field and office teams are two sides of the same project story. Explore how GIS and BIM unite engineers, project managers, and field teams, empowering them to work with the same data using familiar tools. Discover new ways to coordinate construction tasks and share design data for AEC projects, and learn how ArcGIS Field Maps, ArcGIS Survey123, and Esri and Autodesk integrations support these workflows, enabling field-informed design, clearer data collection, faster delivery, and cost savings.

Keywords: BIM, CAD, AEC, Construction, Project Coordination

sessions.title: ArcGIS Instant Apps: Unlocking Web Maps with Bookmarks, Charts, Symbology, and More
sessions.abstract: Learn how to build lightweight, interactive web apps. Discover how web apps can unlock capabilities in your web map—such as symbology, pop-ups, bookmarks, and charts—to help you communicate more effectively with your audience. Speakers also discuss how web apps allow users to edit simple attributes of the data if there is an editable layer in your web map.

Keywords: ArcGIS Instant Apps, Web apps, bookmarks, charts, symbology, pop-ups, interactive, web maps

sessions.title: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World: Analyzing Urban Heat Effects
sessions.abstract: Analyze the heat island effect on urban populations around the world. Learn how you can process, analyze, and derive insights using imagery and raster data from ArcGIS Living Atlas—all within a single secure system. Discover the power of combining ArcGIS raster analysis capabilities with more than 40 years of Landsat data. Speakers also discuss how to create derivative maps and distribute insights with information products in ArcGIS Online.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World
Keywords: Imagery, Landsat, Analysis, ArcGIS Living Atlas, AI, analytics, urban, heat island

sessions.title: ArcGIS Navigator: An Introduction and What's New
sessions.abstract: Explore how ArcGIS Navigator optimizes routing for field teams with accurate, turn-by-turn navigation powered by your organization’s GIS data—even in disconnected environments. This presentation introduces the latest enhancements and also highlights Navigator’s essential capabilities, including custom routing on private roads, seamless integration with other ArcGIS field apps, and support for multiple travel modes.

Keywords: routing, navigation, locate

sessions.title: ArcGIS Notebooks: Automating Administration, Data Management, and Analysis
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Notebooks in ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise can provide orchestration of tools, processes, and resources to automate administrative, data management, and analysis workflows. This presentation details various techniques for scheduling notebook tasks, automating notebooks using webhooks, publishing notebooks as web tools, and calling notebooks programmatically using the ArcGIS API for Python.

RecommendedAudience: Basic experience with Python.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise: Automating Data Updates
sessions.abstract: The number of hosted feature layers is at an all-time high, and many of these layers are frequently updated from local data sources such as comma-separated value (CSV) files or file geodatabases. Learn how to automate data updates using the ArcGIS API for Python. Speakers discuss how to create a notebook that appends a file geodatabase to an existing hosted feature layer, enabling you to add new records and update existing data.

RecommendedAudience: Experience creating and managing hosted feature layers.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise: Best Practices for Distributed Collaboration
sessions.abstract: Are you implementing distributed collaboration? Do you want to know more about communication, syncing, and identifying issues within a distributed collaboration? Learn from Esri technical support experts to understand how they identify network, data, or component issues. These experts share the questions, tests, and tools that they use to identify a problem in a distributed collaboration.

Keywords: Collaboration, Distributed Collaboration, ArcGIS Enterprise, ArcGIS Online
RecommendedAudience: Knowledge of ArcGIS Enterprise or ArcGIS Online.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise: Optimizing Web Maps and Layers
sessions.abstract: Want tips for optimizing maps and layers in ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise? Join us to learn about preparing content for publishing and understanding whether a tile or feature layer should be published. Also, discover how optimizing published web maps can improve performance.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online: Managing Updates to Hosted Feature Layers
sessions.abstract: Ensure seamless updates and improved performance in your hosted feature layers. Whether you are a novice or experienced ArcGIS Online user, you will not want to miss this presentation. Gain practical insights to enhance your data management processes, exploring essential techniques, such as the view swap strategy, and methods to apply edits, append, and overwrite.

RecommendedAudience: Experience creating hosted feature layers.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro Editing: Managing Feature Attributes and Related Data
sessions.abstract: Want to elevate your editing game? Learn best practices for managing feature attributes and related data for new and existing features using the ArcGIS Pro editor. Speakers also discuss topics such as configuring feature templates, working with relationships, using attribute rules, and applying contingent attribute values.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with editing in ArcGIS Pro and working with attributes.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Building Geoprocessing Tools
sessions.abstract: Geoprocessing tools provide an integrated experience for analysts to perform data management and spatial analysis in ArcGIS. When you create a geoprocessing tool, others can reuse your methods and workflows with their own data from an easy-to-use and familiar interface. Speakers describe how to create and design geoprocessing tools, choose appropriate parameters, write custom validation routines to fine-tune the user experience, and author help so that the tool can be used correctly by others. Approaches for debugging, error handling, and messaging are also reviewed.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Detecting Feature Changes and Applying Updates Using Conflation Tools
sessions.abstract: Maintaining up-to-date GIS data is critical to spatial analysis and quality mapping. Change detection and updating data with new spatial and attribute information can be challenging and costly. This presentation introduces the Conflation geoprocessing tools in ArcGIS Pro, which use feature matching algorithms to find spatially corresponding features between datasets and perform change detection, attribute transfer, and spatial adjustment. Speakers demonstrate workflows that involve both automated processes and interactive review and corrections, which result in improved data quality.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Entering Parcels with COGO
sessions.abstract: Interested in learning the many ways that you can enter COGO (coordinate geometry) dimensions for parcels in ArcGIS Pro? Join us to learn the most common data-entry workflows and tips and tricks for data entry in the traverse grid. Learn how to use the COGO Reader tool to automatically extract COGO dimensions from scanned, legal deeds (United States only) and learn how to create a parcel traverse by manually entering COGO dimensions. Additionally, speakers explore topics such as ground-to-grid correction, editing constraints, updating COGO from geometry, and finding features with incorrect COGO values.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with editing in ArcGIS Pro and coordinate geometry (COGO) measurements.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: ModelBuilder Tips and Tricks
sessions.abstract: Want to know more about ModelBuilder, how to think about modeling problems, and how to know which tools are best suited for your workflows? Through real-world examples, speakers share tips and tricks for using inline variable substitution, creating choice lists and restricting input type, understanding compatible data types of a variable and the actual data, recasting data types, using model-specific tools, creating finished outputs, finding help, and sharing models.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions: Gas Utility Solutions
sessions.abstract: Need more reliable insight into your gas assets to improve safety and operational efficiency? Join us for an overview of the gas utility solutions that help organizations manage authoritative asset inventories, support inspections, and conduct gas leak surveys with location-based workflows.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions: State and Local Government
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Solutions help state and local governments improve operations, gain new insights, and enhance services provided to the public. Join us for an overview of this collection of solutions and learn how you can use them to maximize the investment that you are making in location-based data and ArcGIS.

sessions.title: ArcGIS StoryMaps Frames: Mobile-Based Storytelling
sessions.abstract: Expand your ArcGIS StoryMaps experience with frames. Create concise, engaging, mobile-first stories that are easy to build and simple to share across platforms. Discover how educators, utilities, and local governments use frames to highlight key updates, showcase map-based insights, and connect more effectively with their communities.

Keywords: Frames, short-form, mobile
RecommendedAudience: Experience with communication strategy and design for mobile devices.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Survey123: Advanced Smart Form Design Techniques
sessions.abstract: Want to advance your XLSForm skills? This presentation is for existing ArcGIS Survey123 users who are ready to expand their use of XLSForms. Learn advanced XLSForm design techniques to take full advantage of the Survey123 smart form capabilities.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with XLSForm design.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Velocity: Public Safety Workflows
sessions.abstract: With the arrival of ArcGIS Velocity for ArcGIS Enterprise, you can now implement many public safety workflows that require real-time data and analysis in your infrastructure. In this demo theater presentation, speakers discuss many of these different workflows, including Watch Center, the Team Awareness Kit (TAK) connector, and aggregating feeds.

Keywords: Real-time, Public Safety

sessions.title: ArcGIS Workflow Manager: Orchestrating Work Across ArcGIS
sessions.abstract: Most end-to-end processes in ArcGIS span various teams, products, and environments. ArcGIS Workflow Manager provides a no-code or low-code experience to incorporate automation, enabling you to seamlessly orchestrate work across ArcGIS products and ArcGIS Solutions as well as third-party systems. This presentation discusses the common field-to-office patterns that take advantage of advanced automation functionality, such as webhooks, web requests, and geoprocessing services that directly interact with ArcGIS Field Maps and other ArcGIS software.

sessions.title: ArcGIS for Excel: An Overview
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS for Excel enables seamless integration between ArcGIS and Microsoft Excel. This technical session is intended for users of both Excel and ArcGIS, whether you are looking to map tabular or ArcGIS data within Excel, or you are an ArcGIS user seeking to enhance your workflow with Excel’s capabilities. Discover the use of geocoding to enable spatial analysis of Excel data, as well as methods for aggregating, filtering, and sharing this data. Also, learn about functionalities such as editing features and attributes that are synchronized with ArcGIS feature layers.

sessions.title: ArcGIS for SharePoint: Going Spatial
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS for SharePoint enables the integration of maps, spatial data, and GIS functionality into SharePoint sites and applications. Explore how to convert SharePoint lists into point, polyline, or polygon maps and how to import ArcGIS data. The presentation covers functions such as geotagging, geosearch, and EXIF data extraction. Learn about the latest updates, including the ability to geotag by dragging documents to a map, adding document links to layers that are shared with ArcGIS, and creating, editing, and deleting features within ArcGIS for SharePoint.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Getting Started with the ArcGIS Documentation Assistant
sessions.abstract: Find the answers you need, faster! The ArcGIS documentation assistant uses AI to help you query ArcGIS documentation. Experience the documentation assistant in action, and then learn how you can access it in your organization and apply best practices to get the most out of it.

Keywords: AI, assistant

sessions.title: Big Data and ArcGIS GeoAnalytics: An Overview
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS GeoAnalytics tools allow you to analyze large datasets using a Spark environment. Learn how to build big data solutions, integrate data sources, and perform spatial analysis. This presentation highlights ArcGIS GeoAnalytics Engine, a Python-based analytics library that enables you to aggregate, analyze, and build big data workflows with Spark, within your own environment and the cloud. Speakers also introduce recent features for raster analysis and contrast the different GeoAnalytics products so that you know which solution is best for your organization.

sessions.title: Bridging Analysis and Design for Urban Design and Planning
sessions.abstract: Provide 2D or 3D context with analysis for a site anywhere in the world. This presentation demonstrates how you can use ArcGIS Pro and ArcGIS Online to quickly reproduce the existing conditions of a site in 3D. As well as creating a visualization of tangible and intangible assets, you can also include analysis to provide information about factors like proximity to public transit, food deserts, density of vacancy, median income, and so on. This information is then incorporated into a design environment to provide a greater understanding of the site context.

Keywords: CAD

sessions.title: Build It Faster: AI-Assisted Geospatial Apps for Defense Missions
sessions.abstract: Defense missions move quickly, but building the geospatial apps that support them often requires time and specialized development skills. This demo theater presentation shows how AI-assisted development with ArcGIS can accelerate the process. Learn how AI helps to generate code, connect data, and configure workflows so that you can rapidly build and deploy geospatial apps that support operational needs across the geospatial enterprise.

Keywords:
AI-assisted development
Agentic AI
Data integration
Mission apps
Geospatial app development

sessions.title: Creating a Composite Index: Combining Variables to Make Better Decisions
sessions.abstract: Metrics such as equity, vulnerability, sustainability, and risk are crucial for organizations to evaluate needs, track progress, and allocate resources. Creating these metrics involves combining multiple variables into a composite index, which may seem straightforward but can present challenges. This presentation teaches you best practices for using the Calculate Composite Index tool in ArcGIS Pro, leaving you equipped with the skills to create robust indices to help make better decisions.

sessions.title: Data as Advocacy, Community‑Led Policy Change
sessions.abstract: See how GIS helps nonprofits expose inequities, influence policy, and drive smarter community investments. Through two powerful examples, learn how nonprofits can leverage the same mapping and analysis tools governments use to justify decisions, and use them to advocate back for their causes. Attendees will leave with practical strategies to build evidence, strengthen coalitions, and champion more equitable outcomes.

Keywords: housing, nonprofit, policy, equity

sessions.title: Drone Reality Mapping: A Complete Workflow from Capture to Insights
sessions.abstract: Drones can offer a clear, accurate, and up-to-date view of the world. This presentation goes beyond the basics to guide you through a practical end-to-end workflow—from flight planning, image collection, and processing into 2D and 3D data products through reality mapping to insight extraction and dissemination. Gain valuable knowledge that you can apply to your organization's specific needs.

RecommendedAudience: Basic understanding of drone mapping.

sessions.title: Effective Strategies for Connecting Residents to Health Resources
sessions.abstract: GIS can be used as a tool to expand access to essential services and communicate community health action. See how Linn County’s Resource Hub modernizes how residents find food, childcare, and basic needs support through an interactive, continuously updated platform. In San Bernardino County, ambassadors use real time GIS insights to guide outreach, track engagement, and address disparities in underserved neighborhoods.

sessions.title: Geocoding: What’s New
sessions.abstract: Curious about the latest advancements in ArcGIS geocoding? Learn about topics such as geocoding with ArcGIS Pro and ArcGIS Online, interactive geocoding, batch geocoding, address rematching, custom locators, and new features. Also, get recommendations for extending your learning with workshops and sessions that go into more detail on the topics presented here.

Keywords: Geocoding, Locators, Addresses
RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with ArcGIS Pro.

sessions.title: Integrating ArcGIS with Other Business Systems
sessions.abstract: Realize the full potential of ArcGIS across your organization or enterprise. This presentation explores approaches for integrating ArcGIS with business systems, including enterprise asset management (EAM), customer relationship management (CRM), enterprise resource planning (ERP), and enterprise service management (ESM). Learn key integration patterns across applications, services, and data, and discover how a native ServiceNow integration brings geospatial capabilities directly into enterprise workflows.

Keywords: System integration, interoperability, enterprise workflows, API connectivity, data synchronization, EAM, CRM, CAMA, ERP, technical patterns
RecommendedAudience: Experience with enterprise systems, ArcGIS architecture, APIs, and business systems like ERP or CRM.

sessions.title: Maintaining Metadata and Catalog Standards
sessions.abstract: Wondering where to start with metadata standards? Managing metadata is complex, especially when navigating multiple standards such as International Organization of Standardization (ISO), Federal Geographic Data Committee (FGDC), Infrastructure for Spatial Information in the European Community (INSPIRE), and Data Catalog Vocabulary (DCAT)! This presentation demonstrates how the ArcGIS metadata XML format supports and transforms metadata to maintain specific metadata and catalog standards. Learn about the newest metadata style, Dublin Core+, a standards-based style without the complexity.

Keywords: metadata, standards, ISO, OGC, XML, ISO 19115, FGDC, DCAT, Inspire,

sessions.title: Modernizing Public Works Operations With Integrated Asset And Workforce Insights
sessions.abstract: See how public works teams modernize operations by unifying assets, field activities, and workforce performance in a shared operational view. You will learn how digital transformation efforts, integrated field tools, and automation reduce paper, break down silos, and give leaders clear insight into service levels across teams, facilities, and lines of business.

Keywords: Workforce Insights, Service Performance, Operational Modernization, Cross Department Coordination, Executive Visibility
RecommendedAudience: Well suited for operations leaders, supervisors, performance teams, and GIS professionals supporting enterprise programs.

sessions.title: Plan Smarter with 3D Scenario Modeling
sessions.abstract: Traditional comprehensive plan development often produces slow feedback and fragmented analysis.By combining all data sources into one system, planners can analyze changes much more quickly and efficiently and deliver clearer options to the public. Learn how planners can integrate multiple factors like zoning and land use into a single platform to test development scenarios in and gain public feedback.

Keywords: scenario model, scenario planning, 3d, zoning, land use, public feedback, engagement
RecommendedAudience: community leader, planner

sessions.title: Planning for Utility Network Migration: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Are you prepared to migrate to a utility network? In this brief presentation, Esri experts share their experiences working with users, as they discuss successes and give recommendations for planning a migration project and making choices that increase the odds of success when approaching this significant data migration.

Keywords: utility network migration, planning, best practices, risk mitigation
RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with migration planning, data modeling, and utility network concepts.

sessions.title: Revealing Access Gaps: GIS Insights for More Equitable Communities
sessions.abstract: Discover how GIS uncovers hidden inequities and empowers smarter, fairer decision making. This session highlights how automated analysis, authoritative data, and ArcGIS tools reveal where access to essential services falls short—and where strategic investment can create meaningful change. Learn scalable, repeatable methods any organization can use to advance equity and improve community outcomes.

Keywords: service deserts, environmental justice, disadvantaged communities

sessions.title: Strategies for Delivery Enterprise-Wide Asset Management
sessions.abstract: Learn how two organizations modernized their public works operations using GIS to streamline asset management, enhance service request workflows, and support data driven decision making. Each will demonstrate how ArcGIS tools improved accuracy, transparency, and operational efficiency by consolidating data, visualizing performance in real time, enabling smarter maintenance and budgeting decisions.

sessions.title: Sustainable Development Community Conversation
sessions.abstract: Join sustainable development thought leaders and GIS professionals who are using the geographic approach to unite people, empower positive change, and build a more sustainable future for all. Stop by and join in the conversation at the Spotlight Theater in the GIS for Good showcase.

occurrences.room: GIS for Good Demo Expo Theater

Keywords: Sustainability, Impact, Outcomes

sessions.title: The Planner’s Toolkit: Five Steps for Designing Thriving Communities
sessions.abstract: Discover the practical ways in which GIS is enhanced by AI to empower you in resolving housing, compliance, economic mobility, and engagement challenges. Understand how these tools strengthen future-proof development and planning and design for thriving communities. Gain practical insights to implement data-driven solutions that support resilient, inclusive communities.

Keywords: Urban Planning, Economic Development, Transit-Oriented Development, Regional Planning, Community Development, Permitting, Code enforcement, Building & safety

sessions.title: Transforming Biodiversity and Environmental Observation Workflows
sessions.abstract: This session highlights how ArcGIS is strengthening environmental and biodiversity observation workflows—from statewide biodiversity data modernization to community-led post‑disaster assessments. Learn how integrated data systems, mobile data collection, automated validation, and cloud-based sharing improve data quality, transparency, and collaboration. Through real-world examples, see how modern geospatial workflows turn raw observations into actionable information that supports conservation decisions, environmental recovery, and faster responses to urgent challenges facing species, ecosystems, and communities.

Keywords: wildlife protection, habitat management, conservation, imperiled species, natural heritage

sessions.title: Web Apps: The Road Ahead
sessions.abstract: Join this dynamic and exciting overview of the future of web applications for ArcGIS. This presentation tours multiple apps, including ArcGIS Experience Builder, ArcGIS Dashboards, ArcGIS Web Editor, ArcGIS StoryMaps, ArcGIS Instant Apps, Map Viewer, and Scene Viewer. Get a preview of what's coming in late 2026 and into 2027!

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Best Practices for Upgrading
sessions.abstract: Planning ahead when upgrading to a new release of ArcGIS Enterprise allows you to take advantage of new features and capabilities, stability enhancements, and improved functionality with ease. Learn how you can efficiently and effectively upgrade your deployment, including the steps to take before, during, and after an upgrade so that you can approach upgrades with confidence.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Enterprise: Extending with Webhooks
sessions.abstract: Discover how both organization and service webhooks function in ArcGIS Enterprise. Administrators can create organization webhooks in the ArcGIS Enterprise portal to listen for events that are related to items, users, groups, and roles. Service webhooks send events regarding feature service edits or completed geoprocessing tasks. Gain a better understanding of both organization and service webhooks by learning about their common integration and notification patterns.

sessions.title: ArcGIS GeoBIM: Optimizing BIM Workflows with 3D GIS
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS GeoBIM helps design and engineering teams collaborate by bringing geospatial context from ArcGIS together with engineering and design content from Autodesk Forma (previously ACC). In this technical session, learn how teams can locate engineering documents in geographic context, create schedules that link construction activities to engineering documentation, visualize detailed 3D construction-site logistics, capture on-site observations with ArcGIS Field Maps, and communicate project progress through collaborative web applications.

Keywords: BIM&GIS integration, CAD, Autodesk

sessions.title: ArcGIS Image Analyst: Tips and Tricks
sessions.abstract: Gain essential tips and tricks for using advanced ArcGIS Image Analyst capabilities, including efficient feature extraction, insightful time-series analysis, and practical techniques for working with synthetic aperture radar (SAR) data. Speakers provide best practices to maximize your workflows, streamline analysis, and derive valuable insights from imagery in ArcGIS.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Instant Apps: What's New and the Road Ahead
sessions.abstract: Want to quickly transform your maps into interactive, focused web apps without a single line of code? If so, join us to learn about several new and enhanced templates and features in ArcGIS Instant Apps, as well as multilingual features for greater accessibility. Speakers also discuss future capabilities, including a Data Explorer template that uses natural language processing to help your users understand the data on the map.

Keywords: ArcGIS Instant Apps, Web Apps, Templates, Web apps, roadmap, what's new, interactive, template, Data Explorer, AI, natural language, multilingual

sessions.title: ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript: Programming Patterns and Best Practices
sessions.abstract: Want to build apps with ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript? Attend this critical presentation to learn the core concepts and programming patterns for building apps with the SDK's web components. This evolution of the SDK introduces a new way to build web apps, which maximizes productivity, and is the recommended pattern for all new apps. Discover map components, which are the map, scene, and functional equivalents of legacy widgets, as well as charts and coding components. Also, speakers discuss considerations for building apps using frameworks.

RecommendedAudience: Understanding of Esri's web development offerings; experience with JavaScript.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Network Analyst: Understanding How to Route Your Fleet of Vehicles
sessions.abstract: Do you have vehicles that need to service multiple locations? There are multiple options to help you find the most cost-effective routes. This presentation examines the capabilities of the following solvers within ArcGIS Network Analyst: Route, Vehicle Routing Problem (VRP), Last Mile Delivery, and Waste Collection. After a short overview of the four solvers, you will learn how to decide which one is the best to use for your use case and then compare the capabilities, strengths, and weaknesses of each.

RecommendedAudience: Basic understanding of ArcGIS Network Analyst.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise: Optimizing ArcGIS Arcade Workflows
sessions.abstract: Enhance your maps and apps with ArcGIS Arcade. This presentation will empower you to understand and use Arcade expressions effectively in ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise. Learn how to write, test, and debug Arcade expressions; evaluate their performance impact; and explore diverse use cases such as data-driven styling, pop-up customization, and field calculation.

RecommendedAudience: Experience using ArcGIS Arcade.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro Tasks: Tips and Tricks
sessions.abstract: Want helpful best practices regarding repeatable workflows in ArcGIS Pro? Discover how you can run and design tasks that are more efficient. Topics include the step palette, selection management, verification and quality assurance, getting and setting attributes, embedding panes, using a task history table, and more.

RecommendedAudience: Experience with authoring and using ArcGIS Pro tasks.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Getting the Most from the Maplex Label Engine
sessions.abstract: The Maplex Label Engine can help you produce high-quality cartographic output. Join us to learn about this ArcGIS Pro functionality, which can optimize the positions of labels to make your maps look great. This presentation includes practical use cases for various label placement techniques, along with tips about label expressions, text formatting tags, and workflows that maximize the Maplex Label Engine's capabilities to create superior maps.

Keywords: labelling
RecommendedAudience: Experience labeling maps in ArcGIS Pro.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Python Map Automation Beyond the Basics
sessions.abstract: Ready to learn advanced capabilities of the arcpy.mp module in ArcGIS Pro? The arcpy.mp API is growing well beyond the capabilities of arcpy.mapping. Join us to discover the latest improvements to the API. Also, learn about extended support for Esri’s Cartographic Information Model (CIM), which includes new entry points for easier and better-performing Python CIM access.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Roads and Highways: Tools for Local Governments
sessions.abstract: Join us to learn about new and enhanced ArcGIS Roads and Highways tools, including the ability to combine Roads and Highways with the Address Data Management solution to maintain roadway characteristics and address information in a single environment. This presentation provides an overview of common configuration patterns, editing workflows in ArcGIS Pro and on the web, as well as analysis and reporting capabilities that use linear referenced data.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Solutions: Land Records
sessions.abstract: ArcGIS Solutions help assessment agencies maintain an inventory of property data, understand value trends, and inform the public. Join us for an overview of this collection of solutions and learn how you can streamline real-property inventories, analyze residential and agricultural parcel values, and provide engaging public access experiences.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Spatial Analyst: Finding the Best Locations Using Suitability Modeler
sessions.abstract: Suitability modeling can help in a wide variety of scenarios, from identifying the best place to site a housing development or corporate headquarters to choosing which areas to conserve for a park or wildlife habitat. Suitability Modeler’s interactive graphical user interface makes creating a suitability model easy. Learn about the iterative exploratory process of creating a model and how the immediate feedback from interactive maps, plots, and statistics helps you make more-informed decisions. Speakers also discuss submodels and the latest functionality for evaluating your models.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Best Practices and Applied Uses for Gaussian Splats
sessions.abstract: Discover how ArcGIS enables you to generate and work with Gaussian splats. This new layer type depicts the real world with extremely high fidelity and realism. Explore how this new capability can enhance your existing workflow, and learn best practices for capturing imagery with ArcGIS reality mapping applications so that you can generate optimal Gaussian splats.

Keywords: Gaussian Splats
RecommendedAudience: Attendees with all skill levels are welcomed.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Designing Thematic Maps
sessions.abstract: There is rarely a right or wrong way to map empirical data, but thematic maps can look very different as a result of your design decisions. Join us to learn how thematic map types can shape the message of your map. This presentation conveys how well-known maps and map types can be reinvented by using alternative thematic techniques. Learn how to use ArcGIS Pro mapping renderers effectively and explore workflows using ArcGIS Maps for Adobe Creative Cloud.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World
ArcGIS Maps for Adobe Creative Cloud

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Using Pretrained Deep Learning Models for Feature Extraction and Classification
sessions.abstract: Learn how ArcGIS simplifies end-to-end deep learning workflows for imagery. Discover how to use pretrained models for fast feature extraction and classification, as well as how deep learning tools work across ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Enterprise, and ArcGIS Online. Find out how AI-powered apps and new capabilities make advanced analysis easier and help you apply deep learning to your own projects.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World

sessions.title: Authoring Attribute Rules
sessions.abstract: Wishing you had an in-depth guide to attribute rules? If so, then this presentation is for you! Learn all about authoring attribute rules to enhance the editing experience and improve data quality. Starting with the basics of creating simple attribute rules using the Arcade function reference, the speakers then gradually introduce more complex Arcade expressions to develop more comprehensive attribute rules.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with geodatabases.

sessions.title: Best Practices for Scalable, Mission-Critical Systems
sessions.abstract: Building systems that deliver at scale requires more than speed—it demands performance, reliability, and thoughtful architecture. In this technical session, speakers share proven strategies for designing and maintaining mission-critical ArcGIS deployments, including considerations for software as a service (SaaS), using content delivery networks (CDNs) for global availability, and optimizing custom apps for premium experiences. Learn how to ensure scalability without sacrificing resilience or user satisfaction.

Keywords: high availability, load balancing, global reach, CDN integration, SaaS optimization, resilience, performance tuning, premium user experience
RecommendedAudience: Knowledge of ArcGIS Enterprise architecture, performance tuning and scalability strategies, and designing resilient systems.

sessions.title: Field Workflows with ArcGIS: Best Practices
sessions.abstract: Successful field operations require thoughtful planning. This presentation highlights best practices for data collection, location accuracy, and offline workflows using ArcGIS Field Maps, ArcGIS Survey123, and ArcGIS QuickCapture. Explore strategies for scaling field workflows across your organization—for security, integration with business systems, user adoption, and managing updates—whether you are working with a disconnected environment or an enterprise-scale deployment.

sessions.title: Reality Mapping at Scale: Citywide and Countrywide Mapping
sessions.abstract: Explore proven techniques for building accurate 2D and 3D representations of expansive areas—from urban environments to entire states or nations—using diverse imagery sources such as drone, aerial, and satellite data. Gain practical insights into key considerations like imagery selection, ground sampling distance, overlap, flight planning, and radiometric quality to achieve exceptional results. Experience powerful workflows in ArcGIS Reality for ArcGIS Pro and ArcGIS Reality Studio, and get an exclusive look at the latest capabilities in ArcGIS Reality Server that enable scalable, high-fidelity reality mapping.

RecommendedAudience: Basic understanding of the fundamentals of reality mapping.

sessions.title: Space-Time Analysis: Exploring Spatiotemporal Patterns and Forecasting
sessions.abstract: Working with space-time data can be challenging, but with the right tools, it unlocks solutions to today’s pressing issues. This presentation guides you in managing space-time data through space-time cubes, revealing spatiotemporal patterns. Explore new visualization techniques for space-time cubes and learn about analysis tools that offer insights into temporal dependencies within diverse datasets. Also, draw insights from historic data using ArcGIS Pro's Time Series Forecasting tools to better prepare for the future.

sessions.title: Esri UC Party at Balboa Park
sessions.abstract: Wrap up an incredible week at the 2026 Esri User Conference (Esri UC) with a festive evening featuring international cuisine, live entertainment, and activities the whole family can enjoy.

sessions.title: Aligning Geospatial and IT Strategies: Best Practices
sessions.abstract: Need help navigating the complex topics and processes involved in aligning GIS and information technology (IT) strategies? Join us to learn how to develop geospatial and IT strategies that are inclusive, agile, and aligned with business needs. Speakers also share how partnering early in the process allows teams to meet ever-changing technological needs more effectively.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Dashboards: An Overview
sessions.abstract: New to ArcGIS Dashboards? This configurable web app provides data visualization of people, services, assets, and events. Learn how to configure charts, gauges, maps, and other visual elements to reflect the status and performance of the data that matters most. Convey the activities and key indicators that are most vital to meeting your organization's objectives. Speakers also share the current road map for this product.

Keywords: Dashboards, Data Visualization, Configuration, Road Map

sessions.title: ArcGIS Dashboards: Best Practices
sessions.abstract: If you are an ArcGIS Dashboards user, do you know what makes a good dashboard? A dashboard should provide key insights at a glance and effectively communicate what is important. This presentation shares tips for getting the most out of your dashboards with best practices specific to various patterns of use. Discover how to communicate status and evaluate performance. Get ideas for new ways to present information and visualize your data effectively.

Keywords: Dashboards, Tips and Tricks, Best Practices, Data Visualization

sessions.title: ArcGIS Pro: Tips and Tricks for Working with Legends
sessions.abstract: Explore legends in ArcGIS Pro. Learn more about the components that make up legends and some of the many ways that they can be configured. Also, learn how you can use legend styles, shortcuts, and the Contents pane to streamline legend creation for future layouts.

RecommendedAudience: Experience with layouts, mapping, and visualization.

sessions.title: ArcGIS Survey123: Advanced Smart Form Design Techniques
sessions.abstract: Want to advance your XLSForm skills? This presentation is for existing ArcGIS Survey123 users who are ready to expand their use of XLSForms. Learn advanced XLSForm design techniques to take full advantage of the Survey123 smart form capabilities.

RecommendedAudience: Familiarity with XLSForm design.

sessions.title: ArcGIS for Microsoft: An Overview
sessions.abstract: Discover the value that ArcGIS for Microsoft can bring to your organization. Speakers discuss the capabilities in ArcGIS for Microsoft 365, ArcGIS for Power Platform, and ArcGIS for Microsoft Fabric that enable you to take advantage of mapping capabilities and ArcGIS data and extend mapping and analysis to everyone in your organization.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: GeoAI
sessions.abstract: Interested in learning more about geospatial artificial intelligence (GeoAI) in ArcGIS? GeoAI is the integration of AI, machine learning (ML), and deep learning (DL) with GIS to produce knowledge and solve geographic problems. It is a key part of spatial analysis for tasks related to clustering and pattern detection, prediction and forecasting, and information extraction from many types of data. This presentation highlights the application of ArcGIS GeoAI capabilities across imagery, 3D, unstructured text, and feature, tabular, and time-series data and workflows.

sessions.title: ArcGIS: Map Wizardry
sessions.abstract: Sit back and enjoy this inspiring cartographic ride! In this presentation, speakers share some truly mesmerizing maps to demonstrate the cartographic power and flexibility of the ArcGIS system. Go beyond the defaults and discover how you can bend ArcGIS to your every cartographic whim to bring data to life using innovative and imaginative techniques. See maps that you maybe have not even considered, let alone thought possible, and learn ideas and workflows that you can use creatively in your own work. Get inspired to create compelling maps and make them sing and dance in new and exciting ways.

EsriProducts: ArcGIS Living Atlas of the World

sessions.title: Spatial Data Science: The Art and Science of Spatial Analysis
sessions.abstract: Spatial data science is an iterative process that extends beyond the running of one tool—it is about posing questions, figuring out the appropriate data to answer those questions, finding the appropriate methods that truly answer those questions, evaluating the results, iterating until you get it right, and communicating results in compelling and effective ways. In this presentation, speakers share examples of applying this process to an important, real-world problem. The examples provide ideas and approaches to help you connect it all back to the problems that you want to help solve.

sessions.title: User Conference Closing Session
sessions.abstract: Join Jack Dangermond and Esri’s team of directors at this year’s Closing Session to finish out an incredible week of networking and collaboration.  During the Closing Session, we will share what we heard during the conference and solicit feedback on all things Esri and the Esri UC.  Join us for this special conversation with the Esri team to close out the 2026 event and look forward to 2027.

sessions.title: Mappy Hour
sessions.abstract: Welcome to Mappy Hour! In this special presentation, we're taking you behind the scenes with Esri's expert cartographers. They'll delve into four decades of the iconic Esri Map Book, offering an unparalleled retrospective on the art and science of mapmaking.
